STH_MirrorUniverse_tng_event.48.a: A ship worthy of its name!
STH_MirrorUniverse_tng_event.48.desc: The ISS Defiant (NX-74205) was a Terran Defiant-class warship that was in service to the Terran Empire Forces in the latter half of the 24th century. It was rumored to be even more heavily armed than its counterpart.
STH_MirrorUniverse_tng_event.48.name: The ISS Defiant
STH_MirrorUniverse_tng_event.49.desc: Just like the Greek god of Terror, the Deimos class strikes fear into the hearts of our foes, with its slick stealth hull and fearsome array of cutting-edge weapons the Deimos is a true terror in battle.
STH_MirrorUniverse_tng_event.49.name: The ISS Deimos
STH_MirrorUniverse_tng_event.5.desc: So you thought me dead did Sulu? I am afraid you are not so lucky and I am not so easy to kill, but fear not my Emperor, as I am not hear to claim my rightful revenge on those who tried to have me killed, I am here to help you rebuild our Empire and to help us retake our rightful place at the top of the food chain. \n\nLong live the Empire!
STH_MirrorUniverse_tng_event.5.name: Admiral Pike!
STH_MirrorUniverse_tng_event.50.desc: The Aledo, based on the Texas Class from the Prime Universe, takes the AI-driven design from that Universe and replaces it with a cut-throat Terran crew eager to put its cutting edge weapons to good use.
STH_MirrorUniverse_tng_event.50.name: The ISS Aledo
STH_MirrorUniverse_tng_event.6.desc: Out of my way... do you not see before you a legend of the glorious Empire of old? Yet I return home to find the empire in ruins and our power all but gone, but fear not for I have news that could very well change the course of our history.
STH_MirrorUniverse_tng_event.6.name: Captain Killy?
STH_MirrorUniverse_tng_event.7.desc: On my travels to return home, I have come across rumors, stories, and tales of lost relics of the old Empire, a ship of incredible power that could give us the edge and act as a rallying cry to all loyal Terrans. Let me find this ship and let us rebuild the Empire together... stronger and more deadly than ever.
STH_MirrorUniverse_tng_event.7.name: Lost Relic of the Empire
STH_MirrorUniverse_tng_event.8.desc: As the search teams reach their destination, it seems their search has been in vain, but amongst the asteroids, a faint signal can be found. \n\nThe crews move deeper into the to discover the ISS Charon, pride of Emperor Georgiou's fleet! Her magnificent space frame appears to have tossed against the asteroid, as if propelled through time and space by some mysterious force, much of the reactor core is missing, but crucially the ship appears salvagable.
STH_MirrorUniverse_tng_event.8.name: The ISS Charon
STH_MirrorUniverse_tng_event.9.desc: Arriving at the site of a great battle, the search teams immediately set to work looking for their target against the hulls of wrecked starships. Historic records list this as a great battle for the Terran Empire, against a mysterious foe called 'Khan' who claimed to have come from another version of this universe. \n\nAt the centre of the battlefield the prize awaits, the magnificent Vengeance, the great warship of Khan himself. The ship is adrift but salvageable.
STH_MirrorUniverse_tng_event.9.name: The Vengeance
STH_a_matter_of_time.1.desc: La fuente de las partículas de cronitón parece ser rastreable a un pequeño recipiente en órbita de [From.GetName]. Los sensores no pueden penetrar su casco, y parece estar a la deriva.
STH_a_matter_of_time.1.name: Cuestión de tiempo
STH_a_matter_of_time.10.desc:
El buque responde al granizo. 

 '[Root.Owner.GetCaptainTitle] [Root.GetLeaderName], muévase aproximadamente un metro a su derecha.' 

Confused, el [Root.Owner.GetCaptainTitle] se mueve a un lado como [Root.Owner. GetSpeciesName] se materializa en el puente. 

'Excelente! Ahora mi nombre es Profesor [timeHistorian.GetName] y he viajado en el tiempo dos siglos para estudiar a la tripulación del [Root.GetFleetName]. Las hazañas de esta nave y su [Root.Owner.GetCaptainTitle] [Root.GetLeaderName] son legendarias en mi tiempo '. [timeHistorian.GetSheHe] dice.
STH_a_matter_of_time.10.name: Cuestión de tiempo
STH_a_matter_of_time.15.desc: Ignorando la embarcación, [Root.GetFleetName] se prepara para salir de la órbita de [From.GetName]. Finalmente, el pequeño barco se desvanece en un pulso de partículas de cronitón.
STH_a_matter_of_time.15.name: Cuestión de tiempo
STH_a_matter_of_time.20.a: Permita que se borre la memoria.
STH_a_matter_of_time.20.desc:
El tiempo de [FromFrom.GetName] en [Root.GetFleetName] es difícil para muchos miembros de la tripulación. Un flujo constante de cuestionarios y [FromFrom.GetHerHis] no tan sutil condescendencia hacen poco para felicitar. 

Después de un tiempo, [Root.Owner.GetCaptainTitle] [Root.GetLeaderName] entabla una conversación con el viajero en el tiempo y los dos discuten el historial de [Root.Owner.GetName] hasta el año en curso. Sin embargo, [FromFrom.GetName] parece horrorizado, después de hacer referencia accidentalmente a una serie de eventos que aún no se han producido, dentro de cuatro años. 

 Suplicando que se le permita preservar la línea de tiempo, [FromFrom.GetSheHe] le ruega a [Root.Owner.GetCaptainTitle] que le permita a [FromFrom.GetHerHim] realizar un borrado de memoria de su conversación.
STH_a_matter_of_time.20.name: Cuestión de tiempo
STH_a_matter_of_time.21.desc: De acuerdo con el procedimiento, se completa el borrado de memoria. Mientras que [FromFrom.GetName] insiste en que [FromFrom.GetSheHe] está completamente capacitado para operar el dispositivo utilizado, [Root.Owner.GetCaptainTitle] [Root.GetLeaderName] no puede evitar sentir que se perdió más de esa conversación. \n\nEl día siguiente [FromFrom.GetName] sale de la nave, con la lanzadera [FromFrom.GetHerHis] desapareciendo en una explosión de partículas de cronitón.
STH_a_matter_of_time.21.name: Cuestión de tiempo
STH_a_matter_of_time.22.desc:
[FromFrom.GetName] insiste en partir de inmediato. Transmitiendo de vuelta al transbordador [FromFrom.GetHerHis], el barco comienza a alejarse. Vuelve a [Root.GetFleetName] una vez más. 

'No me dejaste otra opción en esto, me temo. Las investigaciones temporales deberán estar involucradas ahora para reparar el daño que hice a la línea de tiempo. '

El transbordador desaparece en una explosión de partículas de cronitón.
STH_a_matter_of_time.22.name: Cuestión de tiempo
STH_a_matter_of_time.25.desc:
El tiempo de [FromFrom.GetName] en [Root.GetFleetName] es difícil para muchos miembros de la tripulación. Una corriente constante de cuestionarios y [FromFrom.GetHerHis] no tan sutil condescendencia hacen poco para felicitar. 

Después de un tiempo, [Root.Owner.GetCaptainTitle] [Root.GetLeaderName] entabla una conversación con el viajero en el tiempo y los dos discuten el historial de [Root.Owner.GetName] hasta el año en curso. 

 Finalmente, el historiador declara completada la investigación [FromFrom.GetHerHis] y se prepara para partir de [Root.GetFleetName].
STH_a_matter_of_time.25.name: Cuestión de tiempo
STH_a_matter_of_time.26.desc: El profesor [FromFrom.GetName] se despide de la tripulación del [Root.GetFleetName] y regresa a la lanzadera [FromFrom.GetHerHis]. Momentos después, el recipiente desaparece en una explosión de partículas de cronitón. \n\nLas lecturas del sensor de la partida del transbordador junto con las pocas ideas sutiles sobre el funcionamiento de la academia del futuro seguramente serán lecturas interesantes para los analistas de [Root.Owner.GetName].
STH_a_matter_of_time.26.name: Cuestión de tiempo
STH_a_matter_of_time.27.desc:
El profesor [FromFrom.GetName] se despide de la tripulación del [Root.GetFleetName] y regresa a la lanzadera [FromFrom.GetHerHis]. Sin embargo, pasan varias horas y el barco no hace nada hasta que se recibe otro granizo. 

'Bueno, esto es lamentable ... la unidad de tiempo del transbordador está muerta. Irreparablemente Estoy atrapado aquí y ahora estoy seguro de que sabían que lo estaría. Una paradoja de predestinación, estoy seguro de ello ... '

El profesor ofrece proporcionar servicios [FromFrom.GetHerHis], pero no antes de configurar la autodestrucción en el transbordador [FromFrom.GetHerHis] para evitar una mayor contaminación de la línea de tiempo .
STH_a_matter_of_time.27.name: Cuestión de tiempo
STH_a_matter_of_time.30.desc: El tiempo de [FromFrom.GetName] en [Root.GetFleetName] es difícil para muchos miembros de la tripulación. Un flujo constante de cuestionarios y [FromFrom.GetHerHis] no tan sutil condescendencia hacen poco para felicitar. \n\nDespués de un tiempo, varios miembros de la tripulación han notado la desaparición de varios elementos, generalmente en partes de la nave que [FromFrom.GetName] ha estado visitando.
STH_a_matter_of_time.30.name: Cuestión de tiempo
STH_a_matter_of_time.31.desc: En pánico, [FromFrom.GetName] activa un transporte de emergencia a la lanzadera [FromFrom.GetHerHis]. Incapaz de evitar la emisión del haz, el [Root.Owner.GetCaptainTitle] ordena un [Root.Owner.GetTractorBeam] colocado en el transbordador. \n\nEl transbordador desaparece en una explosión de partículas de cronitón.
STH_a_matter_of_time.31.name: Cuestión de tiempo
STH_a_matter_of_time.32.a: El profesor era un ladrón y un saboteador ...
STH_a_matter_of_time.32.desc: En pánico, [FromFrom.GetName] activa un transporte de emergencia a la lanzadera [FromFrom.GetHerHis]. Incapaz de evitar la emisión del haz, el [Root.Owner.GetCaptainTitle] ordena un [Root.Owner.GetTractorBeam] colocado en el transbordador. Momentos después, sin embargo, una explosión mece el puente cuando se ve comprometido un relé EPS primario. \n\nEl transbordador desaparece en una explosión de partículas de cronitón.
STH_a_matter_of_time.32.name: Cuestión de tiempo
STH_a_matter_of_time.33.desc:
En pánico, [FromFrom.GetName] activa un transporte de emergencia a la lanzadera [FromFrom.GetHerHis]. El rápido trabajo del oficial científico del [Root.GetFleetName] que dispara un pulso de fermión contrarresta el proceso de emisión del haz. 

El profesor está recluido en el bergantín, y finalmente confiesa ser un estafador del siglo anterior que robó el transbordador de tiempo [FromFrom.GetHerHis] de un historiador en tiempo real. 

Mientras que el transbordador es llevado a la bahía de carga para su análisis, parece haber sido puesto en un temporizador y desaparece dentro del barco en una explosión de partículas de cronitón. Sin embargo, se han dejado algunos componentes retirados del recipiente.
STH_a_matter_of_time.33.name: Cuestión de tiempo
STH_a_matter_of_time.34.desc: Ante la acusación de robo, el profesor [FromFrom.GetName] se defiende [FromFrom.GetHerHim]. Al trabajar con la tripulación, [FromFrom.GetSheHe] puede demostrar que ninguno de los elementos enumerados ha sido robado. Una explicación que ofrece [FromFrom.GetSheHe] es que la ráfaga de cronitón inicial del transbordador [FromFrom.GetHerHis] puede haber causado una 'confusión temporal' menor entre la tripulación, una explicación con la que el equipo médico a bordo está de acuerdo. \n\nFeliz de haberse reivindicado [FromFrom.GetHerHim], el profesor sale del [Root.GetFleetName] hacia [FromFrom.GetHerHis] lanzadera, que desaparece en una explosión de partículas de cronitón.
STH_a_matter_of_time.34.name: Cuestión de tiempo
STH_a_matter_of_time.40.desc: Mientras que el profesor [FromFrom.GetName] está visiblemente molesto por la negativa de [Root.Owner.GetCaptainTitle] a formar parte del estudio [FromFrom.GetHerHis], [FromFrom.GetSheHe] acepta la decisión y regresa a [FromFrom.GetHerHis] lanzadera que desaparece en una explosión de partículas de cronitón.
STH_a_matter_of_time.40.name: Cuestión de tiempo
STH_a_matter_of_time.41.desc: Mientras que el profesor [FromFrom.GetName] está visiblemente molesto por la negativa de [Root.Owner.GetCaptainTitle] a formar parte del estudio [FromFrom.GetHerHis], [FromFrom.GetSheHe] acepta la decisión y regresa a [FromFrom.GetHerHis] lanzadera que desaparece en una explosión de partículas de cronitón. \n\n Varias horas más tarde, sin embargo, parece que se han eliminado varios componentes de la nave con una precisión casi quirúrgica, rastros débiles de partículas de cronitón en los espacios que alguna vez estuvieron.
STH_a_matter_of_time.41.name: Cuestión de tiempo
STH_a_matter_of_time.50.desc:
En pánico, [FromFrom.GetName] activa un transporte de emergencia a la lanzadera [FromFrom.GetHerHis]. El rápido trabajo del oficial científico del [Root.GetFleetName] que dispara un pulso de fermión contrarresta el proceso de emisión del haz. 

El profesor está recluido en el bergantín, y finalmente confiesa ser un estafador del siglo anterior que robó el transbordador de tiempo [FromFrom.GetHerHis] de un historiador en tiempo real. 

Mientras que el transbordador es llevado a la bahía de carga para su análisis, parece haber sido puesto en un temporizador y desaparece dentro del barco en una explosión de partículas de cronitón. Sin embargo, se han dejado algunos componentes retirados del recipiente.
STH_a_matter_of_time.50.name: Cuestión de tiempo

Incapaz de evitar la emisión del haz, el [Root.Owner.GetCaptainTitle] ordena un [Root.Owner.GetTractorBeam] colocado en el transbordador. Momentos después, sin embargo, una explosión mece el puente cuando se ve comprometido un relé EPS primario. \n\nEl transbordador desaparece en una explosión de partículas de cronitón.
STH_a_matter_of_time.51.name: Cuestión de tiempo
STH_a_matter_of_time.52.desc: En pánico, [FromFrom.GetName] activa un transporte de emergencia a la lanzadera [FromFrom.GetHerHis]. Incapaz de evitar la emisión del haz, el [Root.Owner.GetCaptainTitle] ordena un [Root.Owner.GetTractorBeam] colocado en el transbordador. \n\nEl transbordador desaparece en una explosión de partículas de cronitón.

STH_a_matter_of_time.90.desc: Si bien los sensores pudieron detectar una pequeña embarcación cerca de la explosión del cronitón, desapareció antes de que se pudiera completar un análisis completo.
STH_a_matter_of_time.90.name: Cuestión de tiempo
STH_abronath.1.a: Espero que haya valido la pena.
STH_abronath.1.desc: ¡Nuestras fuerzas han derrotado al [From.Fet.GetName] y durante los intensos combates lograron arrebatar parte de la ruina antigua reliquia conocida como Abronath de sus garras.
STH_abronath.1.name: Secuestrado Abronath
STH_abronath.101.b: ¡Destruye el Abronath y aprender lo que podamos
STH_abronath.101.desc: ¡Nuestros arqueólogos y científicos están trabajando juntos para reconstruir los misterios del Abronath§ !. Debido a los peligros involucrados, un sitio en cuarentena se ha preparado para el proyecto de reconstrucción del Abronath en  [Root.Capital.GetName] . \n\n ¡Desbloqueando todo el potencial de Abronath Puede tomar muchos años.
STH_abronath.101.name: Reconstruir el Abronath
STH_abronath.11.desc: El  [abronath_holder.GetName]  ha adquirido todas las partes del Abronath§ !, se rumoreaba en muchos sistemas estelares como una antigua arma biológica de otro tiempo. Debido a la amenaza de esta antigua reliquia, la acción rápida podría ser el curso de acción más prudente.
STH_abronath.11.name: El Abronath
STH_abronath.110.b: ¡Destruye el Abronath y aprender lo que podamos
STH_abronath.110.desc: Estudio de la Abronath ha llevado con éxito a que la primera parte se active en Abronath alojamiento. Actualmente, el dispositivo sigue siendo lamentablemente poco potente, ya que no hemos podido realizar ingeniería inversa de la fuente de energía, ya que utiliza energía a diferencia de cualquier otra encontrada anteriormente, sin embargo, nuestras divisiones científicas continuarán trabajando diligentemente.
STH_abronath.110.name: El Abronath
STH_abronath.111.b: ¡Destruye el Abronath y aprender lo que podamos
STH_abronath.111.desc: Los equipos de ingeniería han logrado enviar energía a través de la carcasa inherentemente inestable del Abronath§ !, permitiendo que se instale la segunda parte. Todos los días se siguen desbloqueando nuevos mecanismos, y nuestros científicos han determinado que este dispositivo es definitivamente un tipo de arma.
STH_abronath.111.name: El Abronath
STH_abronath.112.b: ¡Destruye el Abronath y aprender lo que podamos
STH_abronath.112.desc: Calibraciones en Abronath continúan, con las pruebas iniciales de la arma biológica que resultan en la creación de un vasto enjambre en el aire capaz de devorar y romper enlaces moleculares orgánicos. Los científicos predicen que se requerirá otro año antes de que se complete.
STH_abronath.112.name: El Abronath
STH_abronath.113.a: Excelente. Prepárelo para el despliegue en nuestros buques.
STH_abronath.113.c: Esto es una abominación. Desmóntalo y destrúyelo.
STH_abronath.113.desc: El Abronath ahora está completo, y las pruebas iniciales en materia orgánica han resultado en una aniquilación extremadamente óptima de los enlaces entre toda la materia orgánica en un formato mortal en el aire. Las simulaciones predicen que si Abronath fue liberado en un planeta no preparado, podría aniquilar a toda la población rápidamente.
STH_abronath.113.name: El Abronath
STH_abronath.132.desc: ¡Los arqueólogos han huido de [Root.GetName] debido a la reciente agitación política, y han tomado un pedazo de Abronath en su vuelo La recuperación del artefacto no es probable.
STH_abronath.132.name: Abronath Desaparecido
STH_abronath.133.a: Haz la venta. Lo usaremos para nosotros mismos.
STH_abronath.133.b: Haz la venta. Lo mantendremos a salvo.
STH_abronath.133.desc: Un extranjero que proclama ser un "arqueólogo", aunque nuestros registros muestran un gran historial criminal, nos ha presentado una oferta. ¡Por el costo de una porción de nuestro £ latinum £ latinum para el próximo año, ¡estarían dispuestos a darnos parte de la Abronath§ !. Si rechazamos esta oferta, parece que otros compradores ya están haciendo cola.
STH_abronath.133.name: El comerciante misterioso
STH_abronath.135.desc: ¡Los arqueólogos han huido de [Root.GetName] debido a la reciente agitación política, y han tomado un pedazo de Abronath en su vuelo La recuperación del artefacto no es probable.
STH_abronath.135.name: Abronath Desaparecido

STH_abronath.136.b: Haz la venta. Lo mantendremos a salvo.

Si rechazamos esta oferta, parece que otros compradores ya están haciendo cola.
STH_abronath.139.name: El comerciante misterioso
STH_abronath.2.desc: [From.GetAdj] las fuerzas han derrotado a nuestras fuerzas en la batalla y se han apoderado de la Abronath. \n\nEs un destino extraño que debamos sufrir tanto miedo y dudas sobre algo tan pequeño.
STH_abronath.2.name: Pérdida de Abronath
STH_abronath.202.desc: Los carroñeros y los comerciantes han reunido lo que pudieron de los restos del  [LastKilledCountryName] § !, y una reliquia lo ha conseguido en nuestras manos. Mencionan que es parte de algo llamado Abronath§ !.
STH_abronath.202.name: Carroñeros de [LastKilledCountryName]
STH_abronath.204.desc: ¡Los carroñeros y los comerciantes han reunido lo que pudieron de los restos del  [LastKilledCountryName] , Y una reliquia lo ha conseguido en nuestras manos. Mencionan que es parte de algo llamado Abronath.
STH_abronath.204.name: Carroñeros de [LastKilledCountryName]
STH_abronath.206.desc: Los carroñeros y los comerciantes han reunido lo que pudieron de los restos del  [LastKilledCountryName] § !, y una reliquia lo ha conseguido en nuestras manos. Mencionan que es parte de algo llamado Abronath§ !.
STH_abronath.206.name: Carroñeros de [LastKilledCountryName]
STH_abronath.211.desc: Hemos obtenido el número de componentes que conforman el misterioso dispositivo Abronath, nuestros agentes han identificado los siguientes países que también han obtenido partes del dispositivo. \n\n[r_abronath_1_country.GetName] \N-[r_abronath_2_country.GetName] \N-[r_abronath_3_country.GetName]§. \N-Tenemos varias opciones, podemos destruir nuestra pieza existente del dispositivo para mantenerla fuera de las manos del enemigo, comprar otra sección del dispositivo o intentar vender una de las piezas.
STH_abronath.211.name: El Abronath
STH_abronath.212.desc: Ahora que hemos decidido vender nuestra sección del Abronath, nuestros agentes han llegado a la conclusión de que estos países ya tienen una sección del dispositivo y podrían considerar la posibilidad de comprarnos nuestra sección del dispositivo por un precio.
STH_abronath.212.name: El Abronath
STH_abronath.213.desc: [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName], hemos sido contactados por agentes de [r_abronath_sellor.GetName] que desean vendernos una sección del Abronath por 50 lingotes de £ latinum y 5000 £ energy. Se rumorea que el dispositivo tiene el potencial de causar un ataque devastador, así que debemos elegir con cuidado.
STH_abronath.213.name: Comprar una parte del Abronath
STH_abronath.214.desc: Después de considerarlo [r_abronath_buy.GetName] han aceptado nuestros términos y comprarán nuestra parte del dispositivo Abronath. Los fondos serán transferidos en breve.
STH_abronath.214.name: [r_abronath_buy.GetName] aceptar la compra de la sección
STH_abronath.215.desc: Tras considerar que [r_abronath_reject.GetName] ha rechazado la venta de nuestra parte del dispositivo Abronath, parece que tendremos que encontrar un nuevo comprador.
STH_abronath.215.name: [r_abronath_reject.GetName] rechazar la compra
STH_abronath.216.desc: Hemos encontrado partes del Abronath disponibles en los siguientes estados. \Nn[owns_r_abronath_1.GetName] \N-[owns_r_abronath_2.GetName] \N-[owns_r_abronath_3.GetName]§. \Nuestros agentes pueden dirigirse a estos países para ver si están dispuestos a vendernos una parte del Abronath.
STH_abronath.216.desc_01: Con todas las piezas del Abronath destruidas no hay piezas para comprar o vender.
STH_abronath.216.name: El Abronath
STH_abronath.217.desc: [r_abronath_buy.GetName] nos han hecho una oferta por nuestra sección del dispositivo Abronath. Estarían dispuestos a pagar 50 lingotes de £ latino y 5000 £ energía por esta reliquia.
STH_abronath.217.name: Una oferta para una sección de Abronath
STH_abronath.218.desc: [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName], hemos completado la compra de una de las secciones que faltaban del dispositivo Abronath, lo que nos acerca mucho más a poder utilizar esta misteriosa pieza de tecnología.
STH_abronath.218.name: Compra completa
STH_abronath.219.desc: [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName], hemos visto rechazada nuestra oferta de compra de una sección del dispositivo de Abronath, si queremos obtener el dispositivo deberemos hacerlo por la fuerza o volver a intentarlo más tarde.
STH_abronath.219.name: Oferta rechazada
STH_abronath.91.desc: Ahora estamos en posesión de todas las partes del Abronath§ !, hemos podido unirlas para formar un dispositivo singular. Sigue sin poder y sus secretos requerirán más trabajo sin embargo.
STH_abronath.91.name: Reconstruir el Abronath
STH_abronath_site.101.desc: El extraño vacío biológico donde ni siquiera existen bacterias se ha reducido a una red de cavernas subterráneas, varios kilómetros debajo de la superficie de [From.Planet.GetName]. \n\nDespués de varios días de análisis, los científicos de [Root.GetName] han podido trazar una ruta al centro de este vacío biológico, y al hacerlo han descubierto que la red de cavernas no se forma naturalmente y puede ser El signo de una civilización antigua.
STH_abronath_site.101.name: Nullzone subterráneo
STH_abronath_site.102.a: Ordene fiesta de aterrizaje para desplegar sondas.
STH_abronath_site.102.desc: Un examen más detallado de la red de cavernas subterráneas ha descubierto una red aún más extensa que abarca cientos de kilómetros por debajo de la primera, y las cavernas esculpidas en roca pura. El despliegue de sondas especialmente modificadas a lo largo de la red de túneles en [From.Planet.GetName] podría conducir a descubrir una ruta a la cámara más grande, una que abarca cincuenta kilómetros de ancho. Las tecnologías actuales no pueden reproducir tal efecto, así que lo que sea que haya hecho esa caverna está más allá de nuestra tecnología actual. \n\nCon un mapeo cuidadoso, deberíamos poder encontrar un punto de entrada y obtener más información sobre lo que se encuentra debajo de la superficie.
STH_abronath_site.102.name: Nullzone subterráneo
STH_abronath_site.103.desc: Se ha descubierto un punto de entrada a la red subterránea en [From.Planet.GetName], y las sondas modificadas han comenzado su mapeo de la red de cavernas. \n\nHasta ahora solo se han descubierto ruinas, en grandes edificios que parecen extremadamente avanzados. . Pequeñas piezas de tecnología rota dispersan el suelo, una fuente de información tal vez. Tampoco hay signos de daño de armas externas para ver qué causó la extinción de esta civilización. Se ha ubicado una extraña capa delgada de materia inorgánica en muchas de las viviendas, que parece volverse más gruesa a medida que avanzan las cavernas por las que descienden las sondas. El ambiente parece seguro y puede ser prudente enviar un grupo de aterrizaje.
STH_abronath_site.103.name: Nullzone subterráneo
STH_abronath_site.104.desc: El grupo de aterrizaje ha llegado a la cámara central de [From.Planet.GetName], que parece ser una gran caverna de la ciudad capaz de albergar miles de millones en un punto, sin embargo, ahora ni siquiera existen microbios. \n\nLa investigación adicional de los edificios revela muchos escritos y estructuras arquitectónicas únicas que parecen imposibles con los materiales en funcionamiento. Una gran estructura en la parte norte de la ciudad parece tener una alta concentración de materia inorgánica a su alrededor, que nuestros equipos han podido determinar que es una sustancia manufacturada, aunque ahora inerte.
STH_abronath_site.104.name: Nullzone subterráneo
STH_abronath_site.105.desc: Existen grandes cantidades de registros y escritos parcialmente destruidos dentro del edificio más grande, lo que proporciona una idea de la civilización que una vez vivió en este mundo. Dentro de la gran estructura se encuentra una cámara rodeada de lo que solo se puede traducir como advertencias o precauciones, y una palabra repetida una y otra vez, 'Abronath'. Acostarse con esta cámara es parte de un extraño artefacto, a diferencia de muchos de los otros que impregnan esta ciudad. Parece como si hubiera sido golpeado por un arma de energía, y solo está parcialmente intacto.
STH_abronath_site.105.name: Nullzone subterráneo
STH_abronath_site.106.desc: Otros exámenes de las ruinas han descubierto varias facetas e información de la civilización que las construyó, lo que ha permitido a nuestros científicos obtener más conocimientos y avances en el campo de la construcción de naves.
STH_abronath_site.106.name: Mariposa social
STH_abronath_site.107.desc: Otros exámenes de las ruinas han descubierto varias facetas e información de la civilización que las construyó, lo que ha permitido a nuestros científicos obtener más conocimientos y avances en el campo de la biología.
STH_abronath_site.107.name: Mariposa social
STH_abronath_site.108.desc: Otros exámenes de las ruinas han descubierto varias facetas de terraformación e información de la civilización que las construyó, lo que ha permitido a nuestros científicos obtener más conocimientos y avances en el campo de los nuevos mundos.
STH_abronath_site.108.name: Mariposa social
STH_abronath_site.201.desc: Los exámenes iniciales de la brecha masiva en la superficie muestran que este mundo fue una vez un mundo potencialmente viable, hasta que una antigua catástrofe desgarró una herida en su superficie dejando una superficie turbia de manto hirviendo expuesto en el fondo de los abismos. La extraña ausencia de vida o bacterias biológicas alrededor de la corteza destrozada es otra curiosidad.
STH_abronath_site.201.name: Corteza rota
STH_abronath_site.202.a: Ordene fiesta de aterrizaje para desplegar sondas
STH_abronath_site.202.desc: La investigación adicional de la poderosa herida muestra una estructura artificial oscurecida parcialmente detectada en lo profundo de la corteza. Parece ser el camino de entrada a una estructura incrustada dentro de la pared del abismo. \n\nLos científicos creen que podemos llevar a un grupo de aterrizaje a los túneles cercanos para examinarlo más de cerca y modificar algunas sondas en el sitio para encontrar el camino hacia la entrada.
STH_abronath_site.202.name: Corteza rota
STH_abronath_site.203.desc: Al descender profundamente en los abismos a través de rápeles de gravedad, el equipo visitante ha llegado a la estructura. Tras una inspección más cercana, parece que la estructura es todo lo que queda de una estructura una vez mucho más grande que durante mucho tiempo ha sido destruida y caída en las ondulantes olas del manto debajo. \n\nLos materiales utilizados en la fabricación son desconocidos para nosotros, pero parece que ser antinaturalmente fuerte Se ha asegurado una entrada.
STH_abronath_site.203.name: Corteza rota
STH_abronath_site.204.desc: Las advertencias y los signos de peligro parecen rodear las cámaras que conducen a la estructura, que parece ser parcialmente funcional. De lo que nuestro equipo visitante ha reunido, parece que ocurrió una catástrofe minera masiva y destrozó el planeta. \n\nDetalles de numerosas instalaciones mineras automatizadas ubicadas en lugares en lo que ahora es el abismo, pero no hay registros de sus creadores. Sin embargo, una cámara parece tener importancia, pero los corredores se han colapsado parcialmente y requerirán excavación.
STH_abronath_site.204.name: Corteza rota
STH_abronath_site.205.desc: Las advertencias y las señales de peligro se vuelven más frecuentes a medida que se abre el camino a la cámara. Principalmente intacto debido a la extraña ausencia de bacterias que puedan haber degradado los sistemas, esta sala puede contener una gran cantidad de conocimiento. \n\n Sin embargo, un extraño artefacto se destaca, uno que parece haberse roto, todas las referencias apuntan a esto es algo llamado Abronath.
STH_abronath_site.205.name: Corteza rota
STH_abronath_site.206.desc: Otros exámenes de la instalación han descubierto varias facetas e información de la civilización que los construyó, lo que ha permitido a nuestros científicos obtener más conocimientos y avances en el campo de la construcción de naves.
STH_abronath_site.206.name: Mariposa social
STH_abronath_site.207.desc: Otros exámenes de la instalación han descubierto varias facetas e información de la civilización que los construyó, lo que ha permitido a nuestros científicos obtener más conocimientos y avances en el campo de la biología.
STH_abronath_site.207.name: Mariposa social
STH_abronath_site.208.desc: Otros exámenes de la instalación han descubierto varias facetas de terraformación e información de la civilización que las construyó, lo que ha permitido a nuestros científicos obtener más conocimientos y avances en el campo de los nuevos mundos.
STH_abronath_site.208.name: Mariposa social
STH_abronath_site.301.desc: Los escaneos iniciales de la aguja en espiral en la superficie muestran materiales de construcción extremadamente densos y antiguas vías mecánicas que atraviesan toda la estructura del edificio. \n\n Un grupo de aterrizaje debería poder asegurar el sitio y encontrar un punto de entrada al subsuelo. partes de la estructura.
STH_abronath_site.301.name: Aguja rota
STH_abronath_site.302.desc: Si bien la mayoría de las vías electrónicas se han degradado, parte de la estructura subterránea parece haber estado más protegida del paso del tiempo. \n\n Seguir las vías parece conducir más profundamente bajo tierra hacia su fuente de energía, donde posiblemente podríamos encendernos Algunos de los sistemas.
STH_abronath_site.302.name: Aguja rota
STH_abronath_site.303.desc: La exploración adicional de las consolas eléctricas de la aguja ha revelado un acceso limitado a los sistemas de agujas, pero también apunta hacia una cámara central que parece ser una sala de control cerca de la parte superior de la torre anterior, pero para llegar allí, nuestros equipos deben pasar por un gran sala llena de drones humanoides. \n\n Liderando a un equipo visitante a través de la aguja hacia la cámara central, el sistema de energía parece haber reactivado parcialmente parte de la aguja, lo que está causando fluctuaciones de poder peligrosas.
STH_abronath_site.303.name: Aguja rota
STH_abronath_site.304.desc: Tras un examen y desmontaje adicionales de uno de los drones más intactos, parecen ser drones robóticos de minería avanzados que utilizaron esta aguja como un centro central para la minería en todo el planeta. \n\n Sin embargo, no hay signos de vida en ninguna parte de esta instalación. , y el pasillo ahora está abierto hacia lo que parece ser la sala de control central
STH_abronath_site.304.name: Aguja rota
STH_abronath_site.305.desc: Las advertencias y las señales de peligro parecen rodear esta cámara, que no parece ser una sala de control, sino una cámara de activación de algún tipo. Principalmente intacto debido a la extraña ausencia de cualquier bacteria que pueda haber degradado los sistemas, esta sala podría contener una gran cantidad de conocimiento. \n\n Sin embargo, un extraño artefacto se destaca, uno que parece haberse roto, todas las referencias apuntan a esto es algo llamado Abronath.
STH_abronath_site.305.name: Aguja rota
STH_abronath_site.306.desc: Intentar realizar ingeniería inversa en parte de la tecnología que queda dentro de la aguja rota nos ha permitido descubrir varios avances de ingeniería de la civilización que los construyó, lo que ha permitido a nuestros científicos obtener una mayor comprensión y avances en el campo de los materiales.
STH_abronath_site.306.name: Reingeniería de la Ingeniería
STH_abronath_site.307.desc: Intentar realizar ingeniería inversa en parte de la tecnología que queda dentro de la aguja rota nos ha permitido descubrir varios avances de ingeniería de la civilización que los construyó, lo que ha permitido a nuestros científicos obtener más conocimientos y avances en el campo de la propulsión.
STH_abronath_site.307.name: Reingeniería de la Ingeniería
STH_abronath_site.308.desc: Intentar aplicar ingeniería inversa a parte de la tecnología que queda dentro de la aguja rota nos ha permitido descubrir varios avances de ingeniería de la civilización que los construyó, lo que ha permitido a nuestros científicos obtener más conocimientos y avances en el campo de la industria.
STH_abronath_site.308.name: Reingeniería de la Ingeniería
STH_acamaria_story.1.b: Deja que los acamarianos se encarguen de esto.
STH_acamaria_story.1.desc: Un puesto de avanzada [Root.GetName] ha sido encontrado saqueado y despojado de todo lo valioso. La sangre encontrada en la estación era un raro compuesto de hierro y cobre, único de los acamarianos. Esto sugiere que la estación fue asaltada por los recolectores, una secta nómada de acamarianos que abandonó el mundo natal hace décadas para continuar con sus tradiciones pendencieras y pugnaz que el mundo natal deseaba dejar en el pasado.
STH_acamaria_story.1.name: Sangre acamariana
STH_acamaria_story.100.desc_01: A pesar de los esfuerzos de mediación de [Root.GetName], los Reunidores han movilizado una flota para poner fin a la interferencia acamariana en su forma de vida.
STH_acamaria_story.100.desc_02: Hartos de los intentos acamarianos de capturarlos y controlarlos, los recolectores se han rebelado contra los acamarianos.
STH_acamaria_story.101.desc_01: Los acamarianos han conseguido capturar o matar a un número suficiente de recolectores para que dejen de ser un problema.
STH_acamaria_story.101.desc_02: A pesar de nuestros intentos de mediación, los acamarianos han decidido acorralar a los recolectores y obligarlos a reintegrarse en el mundo natal.
STH_acamaria_story.2.a: Los Recolectores serán encontrados y llevados ante la justicia.
STH_acamaria_story.2.desc: Se ha establecido contacto con los acamarianos. No han podido localizar a los Reunidores, pero dicen que esperan que con la ayuda de [Root.GetName] se pueda hacer.
STH_acamaria_story.3.desc: Los acamarianos nos han hablado de un campamento de recolectores en un grupo cercano. Una vez allí, los escáneres encontraron lecturas de señales de vida, radiación térmica y emisiones de dióxido de carbono que eran indicativas de combustión en Gamma Hromi II.\NDespués de una investigación más profunda, esta es, de hecho, la ubicación de un grupo de recolectores, y mientras que algunos desconfían de las restricciones acamarianas y de la participación de [Root.GetName], muchos de ellos están dispuestos a abrir un diálogo con su mundo natal sobre la posibilidad de reconciliación.
STH_acamaria_story.4.desc: Las conversaciones continúan, aunque ha surgido una complicación: un recolector y miembro del clan Lornak fue encontrado muerto. Por lo demás, el hombre estaba completamente sano; una muerte natural no parece probable. Esto ha puesto algo de tensión en las negociaciones.
STH_acamaria_story.5.a: Debemos averiguar quién es el responsable.
STH_acamaria_story.5.desc: Una investigación más detallada del clan Lornak fallecido reveló que su ataque al corazón fue causado por un microvirus que sólo afecta a un pequeño subgrupo de la especie acamariana. La probabilidad de que un microvirus como éste ocurra de forma natural es casi nula, lo que significa que probablemente fue diseñado y utilizado de forma selectiva para asesinar al clan. Las relaciones entre los acamarianos y los recolectores se han visto gravemente afectadas por este acontecimiento.
STH_acamaria_story.6.desc: Se descubrió que la asesina era una de las ayudantes del líder acamariano. Formaba parte de un clan que se creía muerto hace mucho tiempo, y se vengaba del clan Lornak por la masacre de su pueblo hace muchas décadas.\N-A través de una alteración celular, su envejecimiento se ralentizó lo suficiente como para permitirle vengarse de la matanza de su pueblo. Al encontrar a la asesina, los planes para interrumpir las conversaciones se detuvieron, y los Reunidores han acordado volver a vivir en el mundo natal de los Acamarianos.\NAdemás, el estudio de la alteración celular utilizada en la asesina probablemente conducirá a algunos conocimientos valiosos.
STH_admonition.1002.desc: Our secret agencies recruit only the most cunning and skilled Romulan men and women, who have proven that they have the unique skills that are demanded from all Tal Shiar Operative, or maybe the rumours are true and there is an even more discreet organisation that requires talented Romulans.A high turnover in agents is a hazard of the job and so we must constantly look for those leaders who have the talent we need. Review a list of potential recruits.
STH_admonition.1003.a: Dispatch [tal_shiar_recruit.GetName] to the Tal Shiar facility.
STH_admonition.1003.desc: One of our leaders, [tal_shiar_recruit.GetName], has demonstrated the skills and prowess to be considered for recruitment into the Tal Shiar operation. \n\nWe can now decide whether to approach [tal_shiar_recruit.GetName] and offer them a position within the agency, they would need to complete some 'tests' to assure their handlers that they are up to the challenge... be warned not all agents are suitable.
STH_admonition.1003.name: Tal Shiar Recruitment Candidate
STH_admonition.1004.desc: Agent [tal_shiar_recruit.GetName] has successfully completed their training and is ready to join the Tal Shiar as an agent of the empire. These agents receive special training and have a unique skill set which allows them to serve the Empire at the discretion of the powerful Tal Shiar.
STH_admonition.1004.name: Agent successfully recruited
STH_admonition.1005.desc: Agent [tal_shiar_recruit.GetName] has failed to completed their training and is not ready to join the Tal Shiar as an agent of the empire. They will be eligible to apply to rejoin the Tal Shiar for some time and should consider themselves lucky to be able to continue to serve the Praetor.
STH_admonition.1005.name: Agent recruitment failed
STH_admonition.1006.desc: Leader [zhat_vash_recruit_1.GetName] has come to our attention as a potential new recruit for the Zhat Vash. They would first need to prove themselves via the Admonition, which has claimed the lives of many who have faced it previously, if they are ready to delve deeper into the mysteries of the universe then they should arrive at the coordinates provided and undertake the mysterious Admonition, but be warned few survive the process.
STH_admonition.1006.name: Zhat Vash recruitment candidate
STH_admonition.101.b: It is nothing to be concerned about.
STH_admonition.101.desc: [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName], we' are getting reports across the Romulan system that the primary Star, Eisn, is behaving strangely. Solar activity appears to be up considerably and the star itself seems far more active then it should. \n\nThe majority of our leading experts tell us that it is nothing to worry about and it will pass as quickly as it begun.
STH_admonition.101.name: Strange readings from the Romulan Star
STH_admonition.102.b: This is still nothing to worry about.
STH_admonition.102.desc: [Root.GetRulerTitle] it is troubling to say but the strange readings from the Primary Star of Romulus have only gotten worse, the outer layers of the sun's corona appear to becoming less and less stable. There are now more leading scientists that are voicing concerns that our stars behaviour is not a passing phase. Calls from the Romulan Astrophysical Academy to dedicate funding to investigate this strange behaviour are increasing, yet still many urge caution and to avoid panicking the masses and disrupting the Romulan order.
STH_admonition.102.name: Strange readings from the Romulan Star
STH_admonition.103.b: They must accept there is nothing to fear.
STH_admonition.103.desc: Damage reports across the planets Romulus and Remus are still coming in, it appears that a massive corona eruption occurred in the early hours of the Romulan morning wreaking havoc across the twin worlds. \n\nThere can be no denying the situation playing out within the Romulan home system and the strange goings on within the star at the heart of the system. Voices of concern are now ringing out across both the public and private sectors and even the normally stoical plebeians are daring to speak up and demand action.
STH_admonition.103.name: Solar flare causes system wide damage
STH_admonition.104.desc: The results of the investigation have been checked and cross verified by scientists at the Romulan Ministry of Science and the Romulan Astrophysical Academy and both institutes have concluded the same thing, for reasons that neither can explain the primary star of the Romulan homeworld is quickly heading towards a Supernova. The event cannot be explained as the star should have most of its lifetime ahead of it. However there is no time to dwell on why... an explosion of this magnitude would destroy the system and potentially cripple the empire.\n\nWe must convene the highest ranking individuals within the Star Empire immediately.
STH_admonition.104.name: The Sun is in great danger
STH_admonition.106.desc: There is only one option available to us [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName], we must call for aid, the Star Empire simply does not have the resources to maintain order across our border worlds and evacuate the nearly 18 billion people on the homeworld, without taking into account the pitiful Remans on their dark black rock and the outlining colonies that branched off from Romulus since its founding. \n\nOur advisors have recommended the following empires as potential saviours, the cloyingly sycophantic United Federation of Planets will be dying to help us, the feral Klingon Empire, the Cardassian Union would desire nothing more to hold us in their debt and finally the Tholian's have the ability to raise a fleet fast.
STH_admonition.106.name: Romulan Sun Supernova
STH_admonition.107.a: We will aid you in your time of need.
STH_admonition.107.c: Haha! You deserve this fate Romulan scum.
STH_admonition.107.desc: Greeting [romulan_nova_rescue_country.GetRulerTitle] [romulan_nova_rescue_country.GetRulerName], I am [romulan_star_empire.leader.GetName] of the Romulan Star Empire. As you may now be aware the star Eisn has been facing difficulties, but what you may not know is that the star is on the verge of going Supernova. We require immediate aid to help evacuate our worlds and save countless lives. Whilst we may not have always been the best of terms, our people need you now, will you aid the Romulan Star Empire?
STH_admonition.107.name: The Romulan Empire requires aid
STH_admonition.107.qb: What do you require from us?
STH_admonition.108.desc: We have agreed to provide aid to the Romulan Star Empire, the admiralty has agreed to review which of our core planets should be used as a staging ground for a fleet of rescue vessels to be raised and dispatched to Romulus to render aid and rescue as many citizens of their empire as possible. This will be a huge undertaking costing resources and funds, many of our own people do not support the proposal, but we have deemed it the right thing to do. The newly created task force will notify us when the next action is ready.
STH_admonition.108.name: Romulan Sun Supernova
STH_admonition.109.desc: We have rejected the Romulans plea for help, leaving them to face the upcoming apocalypse by themselves. The news comes as a relief to many who believe in the '[Root.GetSpeciesName] First' movement and do not believe now is the time to over extend our resources in the face of an uncertain future. \n\nIt is however worth remembering that the Romulan people have always had long memories and should they recover from the fate that awaits them, our lack of action will be the first thing they remember.
STH_admonition.109.name: Romulan Sun Supernova
STH_admonition.11.desc: FOR THE EYES OF THE CONCLAVE ONLY: Our operatives have uncovered that a foreign power has produced a leader with a specialism in synthetic life, this dangerous individual could make serious advances in the field of AI and synthetic creations and bring us one step closer to the foretold destruction. We must investigate the source of this report and take action.
STH_admonition.11.name: Zhat Vash - Intelligence Report
STH_admonition.110.desc: The [romulan_nova_rescue_country.GetName] have agreed to help us by providing the aid and ships that we have requested. This offer will allow us to ensure the safety of the senate and the senior members of the navy. With our leadership intact we can ensure that the Romulan Star Empire will continue on into the future and rebuild with only the losses of the lower classes that the [romulan_nova_rescue_country.GetName] cannot save. \n\nWe will not let this destroy us.
STH_admonition.110.name: Romulan Sun Supernova
STH_admonition.111.desc: Plans have been made and our most senior officials, admirals and states people have been rounded up and are gathering for the evacuation of Romulus, we must send transports to the planet to begin the process.
STH_admonition.111.name: Romulan Sun Supernova
STH_admonition.113.b: We need to build up our stockpile.
STH_admonition.113.desc: The task force set up to provide aid to Romulus has reached its conclusion and have recommended the planet [romulan_nova_rescue_planet.GetName] to use as a staging ground to assemble the rescue flotilla. The resources and access to the nearby shipyards will make it an ideal location to begin construction and gathering of the required materials.
STH_admonition.113.name: Romulan Sun Supernova
STH_admonition.114.desc: The first wave of transports have been built over [romulan_nova_rescue_planet.GetName] and whilst the design and quality of the work is of a high standard, progress has been slow and the volume of work is building up. The project leads are complaining that there is just to much work for the teams to complete in too short a time and even if we devote all our resources to this operation the odds of completing the work in time are low.
STH_admonition.114.name: Romulan Sun Supernova
STH_admonition.115.desc: The new Synthetic workforce have managed to quadruple the production rates at our [romulan_nova_rescue_planet.GetName] facilities. The Synthetic units are able to work longer and faster than their biological counterparts, we should now be back on track to ensure that we can deliver the full fleet of ships ahead of the deadlines provided by the taskforce.
STH_admonition.115.name: Romulan Sun Supernova
STH_admonition.116.desc: Our next checkpoint review has been completed and thanks to the hard work by [admonition_scientist.GetName] and their team we are on course to produce the biggest fleet produced in the history of [romulan_nova_rescue_country.GetName]. Our thoughts should start to turn towards preparing the fleet for inspection by the Admiral in charge of leading this undertaking.
STH_admonition.116.name: Romulan Sun Supernova
STH_admonition.117.desc: Our science and engineering teams have approached us with a radical solution to address the production deficit. The use of a Synthetics workforce has never been considered within the [romulan_nova_rescue_country.GetName] before now, however the proposal from one of the countries leading synthetic experts includes adding a workforce of 'Soong-Type' Androids who can work faster, longer and more accurately. If accepted the scientist will offer their services to lead the new android workforce and guarantee the completion of the new fleet ahead of schedule. [admonition_scientist.GetName] working with the leading expert [synth_expert_1.GetName] using the very latest in synthetic technology vow to complete the work ahead of schedule.
STH_admonition.117.name: Romulan Sun Supernova
STH_admonition.118.desc: [admonition_scientist.GetName] has assembled their workforce of androids and is ready to deploy them to the taskforce, the Androids will work alongside the rest of the workers in a historical undertaking.
STH_admonition.118.name: Romulan Sun Supernova
STH_admonition.12.desc: Our failure to successfully complete the investigation has meant that the scientist is now out of our reach and free to continue their research into artificial life. For your sake I hope this does not produce positive results.
STH_admonition.12.name: Zhat Vash - Intelligence Report
STH_admonition.120.desc: Following the incredible progress made by the android workforce, the fleet is ready for an inspection by the Admiral selected to lead the mission into Romulan space. The hull plating has been polished and the consoles wiped down as [admonition_scientist.GetName] is ready to present the fleet for inspection.
STH_admonition.120.name: Fleet ready for inspection
STH_admonition.122.desc_01:
Praetor, all may not be lost, the commander of the Tal Shiar has contacted us via a private link, they claim to speak on behalf of an organisation called the Zhat Vash. They claim to have access to information which could be used to quell the issues within our sun, but it comes at a price. They claim that buried deep within the Borg Artifact is a technology assimilated by the Borg Collective that could be used to stabilize the homeworld's star.

The price for this information is the freedom to sabotage our allies synthetic programme which they are using to build their rescue fleets.

The mysterious figure assures us that there will be no fall back on the Romulan Star Empire, after all, who would sabotage their savours?

This would be a huge gamble.
STH_admonition.122.desc_02: We have been contacted by our liaison within the Tal Shiar, they claim to have information from a group called the Zhat Vash, which could save the homeworld's sun, but it comes at a price and an opportunity for revenge on the those within [romulan_nova_rescue_country.GetName] who previously abandoned us to a fiery death. Deep within the archives of the Borg Artifact hides a technology from a species long assimilated which could be used to save our homeworld.\n\nIn exchange they want to launch a covert attack on the Android factories on the planet [romulan_nova_rescue_planet.GetName], this could be a chance to deal the revenge so many Romulans crave and save our home.
STH_admonition.122.name: Romulan Sun Supernova
STH_admonition.123.a: A clear message from the Zhat Vash.
STH_admonition.123.desc: It appears it was unwise to reject the Zhat Vash's request especially when the fate of the Romulan Star Empire is on the line, in a clear message to the senate former ruler [romulan_ruler_1.GetName] was assassinated, but not before 'approving' the Zhat Vash's plans, it is likely the next ruler will be more open to ideas presented by this shadowy organisation.
STH_admonition.123.name: Romulan Leader assassinated
STH_admonition.124.desc: [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName], devastating news! The planet [romulan_nova_rescue_planet.GetName] burns, our fleets in orbit are in ruin, it appears the Android workforce which had proved so valuable has turned on us. Reports from across the planet's surface and shipbuilding facilities indicate that thousands are dead or injured and sound of explosions ring out across the world.
STH_admonition.124.name: Children of [romulan_nova_rescue_planet.GetName]
STH_admonition.125.desc: The rogue synths continue to destroy civilian and military installation with ruthless precision, efficiency and dedication that only a machine could produce. The synths have begun bombarding the surface, igniting flammable vapors in the stratosphere and causing massive damage to the planet's infrastructure.\n\nA number of ships docked at the shipyards have also been compromised.
STH_admonition.125.name: Children of [romulan_nova_rescue_planet.GetName]
STH_admonition.126.desc: The rogue synths have captured a number of vessels and are looking to make a final bombing run against [romulan_nova_rescue_planet.GetName], should they be successful the death toll would be staggering, all ships in the area must convene on the androids vessels and put an end to this attack before any more lives are lost. \n\nThe attack has been devastating, but we could lose the whole planet if those attack ships are allowed to complete their attack.
STH_admonition.126.name: Children of [romulan_nova_rescue_planet.GetName]
STH_admonition.127.desc_01: Reports are coming in across various channels that the [romulan_nova_rescue_country.GetSpeciesName] world of [romulan_nova_rescue_planet.GetName] has been devastated in an unprovoked attack by a group of rogue A500 synthetic worker units. The planet's atmosphere has been described as an endless fire and the death toll appears to be devastating. The galactic community watches and waits for the official response from those involved.
STH_admonition.127.desc_02: The attack against the [romulan_nova_rescue_country.GetSpeciesName] world of [romulan_nova_rescue_planet.GetName] was spectacular, most of the ship building infrastructure has been crippled and the atmosphere of the planet continues to burn. Our spies confirm that no one suspects our involvement.
STH_admonition.127.name: Devastating Synth attack against [romulan_nova_rescue_country.GetName]
STH_admonition.129.d: We must reform the UFP to survive.
STH_admonition.129.desc_01: The empire rages with fury at the devastation wrought on one of its major colonies, the howls warning the dead of new warriors approaching the gates of Sto-vo-Kor fill the air. Whilst the synthetic units lie defeated at the hands of our warriors, there is little honour to be found in defeating enemies that have no soul or no honour of their own. Many within the High Council chambers are calling for the immediate destruction of all remaining synthetic units and the destruction of the remaining research.\n\nTime has come to start looking at repairing and healing the damage done.
STH_admonition.129.desc_02: The red plains of Mars are still on fire, the once proud world lies in tatters as a result of the rogue synths devastating attack against the planet and the Utopia Planitia fleet yards. The death toll continues to rise, and with each passing day more fatalities come in. Outpouring of grief and anger come in from across the Federation worlds. A motion to ban the use of synthetic life is already being whispered in the streets of Paris, London and in the icy caves on Andoria.\n\nBefore any further actions can be taken, we must look to start the repair work, sending any available Heroic Engineer should speed up the process.
STH_admonition.129.desc_03: All of Cardassian mourns the lives lost on [romulan_nova_rescue_planet.GetName], the once proud facilities and shipyards, the pride of Cardassian engineering still burn and the fires on the surface still rage with a fury that matches the Cardassian peoples hurt pride. The streets on Cardassia prime bristle with the topic of synthetic use and a call for a blanket ban on this technology and a complete removal of all remaining units.\n\nOur next action should be to begin the repair work and mend the damage.
STH_admonition.129.desc_04: The Tholian worlds are use to fire and the heat, but even by the standards seen on Tholia Prime the destruction caused by the A500 synth attack was particularly devastating. The fury and anger from across the Tholian Assembly burns hot and the anger towards artificial life is simmering.
STH_admonition.129.name: Children of [romulan_nova_rescue_planet.GetName]
STH_admonition.13.desc: Our operatives have successfully uncovered the name of the enemy scientist foolishly furthering the research into AI. [zhat_vash_sci_target_leader2.GetName] of the [zhat_vash_sci_target_leader2_country.GetName] has made a name for themselves within the scientific community for their breakthrough work in the field of synthetics and must be dealt with appropriately.
STH_admonition.13.name: Zhat Vash - Intelligence Report
STH_admonition.130.a.tooltip: The United Federation of Planets changes its ethics to egalitarian, militarist and xenophobic with the civic The strongest survive.
STH_admonition.130.a: Now is the time for change.
STH_admonition.130.desc: In the wake of the tragedy that occured on Mars, the United Federation of Planets has spent some time looking into its role within the galactic community, the voices that call for a Federation first policy have grown to an almost deafening number. Member worlds have fiercely debated a radical shift in policy within the Federation and now demand a shift to Starfleet's mission statement.\n\nAll that is needed now is the final agreement and the United Federation of Planets will change it's outlook and become more inward facing with a Federation first mentality.
STH_admonition.130.name: Major United Federation of Planet Reforms
STH_admonition.131.desc: Since its creation the United Federation of Planets has embraced outside species and been a beacon of liberty ready to support anyone in need. Following the events on Mars it appears their council has taken the decision to close their borders and adopt a xenophobic attitude.
STH_admonition.131.name: Major policy shift in the United Federation of Planets
STH_admonition.133.desc: There has been a further solar eruption from the Romulan star, the dying star continues to cause damages to anything in its path. The inhabitants of the system can only hope for a swift evacuation or a miracle to save them.
STH_admonition.133.name: Solar eruption from the Romulan Star
STH_admonition.134.desc: Praise the Romulan gods! \n\nThe scientists trawling through the Borg Artifact have managed to find the promised technology that could save the Star. A species only referred to as Species 5010, the Kessok, appear to have had a technology which can influence the solar stability of a star. The plans appear to be largely intact and with a few adjustments could be used to save Eisn. In order to save the sun we must detonate a trilithium device combined with the Kessok solar-former device at the outer edge of the sun's corona.
STH_admonition.134.name: Salvation for the Romulan Star
STH_admonition.135.desc: The cries of joy sweep across the cities and provinces across the twin planets of Romulus and Remus from the capital to the valley of Chula and even down the dilithium mines of Remus. The state of fear and panic that was hanging over the empire have been removed and Romulans are free to plan their future once again.
STH_admonition.135.name: Romulan Star saved
STH_admonition.136.desc: With little warning the event many feared and most knew would happen finally occurred, the Romulan star explodes in a flash of brilliant pure white light that would blind and destroy sensory equipment. Those still on Romulus would see the nights sky light up as if it was the middle of the brightest day. The shockwave that follows will destroy any individual, ship and planet left in the system. \n\nEscape is the only option.
STH_admonition.136.name: Romulan star goes Supernova
STH_admonition.137.desc: The planet Remus has long suffered under its harsh upbringing, chained to its sibling in a death grip. Its final moments were swift as the solar shockwave hit the tortured world ripping the planet and giving it one final moment of freedom before exploding into a thousand pieces.
STH_admonition.137.name: Romulan star goes Supernova
STH_admonition.138.desc: The heart of the Romulan Star Empire since the first individuals left Vulcan, the planet Romulus has long stood as a symbol of the power and unbending will of the Romulan people. A planet and tradition that had survived generations ended in fire, ash and brimstone and the shockwave hit the planet destroying everything in its path.
STH_admonition.138.name: Romulan star goes Supernova
STH_admonition.139.desc: The unthinkable has happened, the planet's Romulus, Remus and their parent stars destroyed in a Supernova of unexplained origins. But the Empire must prevail, find a new capital and begin the process of rebuilding and restoring all that has been lost. Other empires will look to capitalise and many displaced Romulus will look for new homes with other powers. Evacuating the ruling elite and the senate will allow us to begin the process of restoring the Romulan Empire to its former glory.
STH_admonition.139.name: The Romulan System Destroyed
STH_admonition.14.desc: Council, our operatives within [zhat_vash_sci_target_leader2_country.GetName] arranged a private 'meeting' with [zhat_vash_sci_target_leader2.GetName] where by they were successfully convinced that the this field of research is no longer healthy for them to continue with. Thanks to our advanced processes they will not remember the encounter and as an added bonus we managed to obtain useful intelligence and research from their databases.
STH_admonition.14.name: Zhat Vash - Mission Successful
STH_admonition.140.desc: Our scans have confirmed what many knew was coming for months, the Romulan sun has gone Supernova destroying all life and habitable worlds within the system. The shockwave was felt as far away as Bolax and observable from across the galaxy. The loss of life is imaginable and the future of the Romulan Star Empire is now very uncertain.
STH_admonition.140.name: The Romulan System Destroyed
STH_admonition.141.a: Good news for the Romulan Empire.
STH_admonition.141.desc: It looks like the Romulan Star Empire have found a way of saving their own sun, at present it is unclear how they managed to do this or what technology they used to save themselves. There is no doubt that the Romulan's went to any length to save their homeworld nor have they shown much sympathy for the damaged caused to [romulan_nova_rescue_planet.GetName] but one would expect little else from the Star Empire.
STH_admonition.141.name: Romulan Star Saved
STH_admonition.142.b: The Romulan Star Empire Still lives.
STH_admonition.142.desc: The brutal destruction of our home, Romulus, and many of our core colony worlds has left the Romulan Star Empire in a mortally wounded state. Many within the Empire consider the old system destroyed along with the ruins of our homeworld, and that a new republic should be born from the death and chaos of the supernova that has devastated our worlds. \n\nThe Romulan Free State would represent the start of a new era for the empire.
STH_admonition.142.name: The Romulan Free State
STH_admonition.143.desc: Following the destruction of the Romulan star, the remaining authorities within the Star Empire have reformed the fractured empire as the Romulan Free State. \n\nOnly time will tell how this new power will differ from the Romulan Star Empire.
STH_admonition.143.name: The Romulan Free State
STH_admonition.145.desc: Tireless work has taken place by the relief teams to aid [romulan_nova_rescue_planet.GetName], relief teams were able to stabilize the damaged ship building facilities and put out the majority of the raging fires, the planet will still take time to heal, but the work done by the relief teams will go along way to speed up the process and prevent any further deaths.
STH_admonition.145.name: Aid rendered to [romulan_nova_rescue_planet.GetName]
STH_admonition.146.desc: The work completed by the relief teams sent to aid [romulan_nova_rescue_planet.GetName], were able to repair some of the damage done to the planet. However much of the damage and scars remain. Many of the fires are still active and areas of the planet are still no go zones and will take years to fully heal.
STH_admonition.146.name: Aid rendered to [romulan_nova_rescue_planet.GetName]
STH_admonition.147.desc: Our scientists have uncovered some interesting news about the Romulan primary star, it appears that the once stable primary star has become unstable, our experts believe it is only a matter of time before the star goes Supernova. We can only wait for the proud Romulans to declare a state of emergency and reach out for help.
STH_admonition.147.name: Romulan Star in trouble
STH_admonition.148.desc: The [romulan_nova_rescue_country.GetName] have rejected our offer of help, failing us in our hour of need. We must concentrate our efforts on rescuing those in power and who can help rebuild a stronger empire, those who cannot must be sacrificed for the greater good of our species and the continuation of the Empire, but we will not forget or forgive this betrayal.
STH_admonition.148.name: Romulan Sun Supernova
STH_admonition.149.desc: The Romulan Empire has been abandoned and betrayed, but they will regret turning their backs on us and ignoring our cries for help. Our Tal Shiar operatives have revealed a secret plan to target a core world within [romulan_nova_rescue_country.GetName] using an embedded agent to plant a code into their robotic workforce which will cause them to turn on their former masters, we have been promised the destruction will be spectacular and our involvement will remain a mystery to them.
STH_admonition.149.name: Children of Revenge
STH_admonition.15.desc: [zhat_vash_sci_target_leader2_country.GetRulerTitle] [zhat_vash_sci_target_leader2_country.GetRulerName], one of our leading scientists in the field of artificial life and synthetic development has report to our science directorate that they will no longer be working in this field. When pressed on the matter they declined to answer and seemed to get rather agitated. This is all rather strange.
STH_admonition.15.name: Scientist no longer interested in synthetic research
STH_admonition.150.a: Research into Synthetic Life is banned.
STH_admonition.150.desc: As the dust settles on the devastated world of [romulan_nova_rescue_planet.GetName], it is time to look to the future. In the aftermath of the attack the ruling council of [romulan_nova_rescue_country.GetName] have forced a ban on the further development and research into Synthetic life.\n\nAs a result of the ban [admonition_scientist.GetName] has fled our borders to parts unknown. The remaining A500 units have been terminated, yet suspicion and anger towards A.I linger. For now it is time to heal and recover.
STH_admonition.150.name: Children of [romulan_nova_rescue_planet.GetName] Aftermath
STH_admonition.152.a: This is a loss to our science division.
STH_admonition.152.b: This is a loss to our science division.
STH_admonition.152.desc_01: In a shocking act of defiance [admonition_scientist_exile_2.GetName] has resigned their commission and their role as a leading robotics expert within [Root.GetName] and has gone into exile as a direct result of our recent policy announcements around AI. This is a major setback to our research efforts in this department.
STH_admonition.152.desc_02: In a shocking turn of events [admonition_scientist_exile_2.GetName] has resigned their commision, unable to continue with the guilt in their role in the recent destruction and in the face of public anger at their involvement.
STH_admonition.152.name: Synthetics Expert Resigns
STH_admonition.155.desc:
Greetings, you may know me, I am [romulan_nova_mercy_leader.GetName] and I have served the [Root.GetName] faithfully for many years. 

Never in all my years of service have I come across a more critical humanitarian crisis as that facing the people of Romulus. The destruction of their star poses the biggest loss of life seen in our lifetime. 

I urge us to make every effort to make room for these Romulan's in need.
STH_admonition.155.e: They shall have no home here.
STH_admonition.155.name: Supernova Refugee Crisis
STH_admonition.156.desc: We have now been told that our calls have been answered and a rescue fleet is being massed to come to our aid.
STH_admonition.156.name: Rescue Fleet
STH_admonition.16.desc: [zhat_vash_sci_target_leader2_country.GetRulerTitle] [zhat_vash_sci_target_leader2_country.GetRulerName], it appears that [zhat_vash_sci_target_leader2.GetName] has taken their own life unexpectedly, our security division found them alone in their quarters. They were one of our leading experts in the subject of synthetic life and a promising member of our science team.
STH_admonition.16.name: Scientist found dead
STH_admonition.17.desc: Despite our most persuasive efforts the scientist is refusing to be moved from their interest in AI and before we could use our more hands on methods of convincing our operatives were disturbed by the local security forces. We managed to make a clean escape and our cover remains intact. It will be costly to make a reattempt or to end their life, but it could be done with the appropriate resources.
STH_admonition.17.name: Zhat Vash - Mission Failure
STH_admonition.18.desc: One of our leading scientists [zhat_vash_sci_target_leader2.GetName] has reported to our authorities that they were approached by a shadowy figure, urging them to abandon their research into robotics and synthetic life. Thankfully they were not persuaded and managed to alert the security division. Despite a thorough search our security operatives were unable to uncover who was behind this approach.
STH_admonition.18.name: Scientist approached by mysterious figure
STH_admonition.2.b: We have no time for fairy tales.
STH_admonition.2.desc: [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName] we have been contacted on a untraceable channel by the mysterious Conclave of Eight, a clandestine organisation within the Tal Shiar. They operate outside of all jurisdiction and with a singular purpose - to stop the Seb-Cheneb, the destroyer, from bringing about the 'Day of Annihilation'. The group call themselves the Zhat Vash and they have invited us to send one of our scientists to the coordinates provided to discover why their purpose will not only save the Romulan Star Empire, but the whole Galaxy.
STH_admonition.2.name: The Admonition
STH_admonition.20.a: Send the them to convince them.
STH_admonition.20.desc: Our operatives have identified a leading scientist within the Romulan empire who has made headway into AI and synthetics, despite our efforts to crush this field of research. The scientist has been identified as [zhat_vash_sci_target_leader.GetName] and they clearly have a talent for the subject. We can arrange for one of our handlers to meet with the scientist and convince them to drop the matter, or we could always try to recruit them if they are sufficiently talented.
STH_admonition.20.name: Zhat Vash - Intelligence Report
STH_admonition.2000.desc: In the wake of the destruction of our homeworld and the break up of the Star Empire, we have been hit with a further act of betrayal, the Reman who have long been our slaves have taken this chance to break free and form their own empire!
STH_admonition.2000.name: Reman Indepedance
STH_admonition.21.desc: It appears [zhat_vash_sci_target_leader.GetName] was not willing to give up their life's work and took to our suggestion poorly, the situation appears to have deteriorated and resulted in the scientist taking their own life. A poor result for the Romulan Star Empire, but at least their disgusting line of investigation has now stopped.
STH_admonition.21.name: Scientist died
STH_admonition.3.desc: The location provided by the Zhat Vash message leads to a previously unknown system, containing no less than eight stars! At the centre of this unnatural formation of stars lies a single planet, named Aia and this is where our Scientist has been asked to travel to uncover the secrets of the Admonition.
STH_admonition.3.name: The Admonition
STH_admonition.31.desc: Leader [From.From.GetName] has come to our attention as a potential new recruit for the Zhat Vash. They would first need to prove themselves via the Admonition, which has claimed the lives of many who have faced it previously.
STH_admonition.31.name: Zhat Vash - New Recruit
STH_admonition.4.desc: The effects of the Admonition are more powerful than anyone could have imagined and the message was clearly not designed for organic minds, [leader_trait_zhat_vash_recruit_name.GetName] saw images of death, destruction and the annihilation of all organic life in a firestorm of untold power. Through the chaos inflicted on [leader_trait_zhat_vash_recruit_name.GetHerHis] mind [leader_trait_zhat_vash_recruit_name.GetName] could clearly see the cause of all this pain, synthetics. Once their mind became clear the Zhat Vash handler explained this is their purpose, to prevent this future from occurring.
STH_admonition.4.name: The Admonition
STH_admonition.5.desc: The effects of the Admonition are more powerful than anyone could have imagined and the message was clearly not designed for organic minds, [leader_trait_zhat_vash_recruit_name.GetName] saw images of death, destruction and the annihilation of all organic by a synthetic power. Only the through sheer will of mind was [leader_trait_zhat_vash_recruit_name.GetName] able to prevent themselves from turning their disruptor on themselves as many others around the strange circle had already done. The damage caused will remain with them forever. Once [leader_trait_zhat_vash_recruit_name.GetName] was able to stand, the Zhat Vash handler told them that the singular purpose of the organisation is to prevent this future.
STH_admonition.5.name: The Admonition
STH_admonition.50.desc: [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName]! We have detected a Borg Cube entering the Terix System via a transwarp conduit, the Cube is not responding to any hails nor has it attempted to contact our station within the system. Preliminary scans suggest that the vessel is heavily damaged, with its power grid severely damaged and life support failing. There is no obvious cause of the damage, we must send a fleet to intercept immediately.
STH_admonition.50.name: Borg Cube detected in the Terix System
STH_admonition.51.desc: Closer scans of the Borg Cube reveal that the ship has suffered a full submatrix collapse, disconnecting the ship from the Collective. This catastrophic failure within the Cube has rendered the vessel completely disabled and the thousands of drones are in a deep hibernation. This presents an excellent opportunity to capture the ship and uncover the secrets of the collective. We should send a scientist at once, as well as a fleet to stand guard of our prize.
STH_admonition.51.name: Borg Cube found heavily damaged
STH_admonition.52.b: The risk is too great - abandon the Cube.
STH_admonition.52.desc: Our teams have reported back that they have successfully secured the Borg ship which is being dubbed the The Artifact. The ship still contains thousands of drones and working Borg technology, but without the link to the Collective the vessel is in permanent hibernation mode and our for the taking. The resource we could obtain from the technology and from the data nexus will propel our research forward and give the Romulan Empire a critical edge against other major powers. However, we should never forget that the Borg are never completely docile and a Borg cube will always present a significant threat to our way of life.
STH_admonition.52.name: We now control the Borg Artifact
STH_admonition.53.desc: We now control The Borg Artifact. This megastructure contains all the risks of having a Borg ship parked on the doorstep, but presents a unique chance to study the Collective and syphon off the research stored within the vessels many data stores and within the minds of the hibernating drones. A vast cross species project called the Borg Reclamation Project has been set up to oversee this operation, but as long as the ship remains in Romulan hands, the Star Empire will always have the edge.
STH_admonition.53.name: The Borg Reclamation Project
STH_admonition.54.desc: Reports from the Romulan Star Empire are filtering out despite the best efforts of the Tal Shiar, it appears that a giant Borg Cube was recovered within their territory. Romulan forces were able to secure the ship with minimal casualties. This could be a momentous opportunity as long as their leadership is willing to work with the galactic community.
STH_admonition.54.name: Borg Cube claimed by Romulus
STH_admonition.55.desc_01: It appears there has been a Borg incursion within Romulan space, the normally secretive empire has confirmed that they were forced to destroy a large Borg Cube and whilst details of the event are limited, it seems that the vessel was heavily damaged and the Romulans deemed it to be of no strategic importance.
STH_admonition.55.desc_02: [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName] we have managed to blow up the Borg Cube as ordered, many of our scientists are unhappy at the decision, but the Tal Shiar are already moving in to suppress any of their 'concerns' as always the safety and prosperity of the Romulan Star Empire comes first.
STH_admonition.55.desc_03: Species 3783, the Romulans, have managed to destroy one of our long range tactical vessels orbiting planet designated Terix. The vessel had suffered a catastrophic sub-matrix collapse and was defenceless. Had the vessel been fully operational we would have added their biological distinctiveness to our own. We have elevated the threat level of Species 3783.
STH_admonition.55.name: Borg Artifact destroyed
STH_admonition.56.desc: [Root.GetRulerTitle], the Borg vessel appears to be regaining its functions and we have lost contact with our teams who were on the ship, including the mission leader, we should fear the worse and assume they are now part of the collective. We must send every available vessel to destroy the cube before it can regenerate further.
STH_admonition.56.name: Borg Ship Active!
STH_admonition.58.desc_01: It appears there has been a Borg incursion within Romulan space, the normally secretive empire has confirmed that they were forced to destroy a large Borg Cube and whilst details of the event are limited, it seems unlikely that the battle was as straightforward as the Romulan propaganda division would have us believe.
