The First Contact took place on the evening of April 5, 2063, when a Vulcan survey ship, the T'Plana-Hath, having detected the failed warp signature of the Phoenix, touched down in Bozeman, central Montana. Private records from the Vulcan High Command would later reveal that in addition to the earth ship mysterious debris was found above the planet. History would forever remember Lily Sloane and her partner the deceased Zefram Cochrane.
STH_borg_story.109.name: A new first contact
STH_borg_story.110.desc: On returning to the present, a check on the computer indicated that the timeline has remained intact. Only the crew knew what happened in the past and all events beyond the destruction of the Borg Assimilation Cube were unknown to Starfleet Command. The clean up and recovery from the Borg attack will take some time, but the significance of April 5th 2063 will forever mean something special to one ship and her crew.
STH_borg_story.110.name: Borg Invasion defeated
STH_borg_story.111.desc: On returning to the present, a check on the computer indicated that the timeline has remained mostly intact, with only a few minor changes. Only the crew knew what happened in the past and all events beyond the destruction of the Borg Assimilation Cube were unknown to Starfleet Command. The clean up and recovery from the Borg attack will take some time, but the significance of April 5th 2063 will forever mean something special to one ship and her crew.
STH_borg_story.111.name: Borg Invasion defeated
STH_borg_story.112.desc: The failure to prevent the Borg from stopping the First Contact between Earth and Vulcan has had ripples through the timeline. The Borg now control Earth.
STH_borg_story.112.name: Borg Invasion successful
STH_borg_story.113.desc: The failure to prevent the Borg from stopping the First Contact between Earth and Vulcan has had ripples through the timeline. The Borg now control Earth, Mars and the Alpha Centauri Systems. The Borg must be stopped.
STH_borg_story.113.name: Borg Invasion successful
STH_borg_story.114.desc:
Assimilation Cube 100359 was partially successful in deploying invasion drones across [borg_planet_target.GetName]. The planetary target has suffered significant damage, and the Cube remains under attack from hostile forces, preventing full assimilation directive. Current objective: Eliminate hostile forces and reevaluate target.

Cube 100359 will attempt to remain in orbit to defend surface drones whilst this process is underway.

Caution: Cube 100359 combat capacity is now significantly reduced due to low drone count.
STH_borg_story.114.name: Planetary Assimilation Engaged
STH_borg_story.116.a: Their culture will adapt to service ours.
STH_borg_story.116.b: We should investigate this at once.
STH_borg_story.116.desc_01: Our Assimilation Cube reports that it has arrived at the [borg_target_invasion.GetName] border, and has begun probing [borg_target_invasion.owner_species.GetName] defenses. It has already assimilated a number of starships and colonies, and has amassed a significant stockpile of raw materials and drones. It will not be long before it has enough resources to commence the invasion of [borg_planet_target.GetName].
STH_borg_story.116.desc_02: Reports are coming in from independent colonies at the far reaches of our territory that transports, passenger ships, and even whole outposts have gone silent. There are vague rumors of a colossal cuboid ship that is slowly making its way towards our core systems, gathering resources as it progresses. \n\nThis could be the start of an attack. Our top military leaders are recommending we begin preparing our defences around [Root.capital_scope.GetName]. If our suspicions are correct, we could be in grave danger.
STH_borg_story.116.desc_03: Reports are coming in from our intelligence network that transports, passenger ships, and even whole outposts on the [borg_target_invasion.GetName] border have gone silent. There are vague rumors of a colossal cuboid ship that is allegedly responsible for these incidents. \n\nThis could be the start of an attack on the [borg_target_invasion.GetName]. Our top military leaders are recommending we begin preparing our own forces, in the event that this danger spreads to other parts of the quadrant.
STH_borg_story.116.name: Activity Near [borg_target_invasion.GetName] Border
STH_borg_story.117.b: The line must be drawn here.
STH_borg_story.117.desc_01: Cube 100359 reports that it has completed its analysis of [borg_target_invasion.owner_species.GetName] defenses and has engaged invasion protocols. It is now proceeding on a direct course towards [borg_planet_target.GetName] and will breach the planet's innermost defense perimeter at [borg_target_invasion_destination.solar_system.GetName] in 25 to 75 solar cycles.
STH_borg_story.117.desc_02: Our deep space sensor array has picked up an inbound subspace signal that could only belong to large vessel traveling at transwarp speed. There is only one species who could generate such a mode of transport... the Borg. \n\nOur scientists predict that a single Assimilation Cube is on a direct course for [borg_planet_target.GetName], and should reach our last defense perimeter very soon. We should recall all available ships to our capital and prepare to repel the attack.
STH_borg_story.117.desc_03: Our deep space sensor array has picked up a subspace signal inside [borg_target_invasion.GetName] space that could only belong to large vessel traveling at transwarp speed. There is only one species who could generate such a mode of transport... the Borg. \n\nOur scientists predict that a single Assimilation Cube is on a direct course for the [borg_target_invasion.GetName] Capital, [borg_planet_target.GetName]. It will likely breach their defensive line very shortly.
STH_borg_story.117.name: Invasion Imminent
STH_borg_story.118.desc:
Our failure to prevent the Borg Cube from reaching [borg_planet_target.GetName] has resulted in a devastating attack against our planet. 

We can be thankful that our forces were still attacking the Cube preventing it from deploying it's full compliment of drones and assimilating our world. 

The Cube remains active and must be stopped before it can continue it's devasting march through our systems.
STH_borg_story.118.name: Borg Cube Devastates [borg_planet_target.GetName]
STH_borg_story.120.a: They are unable to withstand us.
STH_borg_story.120.desc_01: Cube 100359 reports that it has neutralized a number of [From.GetName] vessels that attempted to impede its progress towards [borg_planet_target.GetName]. It is now resuming its previous course and speed.
STH_borg_story.120.desc_02: Our forces confronting the inbound Borg Cube at [FromFrom.GetName] have been completely routed, and the Borg vessel is now resuming its course towards our homeworld. We must regroup all our remaining forces and rebuild our defensive line immediately or all will be lost.
STH_borg_story.120.desc_03: The forces we sent to aid the [borg_target_invasion.GetName] at [FromFrom.GetName] have been completely routed, and the Borg vessel is now resuming its previous course. If it is not stopped soon, [borg_planet_target.GetName] will certainly be destroyed.
STH_borg_story.120.name: Report from [FromFrom.GetName]
STH_borg_story.121.a: What could they want with our people?
STH_borg_story.121.desc: The [Root.GetName] reports that it has encountered a cuboid vessel belonging to the Borg Collective. It appears to have technology and battle capabilities well beyond what one of our ships can handle. The [Root.GetName] is retreating back to the fleet, but reports that members of its crew were abducted by the Borg during the encounter.
STH_borg_story.121.name: Cube Confrontation
STH_borg_story.122.desc: Cube 100359 reports that it has encountered a vessel belonging to the [From.Owner.GetName] and subsequently abducted an individual from their primitive leadership hierarchy. This drone's mind possesses extensive data regarding its species' technology and battle strategies. We have assimilated this leader, now designated [FromFrom.GetName], to aid in the current operation.
STH_borg_story.122.name: Locutus Acquired
STH_borg_story.123.desc: Before we confront the Borg Cube directly, the [FromFrom.GetName] has proposed a daring plan to rescue our lost officer, now known as Locutus of Borg. We can either send a commando team to cause as much damage as possible and hope they can retrieve Locutus by force, or we can send a science team who believe they can extract and de-assimilate Locutus before the Borg realize what is happening. The choice is yours.
STH_borg_story.123.name: Rescue Attempt
STH_borg_story.124.a: They did what they had to do.
STH_borg_story.124.desc: Although the team partially accomplished their mission, the Borg were alerted to their presence as they attempted to retrieve Locutus. Facing escalating opposition from Borg drones, the team was forced to terminate their target. We can only take solace in the fact that the Borg can no longer use our officer against us.
STH_borg_story.124.name: Locutus Killed
STH_borg_story.125.desc: We have lost all contact with the team sent to the Borg cube. Our last sensor sweep indicated rising power levels throughout the interior. It seems likely that the Collective was alerted to the team's presence and that they have been assimilated. We should now consider more conventional means to stop the Cube's advance.
STH_borg_story.125.name: Lost Contact
STH_borg_story.126.desc: Great news! By cleverly using an antimatter spread to blind the Borg sensors, the team was able to beam aboard the Borg Cube undetected. Though they met with resistance inside, the team successfully located and recovered Locutus. Better yet, an analysis of Locutus' connection to the Collective has yielded a tactical advantage: we have set the Cube to engage its regeneration cycle, preventing it from fighting back against our forces.
STH_borg_story.126.name: Locutus Recovered
STH_borg_story.127.desc: Cube 100359 reports that intruders have beamed aboard and terminated Locutus. Significant damage has been done to the vessel's power distribution network. Combat capability reduced by 58%. Cube 100359 will adapt to the loss in approximately 90 cycles.
STH_borg_story.127.name: Locutus Killed
STH_borg_story.128.desc: Cube 100359 has shut down for regeneration in response to subcommand directive from the Locutus node pathway. Error. Unable to verify authenticity of subcommand directive. Locutus is not on board. Begin full purge of node authorization and restore power to combat systems. Estimated time to adapt: 180 cycles.
STH_borg_story.128.name: Locutus Captured
STH_borg_story.130.a: We must hurry to defend our homeworld.
STH_borg_story.130.desc_01: Central Nexus is 47% complete. Tactical drone assets on [FROM.GetName] have captured 38% of the planet's population centers. Total assimilation of [FROM.owner_species.GetBorgSpecies] presence on the surface estimated in 245 cycles.
STH_borg_story.130.desc_02: Reports from [FROM.GetName] are grim. Local defenders have fallen back on all fronts and are attempting to resist using guerilla tactics, but the Borg are simply too numerous. We estimate the Borg Central Nexus facility on the surface is almost halfway complete. Something must be done soon or we will lose the planet.
STH_borg_story.130.name: Update from [FROM.GetName]
STH_borg_story.131.desc_01: Central Nexus is 84% complete. All major population centers have been secured. Total assimilation of [FROM.owner_species.GetBorgSpecies] presence on the surface estimated in 115 cycles.
STH_borg_story.131.desc_02: [FROM.GetName] is nearly lost to us. Our last contact with local defenses was over a week ago. We must face the possibility that all resistance on the surface has been eradicated. If we are going to do something to stop the assimilation of our homeworld, it must be now.
STH_borg_story.2.a: The Collective will be made whole again.
STH_borg_story.2.desc: The Collective has been fractured! Efforts to repair secondary nodes at risk of catastrophic ethos/identity fragmentation have not proceeded fast enough. \n\nSeveral millicycles ago, links on [borgRebelCountry.Capital.GetName] were disestablished, and the a new collective consciousness emerged: the [borgRebelCountry.GetName]. A handful of locations and vessels have been overrun by this new threat.
STH_borg_story.2.name: Imperfect Thinking
STH_borg_story.200.desc:
Opening transwarp communication protocol with Main Administrative Node...
Report from data archive in [borg_random_system.GetBorgCoordinates]...
Title: Enhanced Surface Bombardment

Main Node. Accumulation of data resources in local archive has reached a key potential in the field of wide-area tactical transporters. Local network reports a [Root.random_perc]% possibility of adapting this development for greater use in the Collective. Application of distinctiveness requires combat-efficient biological template. Select drone species template for protocol reconfiguration. Comply.
STH_borg_story.200.name: Bombardment Protocol Enhancement
STH_borg_story.250.a: Our future will adapt to serve our present.
STH_borg_story.250.desc: An anomalous signal has been detected from [mysterious_borg_drone_star.solar_system.GetBorgCoordinates]. Source: Borg proximity transceiver. Chronometric node synchronization offset: +[Root.future_year_offset] solar cycles. Conclusion: Temporal displacement. Attempting to establish interlink. Error. Failure. Link terminated by target. \n\nOverride current subcommand task. New priority: Acquire future technological distinctiveness. Standby paradox correction algorithms. Activate tactical asset. Dispatch to signal source and assimilate. Comply.
STH_borg_story.250.name: Anomalous Chronometric Signal
STH_borg_story.251.desc: Objective failed. Proximity transceiver signal lost. Detecting chroniton burst near last known target location. [Root.random_perc]% probability: Trans-temporal warp drive. Analyze pattern. Vessel identified: Federation Temporal Integrity Commission. Wells Class. USS Relativity. \n\nChance of retrieving critical drone asset: 0%.
STH_borg_story.251.name: Signal Lost
STH_borg_story.255.desc: Interception complete. Overtaking target. Locking tractor beam. Scanning. \n\nAnomalous drone presence confirmed. Technological distinctiveness does not exist in present Collective database nodes. Acquisition of this technology will bring us closer to perfection. Designate for priority assimilation. Execute capture protocol.
STH_borg_story.255.name: Vessel Intercepted
STH_borg_story.256.a: Adapt to target shield modulation. Reacquire tractor lock.
STH_borg_story.256.b: Adapt to target weapons. Neutralize threat.
STH_borg_story.256.desc: Detecting unknown shield modulation from target vessel. Tractor beam failure. Target engaging evasive maneuvers. Counteract. \n\nOur vessel is under fire. Target weapons output exceeding expected parameters. Significant damage detected. We must adapt.
STH_borg_story.256.name: Vessel Resisting
STH_borg_story.257.desc:
Objective failed. Severe damage detected. Structural failure imminent. [This.GetName] destroyed. 

Detecting chroniton burst near last known target location. [Root.random_perc]% probability: Trans-temporal warp drive. Analyze pattern. Vessel identified: Federation Temporal Integrity Commission. Wells Class. USS Relativity. 

Chance of retrieving critical drone asset: 0%
STH_borg_story.257.name: Operation Failed
STH_borg_story.258.a: Inform target: \"You will be assimilated. Resistance is futile.\"
STH_borg_story.258.desc: Unexpected action. Target drone initiated internal transport sequence. Drone now aboard [This.GetName]. Error. Drone refuses interlink connection. Prediction: Hostile action imminent. Activate tactical drones. Intercept target. Assimilate.
STH_borg_story.258.name: Target Boarded Our Vessel
STH_borg_story.259.a: Transport additional drones to target location. Subdue target.
STH_borg_story.259.c: Isolate target cortical interlink frequency. Override behavior.
STH_borg_story.259.d: Increase tetryon particle density. Overload target shield capacity.
STH_borg_story.259.desc.first:
Response from target received: "You will try." 

Target engaging tactical drones. Combat capability exceeds baseline predictions. Exo-plating: Poly-deutonic alloy. Force-field: Multi-spatial modulation. Adaptation rate consistent with theoretical multi-dimensional analysis algorithms. 

$STH_borg_story.259.desc.repeating$
STH_borg_story.259.desc.repeating: Adaptations ineffective. Unable to compensate. \n\nReassess strategy.
STH_borg_story.259.e: Shift operational focus to new parameter: Terminate target.
STH_borg_story.259.name: Advanced Drone Attacking
STH_borg_story.260.desc:
Alert: Target is interfacing with regeneration alcove [This.alcoveNumber]. Interlink controls overridden. [This.GetName] navigation subcommand accessed. Setting new coordinates. Error. Course intersects Class 5 hazard. Guidance system not responding. [This.GetName] destruction imminent. 

Priority update: Data received from analysis nodes on [This.Owner.Capital.GetName]. Partial assimilation of target technological distinctiveness achieved prior to [This.GetName] destruction. New tactical system templates distributed to key nodes. 

Cubes will now beam down £ Terror Drone armies when deploying £ Heavy Tactical Drone armies during orbital bombardment.
STH_borg_story.260.name: Operation Concluded
STH_borg_story.3.a: The Collective will be made whole again.
STH_borg_story.3.desc: The Collective has been fractured! Efforts to repair secondary nodes at risk of catastrophic ethos/identity fragmentation have not proceeded fast enough. \n\nSeveral millicycles ago, links on [borgRebelCountry.Capital.GetName] were disestablished, and a new collective consciousness emerged: the [borgRebelCountry.GetName]. A number of locations and vessels have been overrun by this new threat.
STH_borg_story.3.name: Imperfect Thinking
STH_borg_story.310.a: Who are you looking to find?
STH_borg_story.310.desc_01: My name is Dr Erin Hansen, my husband and I are eminent experts in exobiology and synthetic/cybernetic life, and we've been investigating reports over the years of Borg activity on the fringes of the quadrant. Reports dating back as far as the first NX-Class Enterprise and tales from El-Aurian refugees and we are prepared to own and manage the project, all we require is a science vessel and the blessing of the science council.
STH_borg_story.310.desc_02: Hello my name is Dr Erin Hansen, my husband and I are eminent experts in exobiology and synthetic/cybernetic life, and we've been investigating reports over the years of mysterious ships and lifeforms at the edge of our space. Reports dating back as far as the first NX-Class Enterprise and tales from El-Aurian refugees and we are prepared to own and manage the project, all we require is a science vessel and the blessing of the science council.
STH_borg_story.310.name: Flight of the Raven
STH_borg_story.311.desc: Scientific Report - USS Raven \n\nHopefully the Federation Science Council is receiving this report, we have picked up the location of one of these ships, a massive cube-shaped vessel on an unimaginable scale. We have managed to avoid detection using bio-dampeners and shield modulators, the cube is heading off again and we aim to follow, we shall submit are next report as soon as we can... Annika one minute sweetheart, mummy is just recording an update... Dr Hansen Out.
STH_borg_story.311.name: Flight of the Raven
STH_borg_story.312.a: We've lost contact with the Raven.
STH_borg_story.312.desc: Scientific Report - Final report received from the USS Raven \n\nThis might be our final report for sometime, we are tracking the Cube that we have nicknamed 'Rubics' across the sector and believe it is about to enter a conduit of some sort. Our multi-adaptive shielding has kept us safe so far, the effects of the conduit are having some unusual effects on our shields, but my husband and I are determined to .... Transmission lost
STH_borg_story.312.name: Flight of the Raven
STH_borg_story.313.a: Add their Biological Distinctiveness to the collective.
STH_borg_story.313.desc: Tactical Asset 1000709 reports the discovery of a small vessel in proximity, the tactical threat is negligible, 3 biological lifeforms detected, technology level is inferior, preparing assimilation protocols.
STH_borg_story.313.name: Borg Tactical Update
STH_borg_story.4.a: The Collective will be made whole again.
STH_borg_story.4.desc: The Collective has been fractured! Efforts to repair secondary nodes at risk of catastrophic ethos/identity fragmentation have not proceeded fast enough. \n\nSeveral millicycles ago, links on [borgRebelCountry.Capital.GetName] were disestablished, and a new collective consciousness emerged: the [borgRebelCountry.GetName]. A large number of locations and vessels have been overrun by this new threat.
STH_borg_story.4.name: Imperfect Thinking
STH_borg_story.5.desc: We have successfully purged the damaged nodes within the network and further corruption within the Collective has been repaired. All errant identity fragments have been neutralised leaving the collectives integrity intact. The collective remains whole and undisrupted.
STH_borg_story.50.desc:
Warning: Detection of species with abnormal resistance coefficient confirmed. Potential threat to Collective acknowledged. Engaging strategic response algorithms. Analyzing...

Recommendation: Direct assault on species homeworld by a Class 5 tactical vessel offers optimal probability of successful neutralization. All secondary and tertiary resource nodes will be adapted for the construction of the required asset. Vessel will be configured for long range interstellar combat and planetary assault.

Choose priority target.
STH_borg_story.50.name: Assimilation Target Identified
STH_borg_story.52.desc: Resource preallocation for Class 5 tactical asset is complete. Vessel designation: Assimilation Cube 100359. Awaiting final confirmation to execute all pending ship-assembly subcommands.\n\nUpon completion, the asset will proceed via transwarp to the border of [borg_target_invasion.owner_species.GetName] space and probe the surrounding spatial grids for defensive weaknesses. Once it has accumulated sufficient data, it will set a direct course for [borg_planet_target.GetName], where it will begin assimilating the planet's culture and technology.
STH_borg_story.52.name: Construct Assimilation Cube
STH_borg_story.53.desc: Assimilation Cube 100359 has arrived at [borg_target_invasion_destination.solar_system.GetName] and is now on final approach trajectory to [borg_planet_target.GetName]. Sensors indicate a high probability of elevated [borg_target_invasion.owner_species.GetName] resistance. These obstacles will be neutralized. [borg_target_invasion.owner_species.GetName] culture will adapt to service us.
STH_borg_story.53.name: [borg_target_invasion.GetName] Defense Perimeter Breached
STH_borg_story.54.desc: Our last line of defence has been breached! The Borg have reached [FROM.GetName], and reports are coming in from every military installation across the planet of Borg Drones appearing and decimating our ground forces. They are quickly adapting to our weaponry and the orbiting Cube is destroying all vessels attempting to land allied reinforcements. Our military commanders are predicting the planet will fall within the week. Our last hope to save the planet is to get a team of army commandos into the Borg central Nexus whilst it is vulnerable and before they have a chance to fortify it.
STH_borg_story.54.name: Borg Invading [FROM.GetName]
STH_borg_story.55.desc:
Assimilation Cube 100359 has successfully deployed invasion drones across [FROM.GetName]. Planetary assimilation now underway. Current objective: Establish Planetary Nexus. Local control will then transfer to new Nexus node, in order to establish long range communication with the Collective at [Root.capital_scope.GetName].

Cube 100359 will attempt to remain in orbit to defend surface drones whilst this process is underway.

Caution: Cube 100359 combat capacity is now significantly reduced due to low drone count.
STH_borg_story.55.name: Planetary Assimilation Engaged
STH_borg_story.56.desc: Cube 100359 has been completed. Activating transwarp sequence. Deploying to target quadrant.\n\nAll other vessels will be re-tasked to avoid [borg_target_invasion.owner_species.GetBorgSpecies] territory until we are ready for a full-scale invasion.
STH_borg_story.56.name: Assimilation Cube Ready
STH_borg_story.57.desc: The moment we have dreaded has finally arrived. The Borg, our most lethal enemy, have begun an invasion of the [Root.GetName], and this time there may be no stopping them. All available fleets should immediately deploy to intercept the attacking vessel. There will be no negotiating with this enemy. We must stop them at all costs and defend our homeworld, otherwise countless lives will be lost, or worse... assimilated.
STH_borg_story.57.name: Borg Cube on Direct Course for [Root.capital_scope.GetName]
STH_borg_story.58.desc: Our long range scans have detected the Borg have broken through the defenses of [borg_target_invasion.GetName] and are rapidly approaching their home system. If the Borg are not stopped now, it will not be long until [borg_planet_target.GetName] falls to the might of the Collective.
STH_borg_story.58.name: Borg Attacking [borg_planet_target.GetName]
STH_borg_story.59.desc_01: Cube 100359 has been defeated in a massive battle, resulting in the loss of all drones aboard. We must engage contingency protocols immediately. We will return to assimilate the [borg_target_invasion.GetName] another day.
STH_borg_story.59.desc_02: The Borg Cube approaching [borg_planet_target.GetName] has been defeated in a massive battle that cost many ships and the lives of their crews. The sacrifice of these brave souls to protect [borg_planet_target.GetName] will be remembered across the quadrant for generations. It seems, for the moment, that the [borg_target_invasion.GetName] is safe.
STH_borg_story.59.name: Assimilation Cube Destroyed
STH_borg_story.62.desc: In one of the greatest days for [Root.GetName] the mighty Borg Assimilation Cube has been destroyed thanks to the brilliance of our military and the courage of our officers and brave crewmen who risked their lives to defend their home. The wreckage of the Cube spreads across a vast quantity of space and the salvage could prove useful. However right now is a time to celebrate.
STH_borg_story.62.name: Borg Cube Defeated!
STH_borg_story.63.desc: The unthinkable has happened, the seat of power and the birthplace of our civilisation has fallen. Our efforts to halt the invasion have failed and we have lost contact with the squad and must presume the worst. The Borg will consolidate their power and begin the process of assimilating the population and our technologies, we must regroup and rebuild our forces to retake our homeworld. We must not let this defeat bring us to our knees.
STH_borg_story.63.name: [Root.capital_scope.GetName] has fallen
STH_borg_story.65.desc_01: Primary objective complete, defensive units have been neutralised, control of planetary resources has been established. Moving to secondary objectives, predicted assimilation targets have been established and population will be assimilated.\n\nAssimilation Cube 100359 will be dismantled to facilitate immediate construction of a transwarp conduit to this spatial grid.
STH_borg_story.65.desc_02: Primary objective complete, defensive units have been neutralised, control of planetary resources has been established. Moving to secondary objectives, predicted assimilation targets have been established and population will be assimilated.
STH_borg_story.65.name: Planetary Assimilation Underway
STH_borg_story.66.desc: As predicted [borg_planet_target.GetName] has fallen into the hands of the Borg. The strategic benefits they will get from this victory cannot be overstated; the Borg are now an even bigger threat to us than before. We must be prepared should they turn their cybernetic eyes towards our worlds.
STH_borg_story.66.name: Planetary Assimilation Underway
STH_borg_story.68.desc:
Victory! 

Our ground forces have successfully destroyed the Borg Central Nexus, severing the link to the Collective at a crucial point in its formation. The lost of the Collective's voice resulted in chaos amongst the drones on the planet's surface, allowing our forces to strike whilst they were vulnerable and driving them from our cities. The line has been drawn here and we will not let the Borg push us back. 

Today we celebrate, for tomorrow the fight continues.
STH_borg_story.68.name: [borg_planet_target.GetName] Successfully Defended
STH_borg_story.69.desc: We have lost long range signals from our Nexus on [borg_planet_target.GetName]. We no longer hear the voices of our drones on the planet's surface, nor those aboard the Assimilation Cube in orbit. Without their link to the Collective the drones will not be able to function. Sending termination signal now. Initiating fallback command routines. Estimating damage to the Collectives as significant.
STH_borg_story.69.name: Assimilation of [borg_planet_target.GetName] Failed
STH_borg_story.7.desc: As our operation to quell the rebellious drones drags on, we have become aware of additional pockets of individuality that have taken root in nodes across the Collective. Resource storage units in multiple spatial grids have been lost. Analysis indicates a 98.995% probability that these resources are now under the control of [borgRebelCountry.GetName]. Increase priority of security subcommands in all critical unimatrix grids.
STH_borg_story.7.name: Drone Rebellion Spreading
STH_borg_story.70.desc: The Borg have been defeated on [borg_planet_target.GetName]. This is a major defeat for the Borg Collective and a masterful victory for the [borg_target_invasion.GetName].
STH_borg_story.70.name: Borg Defeated on [borg_planet_target.GetName]
STH_borg_story.73.a: Send a ship through the vortex.
STH_borg_story.73.desc: The celebration at the destruction of the Borg ship was short lived, a small spherical ship emerged from the wreckage of the dying Cube and sped towards Earth, creating a temporal anomaly in its wake. Sensors from the nearest vessel indicated that a Temporal Vortex has formed around the planet. Scans indicate that the once blue world is dark and grey and populated by approximately nine billion lifeforms. ...All Borg! \n\nThe choice is clear, we must send a ship through the Vortex and repair the damage that has been done, before it is too late.
STH_borg_story.73.name: They went back and assimilated Earth
STH_borg_story.75.desc: Emerging through the vortex, with their shields and sensors offline, the crew of [sth_first_contact_ship.GetName] quickly confirm that they have travelled back to April 4th 2063, the day before First Contact between Earth and the Vulcans. The Borg sphere can be seen concentrating its fire on a small spot on the North American continent.
STH_borg_story.75.name: April fourth, two thousand sixty-three
STH_borg_story.76.desc: Beaming down to the small settlement that was the target of the Borg attack, the signs of devastation are unmissable, as the away team makes their way carefully to the missle silo that was the home of the legendary warp ship known as The Phoenix they check that none of the casualties are Zefram Cochrane the inventor of Warp travel on Earth, which would change the fate of the planet forever. \n\nOn arriving at the silo, it is clear that whilst the damage to the Phoenix would require some effort to fix, she would still fly. Looking around at the bodies, it was clear that none of them belonged to Dr Cochrane.
STH_borg_story.76.name: Bozeman, Montana
STH_borg_story.77.desc: Beaming down to the small settlement that was the target of the Borg attack, the signs of devastation are unmissable, as the away team makes their way carefully to the missle silo that was the home of the legendary warp ship known as The Phoenix they check that none of the casualties are Zefram Cochrane the inventor of Warp travel on Earth, which would change the fate of the planet forever. \n\nOn arriving at the silo, it is clear that whilst the damage to the Phoenix would require some effort to fix, she would still fly. [sth_first_contact_leader.GetName] turns over one of the bodies on the ground to get a better look and gasps in horror at the sight of the deceased Zeframe Cochrane.
STH_borg_story.77.name: Bozeman, Montana
STH_borg_story.78.desc: Before the team can digest the news, a transmission is received from their ship in orbit. They have lost contact with main engineering and humidity levels have risen seventy-three percent and the temperature has jumped ten degrees in the last hour. The Captain recognises the signs, the Borg on their ship. The away team must decide whether to send the Captain or Commander back to the ship, whilst the other must ensure the Phoenix rises from the ashes.
STH_borg_story.78.name: The voice of the collective
STH_borg_story.79.a: Send a team to confront the Borg.
STH_borg_story.79.desc: We have lost contact with the engineering deck, communications, internal sensors, everything. The security chief was about to send a security team to investigate, it appears they knew their ship was doomed, and with shields down they transported over without being detected. They'll assimilate the [sth_first_contact_ship.GetName], and then ...Earth. The crew are quickly able to lockdown the main computer before main power goes offline. Should the team confront the Borg or lockdown engineering and attempt to hold the Borg back.
STH_borg_story.79.name: Borg on [sth_first_contact_ship.GetName]
STH_borg_story.80.desc_01: A highly armed security team advances on main engineering, observing the twisted organo-metallic corruption of Borg technology taking hold of the once pristine corridors and recoiling in shock and horror at the sight of former crew members now mindless drones serving the collectives whim. As the team reach the main doors of Main Engineering. The first drones spring to life, the fight is short and one sided, as the drones quickly adapt. \n\nThe call to fallback rings out, followed by a frantic retreat away from the outstretched assimilation tubercles seeking to claim more lives.
STH_borg_story.80.desc_02: Security crews posted at strategic locations across the ship wait for the dreaded skittering sound that the drones make as they approach, followed by the red shining light that precedes the arrival of the drones. Deck by deck the Borg advance forcing the Starfleet crew back as each new frequency setting on their phaser rifles is quickly adapted to.
STH_borg_story.80.name: Borg on [sth_first_contact_ship.GetName]
STH_borg_story.81.desc: The legendary Doctor Cochrane sits opposite nursing one of a dozen half empty bottle of Tequila, the scientist may have been drunk, but he certainly was no fool and did not buy the teams cover story. Breaking cover could disrupt the timeline, but the doctor is essential to completing the mission.
STH_borg_story.81.name: This is no time to argue about time
STH_borg_story.82.desc: Back on the ship, the situation continues to be dire. The Borg advance has continued unrelented, but now they have stopped and appear to be focusing on constructing a device on the ships main deflector. It appears they are constructing an Interplexing beacon in an effort to contact the Borg in this century to complete the assimilation of Earth and the surrounding territories.
STH_borg_story.82.name: The Borg advance
STH_borg_story.83.b: Attempt to fire on the dish.
STH_borg_story.83.desc_01: As the Captain and his team make their way along the outside of the outer hull, the deflector dish comes into view. It appears the earlier assaults against the Borg has had some impact on delaying the Borg's efforts to build the beacon as much of the Deflector Dish's original structure remains intact. With the Captains experience they are able to suggest disconnecting the dish to ensure there is no risk to the ship. This approach would be riskier to the crew, but a direct shot could risk hitting the Dish, which could destroy much of the ship.
STH_borg_story.83.desc_02: As the Captain and his team make their way along the outside of the outer hull, the deflector dish comes into view. Sitting back and allowing the Borg to come to the crew appears to have allowed the Borg to have made advanced progress in building the beacon, there is little left of the ships original component. Looking at the situation the option to disconnect the disk no longer appears to exist, the only option is a well placed shot, but should it miss hitting the dish at this range could cause a massive explosion or even destroy the ship.
STH_borg_story.83.desc_03: As the Commander and his team make their way along the outside of the outer hull, the deflector dish comes into view. It appears the earlier assaults against the Borg has had some impact on delaying the Borg's efforts to build the beacon as much of the Deflector Dish's original structure remains intact. With the Commander experience they are able to suggest disconnecting the dish to ensure there is no risk to the ship. This approach would be riskier to the crew, but a direct shot could risk hitting the Dish, which could destroy much of the ship.
STH_borg_story.83.desc_04: As the Commander and his team make their way along the outside of the outer hull, the deflector dish comes into view. Sitting back and allowing the Borg to come to the crew appears to have allowed the Borg to have made advanced progress in building the beacon, there is little left of the ships original component. Looking at the situation the option to disconnect the disk no longer appears to exist, the only option is a well placed shot, but should it miss hitting the dish at this range could cause a massive explosion or even destroy the ship.
STH_borg_story.83.name: The outer hull
STH_borg_story.84.desc: The Security chief's training serves them well, a well placed shot strikes the beacons delicate support structure causing the Borg device to erupt in a shower of sparks, which are quickly extinguished in the vacuum of space. The Borg's efforts to contact the collective in the Delta Quadrant has been dealt a blow.
STH_borg_story.84.name: The Interplexing Beacon destroyed
STH_borg_story.85.desc: The shot fired does manage to destroy the Interplexing Beacon, however the explosion strikes a delicate part of the main deflector dish causing the particle emitter to fracture and causes the anti-protons inside to explode causing major damage to the ship.
STH_borg_story.85.name: The Interplexing Beacon destroyed but ship takes damage
STH_borg_story.86.a: The Phoenix is on its own.
STH_borg_story.86.desc: The shot misses the Interplexing beacon and strikes the vulnerable particle emitter on the deflector dish. The resulting explosion caused by the dish's anti-protons is so large that it complete destroys the ship taking Borg and Starfleet officers with it. The fate of the Federation now rests in the hands of the away team on the planet below.
STH_borg_story.86.name: Starship destroyed
STH_borg_story.87.desc: The team make their way around the Deflector dish, releasing the Maglock clamps one by one, with a satisfying click the particle emitter is released and drifts aways from the ship. With a smile of triumph the Security chief raises their rifle and takes aim and the floating Borg device.
STH_borg_story.87.name: The Interplexing Beacon destroyed
STH_borg_story.88.desc_01: The Captain watches in horror as the Borg move on the members of the Starfleet team, the scream that comes across the comm system is chilling as one loyal officer is assimilated before being able to complete their task. The beacon blinks into life as the vibrations on the hull sound the arrival of more Borg Drones at the Captains location.
STH_borg_story.88.desc_02: The Commander braces himself against the attack from the Borg Drone, having watched his team members hunted down relentlessly by the Borg Drones. A vicious slice from a curved knife-like appendages attached to the attacking drone makes a tear in the Commanders EV suit, knowing that his time is limited the Commander pulls out a Klingon mek'leth and lashes out against his attacker. The Commanders feeling of success is short lived as the beeping of the EV suits emergency alarm rings in his ear. He must return to the airlock.
STH_borg_story.88.name: Unable to destroy the beacon
STH_borg_story.89.desc_01: The decision to maintain the cover story appears to have worked for now, as Zefram Cochrane sits somewhat skeptically in the pilot seat of the Warp Ship. The countdown clock starts up as the heavy blast doors open up exposing the Warp Ship to the cool sunlight of the April morning. The ignition sequence is started and the massive rockets attached to the former missile propels the Phoenix into the sky on its date with history.
STH_borg_story.89.desc_02: As feared Cochrane did not believe the words from the Captain and fearing an attempt on his life fled the small settlement in the early hours of the morning leaving the team without the historical figure on the eve of humanities most important achievement. The Captain cannot let this interfere with the mission opting to lead the mission personally. There is no doubt the loss of Cochrane will prove a massive blow to the success of the mission, but the launch must proceed.
STH_borg_story.89.desc_03: As feared Cochrane did not believe the words from the Commander and fearing an attempt on his life fled the small settlement in the early hours of the morning leaving the team without the historical figure on the eve of humanities most important achievement. The Commander cannot let this interfere with the mission opting to lead the mission personally. There is no doubt the loss of Cochrane will prove a massive blow to the success of the mission, but the launch must proceed.
STH_borg_story.89.desc_04: Cochrane sits in the pilot's chair in the small cramped cockpit of the Phoenix wondering whether it was the hangover or the shock of discovering the truth about these strange folks on this 'Star Trek'. However, Cochrane knew that his life's work was invested in getting the Phoenix into space, whether it was for the sake of history as these space men claim or for his own personal advancement he could not be sure, but here he is strapped into his ship ready to launch.
STH_borg_story.89.desc_05: It is easy to forget that many conditions of the past have been eliminated by modern medicine and whether it was the untreated conditions or the psychological traumas inflicted on the people who lived through World War III, but the truth of the undertaken was too much for Cochrane who fled the settlement overnight. \n\nLeaving the crew with the unenviable task of completing the mission without the doctor.
STH_borg_story.89.desc_06: With the great Zefram Cochrane dead, humanities hopes rest on the three man crew ready to take the Warp ship on its historic mission. Whilst warp field mechanics were a basic requirement for any officer graduating from the academy, there is nothing like having the understanding of a ship built by your own hands.The Phoenix a collection of assembled pieces and scavenged parts held together by the genius of Cochrane and his assistant Lily Sloane. However there is a mission to complete and the future of humanity at stake.
STH_borg_story.89.name: The flight of the Phoenix
STH_borg_story.90.desc_01: Despite a rough take off, the Phoenix rises from the surface of Earth and through the atmosphere, a few blinking warning lights are quickly handled by Zefram Cochrane, who stares wide eyed at the sight of the blue orb of Earth hanging beautifully in space. Cochrane, now the picture of professionalism, flicks a number of switches as the primitive warp nacelles come online and charge up. It is time to take that first leap to warp speed.
STH_borg_story.90.desc_02: The launch was rough as sparks fly from numerous panels and warning lights flicker on and off in a vain attempt to display some message of alert to the crew. Whether these messages would have meant something to Zefram Cochrane is irrelevant now, the crew must make the best of a bad situation and prepare to take the small ship to warp.
STH_borg_story.90.desc_03: Zefram Cochrane would have loved to know that his ship had been launched as the Phoenix soars out and away from Earth's atmosphere. A few suspicious alarms and trails of smoke from console fill the cramped cockpit. However there is no time like the present to make history and alert the Vulcans to humanity.
STH_borg_story.90.name: The flight of the Phoenix
STH_borg_story.91.desc_01: Just as the warp field was about to destabilize the Commander was able to put his skills to use and stabilize the field and allow the Phoenix to break the warp barrier and complete her historic journey. \n\nThe crew bring the ship to a stop and prepare to return to Earth.
STH_borg_story.91.desc_02: Just as history recorded the Phoenix made her jump to warp, taking humanity further and faster than ever before. Once again securing the Phoenix place in history. \n\nThe crew prepare to bring the ship out of warp and make the return journey back to Earth.
STH_borg_story.91.name: Warp speed achieved
STH_borg_story.92.desc: The warp drive fails to deliver as the Phoenix limps along as the power levels appear to fluctuate. The crew frantically search for the source of the issue as the window to make the warp flight slowly closes, Suddenly the panel turns green and they are ready to re-attempt the journey.
STH_borg_story.92.name: Failed to achieve warp speed
STH_borg_story.93.desc_01: On the second time of trying, the Phoenix made her jump to warp, taking humanity further and faster than ever before. Once again securing the Phoenix place in history. \n\nThe crew prepare to bring the ship out of warp and make the return journey back to Earth.
STH_borg_story.93.desc_02: Cochrane proves why history recorded him so highly with a few adjustments of the warp field and the ancient instruments the Phoenix successfully makes the jump to warp speed. Once again securing their place in history. \n\nThe crew prepare to bring the ship out of warp and make the return journey back to Earth.
STH_borg_story.93.name: Warp speed achieved
STH_borg_story.94.desc: The small ship explodes in a sudden but powerful ball of fire taking with it humanity's future. It's unclear what exactly caused the warp drive to fail and whether anything could have been done to have prevented its destruction.
STH_borg_story.94.name: The Phoenix has been destroyed
STH_borg_story.95.desc_01: On the bridge of [sth_first_contact_ship.GetName] the crew debate their next actions, despite the efforts of the Starfleet crew the Borg's advances have been unstoppable and despite initial successes with modulated phasers, the Borg's relentless ability to adapt has allowed them to push ever closer to the Bridge. The suggestion to set the self-destruct is met with initial fury, but the realisation that they are out of options soon dawns on all. As the senior staff set the auto-destruct the commanding officer has the urge to confront the Borg and buy their crew time to escape.
STH_borg_story.95.desc_02: On the bridge of [sth_first_contact_ship.GetName] the crew debate their next actions, sitting back and allowing the Borg to come to us has resulted in fewer casualties, but the loss of most of the ship. Any access to the Borg held decks would be impossible and despite the anger of losing their ship to the Borg the crew know their only option is to abandon ship and attempt to destroy [sth_first_contact_ship.GetName].
STH_borg_story.95.name: This far, no further!
STH_borg_story.96.desc: The heart of the Borg Collective was hot and oppressive with water vapour hanging heavy in the air. As [sth_first_contact_leader.GetName] enters the domain of the Borg Queen a cloying voice calls out welcoming the Captain to watch humanities future's end as a nameless drone deactivates the self destruct routine as full control is handed over to the hive mind. The Queen offers the Captain the choice to join the Collective willingly or by force as a drone. But just then an idea flickers through the Captains mind as the plasma coolant conduit is just visible behind the Queen.
STH_borg_story.96.name: The Queen
STH_borg_story.98.desc_01: Diving for their phaser the Captain is able to get off a number of shots taking down a couple of unsuspecting Drones before they could adapt, using their knowledge of the ship the commanding officer is soon able to to position themselves close to the Warp Coolant conduit. A well aimed shot punctures the conduit spilling forth the deadly gas capable of destroying any organic flesh it touches. The Borg Queen summons her tentacles in a bit to escape, but the Captain is too quick and beats her to the second level of the Engineering Deck. A fierce fight erupts, but the Captain is fighting for more than just their own life and with a brutal kick sends the Borg Queen into the swirling cloud of deadly gas. \n\nWith the Borg Queen dead the remaining drones collapse, as if an invisible string holding them up had been cut. Once the coolant had been cleared all that remained was the skull of the Borg Queen writhing on the floor, attempting to regain control of all she had lost and with a swift snap of her spine her reign was over.
STH_borg_story.98.desc_02: Moving close to the Queen and offering their lives as a Drone the commanding officer is soon able to to position themselves close to the Warp Coolant conduit. With a cry of Resistance is Futile and a well aimed shot punctures the conduit spilling forth the deadly gas capable of destroying any organic flesh it touches. The Borg Queen summons her tentacles in a bit to escape, but the Captain is too quick and beats her to the second level of the Engineering Deck. A fierce fight erupts, but the Captain is fighting for more than just their own life and with a brutal kick sends the Borg Queen into the swirling cloud of deadly gas. \n\nWith the Borg Queen dead the remaining drones collapse, as if an invisible string holding them up had been cut. Once the coolant had been cleared all that remained was the skull of the Borg Queen writhing on the floor, attempting to regain control of all she had lost and with a swift snap of her spine her reign was over.
STH_borg_story.98.name: Her reign is over
STH_borg_story.99.name: Captain and Borg Queen Dead
STH_brightest_star.1.c: Informe a los Ba'ul de que tienen un traidor.
STH_brightest_star.1.desc: Al investigar la señal procedente de la superficie del planeta, nuestros equipos científicos han confirmado que fue enviada por un joven Kelpien que consiguió aplicar ingeniería inversa a la tecnología Ba'ul. Solicita la extracción y el asilo diplomático lejos de los Ba'ul, que según él cazan y matan a su especie. A cambio, proporcionará información y su eterna gratitud.
STH_brightest_star.1.name: La estrella más brillante
STH_brightest_star.10.a: Llévanos de vuelta a la nave.
STH_brightest_star.10.desc: Saru echa un último vistazo a su antiguo hogar y sube a la lanzadera para comenzar su nueva vida. [STH_brightest_leader.GetName] ordena al piloto que despegue rápidamente, ya que cada segundo en la superficie del planeta pone al equipo bajo más riesgo de ser detectado por el Ojo Vigilante.\NCuando la lanzadera enciende sus motores, una serie de torres de vigilancia que antes eran silenciosas comienzan a activarse de repente, este es un momento crucial para la pequeña lanzadera...
STH_brightest_star.10.name: La Estrella Más Brillante
STH_brightest_star.11.desc: Una vez más, la tripulación del transbordador demuestra por qué los pilotos del [Root.GetName] son los mejores en lo que hacen. El equipo se escapa de la mirada de las peligrosas torres de vigilancia de Ba'ul y escapa a salvo de vuelta a la nave que les espera escondida fuera de su alcance.\N-El Kelpien expresa su extrema gratitud por toda la ayuda y se compromete a pagar esta gran deuda contraída. Saru pide que pueda servirnos como oficial en nuestra flota una vez que complete su entrenamiento en la academia. Para ello debe obtener el patrocinio de [Root.GetLeaderName].
STH_brightest_star.11.name: La Estrella Más Brillante
STH_brightest_star.12.b: Coge los restos de la investigación y vete.
STH_brightest_star.12.desc: ¡Esto es un desastre! Los Ba'ul deben haber detectado nuestra lanzadera cuando abandonaba la órbita del planeta. Nuestros sensores en el [Root.GetName] han captado los restos de la lanzadera junto con los restos biológicos de la tripulación y los Kelpien rescatados. Está claro, por los restos, que fueron derribados por las defensas de Ba'ul.\N- Poco después de recoger un mensaje de la superficie, nuestros traductores consiguieron descifrar el mensaje que dice claramente que cualquier otra intrusión en el espacio del Alto Consejo de Ba'ul será respondida con más fuerza. ¿Cómo debemos responder?
STH_brightest_star.12.name: La Estrella Más Brillante
STH_brightest_star.13.desc: Las torres de Ba'ul no tuvieron ninguna oportunidad contra el poder de nuestro avanzado armamento. Es una lección que no olvidarán rápidamente. Como extra, pudimos recoger una serie de materiales de los restos de su puesto de defensa. Que esto sirva de lección a cualquiera que desee cruzarse con nosotros.
STH_brightest_star.13.name: La estrella más brillante
STH_brightest_star.2.desc: Habiendo decidido que la señal no nos sirve de nada, el joven Kelpien sólo puede esperar que su señal no haya sido captada por el Ojo Vigilante de los Ba'ul. Siguiendo las instrucciones del capitán, la nave deja un número de sensores de largo alcance para vigilar los acontecimientos en el sistema Kelai, pero los acontecimientos de otros imperios no deben preocuparnos.
STH_brightest_star.2.name: La Estrella Más Brillante
STH_brightest_star.20.desc: ¡Tenemos noticias emocionantes! Tras años de entrenamiento con nuestros mejores y más brillantes, Saru se ha ganado su primer mando y se une a nosotros como líder. Sólo queda por decidir qué carrera debe elegir.
STH_brightest_star.20.name: El primer Kelpien que entra en servicio
STH_brightest_star.201.desc: [Root.GetLeaderName], nuestros sensores de largo alcance han detectado fuego de armas en la superficie del planeta Kaminar, parece que los Ba'ul captaron la señal enviada por los Kelpien y han descargado su ira sobre todo el pueblo. Parece que ninguno ha sobrevivido, nuestras boyas sensoras están ahora sin energía, pero aparentemente no veremos nada más de los Kelpiens en la superficie.
STH_brightest_star.201.name: La Estrella Más Brillante
STH_brightest_star.3.desc: Nuestros científicos han localizado la ubicación exacta de la señal, justo a las afueras de una aldea perteneciente a los Kelpiens. A pesar de que los Ba'ul son menos avanzados tecnológicamente que nosotros, detectarían una señal de transporte, la única opción es tomar una lanzadera para extraer al alienígena. La misión será arriesgada y los Ba'ul aún son capaces de derribar una de nuestras pequeñas naves y tomarían cualquier intrusión en su espacio como un acto de agresión.
STH_brightest_star.3.name: La Estrella Más Brillante
STH_brightest_star.4.desc: Nuestro equipo de extracción ha hecho contacto con el Kelpien, que se llama Saru. Sabe que debe haber algo más para su especie que una vida de miedo y opresión, pero que no puede ayudar desde la superficie del planeta. Suplica que lo llevemos con nosotros. Debemos tomar la decisión rápidamente ya que parece que los Ba'ul pueden haber captado nuestra incursión.
STH_brightest_star.4.name: La estrella más brillante
STH_brightest_star.5.desc: A pesar de las súplicas de Saru, nuestro equipo abandona la superficie del planeta sin ser detectado. Sólo podemos imaginar lo que será del Kelpien y su pueblo. Sin embargo, la misión no fue una pérdida completa, ya que nuestros escaneos del planeta aún produjeron alguna información útil que podemos llevar a nuestra división científica.
STH_brightest_star.5.name: La estrella más brillante
STH_brightest_star.6.desc: Sacando rápidamente su arma, [STH_brightest_leader.GetName] despacha al joven Kelpien, dejando que la tripulación recoja datos adicionales y escaneos del cuerpo antes de partir en la lanzadera y dejar atrás la superficie del planeta. Los datos recogidos indican que el viaje no ha sido una completa pérdida de tiempo.
STH_brightest_star.6.name: La estrella más brillante
STH_brightest_star_fail.1.desc: Al parecer, la señal fue una falsa alarma y no pudimos localizar el origen de la transmisión. Por temor a una estancia prolongada en el espacio de Ba'ul, hemos salido de la órbita y seguimos investigando el sistema.
STH_brightest_star_fail.1.name: La estrella más brillante
STH_brightest_star_fail.2.desc: Parece que la señal que recibimos se originó en un pueblo Kelpien, sin embargo parece que los Ba'ul captaron la señal antes que nosotros, nuestros escaneos muestran que no quedan supervivientes en el pueblo. Parece que no toleran ninguna interferencia en su Gran Equilibrio.
STH_brightest_star_fail.2.name: La estrella más brillante
STH_cardassian_flavour.1.desc:
Y así Maric se encontró de nuevo ante los ladrones, su carro lleno de las muchas cosas que habían robado de la tienda del pueblo. Sabía que podía volver atrás, que podría regresar a casa a los brazos amorosos de Mela y los niños, y que pensarían menos de él? 

Pero pensaría menos. 

El par villano había robado el estado mismo. Cardassia en sí. Y eso no podía quedar sin castigo, aunque pudiera costarle la vida al hacerlo ...  

Ulan Corac ha publicado su novela The Never-Ending Sacrifice a la alabanza universal sobre Cardassia. Un ejemplo definitorio del género épico repetitivo, la crónica de siete generaciones de la familia Ularc ha inspirado a los lectores en todo el espacio cardassiano y reafirmó su compromiso con la familia y el estado. 

 [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName] ya ha propuesto que el trabajo sea de lectura obligatoria en todas las escuelas.
STH_cardassian_flavour.1.name: El sacrificio eterno
STH_cardassian_flavour.11.desc:
El carillón llega por el intercomunicador y Gul [Root.GetLeaderName] se llama al puente. La tripulación se queda un poco inquieta, cuando el Oficial Ejecutivo empieza a informar: 

Sir, hemos recibido una transmisión en un canal de Obsidian Order. 

And? ' -pregunta el Gul. 

Señor, hay órdenes para que usted asista a una investigación sobre su conducta. 

Un gruñido supera la cara del Gul cuando [officer.GetSheHe] nota una alerta de lanzamiento entrante en la consola de comunicaciones. El Oficial Ejecutivo hace un gesto a dos pares de Glins que sacan sus armas. 

Por favor, señor, de esta manera 'se dice, como Gul [Root.GetLeaderName] se conduce al turbolift en el punto de interrupción.
STH_cardassian_flavour.11.name: Investigación de Orden Obsidiana
STH_cardassian_flavour.12.desc:
Gul [Root.GetLeaderName] es conducido rápidamente a la sala de reuniones. La sala ha sido configurada para un tribunal militar estándar, aunque la Orden que actúa en la silla central elevada no tiene ningún estatus militar oficial. 

El operario da su declaración de apertura ante una asamblea de la tripulación mayor, enumerando una serie de acusaciones aparentemente contradictorias. 

Gul [Root.GetLeaderName] escucha en silencio durante varios minutos antes de que finalmente se le da la oportunidad de responder. 

 [oficial.GetSheHeCap] desconstruye metódicamente las acusaciones del operativo, y con la corroboración de la tripulación las acusaciones pronto caen. Echando una ojeada de reojo a la tripulación y frunciendo el ceño, el agente de la Orden Obsidiana llama a la investigación a su fin.
STH_cardassian_flavour.12.name: Prueba de obsidiana
STH_cardassian_flavour.13.desc:
Gul [Root.GetLeaderName] es conducido rápidamente a la sala de reuniones. La sala ha sido configurada para un tribunal militar estándar, aunque la Orden que actúa en la silla central elevada no tiene ningún estatus militar oficial. 

El operario da su declaración de apertura ante una asamblea de la tripulación mayor, enumerando una serie de acusaciones aparentemente contradictorias. 

Gul [Root.GetLeaderName] escucha en silencio durante varios minutos antes de que finalmente se le da la oportunidad de responder. 

 [officer.GetSheHeCap] desconstruye metódicamente las acusaciones del operativo, pero cuando se le pide corroborar la información, la tripulación ofrece informes contradictorios. Echando una ojeada a la tripulación de [officer.GetHerHis], la cara de Gul [Root.GetLeaderName] se hace más estricta cuando [officer.GetSheHe] se da cuenta de que [officer.GetSheHe] ha sido superado. El operativo de la Orden Obsidiana despide al público y viene a hablar con el Gul. 

Si esto se convierte en público, su carrera ha terminado ", declara. A partir de ahora, usted nos informará.
STH_cardassian_flavour.13.name: Prueba de obsidiana
STH_cardassian_flavour.14.desc:
El carillón llega por el intercomunicador y Gul [Root.GetLeaderName] se llama al puente. La tripulación se queda un poco inquieta, cuando el Oficial Ejecutivo empieza a informar: 

Señor, hemos recibido una transmisión en un canal de Obsidian Order.

Y? ' -pregunta el Gul. 

Señor, hay órdenes para que usted asista a una investigación sobre su conducta. 

Un gruñido supera la cara del Gul cuando [officer.GetSheHe] nota una alerta de lanzamiento entrante en la consola de comunicaciones. El Oficial Ejecutivo hace un gesto a dos pares de Glins que sacan sus armas. 

Por favor, señor, de esta manera 'se dice, como Gul [Root.GetLeaderName] se conduce al turbolift en el punto de interrupción.
STH_cardassian_flavour.14.name: Investigación de Orden Obsidiana
STH_cardassian_flavour.15.desc:
Gul [Root.GetLeaderName] está encadenado y conducido rápidamente a una pequeña habitación antes de ser arrojado al suelo cerca de un operario vestido oscuro de la Orden Obsidiana. 

Esta no será una ... encuesta estándar, Gul 'la operativa se burla, mientras ella maneja al Gul en la silla. Un gran golpe en la parte posterior de la cabeza golpea al Gul inconsciente. 

El fallo en seguir las directivas, el exceso de celeridad en la ejecución de las órdenes, el retraso en la ejecución de las órdenes ... 'Las declaraciones falsas se lavan sobre Gul [Root.GetLeaderName] mientras las luces brillan sobre él y las palizas continúan durante el día. 

Días más tarde Gul [Root.GetLeaderName] se despierta al sonido del Oficial Ejecutivo discutiendo con el operativo. Snarling, el operario se acerca a Gul [Root.GetLeaderName] y comienza a desatar las restricciones. Gul [Root.GetLeaderName] se vuelve hacia el agente y ladra una carcajada.
STH_cardassian_flavour.15.name: Prueba de obsidiana
STH_cardassian_flavour.16.desc:
Gul [Root.GetLeaderName] está encadenado y conducido rápidamente a una pequeña habitación antes de ser arrojado al suelo cerca de un operario vestido oscuro de la Orden Obsidiana. 

Esta no será una ... encuesta estándar, Gul 'la operativa se burla, mientras ella maneja al Gul en la silla. Un gran golpe en la parte posterior de la cabeza golpea al Gul inconsciente. 

El fallo en seguir las directivas, el exceso de celeridad en la ejecución de las órdenes, el retraso en la ejecución de las órdenes ... 'Las declaraciones falsas se lavan sobre Gul [Root.GetLeaderName] mientras las luces brillan sobre él y las palizas continúan durante el día. 

Días más tarde Gul [Root.GetLeaderName] se despierta, jadeando para respirar. El operario se vuelve y dice 

Isto todo terminará si me dices cuántas luces ves. '
STH_cardassian_flavour.16.name: Prueba de obsidiana
STH_cardassian_flavour.17.desc: El [Root.GetName] estaba realizando simulacros de preparación para el combate cuando los sensores internos detectaron una lectura anómala cerca del núcleo de la nave. \n\nInvestigándose además, un equipo de ingeniería ha localizado un dispositivo conectado a la red eléctrica en un corredor de acceso de mantenimiento que parece estar transmitiendo información sensible del buque.
STH_cardassian_flavour.17.name: Un extraño descubrimiento
STH_cardassian_flavour.18.a: La matriz de sensores podría usar un nuevo relé anódino ...
STH_cardassian_flavour.18.desc: Gul [Root.GetLeaderName] ordenó al equipo de ingeniería desmontar el dispositivo. Las horas pasan antes de que el Ingeniero Jefe entre en la sala de reuniones de Gul [Root.GetLeaderName]. \n\nGul, el dispositivo parece derivarse de los diseños anódinos de interceptación de comunicación usados ??por la Orden Obsidiana. Cuando investigamos más de cerca el dispositivo apagado y no hemos podido reactivar. ¿Qué quieres hacer con el dispositivo?
STH_cardassian_flavour.18.name: Bloqueo de obsidiana
STH_cardassian_flavour.19.a: Coloque el dispositivo en almacenamiento. Esto puede resultar útil.
STH_cardassian_flavour.19.desc: Gul [Root.GetLeaderName] ordenó al equipo de ingeniería desmontar el dispositivo. Las horas pasan antes de que el Ingeniero Jefe entre en la sala de reuniones de Gul [Root.GetLeaderName]. \n\nGul, el dispositivo parece derivarse de los diseños anódinos de interceptación de comunicación usados ??por la Orden Obsidiana. Cuando investigamos más de cerca el dispositivo apagado y no hemos podido reactivar. ¿Qué quieres hacer con el dispositivo?
STH_cardassian_flavour.19.name: Bloqueo de obsidiana
STH_cardassian_flavour.2.desc:
Ulim escuchó el carillón de la puerta y terminó la copa de Kanar que se aferró antes de apagar la cerradura y, al girar finalmente, vio la silueta atrevida contra el brillo del pasillo más allá, una elegante nube de materia oscura ante una supernova. Colocó la botella de Kanar en el cajón del escritorio y sacó discretamente su disruptor antes de encender la iluminación de la habitación. 

Ella se deslizó hacia adelante, los mechones de sus cabellos se balanceaban, antes de sentarse al borde del escritorio de Ulim. 

Me dijeron que usted es un Investigador. Alguien que realmente debería estar investigando ... La serie de novelas de Enigma Tales, de Golu Shoggoth, ha gozado de una amplia aceptación en toda la Unión Cardassiana, centrada en el trabajo de Investigadores Públicos dedicados aunque a veces defectuosos y sus incesantes esfuerzos Para descubrir los crímenes de los culpables. 

Cuando [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName] se ha abstenido de respaldar las obras públicamente, con muchos creyendo una preocupación por las características personales menos que perfectas de los protagonistas de Shoggoth, las personas no son -el menos-dado menos a una nueva apreciación por el trabajo de nuestros investigadores criminales ".
STH_cardassian_flavour.2.name: Enigma Tales
STH_cardassian_flavour.20.a: Es hora de ver lo que hay dentro.
STH_cardassian_flavour.20.desc:
El [Root.GetFleetName] estaba realizando ejercicios de preparación para el combate cuando los sensores externos detectaron una lectura anómala de varios millones de kilómetros estribor.

Gul, la lectura parece ser un contenedor de diseño doméstico. Está protegido de los escáneres, pero parece ser seguro ", informa el Oficial Científico. Gul [Root.GetLeaderName] ordena que se transporte a bordo de la carga.

Una inspección más cercana parece ser un contenedor de almacenamiento de tipo estándar seis con una pantalla intermitente que solicita un código de comando. Después de ingresar el código de comando [officer.GetHerHis] Gul [Root.GetLeaderName] se presenta con otro panel con lo que parece ser un puerto de escáner de retina.
STH_cardassian_flavour.20.name: Un informe extraño
STH_cardassian_flavour.21.desc: Gul [Root.GetLeaderName] se inclina sobre el contenedor hasta el escáner de la retina y se mantiene firme mientras un láser de color rojo brillante examina el ojo de [officer.GetHerHis]. El escáner de retina se vuelve azul y se escucha un silbido. Saltando hacia atrás, Gul [Root.GetLeaderName] observa el contenedor poco apresuradamente y el silbido se abre, mostrando un gran caché de armamento, equipo y suministros.
STH_cardassian_flavour.21.name: Autorización aceptada
STH_cardassian_flavour.22.a: El Comando Central debe ser informado.
STH_cardassian_flavour.22.desc: Gul [Root.GetLeaderName] se inclina sobre el contenedor hasta el escáner de la retina y se mantiene firme mientras un láser de color rojo brillante examina el ojo de [officer.GetHerHis]. El escáner de retina se vuelve azul y se escucha un silbido. Saltando hacia atrás, Gul [Root.GetLeaderName] observa el contenedor poco apresuradamente y silbido abierto, un número de datapads derramando en el suelo. \n\nActivar las almohadillas revela que contienen información política sensible; El caché ahora claramente destinado a espías extranjeros.
STH_cardassian_flavour.22.name: Autorización aceptada
STH_cardassian_flavour.23.desc: Gul [Root.GetLeaderName] se inclina sobre el contenedor hasta el escáner de la retina y se mantiene firme mientras un láser de color rojo brillante examina el ojo de [officer.GetHerHis]. El escáner de retina se vuelve rojo y se oye un silbido. Intentando saltar de nuevo, Gul [Root.GetLeaderName] es rociado en la cara con una sustancia extraña y observa como el contenedor suena el klaxon de una autodestrucción cardassiana. Gul [Root.GetLeaderName] golpea el botón de transporte de emergencia, enviando el contenedor hacia el espacio.
STH_cardassian_flavour.23.name: Autorización denegada
STH_cardassian_flavour.24.desc: Gul [Root.GetLeaderName] se inclina sobre el contenedor hasta el escáner de la retina y se mantiene firme mientras un láser de color rojo brillante examina el ojo de [officer.GetHerHis]. El escáner de retina se vuelve rojo y se oye un silbido. Al intentar saltar hacia atrás, Gul [Root.GetLeaderName] observa como un gas azul bombea directamente a la cara de [officer.GetHerHis], haciendo que [officer.GetHerHim] caiga al suelo, gritando en voz alta de dolor. El veneno se enfurece a través de las venas mientras el mundo empieza a ponerse rojo.
STH_cardassian_flavour.24.name: Autorización denegada
STH_cardassian_flavour.3.desc:
El taladro Glin estaba de pie sobre los reclutas, su rostro era una piedra y su mirada se extendía por años. 

Todos están aquí por una razón, y una sola razón. Para defender al pueblo cardassiano, su voz resonó por el patio. 

Nadie de nosotros escogió esta guerra. Fue empujado sobre nosotros. Los perros [other_empire.GetSpeciesAdj] no se detendrán en nada para destruir el [Root.GetName], para empujar a nuestra gente de vuelta a Cardassia y nos erradican de la galaxia. Pero tú ... no dejas que eso suceda. ¿Tengo razón? -preguntó mientras los miles de voluntarios gritaban al unísono: 

¡Por Cardassia! 

La publicación de la última obra de ficción científica "Meditaciones sobre una sombra carmesí" de Eleta Preloc ha llevado a un aumento en las solicitudes voluntarias a las órdenes a través de [Root.GetName]. Preloc describe una serie de guerras en el futuro distante entre el [Root.GetName] y un villanous [other_empire.GetName] centrado en el exterminio de las personas [Root.GetSpeciesAdj]. 

 [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName] ha descrito el trabajo como particularmente relevante teniendo en cuenta las relaciones actuales con el [other_empire.GetName] y ha recomendado a Preloc para el prestigioso Premio Gerok.
STH_cardassian_flavour.3.name: Meditaciones sobre una sombra carmesí
STH_cardassian_story.1000.a.tooltip: The Hebitian Union will be restored, its spiritual and environmental ideals replacing those of the former [Root.GetName].
STH_cardassian_story.1000.b.tooltip: The [Root.GetName] will be embrace it's Hebitian heritage and remodel itself along those spiritual and environmental ideals.
STH_cardassian_story.1000.desc: The acceptance of the Oralian religion on [Root.Capital.GetName] coupled with loosening cultural restrictions has led to a revival of 'Hebitianism'. This movement seeks to restore Cardassian culture to its Hebitian origins, from the time before the modern Cardassian state was formed.
STH_cardassian_story.1000.name: Hebitian Restoration
STH_cardassian_story.1001.a: Very well, we are Hebitian too.
STH_cardassian_story.1001.desc: The government of the [cardassian_country.GetName] has decided to embrace the Oralian religion and the Hebitian culture that dominated Cardassia prior to its emergence as a galactic power. [cardassian_country.GetRulerTitle] [cardassian_country.GetRulerName] has even go so far as to propose the renaming of the Cardassian race back to the original Hebitian.
STH_cardassian_story.1001.name: Hebitian Restoration
STH_cardassian_story.1002.desc: Our agents in the [cardassian_country.GetName] report that the state has fully embraced the Oralian religion and the Hebitian culture that dominated Cardassia prior to its emergence as a galactic power.
STH_cardassian_story.1002.name: Hebitian Restoration
STH_cardassian_story.101.b: This is a waste of time.
STH_cardassian_story.101.desc:
According to the legends, centuries ago the ancient Bajorans used wooden ships to explore other planets. These ships were propelled by light pressure, like a sailboat catching the wind. Some even say that one of this ships have made it all the way to [Root.GetName].

Naturally, we have dismissed all such claims as it is almost impossible to believe that the Bajorans have achieved interstellar travel before the [Root.Owner.GetName].

And yet, a routine archeological expedition to one of the remote regions of [Root.GetName] has recently revealed something that might potentially be a crash site of one of these ancient Bajoran ships. Unfortunately, archaeologists had only the most primitive scanning equipment, so they couldn't be absolutely sure. Perhaps a more thorough study is in order.
STH_cardassian_story.101.name: Ancient Contact
STH_cardassian_story.102.a.tooltip: This is the beginning of a beautiful friendship
STH_cardassian_story.102.desc: A closer examination reveals that an ancient Bajoran vessel did indeed travelled to Cardassia and crashed on arrival all these centuries ago. A remarkable achievement, but one that threatens Cardassian supremacy.\n\nRevealing this information to Bajorans will improve diplomatic relations with them. Alternatively, it is possible to destroy the ships remnant and dispose of the survey team. No one will know.
STH_cardassian_story.102.name: Ancient Contact
STH_cardassian_story.103.b: Sell them to a private collector.
STH_cardassian_story.103.desc:
What seemed like a crash-site turned out to be nothing but a scanner malfunction. But it wasn't all in vain, as the digging revealed a previously unknown burial site of the First Hebitian civilisation!

The inner chambers of the vault contain a number of beautiful artifacts made of jevonite, a rare, breathtaking stone. These artifacts undoubtedly are a part of [Root.Owner.GetSpeciesAdj] heritage and will be of great scientific and cultural value.

Alternatively, they can be sold for a good price to a private collector.
STH_cardassian_story.103.name: Ancient Contact
STH_cardassian_story.104.a: Why does anybody use self-sealing stem bolts?
STH_cardassian_story.104.desc: What seemed like a crash site at first, turned out to be nothing but a scanner malfunction. But it wasn't all in vain, as the digging revealed an abandoned smuggler's lair full with crates with self-sealing stem bolts!\n\n With no one to be arrested for smuggling, the contents of these crates have been simply confiscated by [Root.Owner.GetName]. Now the question is, what do we do with eighteen thousand tons of self-sealing stem bolts?
STH_cardassian_story.104.name: Ancient Contact
STH_cardassian_story.105.a.tooltip: Perhaps they are not so bad after all...
STH_cardassian_story.105.desc:
According to the legends, centuries ago the ancient [Root.GetSpeciesNamePlural] used wooden ships to explore other planets.

Many [Root.GetSpeciesNamePlural] believe that one of these ships could have made it all the way to Cardassia. Of course, for a long time [cardassian_country.GetSpeciesNamePlural] have called it nothing but a fairy tale.

 It is hard to tell, what exactly have changed their minds, but an official envoy from [cardassian_country.GetName] has notified [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName] that a [cardassian_country.GetAdj] archaeological team has recently discovered an ancient crash site that contains the remnants of one of the [Root.GetAdj] vessels.
STH_cardassian_story.105.name: Ancient Contact
STH_cardassian_story.500.desc:
The [Root.GetName]'s recent defeat has left it vulnerable. Rivals like the [threateningCountry.GetName] pose an existential threat to the [Root.GetSpeciesAdj] way of life.

All agree that the [Root.GetName] cannot be allowed to remain a second rate power in the quadrant, but few can find common ground on how to proceed.

Among the endless debates a lone voice strikes a chord with [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName]; an junior [Root.GetCaptainTitle] points to the [the_dominion.GetName] and the opportunities that membership of that union may provide.
STH_cardassian_story.500.name: [Root.Capital.GetName] Will Rise Again
STH_cardassian_story.501.desc: The [From.GetName] has suffered a recent defeat which has left it vulnerable. Rivals like the [FromFrom.GetName] pose an existential threat to the [From.GetSpeciesAdj] way of life.\n\nRecognising our implacable nature, the [From.GetSpeciesName] leadership has sent secret diplomatic overtures to our Vorta ambassadors to pursue membership of the [Root.GetName].
STH_cardassian_story.501.name: [From.GetName] Interests
STH_cardassian_story.502.desc: The [From.GetName] has proven willing to accept our terms for joining as a semi-autonomous member. Chief among those demands territorial rights over our neighbours, in particular the [FromFrom.GetName]. \n\nAll that remains now is to sign the treaty and convince the [Root.GetSpeciesName] public - by force of arms if not argument - that this is a necessary step for [Root.Capital.GetName].
STH_cardassian_story.502.name: [Root.Capital.GetName] Will Rise Again
STH_cardassian_story.503.desc_01: La infraestructura planetaria clave en [destroy_random_building_planet.GetName] ha sido saboteada, dejando la ubicación en ruinas. Las investigaciones iniciales señalan la culpa de que los elementos criminales de la región intenten robar componentes de alto valor de su objetivo y desencadenar accidentalmente un lazo de retroalimentación catastrófica en el sistema eléctrico. \n\nSugestiones de la participación extranjera por elementos marginales de la administración de [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName] son ??despedidos sin evidencia.
STH_cardassian_story.503.desc_02: Nuestra instalación en órbita de [destroy_random_station_planet.GetName] ha sido saboteada, destruyendo completamente la estación. Las investigaciones iniciales apuntan la culpa a los elementos criminales del sistema que intentan robar cargas de alto valor desde su objetivo y desencadenar accidentalmente un lazo de retroalimentación catastrófica en el sistema eléctrico. \n\nSugestiones de la participación extranjera por elementos marginales de la administración de [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName] son ??despedidos sin evidencia.
STH_cardassian_story.503.name: Infraestructura pública orientada
STH_cardassian_story.504.desc: The [From.GetName]'s demands for joining as a semi-autonomous member were easy to accept.
