The meeting could only be described as a success and provides the Captain and crew with new experience and skills and after sharing cultural gifts and stories it is time to move on with the expedition.
STH_expedition.435.name: Flagship 3-Year Mission Year 1: First Contact
STH_expedition.436.desc: It does not take long to realise that the meeting is not going to plan, there are many more differences than similarities and the meeting rapidly turns hostile. The Captain quickly decides that it is time to leave before the hosts turn hostile. This could not have gone worse.
STH_expedition.436.name: Flagship 3-Year Mission Year 1: First Contact
STH_expedition.44.desc_01:
A pesar de los mejores esfuerzos de todos los involucrados, no pudimos evitar que el asteroide golpeara la superficie del planeta. Toda la vida en la superficie parece haber sido destruida y el clima severamente dañado, un final prematuro y una terrible pérdida de vidas. 

Puede haber consecuencias una vez que la tripulación regrese a [Root.Capital.GetName] por violar uno de los [ Los inquilinos principales de Root.GetName]. 

La pérdida de vidas pesará mucho en la conciencia de [sth_expedition_Leader_2.GetName].
STH_expedition.44.name: Misión de tres años 2: incapaz de salvar primitivas
STH_expedition.440.desc: We have been asked to provide a diplomatic envoy service between two species that have been hostile for many years. As [sth_expedition_Leader_flag.GetLeaderName] stands in a grand chamber in front of the Alien leader, who demands to know why they should accept any offering from their rivals.
STH_expedition.440.name: Flagship 3-Year Mission Year 1: Diplomatic Envoy
STH_expedition.441.desc: The Alien leader pauses, tension in the room mounts... then all of a sudden he breaks into a broad smile showing rows of small fangs. The alien loves the offering provided and agrees to hear our words, the first step towards creating peace.
STH_expedition.441.name: Flagship 3-Year Mission Year 1: Diplomatic Envoy
STH_expedition.442.desc: The Alien leader pauses and tension in the room mounts... then all of a sudden a loud growl breaks out from the small creature's fanged mouth. Clearly insulted by our offering they are not prepared to engage further - it looks like we will not be able to bring peace to this sector.
STH_expedition.442.name: Flagship 3-Year Mission Year 1: Diplomatic Envoy
STH_expedition.443.desc: [sth_expedition_Leader_flag.GetLeaderName]'s diplomatic skills are formidable and they are able to successfully negotiate with the alien leader. It appears as if a breakthrough has been made and the first steps towards opening up a dialogue has been reached.
STH_expedition.443.name: Flagship 3-Year Mission Year 1: Diplomatic Envoy
STH_expedition.444.desc: [sth_expedition_Leader_flag.GetLeaderName]'s diplomatic skills are formidable but they are unable to negotiate with the alien leader. Despite the best efforts a compromise could not be reached and the aliens are unwilling to allow the talks to progress any further.
STH_expedition.444.name: Flagship 3-Year Mission Year 1: Diplomatic Envoy
STH_expedition.445.desc: The alien leader takes the challenge in good spirits, impressed by our brashness and courage, the Alien leader agrees to accept our demands and open up a dialogue with their adversaries. Sometimes it pays to be forceful.
STH_expedition.445.name: Flagship 3-Year Mission Year 1: Diplomatic Envoy
STH_expedition.446.a: Starfleet officers do not engage in fights.
STH_expedition.446.desc: The alien leader does not take our demand well, and bellows 'You dare challenge us, this is an insult!'\n\nThe assembled crowd begin to be whipped up into a frenzy and the noise level in the chamber rises. The alien leader stands a mere 2 foot tall, but bristles with teeth and claws, and issues a challenge, a fight for honour...
STH_expedition.446.name: Flagship 3-Year Mission Year 1: Diplomatic Envoy
STH_expedition.447.desc: The fight is swift and brutal - the small alien attacks with the fury of a rabid bunny and the tenacity of an earth canine called a Chihuahua.\n\nThe superior training given to all officers combined with a righteous left boot quickly ends the battle though as the Alien leader reluctantly surrenders and submits to our demands.
STH_expedition.447.name: Flagship 3-Year Mission Year 1: Diplomatic Envoy
STH_expedition.448.desc: The fight is swift and brutal - the small alien attacks with the fury of a rabid bunny and the tenacity of an earth canine called a Chihuahua.\n\nA series of bites to the legs of the captain quickly incapacitate them, forcing them to yield before any more injuries can be done. The captain reluctantly surrenders and agrees to leave the planet as soon as possible. The physical wounds will take time to heal, but the dented pride may take longer.
STH_expedition.448.name: Flagship 3-Year Mission Year 1: Diplomatic Envoy
STH_expedition.45.desc: [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName], lamento informar que nuestra expedición ha llegado a un final temprano. El último registro indicó que la tripulación intentó desviar un asteroide con un rayo tractor, sin embargo, parece que no tuvieron éxito y quedaron atrapados en el campo de rocas. La nave y la tripulación se perdieron con toda la tripulación.
STH_expedition.45.name: Misión de tres años 2: tripulación perdida
STH_expedition.450.a: It is our duty as Starfleet officers.
STH_expedition.450.c: They do not deserve our help.
STH_expedition.450.desc: We have picked up a distress call from an unknown vessel, they are losing life support and have declared that their warp core is heading towards a breach.They are requesting assistance from any vessel within range, we should alter course to offer assistance, but if the vessel explodes with us in range it could destroy our Flagship.
STH_expedition.450.name: Flagship 3-Year Mission Year 1: SOS
STH_expedition.451.desc: Our expert engineering away team were able to bring the warp core breach back under control, resulting in saving the lives of all on the alien ship. The captain of the ship would like to offer us a reward.
STH_expedition.451.name: Flagship 3-Year Mission Year 1: SOS
STH_expedition.452.desc: Whilst not every distress call can be answered, each failure to respond to incidents reflects poorly on the [Root.GetName]'s reputation is this part of space.
STH_expedition.452.name: Flagship 3-Year Mission Year 1: SOS
STH_expedition.455.desc: Our expedition has come across an enormous cloud in space travelling at warp speed - a strange phenomenon! The Operations Officer has asked if a sensor sweep could be carried out.\n\nShould we slow down and take a scan or carry on with the mission?
STH_expedition.455.name: Flagship 3-Year Mission Year 2: Strange Events
STH_expedition.456.desc: The scan of the cloud managed to provide additional physics research, scanning the cloud was worth the detour.
STH_expedition.456.name: Flagship 3-Year Mission Year 2: Strange Events
STH_expedition.457.desc: On scanning the cloud the ship was struck with a small blast of lightning, no damage done to the ship. The scan was able to provide additional research which will provide useful for our scientists back home.\n\nThe chief medical officer does report that one of the engineering techs was found unconscious in the sensor maintenance bay.
STH_expedition.457.name: Flagship 3-Year Mission Year 2: Strange Events
STH_expedition.458.desc: A few days have passed since scanning the cloud in space and the number of strange events have been reported on the ship, unexplained malfunctions and crew members reporting gaps in time. Suddenly [sth_expedition_Leader_flag.GetName] appears on the bridge and alters the ships course, back to the travelling cloud!
STH_expedition.458.name: Flagship 3-Year Mission Year 2: Strange Events
STH_expedition.459.desc: The ship stops directly in front of the space cloud, before they can be stopped the Captain activates the transporter and beams into the cloud. Numerous lightning strikes rattle the hull, each causing more damage than the last, we will not be able to stay here much longer
STH_expedition.459.name: Flagship 3-Year Mission Year 2: Strange Events
STH_expedition.46.desc: Los escaneos han recogido una pequeña nave civil bajo ataque de varios combatientes de ataque. Los escudos de sus naves están fallando y no podrán durar mucho más tiempo. \n\n Parece que los agresores tienen poco interés en nuestra nave, pero eso cambiaría si los atacáramos. ¿Cuáles son nuestras órdenes señor?
STH_expedition.46.name: Misión de tres años 2: Nave bajo ataque
STH_expedition.460.desc:
After much searching, the crew are ready to abandon the search when suddenly one of the consoles displays a single letter - could it be the Captain?

The First Office races to the transporter room and orders the chief to recombine the last used energy pattern, hoping that [sth_expedition_Leader_flag.GetName] has worked their way into the transporter.

The transporter chief energizes and mercifully the Captain materializes on the transporter pad. with only a vague memory of what happened to them.
STH_expedition.460.name: Flagship 3-Year Mission Year 2: Strange Events
STH_expedition.461.desc: With the damage to the ship increasing rapidly, the First Officer is forced to abandon the search. The Captain has been declared missing in action.\n\nThe crew set a course back to known space, morale slumped at the loss of their captain.
STH_expedition.461.name: Flagship 3-Year Mission Year 2: Strange Events
STH_expedition.462.desc: With the damage to the ship increasing, the First Officer must make a choice: continue to risk the ship in the search or to abandon their captain.\n\nThe risk to the ship is increasing, but the captain does not have long left to live.
STH_expedition.462.name: Flagship 3-Year Mission Year 2: Strange Events
STH_expedition.463.desc: The ship has remained too close to the lightning cloud for too long, and a lightning bolt has struck the warp nacelles resulting in a cascade to the warp core. The chief engineer informs the bridge that there is nothing that they can do, the warp core will breach imminently.\n\nThe First Officer signals for the crew to abandon ship and can only hope the crew can reach the escape pods in time...
STH_expedition.463.name: Flagship 3-Year Mission Year 2: Strange Events
STH_expedition.465.desc: Our long range scans have found a pre-warp culture, our on-board anthropologists would like to take a closer look as this will provide an excellent insight into our own history, we must ensure that the Prime Directive is followed, but we maybe able to view a glimpse of our own development and further our research into this area.
STH_expedition.465.name: Flagship 3-Year Mission Year 2: Primitive Culture
STH_expedition.466.desc: The people on the planet below are fascinating, clearly undergoing a renaissance, exploring new forms of art and culture. Whilst extremely interesting, there is nothing further we can learn and should leave before our presence is detected. This has been a most interesting endeavour.
STH_expedition.466.name: Flagship 3-Year Mission Year 2: Primitive Culture
STH_expedition.467.a: We must respect the Prime Directive.
STH_expedition.467.desc_01: [sth_expedition_Leader_flag.GetName] has uncovered a worrying sign, it appears that a recent undersea earthquake has sent a massive tsunami towards the settlement, It would wipe out the entire colony. The prime-directive strictly states that we cannot interfere with the natural development of this civilisation, however not doing so would almost certainly result in the deaths of all in the colony and end this civilisation.
STH_expedition.467.desc_02: [sth_expedition_Leader_flag.GetName] has uncovered a worrying sign, it appears that a recent undersea earthquake has sent a massive tsunami towards the settlement, It would wipe out the entire colony. Our leadership has stated that we cannot interfere with the natural development of this civilisation, however not doing so would almost certainly result in the deaths of all in the colony and end this civilisation, what decision should be made.
STH_expedition.467.name: Flagship 3-Year Mission Year 2: Primitive Culture
STH_expedition.468.desc: [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName] the mission report indicates that [sth_expedition_leader_1.GetName] was successful in saving the lives of all those on the planet. Who knows what impact this will have on the future, our influence has had a direct impact on the natural development of this species. Let's hope it is for the better. The captain will need to justify their actions on return to [Root.GetName] space.
STH_expedition.468.name: Flagship 3-Year Mission Year 2: Primitive Culture
STH_expedition.469.desc: Despite the best efforts by all involved the team were unable to evacuate the colony, wave hit as expected destroying all in its path. There were no survivors.
STH_expedition.469.name: Flagship 3-Year Mission Year 2: Primitive Culture
STH_expedition.47.desc: Los agresores no podían competir con la habilidad y el poder de nuestra nave y logramos hacer una declaración contra aquellos que desafiarían [Root.GetName]. \n\n Como un bono adicional, pudimos obtener una cantidad decente de salvamento de Los restos de la nave destruida. Deberíamos recogerlo antes de continuar con la misión
STH_expedition.47.name: Misión de tres años 2: Victoria
STH_expedition.470.desc: [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName], I am sorry to report that our expedition has come to an early end, the first officer has reported back that the Captain and senior members of the crew were killed in action attempting to save the lives of a primitive culture on a planet on the far edges of known space. It appears the rescue went badly wrong, as a result we have ordered the crew home and the ship will be mothballed whilst a full internal investigation takes place.
STH_expedition.470.name: Flagship 3-Year Mission Year 2: Primitive Culture
STH_expedition.471.desc: Our expedition has meet up with delegates from the people of Angosia III who are in the process of recovering from the Tarsian War. Little is known about the war, but it was suspected that both sides used enhanced soldiers to wage war.
STH_expedition.471.name: Flagship 3-Year Mission Year 2: The Hunted
STH_expedition.472.desc: [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName] we have been contacted by a former soldier from the Tarsian War, who is requesting asylum within [Root.GetName], they claim that all former soldiers from the war have been locked away pending execution and that they just want the chance to live a life of their own. We could offer them an asylum within our systems, but doing so could anger the Angosians.
STH_expedition.472.name: Flagship 3-Year Mission Year 2: The Hunted
STH_expedition.473.desc: As suspected granting asylum to the former solder has angered the Angosians, who are demanding the return of their former soldier. Failing to do so will result in retribution.
STH_expedition.473.name: Flagship 3-Year Mission Year 2: The Hunted
STH_expedition.474.desc: The Angosian ships were no match for our vessels firepower and have broken off their attacks. The prime minister of Angosia III declares that we may take the former soldier, but that there will be no future alliance between our races. It would be wise to leave this sector immediately.
STH_expedition.474.name: Flagship 3-Year Mission Year 2: The Hunted
STH_expedition.475.desc: The Angosian ships were no match for our vessels firepower however they are attacking with fury, which allows a boarding party to get onto our ship. Reports from security confirm that one of the boarding parties made it to the location of the asylum seeker and managed to kill them.
STH_expedition.475.name: Flagship 3-Year Mission Year 2: The Hunted
STH_expedition.477.b: The crew should have more important things to do.
STH_expedition.477.desc: Recent shipboard activity has included the increasing use of a new holodeck program based on one of the 18th/19th centuries greatest fictional detectives, Sherlock Holmes. The programme has been designed to adapt and provide a villian worthy of the player. The crew are thoroughly enjoying the increasingly difficult stories and the devious arch villain Moriarty.
STH_expedition.477.name: Flagship 3-Year Mission Year 2: Elementary
STH_expedition.478.desc: [sth_expedition_Leader_flag.GetName]! We need to make you aware that the Moriarty programme appears to have taken over the holodeck and is demanding his freedom and a shuttle. Failure to grant his demands would result in further attempts to take over the ship. Gathering the senior staff two options are presented, we could attempt to deceive the hologram and trap him in another program emulating his freedom or we could destroy the holodeck completely.
STH_expedition.478.name: Flagship 3-Year Mission Year 2: Elementary
STH_expedition.479.desc: We have successfully trapped the Moriarty programme, they are none the wiser that their freedom is just another holographic illusion. The science council back on [Root.GetHomeWorldName] believe that they could use the program to aid our scientists. We have our very own ship in a bottle ready to use as we need. Whilst many do not feel comfortable with this approach, the knowledge and cunning of 'Moriarty' will only advance our studies.
STH_expedition.479.name: Flagship 3-Year Mission Year 2: Elementary
STH_expedition.48.desc:
[Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName], lamento informar que nuestra expedición ha llegado a un final temprano, recogimos su llamada de socorro hoy a través de nuestras comunicaciones de largo alcance. 

 Parece que intentaron defenderse contra un atacante desconocido, pero finalmente no tuvieron éxito. 

Simplemente, la nave parece haber sido destruida con todas la tripulación.
STH_expedition.48.name: Misión de tres años 2: Derrota
STH_expedition.480.desc: Our Flagship has encountered a repository in space, containing many thousands of years worth of knowledge, we are being offered the opportunity to take a sample of this information back before the repository expires and explodes. Our officers advise that we could accept the offer or fall back and collect some rare materials from the wreckage of the ancient computer.
STH_expedition.480.name: Flagship 3-Year Mission Year 3: Knowledge
STH_expedition.481.desc: Scans have picked up a small civilian passenger ship under attack by a group of interstellar pirates, the ships shields are failing and they will not be able to last much longer. It looks like they are after the crew to sell into slavery. We could engage the attackers but it would be a risky venture, with small reward.
STH_expedition.481.name: Flagship 3-Year Mission Year 3: Attacked
STH_expedition.482.desc: The aggressors were no match for the skill and power of our ship, we managed to make a statement against those who would challenge [root.GetName] and as an added bonus we were able to collect a decent amount of salvage from the wreckage of the destroyed ship. We should collect it before moving on with the mission.
STH_expedition.482.name: Flagship 3-Year Mission Year 3: Attacked
STH_expedition.483.desc: [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName], I am sorry to report that our expedition has come to an early end. We picked up their distress call earlier today via our long range communications.\n\nIt appears they attempted to defend themselves against an unknown attacker but were ultimately unsuccessful. Sadly the ship appears to have been destroyed with all hands.
STH_expedition.483.name: Flagship 3-Year Mission Year 3: Attacked
STH_expedition.486.desc: We have been contacted by a local trader, who has a number of minerals they would be willing to trade with us.
STH_expedition.486.name: Flagship 3-Year Mission Year 3: Trader
STH_expedition.487.desc: [sth_expedition_Leader_flag.GetName] has been contacted by the admiralty back in Starfleet HQ, asking the flagship to retrieve one of our old probes 'Friendship Two' a follow up to the Friendship One mission that was sent out so many years ago.
STH_expedition.487.name: Flagship 3-Year Mission Year 3: Mission from home
STH_expedition.49.desc_01: [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName], tenemos una excelente oportunidad para proporcionar a nuestros científicos de nuevo en [Root.Owner.Capital.GetName] algunas muestras de investigación de una nebulosa cercana. Desvíe el curso para tomar estas muestras de la nebulosa.
STH_expedition.49.desc_02: [sth_expedition_Leader_2.GetName] uno de nuestros principales generales nos ha contactado para pedirnos que completemos una tarea para ellos. Se ha detectado una sonda militar perdida hace mucho tiempo cerca y se nos ha encomendado la tarea de recuperarla.
STH_expedition.49.name: Misión de tres años 2: Misión de científicos principales
STH_expedition.50.desc: Hemos recibido una llamada de socorro de un carguero, parece que transportan una sustancia volátil, que se ha vuelto inestable en el transporte. \n\nEl capitán del carguero informa que no podrán contener la explosión inminente. La explosión podría causar daños severos a nuestra nave, pero sin nuestra ayuda el carguero será destruido.
STH_expedition.50.name: Misión de tres años 2: misión de rescate
STH_expedition.500.b: Not our business what they are up to. Leave.
STH_expedition.500.desc:
We have detected what appears to be a derelict alien vessel floating in space that is not responding to our hails.

Moving closer to the ship a more detailed scan reveals that the ship has no working propulsion, communications or weapons. Further inspection also shows what appear to be marks of weapon damage on the ship's hull. Running a bio scan on the vessel shows weak readings of life forms aboard.

With all external scans now complete the question becomes should we board the vessel to continue finding out what happened to it or even offer possible assistance or should we just leave it be and not interfere with whatever is going on?
STH_expedition.500.name: Three Year Mission 1: A derelict ship discovered
STH_expedition.5001.desc: All expeditions have now been recalled back to [Root.Capital.GetName] and the expedition leaders are free to return to active service. They will be able to free to resume future expeditions after a short duration.
STH_expedition.5001.name: Recall Expeditions
STH_expedition.501.name: Three Year Mission 2: A derelict ship discovered
STH_expedition.502.name: Three Year Mission 3: A derelict ship discovered
STH_expedition.503.a: Try to contact the alien homeworld.
STH_expedition.503.desc:
Upon entering the alien ship our away team discovers the ship to have a nitrogen-methane atmosphere and temperatures below zero. However there is no sign of the crew anywhere.

The away team enters into a large hall where their work lights catch a glimpse of something hanging from the sealing. This turns up to be members of the alien crew hanging upside down hooked to a pumping device that is flushing their bodies with some sort of fluid. It now becomes obvious that someone is harvesting something from the alien bodies and thus will most likely be coming back at some point to collect it. Also judging from the probable battle damage on the alien ship and the condition of the crew it seems very likely that who ever these collectors are they probably aren't too friendly.

Taking this into account should we continue our voyage and not risk a confrontation or should we stay and try to find away to contact the homeworld of the derelict ship?
STH_expedition.503.name: The faith of the crew
STH_expedition.505.desc: With hailing frequencies opened it is discovered that the crew of the ship haling us is infact of the same species as the ones discovered on the derelict ship. The aliens seem puzzled in finding our ship next to their damaged vessel that send a distress call back to their homeworld and seem to be powering weapons. This might take some explaining to do.
STH_expedition.505.name: An alien ship is hailing us
STH_expedition.506.desc: After a lengthy exchange of communications between the ships we managed to explain to the aliens that we were not responsible for attacking their ship as our technology doesn't match the weapon damage on the derelict ship's hull or the collecting equipment found on board of it.\n\nThankful of our attempts to both try and help the derelict vessel and also for contacting them when the faith of the crew was discovered the aliens offer us a small reward and take their damaged vessel in tow back to their homeworld.
STH_expedition.506.name: The aliens power down weapons
STH_expedition.507.a: Try to convince the new ship.
STH_expedition.507.b: Ignore the ship. Focus on the battle.
STH_expedition.507.desc:
We have detected a ship dropping out of warp very close to our current position. The ship appears to be equipped with similar weapon systems that were used against the derelict vessel.

However before we have time to react the ship opens fire and lands a couple of direct hits on our engines disabling our warp drive. At the same time a scanning beam coming from the alien vessel is detected on board the ship possibly trying to find out if our crew would also be suitable for the collecting operation.

With our weapons now online we manage to land a few hits on the alien ship, but the damage seems to be minimal. This will clearly be a very tough fight.

During the first exchange of fire between the ships a third ship appears at the scene. This new ship appears to be similar to the original derelict alien ship. Perhaps we could show them that we send the distress call and convince them to help us in the fight?
STH_expedition.507.name: An alien ship is closing fast
STH_expedition.508.desc:
It seems that our attempts to convince the new ship were successful as after asking it to scan both our and the attacking alien ship and to compare the results to the technology used against the derelict ship it too opens fire against our attackers.

By coordinating the fire of the two ships against a specific area of the attacking ship our ships are successful in penetrating the enemy's defences and cause significant damage to it. After taking a couple of such hits the hostile alien ship gives up the fight and withdraws quickly.

Thankful of our attempts to both try and help the derelict vessel and also for contacting them when the faith of the crew was discovered the aliens offer us a small reward and take their damaged vessel in tow back to their homeworld.
STH_expedition.508.name: The new ship opens fire on our attackers
STH_expedition.509.desc: It seems that our attempts of convincing the new ship to help us were unsuccessful as unsure of who is who in the ongoing battle the ship withdraws all together. Worse still while trying to convince the new aliens of our innocence the hostile alien ship attacking us has scored several critical hits on our ship and after violent chain of explosions our ship is destroyed.
STH_expedition.509.name: The new ship withdraws from battle
STH_expedition.51.desc: Gracias a nuestro equipo de ingenieros expertos, los componentes volátiles fueron descartados con éxito y el carguero se salvó. \n\nAunque el Capitán del carguero estaba enojado por la pérdida de su preciosa carga, están dispuestos a proporcionar una pequeña recompensa por nuestra ayuda.
STH_expedition.51.name: Misión de tres años 2: Carguero salvado
STH_expedition.510.desc:
We have detected a ship dropping out of warp very close to our current position. The ship appears to be equipped with similar weapon systems that were used against the derelict vessel.

However before we have time to react the ship opens fire and lands a couple of direct hits on our engines disabling our warp drive. At the same time a scanning beam coming from the alien vessel is detected on board possibly trying to find out if our crew would also be suitable for the collecting operation.

With our weapons now online we manage to land a few hits on the alien ship, but the damage seems to be minimal. This will clearly be a very tough fight.
STH_expedition.510.name: An alien ship is closing fast
STH_expedition.511.desc: Several rounds of shots are exchanged with the hostile alien ship. After which our ship has already sustained heavy damage while the damage to the alien attacker is only light. With the hostile ship continuing its fire several heavy explosions rock our ship before it is finally completely destroyed.
STH_expedition.511.name: Our ship is taking heavy damage
STH_expedition.512.a: This is it. All available power to weapons!
STH_expedition.512.desc:
After a couple of shots with minimal damage done to the alien attackers the captain of our ship orders power to be transferred from weapons to scanners and to do a detailed scan of the enemy ship's power usage when it fires it weapons the next time.

While a fresh wave of enemy weapon fire rocks the ship and knocks even more systems off-line the scan reveals a potential weakness on the enemy ship. A faint reading of an energy conduct junction where energy is channelled from the ship's powercore to various weapon systems is detected on the ship's hull.

However with our ship already in such a weak condition it is likely we will only get one shot at this. The captain orders the ship to move to an ideal firing angle.
STH_expedition.512.name: Minimal damage done to the alien ship
STH_expedition.512.o: This is it. All available power to weapons!
STH_expedition.513.desc: When the ship has achieved optimal firing angle the captain gives the order to concentrate the fire of all available weapons on the detected energy conduit junction and to give it everything the ship has got, knowing that this will most likely be the last chance they have left.\n\nWith multiple weapons hitting the junction at once a violent explosion strikes the alien ship with power levels seemingly fluctuating on board of it. The alien vessel turns slowly around, with seemingly it's weapons disabled, and engages its warp drive leaving the system.
STH_expedition.513.name: Big explosion on the hostile alien vessel
STH_expedition.514.a: Take the scan and set course for the next destination.
STH_expedition.52.desc: Si bien no se puede responder a todas las llamadas de socorro, cada falta de respuesta a incidentes se refleja mal en la reputación de [Root.GetName] en esta parte del espacio
STH_expedition.52.name: Misión de tres años 2: carguero destruido
STH_expedition.53.desc: Nos ha contactado un comerciante local, que tiene una serie de minerales que estarían dispuestos a comerciar con nosotros.
STH_expedition.53.name: Misión de tres años 2: comerciante encontrado
STH_expedition.600.desc: Our flagship has returned home to [Root.GetHomeWorldName] and the experiences gained by [sth_expedition_Leader_flag.GetName] and their crew will be invaluable. Once the crew has rested and recovered they are free to start their next adventure.
STH_expedition.600.name: Flagship 3-Year Mission Year 3: Home
STH_expedition.63.desc: [sth_expedition_Leader_3.GetName] ha identificado un planeta con propiedades inusuales en escaneos de largo alcance que merece una mirada más cercana. \n\nNuestros científicos recomiendan un estudio en profundidad del planeta para ver qué misterios se pueden descubrir.
STH_expedition.63.name: Misión de tres años 3: encuesta del planeta
STH_expedition.65.desc:
[sth_expedition_Leader_3.GetName] ha encontrado un raro mundo de clase 'Gaia' lleno de vida, que nuestros científicos sospechan que ha sido creado artificialmente. 

Una gran instalación fuertemente blindada y abandonada parece estar en el centro del misterio. Podríamos pasar el resto de la expedición estudiando el planeta, sin embargo, debemos continuar la misión. 

Hemos marcado el planeta para un estudio de seguimiento por parte de un equipo dedicado.
STH_expedition.65.name: Misión de tres años 3: nuevos mundos extraños
STH_expedition.66.desc: Nuestros equipos han encontrado un excedente de minerales producidos como un subproducto de la misteriosa instalación. \n\n Creemos que podemos recolectar algunos de estos minerales y recursos sin dañar el ecosistema.
STH_expedition.66.name: Misión de tres años 3: nuevos mundos extraños
STH_expedition.67.desc: Nuestros escaneos han recogido los restos de una base estelar abandonada que parece estar equipada con instalaciones de reparación adaptables a una amplia variedad de naves. No hay signos de vida a bordo de la estación.
STH_expedition.67.name: Misión de tres años 3: misteriosa base estelar
STH_expedition.68.desc: Nuestros equipos de encuesta encontraron una cantidad de equipos de aspecto extraño, pero finalmente no pudieron reiniciar la instalación. El Capitán pudo recoger alguna experiencia valiosa, pero lamentablemente no mucho más.
STH_expedition.68.name: Misión de tres años 3: misteriosa base estelar
STH_expedition.69.desc: Nuestros equipos de encuesta han descubierto los restos de las vastas series de armas equipadas a bordo de la estación. Si bien consideramos antiguas según nuestras normas, estas armas tienen un componente biológico único que aún podría ser mortal. \n\nTenemos dos opciones, reiniciar la instalación y mantener estas armas mortales y poco éticas o destruir la instalación para garantizar que nunca se puedan usar para dañar de nuevo.
STH_expedition.69.name: Misión de tres años 3: Base estelar antigua
STH_expedition.70.desc: Nuestros equipos han investigado la base en ruinas pero no han podido descubrir nada ni valor. \n\n Parece que nunca entenderemos lo que sucedió aquí, el Capitán marca la ubicación en su registro y ordena a la nave que continúe en la misión.
STH_expedition.70.name: Misión de tres años 3: no se encontraron datos
STH_expedition.71.desc: Nuestras sondas han encontrado una cultura previa a la urdimbre, y nuestros antropólogos a bordo quisieran mirar más de cerca y tomar todas las precauciones para no interferir con el desarrollo de estas personas. Tal vez podamos ver un vistazo de cómo nuestra propia civilización se convirtió en la cultura próspera que tenemos hoy.
STH_expedition.71.name: Misión 3 años 3: cultura primitiva encontrada
STH_expedition.72.desc: Las personas en el planeta a continuación son fascinantes, claramente están experimentando un renacimiento, explorando nuevas formas de arte y cultura. \n\nAunque es extremadamente interesante, no hay nada más que podamos aprender y debemos continuar la misión.
STH_expedition.72.name: Misión 3 años 3: cultura primitiva encontrada
STH_expedition.73.desc: [sth_expedition_Leader_3.GetName] ha descubierto una señal preocupante, parece que un súper volcán inactivo está a punto de entrar en erupción. \n\nEliminaría a toda la civilización. La Directiva principal establece estrictamente que no podemos interferir con el desarrollo natural de esta civilización, sin embargo, no hacerlo casi seguramente provocaría la muerte de todos en la superficie y el fin de esta gente emergente.
STH_expedition.73.name: Misión de tres años 3: Cultura primitiva: ¡desastre!
STH_expedition.74.desc: [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName] el informe de la misión indica que [sth_expedition_Leader_3.GetName] tuvo éxito al salvar las vidas de todos aquellos en el planeta. \n\n ¿Quién sabe qué impacto tendrá esto en el futuro? Nuestra influencia ha tenido un impacto directo en el desarrollo natural de esta especie. Esperemos que sea para mejor
STH_expedition.74.name: Misión 3 años 3: cultura primitiva salvada
STH_expedition.75.desc: A pesar de los mejores esfuerzos de todos los involucrados, el equipo no pudo evacuar la colonia cuando el volcán entró en erupción antes de lo previsto y arrasó con el asentamiento. \n\n Desafortunadamente, ninguno de los nativos sobrevivió a la erupción y el consiguiente daño ecológico. Es hora de seguir adelante.
STH_expedition.75.name: Misión de tres años 3: incapaz de salvar la cultura primitiva
STH_expedition.76.desc: [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName], lamento informar que nuestra expedición ha llegado a un final temprano. \n\nEl primer oficial ha informado que el Capitán y los miembros superiores de la tripulación fueron asesinados en acción intentando salvar la vida de una cultura primitiva en un planeta en los confines del espacio conocido. Parece que el rescate salió mal y, como resultado, hemos ordenado que la tripulación vuelva a casa. El barco será desequilibrado mientras se lleva a cabo una investigación interna completa.
STH_expedition.76.name: Misión 3 años 3: tripulación perdida
STH_expedition.77.desc: Los escaneos han recogido una pequeña nave civil bajo ataque de varios combatientes de ataque. Los escudos de sus barcos están fallando y no podrán durar mucho más tiempo. \n\n Parece que los agresores tienen poco interés en nuestro barco, pero eso cambiaría si los atacáramos. ¿Cuáles son nuestras órdenes señor?
STH_expedition.77.name: Misión 3 años 3: Nave bajo ataque
STH_expedition.78.desc: Los agresores no podían competir con la habilidad y el poder de nuestro barco y logramos hacer una declaración contra aquellos que desafiarían [Root.GetName]. \n\n Como un bono adicional, pudimos obtener una cantidad decente de salvamento de Los restos de la nave destruida. Deberíamos recogerlo antes de continuar con la misión
STH_expedition.78.name: Misión 3 años 3: Victoria
STH_expedition.79.desc: [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName], lamento informar que nuestra expedición ha llegado a un final temprano. Recogimos su llamada de socorro hoy temprano a través de nuestras comunicaciones de largo alcance. \n\n Parece que intentaron defenderse contra un atacante desconocido pero finalmente no tuvieron éxito. Lamentablemente, el barco parece haber sido destruido con todas las manos.
STH_expedition.79.name: Misión de tres años 2: Derrota
STH_expedition.8.desc: [sth_expedition_Leader_1.GetName] ha identificado un planeta raro en escaneos de largo alcance que podrían tener una mirada más cercana. Nuestros científicos recomiendan un estudio en profundidad del planeta para ver qué misterios se pueden descubrir
STH_expedition.8.name: Misión de tres años 1: encuesta del planeta
STH_expedition.80.desc_01: [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName], tenemos una excelente oportunidad para proporcionar a nuestros científicos de nuevo en [Root.Owner.Capital.GetName] algunas muestras de investigación de una nebulosa cercana. Desvíe el curso para tomar estas muestras de la nebulosa.
STH_expedition.80.desc_02: [sth_expedition_Leader_3.GetName] uno de nuestros principales generales nos ha contactado para pedirnos que completemos una tarea para ellos. Se ha detectado una sonda militar perdida hace mucho tiempo cerca y se nos ha encomendado la tarea de recuperarla.
STH_expedition.80.name: Misión de tres años 3: Misión de nuestros científicos principales
STH_expedition.81.desc:
Al llegar a la fuente de la llamada de socorro cerca del equipo se encontró con un transporte de pasajeros en problemas. 

 Respondiendo al granizo, el [Root.Owner.GetCaptainTitle] del carguero aparece desaliñado y herido. 

'Gracias por responder nuestra llamada de socorro, [Root.Owner.GetCaptainTitle]. Llevamos pasajeros en un recorrido por el sistema, nuestra unidad de deformación se dañó en una tormenta de iones. 

Un equipo de ingeniería enviado a la embarcación puede reparar rápidamente los motores de la nave.
STH_expedition.81.name: Misión de tres años 3: Llamada de socorro
STH_expedition.82.desc: Si bien no se puede responder a todas las llamadas de socorro, cada falta de respuesta a incidentes se refleja mal en la reputación de [Root.GetName] en esta parte del espacio
STH_expedition.82.name: Misión de tres años 3: Llamada de socorro
STH_expedition.83.desc: Gracias a nuestro equipo de ingenieros expertos, los componentes volátiles fueron descartados con éxito y el carguero se salvó. \n\nAunque el Capitán del carguero estaba enojado por la pérdida de su preciosa carga, están dispuestos a proporcionar una pequeña recompensa por nuestra ayuda.
STH_expedition.83.name: Misión 3 años 3: Carguero salvado
STH_expedition.84.desc: Nos ha contactado un comerciante local, que tiene una serie de minerales que estarían dispuestos a comerciar con nosotros.
STH_expedition.84.name: Misión 3 años 3: se encuentra el comerciante
STH_expedition.9.desc_01:
En una inspección más cercana, el planeta tiene muchos descubrimientos nuevos e interesantes, incluidas nuevas especies que no se han visto en ningún planeta dentro de nuestras fronteras. 

Nuestros científicos podrían pasar los próximos años pasando por los nuevos descubrimientos, pero los escaneos tendrán que hacer lo mismo que nosotros. debe continuar con la misión. 

 [sth_expedition_Leader_1.GetName] ha adquirido mucha experiencia del breve tiempo que pasó aquí.
STH_expedition.9.desc_02: El planeta está repleto de nuevas especies animales exóticas, pero nada de valor estratégico. Podría clasificarse como un sueño antropólogo. \n\nNuestros científicos han pedido pasar los próximos días catalogando las especies que han visto desde la órbita. El conocimiento adquirido aquí contribuirá en gran medida a nuestra comprensión de la evolución de nuestro propio planeta.
STH_expedition.9.desc_03: Nuestros científicos creen que este es un mundo joven en las primeras etapas de desarrollo que demuestra una belleza y volatilidad excepcionales, pero poco en términos de minerales útiles u otros recursos recuperables. Sin embargo, ha proporcionado una experiencia útil para la nave y la tripulación.
STH_expedition.9.name: Misión de tres años 1: encuesta del planeta
STH_expedition.9999.b: We will return to the Lembatta later.
STH_expedition.9999.desc_lembatta_enable: The Lembatta Cluster is a stellar cluster located near the border between the United Federation of Planets and the Klingon Empire, consisted mainly of large, old red giants, giving the Cluster its distinctive red colour. \n\nThe cluster emits high levels of radiation, which would normally make travel across the cluster impossible, however our science and expeditionary teams believe that we have sufficent technology levels to safely explore the cluster.
STH_expedition.9999.desc_lembatta_pendin: The Lembatta Cluster is a stellar cluster located near the border between the United Federation of Planets and the Klingon Empire, consisted mainly of large, old red giants, giving the Cluster its distinctive red colour. \n\nThe cluster emits high levels of radiation, which our science teams inform us is currently too dangerous for our ships to travel through, recommending at least level 4 warp technology.
STH_expedition.9999.name: Star Cluster Expeditions
STH_expedition_1.1002.desc: [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName], I am receiving reports that following a battle we have picked up reports of [Root.GetName] escape pods and debris coming from the last known location of our expeditionary fleet. We have dispatched a team to retrieve the survivors and bring them home.
STH_expedition_1.1002.name: Ship Destroyed, escape pods found!
STH_expedition_4.500.desc: Captains Log: Captain [sth_expedition_hero_Leader_1.GetName] updating whilst on an expedition, my communications officer has reported we have received the location of a previously undiscovered empire. \n\nShould we proceed with First Contact?
STH_expedition_4.500.name: New Empire Discovered!
STH_expedition_4.510.desc: Captains Log: Captain [sth_expedition_hero_Leader_1.GetName] reporting in from our expedition. Whilst exploring a previously unclaimed region of space we have been approached by a vessel from [expedition_4_tresspass_country.GetName], they have not stated their intentions, but their demeanor is clearly not friendly.
STH_expedition_4.510.name: Ship Approaching
STH_expedition_4.511.desc_01: Greeting Captain [sth_expedition_hero_Leader_1.GetName], we notice that your ship appears to have wandered into territory that has recently been claimed by [expedition_4_tresspass_country.GetName], no doubt you were unaware of this and already planning on leaving?
STH_expedition_4.511.desc_02: Attention Captain [sth_expedition_hero_Leader_1.GetName], your vessel has trespassed in recently claimed [expedition_4_tresspass_country.GetName] territory, you will vacate this region immediately and hand over any research that you illegally obtained, before we turn our ships weapons on you.
STH_expedition_4.511.desc_03: You are in the space of [expedition_4_tresspass_country.GetName] we do not care why you are here, only that you leave now!
STH_expedition_4.511.desc_04: Hello Captain [sth_expedition_hero_Leader_1.GetName], you may not be aware that we have laid claim to this region and the research opportunities within, we do not desire conflict, but we are not looking to share this region of space. For now, we are content to discuss the situation peacefully.
STH_expedition_4.511.name: Inbound Communications
STH_expedition_4.512.desc_01: You speak well [sth_expedition_hero_Leader_1.GetName], if this is how all members of your species do business then perhaps there is hope for a better relationship between [Root.GetName] and my people.
STH_expedition_4.512.desc_02: Hmmm you have convinced us for now Captain, but only just we shall let this incident pass and allow you to continue on your way with no further ill will between our two sides.. But do not test our patience again.
STH_expedition_4.512.desc_03: You are perhaps lucky that your vessel is so well-armed and that we are not equipped to do battle today. We concede and you may continue on your way with the begrudging respect of those you have faced today.
STH_expedition_4.512.name: Inbound Communications
STH_expedition_4.513.desc_01: If you think that pathetic attempt at convincing us stood any chance of succeeding then you are a bigger fool than we 1st thought [sth_expedition_hero_Leader_1.GetSpeciesName], you will leave our territory now or you will leave it chased by torpedoes!
STH_expedition_4.513.desc_02: Despite your best attempts and obvious charm, our leadership remains unconvinced that we should allow you to remain in our space, so with regret we must asked you to leave or risk a much larger diplomatic incident.
STH_expedition_4.513.desc_03: Your pathetic attempts to force us to back down have only strengthened our resolve to see the back of your Starship. Leave our space now or we open fire!
STH_expedition_4.513.name: Inbound Communications
STH_expedition_4.514.desc_01: Having discussed the conflict with [sth_expedition_hero_Leader_1.GetSpeciesName] we see that nothing can be gained from continued conflict here and whilst we remain unhappy that this situation has occurred, we shall overlook it for now. Take your readings and move on before we change our mind.
STH_expedition_4.514.desc_02: We have evaluated the situation tactically and can see that both our ships are evenly matched a battle here today would not be in anyone's best interests, I suggest we both take what we need from this system and depart without the use of weaponary.
STH_expedition_4.514.desc_03: Your pathetic attempts to force us to back down have only strengthened our resolve to see the back of your Starship. Leave our space now or we open fire!
STH_expedition_4.514.name: Inbound Communications
STH_expedition_4.520.desc: Captains Log: Captain [sth_expedition_hero_Leader_1.GetName] reporting in from our expedition. whilst traversing deep space we have been contacted by [STH_expedition_4_rival_1.GetName] and [STH_expedition_4_rival_2.GetName]. Two rival empires who are spiralling closer and closer to the brink of war. We have been asked to facilitate a conference between the two sides in a bid to stablise the region.
STH_expedition_4.520.name: Diplomatic Conference
STH_expedition_4.521.desc: We have been approached by [sth_expedition_hero_Leader_1.GetName] of the [From.GetName] offering to host a conference between us and our rivals [STH_expedition_4_rival_2.GetName].
STH_expedition_4.521.name: Diplomatic Conference
STH_expedition_4.522.desc: We have been approached by [sth_expedition_hero_Leader_1.GetName] of the [From.GetName] offering to host a conference between us and our rivals [STH_expedition_4_rival_1.GetName].
STH_expedition_4.522.name: Diplomatic Conference.
STH_expedition_4.523.desc: Despite the best efforts of [sth_expedition_hero_Leader_1.GetName] and their crew, it appears [STH_expedition_4_rival_1.GetName] are unwilling to engage in talks with their rivals, it seems that they are not interesting in talking with their foes.
STH_expedition_4.523.name: Diplomatic Conference Rejected
STH_expedition_4.524.desc: Despite the best efforts of [sth_expedition_hero_Leader_1.GetName] and their crew, it appears [STH_expedition_4_rival_2.GetName] are unwilling to engage in talks with their rivals, it seems that they are not interesting in talking with their foes.
STH_expedition_4.524.name: Diplomatic Conference Rejected
STH_expedition_4.525.desc: It appears [STH_expedition_4_rival_2.GetName] are unwilling to engage in talks with us, perhaps they are too afraid to engage with us.
STH_expedition_4.525.name: Diplomatic Conference Rejected
STH_expedition_4.526.desc: It appears that both [STH_expedition_4_rival_1.GetName] and [STH_expedition_4_rival_2.GetName] have been persuaded by [sth_expedition_hero_Leader_1.GetName] to come together for peace talks hosted onboard [sth_expedition_hero_Leader_1.GetName]'s ship.
STH_expedition_4.526.name: Diplomatic Conference Accepted
STH_expedition_4.527.desc: The diplomatic conference has been agreed and both sides will be in attendance.
STH_expedition_4.527.name: Diplomatic Conference Accepted
STH_expedition_4.528.desc: Following an intense series of discussions with [STH_expedition_4_rival_1.GetName] and [STH_expedition_4_rival_2.GetName] a major breakthrough has been made providing a significant improvement in the relationship between both countries. \n\nEnvoys from both side credit the work done by [sth_expedition_hero_Leader_1.GetName] as having a huge influence on the success of the talks.
STH_expedition_4.528.name: Diplomatic Success
STH_expedition_4.529.desc: As all parties leave the conference room, it is clear that no agreement has been reached, with the situation possibly even having worsened. The likelihood of peace being brokered anytime soon seems further away following the meeting.
STH_expedition_4.529.name: Diplomatic Failure
STH_expedition_4.530.desc_01: Captains Log: Captain [sth_expedition_hero_Leader_1.GetName] reporting in from our expedition. We have been approached by an individual fleeing the [STH_expedition_4_530_a_target.GetName] they claim to be a high-ranking civilian industrialist who has important intel on his former employers and is willing to share in exchange for asylum. \n\nWhilst [STH_expedition_4_530_a_target.GetName] will be unlikely to be able to prove we are sheltering their former citizen, it is likely they will still suspect our involvement.
STH_expedition_4.530.desc_02: Captains Log: Captain [sth_expedition_hero_Leader_1.GetName] reporting in from our expedition. We have been hailed by a trader who claims to have intel on [STH_expedition_4_530_b_target.GetName], our security experts have taken a look at the findings and believe that whilst most of the information on sale is worthless, there are elements of useful data within the offering. \n\nThis information is on sale for a no doubt inflated Latinum price.
STH_expedition_4.530.desc_03: Captains Log: Captain [sth_expedition_hero_Leader_1.GetName] reporting in from our expedition. [Root.GetName] Intel has contacted us, to request we extract one of their operatives, who has been operating undercover within [STH_expedition_4_530_c_target.GetName] space. \n\nThe data obtained will no doubt be of the highest quality, but retrieving will come at a cost.
STH_expedition_4.530.name: Intelligence Report Received
STH_expedition_situations.1050.desc_01: Sufficient information has been gained on the Lembatta Cluster, that the Science Council has agreed to authorise a new Five Year Mission into the Cluster.
STH_expedition_situations.1050.name: Five Year Mission Available
STH_expedition_situations.50.desc_01: The [This.GetName] stands ready to join the ongoing expedition into the Lembatta Cluster to begin the survey mission across the cluster to chart the space and investigate the mysteries of this area of the galaxy.
STH_expedition_situations.50.desc_02: The [This.GetName] stands at the gateway system to the Lembatta Cluster, ready to begin the expedition into the heart of the Cluster on behalf of the [This.Owner.GetName]. Over the course of the expedition new missions will be available and exciting content will unlock.
STH_expedition_situations.50.name: Expedition to the Lembatta Cluster
STH_expedition_situations.55.desc_01: With the expedition to the Lembatta Cluster drawing to a close, the ships assigned to the survey can begin to pack up their sensor equipment and round up their scientists and set a course for home. \n\nWith the Cluster fully charted we can now lay claim to the area.
STH_expedition_situations.55.name: Expedition to Cluster Complete
STH_federation_council.1.a: We wage this war to defend the ideals of the Federation.
STH_federation_council.1.b: We are seeking a swift end to the conflict.
STH_federation_council.1.d: Give a passionate speach in favour of unity.
STH_federation_council.1.desc_01: [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName], this is Federational council member [council_member.ruler.GetName] representing the interests of [council_member.GetName]. We have grave concerns that need to be addressed by the Federation Council and we demand an audience and we request an emergency session with yourself to discuss our issues.
STH_federation_council.1.desc_02: Pleasant greeting [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName], I am Federational council member [council_member.ruler.GetName] representing the interests of [council_member.GetName]. It is always good to join a council session and discuss Federation policies.
STH_federation_council.1.desc_03: Hello [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName], I am Federational council member [council_member.ruler.GetName] representing the interests of [council_member.GetName] the recent declaration of war against [ufp_war_country.GetName] has us extremely concerned. The Federation is supposed to idealise peace and harmony, which is why we have joined this grand union. This war has us worried about the future of the United Federation of Planets and what it impact it will have on our core principles.
STH_federation_council.1.e: Offer to send a team to their homeworld.
STH_federation_council.1.name: United Federation of Planets Council Session
STH_federation_council.1.z: Bring Council Session to an end.
STH_federation_council.10.desc: The emergency council session on [ufp_member_war_concerns.capital_scope.GetName] was a success thanks in part to the efforts of [ufp_envoy.GetName] and [ufp_ship_leader.GetName]. The experience of the team was able to convince the politicians that whilst war is never the desired result sometimes a stance must be taken. The Federation council is once again united.
STH_federation_council.10.name: Federation War Council Emergency Session
STH_federation_council.100.a: What is the role of the council.
STH_federation_council.100.c: How often can the council be called.
STH_federation_council.100.desc: With the formation of the United Federation of Planets, the founding members establish the Federation Council, a democratic voice with a seat for all members of the Federation. The council can be called for an extraordinary session to enact matters of importance to the Federation and Starfleet.
STH_federation_council.100.name: Federation Council Formed
STH_federation_council.102.b: Why am I unable to make a decision today.
STH_federation_council.102.desc_01: Greetings President [root.GetRulerName], the United Federation of Planets extra-ordinary session of the council is now in session. There are a number of topics on the table for discussion today.
STH_federation_council.102.desc_02: Greetings President [root.GetRulerName], the Federation Council is still reconvening following the previous session, please allow us more time before calling the next unplanned session.
STH_federation_council.102.f.tooltip: Warning: This is a resource intensive activity and can only be done every 90 days.
STH_federation_council.102.f: Share all Tech with Member Worlds.
STH_federation_council.102.name: Federation Council Session
STH_federation_council.11.desc: The emergency council session on [ufp_member_war_concerns.capital_scope.GetName] was a failure despite the efforts of [ufp_envoy.GetName] and [ufp_ship_leader.GetName]. The government of [ufp_member_war_concerns.GetName] remain steadfast in their view that all war is wrong and they remain vocal in this view at every opportunity.
STH_federation_council.11.name: Federation War Council Emergency Session
STH_federation_council.110.desc: Greetings President, I am [ufp_war_member.GetName] leader of the pro-war faction within the Federation Council, we have won the vote on providing extra support to aid us in the war. \n\nWe can provide a small bonus which should help Starfleet prevail.
STH_federation_council.110.name: Federation Council Session
STH_federation_council.111.desc: Greetings President, I am [ufp_war_peace.GetName] leader of the pro-peace faction within the Federation Council. We have won the vote and we cannot provide an additional offensive bonus in this war, which is costing the lives of many of our brave officers. \n\nWe have agreed on a small defensive bonus to help ensure our forces can protect our brave citizens.
STH_federation_council.111.name: Federation Council Session
STH_federation_council.12.desc: Failure to attend the conference set up to discuss the war effort has led to [ufp_member_war_concerns.GetName] feeling undervalued and unappreciated as part of the Federation council, they have made it clear that should they continue to be made to feel this way they will strongly consider their place within the United Federation of Planets.
STH_federation_council.12.name: Federation War Council Emergency Session
STH_federation_council.15.desc: Greetings [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName]! \n\nWe want to commend the success of Starfleet in their recent victory and let them know that all of the Federation is behind them and the people of [ufp_war_good.GetName] support the brave efforts of our troops! Let's win this war for all of the United Federation of Planets.
STH_federation_council.15.name: Federation War Council Emergency Session
STH_federation_council.20.b: We shall arrange a conference between both sides.
STH_federation_council.20.desc: Greetings [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName], we wish to discuss the ongoing plans to bring [fed_pacifist_state.GetName] into the United Federation of Planets, we have researched the ethics of this state and have some concerns about their pacifistic views and whether this will weaken the position of Starfleet and allow other nations to take advantage of the United Federation of Planets. \n\nI look forward to your response [Root.GetRulerTitle].
STH_federation_council.20.name: Federation Council Session
STH_federation_council.200.desc: With the Federation Council in place, the council sets about the business of bringing the founding worlds together as one organisation. \n\nThe construction of a new Starbase, simply called Starbase 1 on the site of one of the oldest starbases in our home system seems both logical and symbolic.
STH_federation_council.200.name: The First Federation Starbase
STH_federation_council.21.desc: Federation councils have arranged a conference between [fed_pacifist_state.GetName] and the concerned dignitaries from Member World [fed_concerned_state.GetName] to be held on the homeworld of [fed_pacifist_state.capital_scope.GetName]. \n\nThe Federation ambassador for [fed_concerned_state.GetName] needs to be escorted from their homeworld to meet with [fed_pacifist_state_leader.GetName].
STH_federation_council.21.name: Federation Diplomatic Escort
STH_federation_council.22.desc: Federation councils have arranged a conference between [fed_military_state.GetName] and the concerned dignitaries from Member World [fed_concerned_state.GetName] to be held on the homeworld of [fed_military_state.capital_scope.GetName]. \n\nThe Federation ambassador for [fed_concerned_state.GetName] needs to be escorted from their homeworld to meet with [fed_military_state_leader.GetName].
STH_federation_council.22.name: Federation Diplomatic Escort
STH_federation_council.23.a: Prepare to depart for the next destination.
STH_federation_council.23.b: Set a course for the next destination.
STH_federation_council.23.desc: The crew of the [diplomatic_ship.GetName] have successfully picked up the diplomats from [fed_concerned_state.capital_scope.GetName] and are now prepared to escort them to the awaiting conference that has been arranged on [fed_new_state.capital_scope.GetName]. \n\nAll of the Federation are eagerly awaiting the results of the conference and the resumption of a united federation council.
STH_federation_council.23.name: Federation Diplomatic Escort
STH_federation_council.24.desc: Federation diplomats met with the potential new Federation member to discuss the concerns and it looks like the spirit of the Federations original charter was able to help both sides understand the different philosophies and how they strengthen the ideals the Federation was built on by it's founders.
STH_federation_council.24.name: Federation Diplomatic Escort
STH_federation_council.25.desc: Failure to attend the planned conference has resulted in insult to both sides and a lot of anger being vented back in the Federation Council chambers. \n\nHopefully our diplomatic service can undo some of this damage.
STH_federation_council.25.name: Federation Diplomatic Escort
STH_federation_council.26.b: We shall arrange a conference between both sides.
STH_federation_council.26.desc: Greetings [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName], we would like to take some time to discuss the ongoing plans to bring [fed_military_state.GetName] into the United Federation of Planets, we have understand that this new state are not afraid of taking the military route and we have some concerns about whether this will create a dangerous tone within the United Federation of Planets. \n\nYour urgent response would be appreciated [Root.GetRulerTitle].
STH_federation_council.26.name: Federation Council Session
STH_federation_council.27.a: The Federation values welcome and embrace all.
STH_federation_council.27.b: We shall arrange a conference between both sides.
STH_federation_council.27.desc: Greetings [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName], we would like to take some time to discuss the ongoing plans to bring [fed_auth_state.GetName] into the United Federation of Planets, the rights of all members of this great union is at the core of our egalitarian beliefs and we find it hard to understand what [fed_auth_state.GetName] will bring to the table or how they will fit into the federation family. \n\nPlease help us understand.
STH_federation_council.27.name: Federation Council Session
STH_federation_council.28.desc: Federation councils have arranged a conference between [fed_auth_state.GetName] and the concerned dignitaries from Member World [fed_concerned_state.GetName] to be held on the homeworld of [fed_auth_state.capital_scope.GetName]. \n\nThe Federation ambassador for [fed_concerned_state.GetName] needs to be escorted from their homeworld to meet with [fed_auth_state_leader.GetName].
STH_federation_council.28.name: Federation Diplomatic Escort
STH_federation_council.3.a: We will arrange a council session.
STH_federation_council.3.b: We are at war, now is not the time!
STH_federation_council.3.desc: Following the outbreak of the war against [ufp_war_country.GetName] the Federation council representative on behalf of the member world [ufp_member_war_concerns.GetName] has requested an urgent session of the Federation council to be held to discuss their concerns about the war. They have requested that we contact them immediately via the Contact screen to discuss their concerns.
STH_federation_council.3.name: Federation War Council Emergency Session
STH_federation_council.301.desc: Dear [Root.GetName], we come to you in need of aid, we have critical resource shortages and we request your aid to deliver these much-needed supplies to our capital. \n\nWe hope you live up to your end of our subject agreement.
STH_federation_council.301.name: Request for Aid
STH_federation_council.302.desc_01: We have successfully delivered the energy cells to [subject_in_need.capital_scope.GetName] and the teams have begun working on providing assistance to their infrastructure to help them help themselves.
STH_federation_council.302.desc_02: We have successfully delivered the package of minerals to [subject_in_need.capital_scope.GetName] and the teams have begun working on improving the mining facilities on the planet to aid [subject_in_need.GetName] in the future. \n\nAdditional our engineers have installed a new Matter Fabricator at their capital.
STH_federation_council.302.desc_03: We have successfully provided the delivery Food to [subject_in_need.capital_scope.GetName] and the teams have worked on the fields providing tools and crops to aid them in future harvests. \n\nThe new farm will provide food for future generations.
STH_federation_council.302.desc_04: We have successfully delivered the agreed Alloys to [subject_in_need.capital_scope.GetName] and the teams have begun working on providing assistance to their infrastructure to help them help themselves.
STH_federation_council.302.desc_05: We have successfully delivered the agreed Dilithium Crystals to [subject_in_need.capital_scope.GetName] and the teams have begun working on a new synthetic Dilithium manufacturing facility to aid in their navy production capacity.
STH_federation_council.302.name: Resource Convoy Arrives
STH_federation_council.303.desc: You have failed to provide us with the agreed aid as we have discussed, and this has seriously impacted our faith in you!
STH_federation_council.303.name: Failed to provide agreed aid
STH_federation_council.304.desc: A band of pirates have intercepted the convoy before it can reach its destination. Defend the shipment and ensure that it reaches its destination.
STH_federation_council.304.name: Pirates Attack
STH_federation_council.4.a.tooltip: Our Starfleet crews are buoyed by the support from the Federation member worlds.
STH_federation_council.4.a: Starfleet will act swiftly to bring us victory.
STH_federation_council.4.desc: Greetings [Root.GetRulerName] we have considered your impassioned speech and whilst our stance on war remains unchanged, we understand that this attack against the principles in which the Federation holds most dear cannot remain undefended. We ask that our brave Starfleet officers do their best to end this conflict swiftly and with minimal casualties.
STH_federation_council.4.name: Federation War Council Emergency Session
STH_federation_council.5.b: Send a diplomatic team to discuss the war.
STH_federation_council.5.desc: Hello [Root.GetRulerName] we have considered the message given in the last Federation council meeting and whilst we understand the reasoning on why you feel you must lead this Federation into a war, we must stand firm behind our pacifist views and lodge an official protest against this war.
STH_federation_council.5.name: Federation War Council Emergency Session
STH_federation_council.6.desc: The [ufp_member_war_concerns.GetName] have accepted our offer to send an envoy escorted by one of our Starships. The team will attempt to offset the fears of the [ufp_member_war_concerns.GetName] government and despite the ongoing war this is a vitally important mission to for the Federation council to ensure that it remains unified during this trying time. Our envoy [ufp_envoy.GetName] will accompany the ship and crew to [ufp_member_war_concerns.capital_scope.GetName].
STH_federation_council.6.name: Federation War Council Emergency Session
STH_federation_council.7.a: We cannot let this distract us.
STH_federation_council.7.desc: The [ufp_member_war_concerns.GetName] have rejected our offer further discuss the ongoing war efforts and have formally lodged a complaint at the Federation council, this lack of cohesion within our member worlds will only seek to embolden our enemies efforts to undermine Starfleet's efforts.
STH_federation_council.7.name: Federation War Council Emergency Session
STH_federation_council.8.desc: Federation member world [ufp_member_war_concerns.GetName] has formally lodged an objection against the ongoing war and feel they have been brought into a war against their wishes. Their view of the United Federation of Planets has been harmed as a result.
STH_federation_council.8.name: Federation War Council Emergency Session
STH_federation_council.9.desc: What started off as a small debate over the ongoing war effort has escalated as the government on [ufp_member_war_concerns.capital_scope.GetName] have been lobbying against the war. They have gained a number of key allies in the Federation council chambers as fellow member worlds [ufp_member_war_concerns_1.GetName], [ufp_member_war_concerns_2.GetName] have joined their voices together against the war and protesting against how the situation has been handled by the Federation leadership and Starfleet's admiralty.
STH_federation_council.9.name: Federation War Council Emergency Session
STH_federation_flavour.1.a.tooltip: Reunión con Kai [bajoranKai.GetName] programada dentro de 24 a 36 días.
STH_federation_flavour.1.a: Programe una reunión con los Kai.
STH_federation_flavour.1.b.tooltip: El proceso de adhesión a la Federación de [bajoran_republic.GetName] retrocedió un 15%.
STH_federation_flavour.1.b: El código de vestimenta está ahí por una razón.
STH_federation_flavour.1.desc:
La posición única de la que [bajoran_republic.GetSpeciesName] Faith disfruta entre los [bajoran_republic.GetSpeciesNamePlural] ha planteado en ocasiones un problema para los diplomáticos y los funcionarios públicos que participan en el proceso de acceso de [bajoran_republic.Capital.GetName]. 

Mientras la mayoría de los problemas que han surgido se resuelven rápidamente, un punto aparentemente menor se ha intensificado hasta el punto de que el líder de [bajoran_republic.GetSpeciesName] Faith, Kai [bajoranKai.GetName], ha solicitado una audiencia con [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName] para resolver el problema. 

El problema se refiere al código de vestimenta de la Flota Estelar y su enmienda para permitir que los oficiales de [bajoran_republic.GetSpeciesNamePlural] usen los aretes familiares, que forman uno de los componentes más visibles de su religión.
STH_federation_flavour.1.name: [bajoran_republic.GetName] Proceso de adhesión
STH_federation_flavour.10.desc: [diplomacy_leader.GetName]'s efforts on [From.GetName] have been successful and the previous objections to the ongoing integration of the Ferengi have been dropped. The strength and unity of the Federation continues to grow.
STH_federation_flavour.10.name: Successful Negotiations
STH_federation_flavour.100.a.tooltip: Alta posibilidad de cambiar la ética de [accessionState.GetName] hacia el igualitarismo.
STH_federation_flavour.100.b.tooltip: Posibilidad moderada de cambiar la ética de [accessionState.GetName] hacia el igualitarismo.
STH_federation_flavour.100.c.tooltip: Ningún cambio en la ética de [accessionState.GetName].
STH_federation_flavour.100.desc:
Saludos [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName]. Las conversaciones para llevar a las personas [accessionState.GetSpeciesName] a [Root.GetName] están progresando, pero aún quedan varias preguntas sobre las diferentes ... visiones del mundo que tenemos en ciertas áreas. 

Se nos dice que la diversidad es la fuerza Pero, ¿cómo podemos conciliar la tendencia de mi pueblo hacia un estado decisivo y un orden social jerárquico con su "igualitarismo"? 

Estoy abierto a cualquier sugerencia que pueda tener.
STH_federation_flavour.100.name: [accessionState.GetName] Proceso de adhesión
STH_federation_flavour.101.a.tooltip: Alta posibilidad de cambiar la ética de [accessionState.GetName] hacia el pacifismo.
STH_federation_flavour.101.b.tooltip: Posibilidad moderada de cambiar la ética de [accessionState.GetName] hacia el pacifismo.
STH_federation_flavour.101.c.tooltip: Ningún cambio en la ética de [accessionState.GetName].
STH_federation_flavour.101.desc:
Saludos [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName]. Las conversaciones para llevar a las personas [accessionState.GetSpeciesName] a [Root.GetName] están progresando, pero aún quedan varias preguntas sobre las diferentes ... visiones del mundo que tenemos en ciertas áreas. 

Se nos dice que la diversidad es la fuerza , pero ¿cómo podemos conciliar la tendencia de mi pueblo hacia el uso de la fuerza para lograr la paz con su 'pacifismo'? 

Estoy abierto a cualquier sugerencia que pueda tener.
STH_federation_flavour.101.name: [accessionState.GetName] Proceso de adhesión
STH_federation_flavour.102.a.tooltip: Alta posibilidad de cambiar la ética de [accessionState.GetName] hacia Xenophilia.
STH_federation_flavour.102.b.tooltip: Posibilidad moderada de cambiar la ética de [accessionState.GetName] hacia la Xenofilia.
STH_federation_flavour.102.c.tooltip: Ningún cambio en la ética de [accessionState.GetName].
STH_federation_flavour.102.desc:
Saludos [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName]. Las conversaciones para llevar a las personas [accessionState.GetSpeciesName] a [Root.GetName] están progresando, pero aún quedan varias preguntas sobre las diferentes ... visiones del mundo que tenemos en ciertas áreas. 

Se nos dice que la diversidad es la fuerza , pero ¿cómo podemos conciliar la desconfianza de mi pueblo hacia los extraterrestres y las culturas alienígenas con tu 'xenofilia'? 

Estoy abierto a cualquier sugerencia que puedas tener.
STH_federation_flavour.102.name: [accessionState.GetName] Proceso de adhesión
STH_federation_flavour.105.a.tooltip: Agree on a special project to demonstrate Starfleet's abilities, better leaders & squadron commanders have a higher chance of success.
STH_federation_flavour.105.desc: Greetings, I am [accessionStateLeader.GetName] representing [accessionState.GetName], as you well know we are a proud society with a strong and proud military, many amongst our admiralty have raised concerns about your ability to protect our planet, with Starfleet being seen as more of a scientific organisation and not fit to protect our interests.
STH_federation_flavour.105.name: [From.GetName] Membership Process
STH_federation_flavour.106.desc: After a fine demonstration of fleet power and coordination by [diplomacy_leader.GetName] onboard [This.GetName], the integration of our potential member world can now continue with both sides pleased with the outcome of the fleet activity.
STH_federation_flavour.106.name: Fleet Operations Successful
STH_federation_flavour.107.desc: Despite a good display of our fleet capabilities [diplomacy_leader.GetName] could not convince our integration target of the abilities of Starfleet. This is a significant setback to the membership process.
STH_federation_flavour.107.name: Fleet Operations Failed
STH_federation_flavour.108.desc: Greetings, I am [accessionStateLeader.GetName] representing [accessionState.GetName], as you know we value peace and we have seen our peaceful ways taken advantage of over the years, we are keen for reassurance that Starfleet will be able to protect us without becoming a war mongering society.
STH_federation_flavour.108.name: [From.GetName] Membership Process
STH_federation_flavour.109.desc: After a fine demonstration of the skill and abilities of [diplomacy_leader.GetName] onboard [This.GetName], the integration of our potential member world can now continue with both sides pleased with the outcome of the fleet activity.
STH_federation_flavour.109.name: Fleet Operations Successful
STH_federation_flavour.11.desc: [diplomacy_leader.GetName]'s efforts on [From.GetName] have failed to resolve the member world's concerns and harmed the overall efforts to integrate the Ferengi into the [Root.Owner.GetName].
STH_federation_flavour.11.name: Failed Negotiations
STH_federation_flavour.110.desc: Despite a good display of our fleet capabilities [diplomacy_leader.GetName] could not convince our integration target of the abilities of Starfleet, it's unclear if they saw the display as too aggressive or not going far enough to protect them, but it has set back the integration process.
STH_federation_flavour.110.name: Fleet Operations Failed
STH_federation_flavour.111.desc: Captain's Log - Captain [accessionStateLeader.GetName]: With the ongoing negotiations to bring [From.GetName] into [Root.GetName] in full swing, Starfleet has tasked us with an important diplomatic mission to [From.capital_scope.GetName] to provide some additional impetus to the ongoing discussions and help smooth out some of the sticking points. \n\nWe shall make our way to their homeworld and demonstrate the best that [Root.GetName] has to offer.
STH_federation_flavour.111.name: Diplomatic Mission
STH_federation_flavour.112.desc: Reports are in from [diplomacy_leader.GetName] with word returning that the recent diplomatic conference went well, [diplomacy_leader.GetName] was able to demonstrate the finer nuances of [Root.GetName], even managing to smooth out many issues within the negotiation process.
STH_federation_flavour.112.name: Successful Diplomatic Mission
STH_federation_flavour.113.desc: Concerning reports are being raised by [diplomacy_leader.GetName], despite a series of long conversations and diplomatic meets the Captain and their team were unable to add any momentum to the integration talks, and more concerning a number of new issues have now been raised.
STH_federation_flavour.113.name: Unsuccessful Diplomatic Mission
STH_federation_flavour.114.desc: Greetings [Root.GetRulerName], I am representing the Trill Symbiosis Commission, for a long time we Trill have kept the nature of our symbiotic nature existence, but with our joining the Federation, we feel the need to declare the secrets of the Trill. \n\nWe require your support to ensure the continued existence of our way of life and our very nature.
STH_federation_flavour.114.name: Trill Symbiosis Commission
STH_federation_flavour.115.desc_01: My name is [accessionStateLeader.GetName] and I have been part of the [From.GetName] military for many years and I want to publicly back the [From.GetName]'s plans to join the [Root.GetName] together we will build a stronger alliance and a safer galaxy!
STH_federation_flavour.115.desc_02: To my fellow scientists, my name is [accessionStateLeader.GetName] and I speak on behalf of many within the scientific community of [From.GetName], we welcome the opportunity to unite with the many fine Federation scientists and are excited by the prospect of uniting our Science institutions and research centres. We look forward to the many breakthroughs we will bring forth together.
STH_federation_flavour.115.desc_03: The government of [From.GetName] have been working hard with our friends from the [Root.GetName] and I want to publically thank all involved and declare how excited we all are at the prospect of joining this great alliance.
STH_federation_flavour.115.name: High Profile Leader Backs Federation Integration
STH_federation_flavour.116.desc_01: Admirals! I am [accessionStateLeader.GetName], an admiral in the [From.GetName] fleet and we have protected our empire from threats for many years, what assurances can be given that letting outsiders provide our protection will keep us safe?
