Hemos encontrado los escombros de un barco, no parece lo suficientemente grande como para pertenecer al Hombre de hojalata, pero no podemos estar seguros. informa a un oficial. \n\n Se requerirá más investigación para comprender lo que ha sucedido aquí. Deberíamos escanear los escombros para obtener más información.
STH_gomtuu.3.name: Escombros encontrados
STH_gomtuu.30.desc: Gomtuu ha superado su batalla contra los piratas, no habrá pasado desapercibido que no acudimos en su ayuda. Solo podemos preguntarnos cuál será su próxima acción, podría ocurrir hoy, mañana, simplemente no lo sabemos.
STH_gomtuu.30.name: Gomtuu derrota a los piratas
STH_gomtuu.4.desc: Al comenzar nuestras investigaciones, está claro que se ha establecido una trampa y hemos caído en ella. Como se predijo, no somos los únicos barcos que siguen al Hombre de hojalata. \n\n Los piratas están en curso directo con nuestra flota y parecen tener la intención de luchar. Si queremos localizar a esta misteriosa entidad, primero tendremos que derrotar a estos piratas.
STH_gomtuu.4.name: ¡Es una trampa!
STH_gomtuu.5.desc: Durante la batalla, nuestro Oficial Científico localizó el núcleo de la computadora de uno de los barcos piratas. Si nuestros científicos pueden obtener los datos almacenados dentro de este núcleo, deberíamos ser capaces de reconstruir lo que sucedió y rastrear hacia dónde ha huido nuestro objetivo. \n\n Siempre que sea seguro hacerlo, debemos llamar una nave de ciencia para investigar esta pista.
STH_gomtuu.5.name: Una pista vital encontrada
STH_gomtuu.6.desc: Los escaneos detallados de los restos indican que fue destruido por una descarga eléctrica masiva, que contiene una firma única. Nuestros científicos creen que podemos usar esto para reducir la ubicación de nuestro objetivo. \n\n ¿Quién sabe en qué estado estará cuando lo encontremos? Esperemos que no sea demasiado tarde.
STH_gomtuu.6.name: Tin Man localizado!
STH_gomtuu.8.c: No podemos dejar que la inmundicia viva así
STH_gomtuu.8.desc:
Hemos logrado rastrear a la criatura, que parece ser un barco vivo. La entidad no parece ser peor por su terrible experiencia a manos de los piratas. 

La criatura se acerca y de repente el oficial al mando puede sentir que la criatura se extiende telepáticamente. 

Podemos responder y hablar con la criatura o usar Esta oportunidad de atacar.
STH_gomtuu.8.name: Tin Man encontrado seguro
STH_gomtuu.9.desc:
Hemos logrado rastrear a la criatura, que parece ser un barco vivo. La entidad parece haber sufrido daños por su encuentro con los piratas. 

La criatura se acerca con cautela, cuando de repente el oficial al mando puede sentir que la criatura se extiende telepáticamente. 

Podemos responder y hablar con la criatura o usar Esta debilidad como nuestro momento para atacar.
STH_gomtuu.9.name: Tin Man encontrado herido
STH_gorn_mechanics.12.desc: We have received news that some of the Gorn armies that landed on [This.GetName] are ruthlessly attacking the civilian population and spreading fear and destruction in their wake. As a result of this recently one of the buildings on the planet´s surface was ruined.
STH_gorn_mechanics.12.name: Gorn armies terrorising the population!
STH_gorn_mechanics.13.desc: We have received news that some of the Gorn armies that landed on [This.GetName] are ruthlessly attacking the civilian population and spreading fear and destruction in their wake. As result of this a pop has been killed.
STH_gorn_mechanics.13.name: Gorn armies terrorising the population!
STH_gorn_mechanics.14.desc: Our infant hordes have succeeded in their terror operations on [From.GetName] and ruined a building.
STH_gorn_mechanics.14.name: Our infant hordes are doing their job
STH_gorn_mechanics.15.desc: Our infant hordes have succeeded in their terror operations and killed a pop on [From.GetName].
STH_gorn_mechanics.15.name: Our infant hordes are doing their job
STH_gravity.2.b: We should leave the dead in peace.
STH_gravity.2.desc:
Arriving at the scene all of sudden a gravity well opens up directly below the [Root.GetName] pulling the ship violently towards it. By quickly venting plasma from the engines while at the same time increasing power to the impulse engines [Root.GetName] narrowly avoids getting sucked into the well. 

After escaping the pull of the gravity well [Root.GetName]´s sensors are able to detect a planet located in a pocket of subspace inside the gravity well. Sending a probe in to further explore the planet it is discovered that on its surface there are several alien vessels that most likely fell victim to the well and crashed there. However no life signs are detected coming from the vessels. 

We could explore the option of trying to lift the crashed alien ships up from the surface of the planet and exploit their resources and possible new technologies.
STH_gravity.2.name: Crashed ships detected
STH_gravity.3.b: We can`t spare the resources for it.
STH_gravity.3.desc:
Arriving at the scene of the unusual readings all of sudden a massive gravity well appears seemingly out of nowhere, and despite of [Root.GetName] crew´s best efforts the ship is sucked in while having just enough time to send a distress call out. 

The distress call states that an entire solar system with 3 uninhabitable planets were detected on the other side of the gravity well existing in a subspace pocket. It is estimated that the crew of [Root.GetName] has enough rations to last for a year, assuming that the ship survived the entry into the well. 

Should we start a rescue mission for the lost crew and [gravity_leader.GetName]?
STH_gravity.3.name: Our ship has crashed
STH_gravity.4.desc: By first scanning the gravity well in detail and then heavily modifying some of our shuttlecrafts and their engines we were successful in entering the well and lifting up the remains of the alien ships. Both the materials gained and the knowledge of alien ship designs should benefit our future efforts.
STH_gravity.4.name: Lifting the remains of alien ships successful
STH_gravity.5.a: They were lucky to have survived this ordeal.
STH_gravity.5.desc: After arriving at the scene and scanning the massive gravity well it is clear that this is a monodirectional phenomenon, meaning that no ship going in can get out. However by sending a reinforced probe inside the well [Root.GetName]´s crew are able to first locate the missing leader and most of the crew of our crashed ship alive. And then by using the probe as a signal transmitter slowly beam them out of the gravity well in small groups. The survivors seem to have suffered only minor injuries and some malnourishment while waiting to be rescued. \n\nThe rescued leader assumes command of [Root.GetName].
STH_gravity.5.name: Saving crew successful
STH_gravity.6.desc: We have failed to rescue our leader and the crew of the crashed ship in time from the gravity well. It is estimated that by now all possible survivors have run out of supplies.
STH_gravity.6.name: Saving crashed ship's crew failed
STH_hazards.3.a: The Badlands is a dangerous area.
STH_hazards.3.desc: Whilst traversing the plasma storms in the region known as the [badlands_ship_damaged_minor.solar_system.GetName] the [badlands_ship_damaged_minor.GetName] has taken minor damage from one of the many plasma storms that occupy the region.\n\nThe risk of further damage will only increase the longer the ship remains in proximity to the area. [Root.GetName] Fleet Control recommends immediate withdrawal from the vicinity.
STH_hazards.3.name: [badlands_ship_damaged_minor.GetName] damaged in Hostile Region
STH_hazards.4.a: We must conclude our business there swiftly.
STH_hazards.4.desc: The [badlands_ship_damaged_major.solar_system.GetName] are a hostile and dangerous region of space and unfortunately the [badlands_ship_damaged_major.GetName] has suffered severe damage at the hands of one of the graviational anomalies that exist in the region.\n\nThe risk of further damage will only increase the longer the ship remains in proximity to the area. [Root.GetName] Fleet Control recommends immediate withdrawal from the vicinity.
STH_hazards.4.name: [badlands_ship_damaged_major.GetName] suffers serious damage in the Badlands
STH_hazards.5.desc: Many ships have been lost whilst attempting to travel within the region of space known as the Badlands and we have lost all contact with the [badlands_ship_destroyed.GetName], long range scans have picked up signs of debris along the last known coordinates of the ship. We must sadly assume the vessel was destroyed and hope that some of the crew were able to make it to safety.
STH_hazards.5.name: [badlands_ship_destroyed.GetName] lost in the Badlands
STH_hazards.50.d.tooltip: You will not receive any further chances to avoid this system or others like it. You know what you are doing after all.
STH_hazards.50.desc: The [This.GetName] has reached a galactic barrier, this signifies a tremendous distance across a vast stretch of space, and through the dangerous galactic barrier. \n\nBefore [This.GetName] begins the journey do you wish to abandon the venture across this void.
STH_hazards.50.name: The edge of the Galaxy
STH_hazards.6.desc: There has been some good news following the destruction of their ship, [badlands_ship_leader.GetName] and a number of their crew have been found alive in escape pods drifting on the outskirts of the storms. \n\nDespite the Captain and crew being severally shaken, there were in generally good health despite their experience.
STH_hazards.6.name: Crew Recovered
STH_hazards.7.desc: We have lost all contact with [caretakered_ship.GetName] whilst they were traversing the region known as the Badlands. \n\nThere has been no sign of the ship or its crew and no wreckage has been spotted. Plasma storms in the region are making any further investigation too dangerous and we can only assume the ship was lost with all hands.
STH_hazards.7.name: Ship Lost in the Badlands
STH_hero_age.21.desc: Following her joining with the Dax symbiont over 10 years ago, Ezri Dax has undergone a real transformation, both host Ezri and Dax have grown in confidence together and may see a future outside of the science division. A recent opportunity to take command has left Dax wondering if a transfer to the command division is in order.
STH_hero_age.21.name: Ezri Dax takes command
STH_hero_age.22.desc: Since being liberated from the Borg, the ex-drone, known now as simply 'Seven' has continued to demonstrate her exceptional skills and the admiralty believes the time has come to promote her to the command track.
STH_hero_age.22.name: Fun will now commence
STH_hero_age.29.a.tooltip: Convert Data to an Admiral capable of leading military ships. We will need to recruit him from the leader pool once again.
STH_hero_age.29.b: This position is not for him.
STH_hero_age.29.c.tooltip: Add the 'Hero Admiral' Trait, but remain scientist leader class.
STH_hero_age.29.desc: Following years of dedicated service to Starfleet, the Android known as Data has put in a request for a promotion and a command of his own. Data has an implecable service record, but not everyone in the fleet would be comfortable following the command of an artificial lifeform.
STH_hero_age.29.name: First Android in Starfleet
STH_hero_age.35.b: This position is not for him.
STH_hero_age.35.desc: Following years of dedicated service to Starfleet security, Worf has demonstrated leadership and the ability to command a crew his peers are recommending that he switches to the command division and put his considerable skills to use in this area.
STH_hero_age.35.name: An honourable opportunity
STH_hero_age.41.b: Remain amongst the rank and file.
STH_hero_age.41.desc: Following her rescue from her transport ship as a young girl, the young La'an Noonien-Singh was determined to repay Starfleet by joining the fleet and serving alongside Starfleet's finest.\n\nIs there a position within the fleet for Cadet Noonien-Singh?
STH_hero_age.41.name: La'an Noonien-Singh
STH_hero_age.44.b: Ask Chapel to remain on the ship.
STH_hero_age.44.desc: Having served with distinction during the expedition alongside their commanding officer [From.GetName], Nurse Chapel has excelled in her duties and has been recommended for a promotion form [From.GetName].
STH_hero_age.44.name: Nurse Chapel I presume?
STH_hero_age.45.b: Ask Ortegas to remain on the ship.
STH_hero_age.45.desc: Having served with distinction during the expedition alongside their commanding officer [From.GetName], Lt Ortegas has excelled in her duties and has been recommended for a promotion form [From.GetName] and a command of their own on her return to Earth.
STH_hero_age.45.name: Lt Ortegas
STH_hero_age.50.desc: Admiral Spock has served Starfleet with distinction for more years than almost any other commander in the fleet. As a distinguished officer, it is time for him to make the career decision that he has earnt. \n\nRemain within Starfleet, use his considerable political might as an Ambassador (governor) or retire from public life. The needs of the many really do outweigh the needs of the few... or the one.
STH_hero_age.50.name: The Logical Next Step
STH_hero_age.51.b: Move to the Admiralty governors board.
STH_hero_age.51.desc: Robert April is one of Starfleet's most decorated officers, his role on the bridge of the Enterprise and other notable Starships has become legend. Is now the time for his next career move?
STH_hero_age.51.name: This bridge is more like home than anywhere else
STH_hero_leader.20.desc: Georgiou attended Starfleet Academy from 2220 to 2224, meeting and befriending other notable Starfleet admirals such as Christopher Pike, who described her as the sharpest tool in the shed and a force to be reckoned with. He also remembered her drinking Pike and their fellow cadets underneath the table, but also for having every Starfleet regulation down by the second week. As a young Lieutenant, Georgiou was serving aboard the USS Archimedes and was involved in numerous first contacts. As a rising star in the fleet, many knew it wouldn't be long before this promotion came about. Should Georgiou become a heroic Scientist capable of commanding a Flagship or a heroic Admiral?
STH_hero_leader.20.name: Career decision to be made
STH_hero_leader.21.desc: Having been convinced to join Starfleet by renowned Starfleet admiral Alexander Marcus, Christopher Pike soon rose up the ranks, serving as a test pilot and served with distinction on at least three ships before earning this promotion. Brave and honourable Pike embodies what it means to be 'Starfleet'. Should Pike become a heroic Scientist capable of commanding a Flagship or a heroic Admiral?
STH_hero_leader.21.name: Career decision to be made
STH_hero_leader.22.desc: James Tiberius Kirk was the descendant of late 19th century American frontier pioneers and was inspired to join Starfleet due to the actions of his father. During his time in the Command Training Program, Kirk became famous for being the only cadet in history to beat the 'no-win' Kobayashi Maru scenario, and earning a commendation for original thinking. Should Kirk become a heroic Scientist capable of commanding a Flagship or a heroic Admiral!?
STH_hero_leader.22.name: Career decision to be made
STH_hero_leader.23.desc: Jean-Luc Picard caused a stir in rural France when he left his family's vineyard to join Starfleet Academy, he became one of the most successful cadets in his class becoming an expert in archeology and command. Picard served on a number of prominent ships before taking the initiative during a crisis on the USS Stargazer and saving the lives of the crew with the now famous 'Picard Manoverver'. Should Picard become a heroic Scientist capable of commanding a Flagship or a heroic Admiral?
STH_hero_leader.23.name: Career decision to be made
STH_hero_leader.24.desc: John Harriman II, son of former Commander-in-chief 'Blackjack' Harriman, is an exceptional student who has risen up the ranks rapidly - in only a small part due to his father's influence. Born onboard the USS Sea of Tranquility, Harriman has spent only weeks planetside with most of his life spent onboard starships. Now, with his training complete, should Harriman become a Scientist capable of commanding a Flagship, or instead a heroic Admiral?
STH_hero_leader.24.name: Career decision to be made
STH_hero_leader.25.desc: Rachel Garrett was born in Madison, Indiana on Earth. She has served with distinction on numerous vessels, though the psychological profile shows that her family life could be strained. Should Garrett become a heroic Scientist capable of commanding a Flagship or a heroic Admiral?
STH_hero_leader.25.name: Career decision to be made
STH_hero_leader.26.desc: Georgiou attended the Imperial Starfleet Academy from 2220 to 2224. Through flattery and seduction she gained a lot of influential allies among notable Starfleet admirals, including Christopher Pike himself, who credited her as a 'dependent, but effective weapon of destruction'. With such connections it did not take her too long to graduate from the Academy and be assigned to the ISS Archimedes. As a Lieutenant, Georgiou participated in numerous combat engagements against alien fleets, while also steadily climbing the ranks, removing the obstacles with favours and assassinations. With her achievements appearing more and more in Starfleet Command's internal documentation, her accelerated promotion to the highest ranks was just a matter of time. Should Georgiou become a heroic Scientist capable of commanding a Flagship or a heroic Admiral?
STH_hero_leader.27.desc: Joining the Imperial Starfleet on Admiral Alexander Marcus' recommendation, Christopher Pike quickly rose through the ranks - some say that he has been pushed by Starfleet Command to become a poster boy for the Empire, but his impressive kill record is a simple enough explanation. Ruthless and ambitious, Pike embodies what it means to be a Terran. Should Pike become a heroic Scientist capable of commanding a Flagship or a heroic Admiral?
STH_hero_leader.28.desc: James Tiberius Kirk was the descendant of late 19th century American naval officers and joined the Imperial Starfleet for sake of the prestige and power he could obtain in the military. One of his very first achievements was eliminating more than two hundred Imperial officers, including Captain Stephen Garrovick, in what is now known as 'The Farragut Incident'. Should Kirk become a heroic Scientist capable of commanding a Flagship or a heroic Admiral!?
STH_hero_leader.29.desc: A dedicated Starfleet officer, Commander Archer's desire for his own command landed him in the center seat of an advanced starship. However, his quest for power and glory, and his ultimate desire to take over the Terran Empire were both his biggest strength and biggest weakness. Should Archer become a heroic Scientist capable of commanding a Flagship or a heroic Admiral?
STH_hero_leader.30.desc: Captain Maximilian Forrest was a notable Terran leader in the 2150s. He was the commanding officer of the ISS Enterprise, developing an adversarial relationship with his first officer, Commander Jonathan Archer. Although more harsh and stern than his counterpart in the prime universe, relatively speaking, Forrest seemed to honestly care about the overall well-being of his ship and crew, and was legitimately concerned when a Terran Empire task force was destroyed fighting rebels at Tau Ceti. Should Forrest become a heroic Scientist capable of commanding a Flagship or a heroic Admiral?
STH_hero_leader.31.desc: Self-serving and cruel, few would have predicted Sato's rise to power. Before serving on the Enterprise, Sato had been a teacher in Brazil. Serving mainly as Captain Forrest's woman, Sato had previously been involved with Jonathan Archer but left him for Forrest when he was promoted to a position of higher power.\nShould Sato become a heroic Scientist capable of commanding a Flagship or a heroic Admiral?
STH_hero_leader.32.desc: Very few people could be considered as determined as Erika Hernandez. Having been passed up for command of Earth's first warp 5 starship, Hernandez vowed never to come in second place again and that she would take any means to ensure this she never did. Should Hernandez become a heroic Scientist capable of commanding a Flagship or a heroic Admiral?
STH_hero_leader.33.desc: Seven of Nine (full designation: Seven of Nine, Tertiary Adjunct of Unimatrix 01) was a former Borg drone, born Annika Hansen, a Human female. Assimilated by the Borg at the age of six, Seven was liberated by the crew of the USS Voyager in 2374. She was both a brilliant scientific mind and possessed considerable leadership skills forged through her service within the Fenris Rangers. Should Seven of Nine become a Scientist or an Admiral?
STH_hero_leader.34.desc: Bruce Maddox has had a chequered past: once a promising scientist in the field of cybernetics, his objection to giving Androids the status of living beings was well known within the scientific community. Since that point he has come to revere the work of Soong and Graves and can now be considered a leading mind in the field of positronic research.
STH_hero_leader.34.name: Synthetic expert recruited from the Daystrom Institute
STH_hero_leader.35.b: We do not need any more leaders.
STH_hero_leader.35.desc: We have been approached by a young warrior from the planet Capella, a number of years ago during an expedition to the planet one of our leaders saved the young man's life. He wishes to apply for Starfleet Academy and serve the country which saved his life all those years ago.
STH_hero_leader.35.name: Young Capellan Hero
STH_hero_leader.36.desc: Young Capellan, Leonard James Akaar has completed his training in Starfleet Academy with exemplary scores. Ready to take his place in the fleet and bring honour to his name.
STH_hero_leader.36.name: Capellan graduates with honours
STH_hero_leader.37.a: I'm a doctor, not a doorstop.
STH_hero_leader.37.desc: Having served as Voyager's Physician during its long journey home, the EMH has returned to Starfleet as a one of the most advanced and experienced Holograms in the fleet and after convincing many of his doubters wrong and thus earning recognition as a lifeform in his own right, the Doctor is ready to serve.
STH_hero_leader.37.name: Hero of the Delta Quadrant
STH_hero_leader.38.a: I didn't notice a little box on my chair!
STH_hero_leader.38.desc: Despite managing to serve Voyager's tour of the Delta Quadrant as an Ensign, the unsung hero of the Starship Voyager Harry Kim has finally earned himself that promotion.
STH_hero_leader.38.name: Hero of the Delta Quadrant
STH_hero_leader.39.a: Nothing is impossible if you want it badly enough.
STH_hero_leader.39.desc: The former Starfleet dropout and former Maquis engineer, B'Elanna Torres has been described by many as one of the finest engineers in the business. Torres' half-klingon temper often gets her in trouble, but her undoubted skills and abilities saved the crew more times than they could count, quickly earning her a full commission on Voyager's return.
STH_hero_leader.39.name: Hero of the Delta Quadrant
STH_hero_leader.40.a: Are you telling me I'm impossible to resist?
STH_hero_leader.40.desc: Regarded as some as the best pilot in the fleet, and by others as a no good rogue Tom Paris has had a troubling career thus far. Son of famed Admiral Owen Paris and kicked from Starfleet for covering up a tragic accident, Paris has reformed his ways and served Voyager with distinction. His return to home and glowing references have convinced the Admiralty to give him another chance.
STH_hero_leader.40.name: Hero of the Delta Quadrant
STH_hero_leader.41.a: The road to hell is paved with good intentions.
STH_hero_leader.41.desc: Once considered a promising officer before he defected to the Maquis to defend his homeland against the Cardassians, Chakotay served as Voyagers second in command throughout its journey home. Not afraid to challenge his captain, but fiercely loyal Chakotay was welcomed back into the fleet with open arms upon Voyager's return.
STH_hero_leader.41.name: Hero of the Delta Quadrant
STH_hero_leader.42.a: Mr. Vulcan, nice to meet you!
STH_hero_leader.42.desc: A man of many talents Neelix served as guide, cook and moral officer... not always with permission! Far from his home, but with plenty to offer Neelix has the skills to govern and host his own banquets.
STH_hero_leader.42.name: Hero of the Delta Quadrant
STH_hero_leader.43.b: Offer a role as a Starfleet Commander.
STH_hero_leader.43.desc:
As Earth propels itself into the stars, the role of Starfleet becomes ever more important as it begins the process of rolling Earth's other military organisations into a singular spacefaring entity. 

Balthazar Edison, hero of United Earth's multiple early conflicts whilst serving with the MACOs is seeking a new role within Starfleet. 

Edison is one of many war veterans seeking a place in an unfamiliar world.
STH_hero_leader.43.name: A new role for a decorated Military Hero
STH_hero_leader.45.a.tooltip: This is not necessarily the end of Seven's journey, she is a force of nature and is determined to do good in the STNH galaxy.
STH_hero_leader.45.desc: Despite two of the finest captains in Starfleet going into battle for Seven, Starfleet's concerns and prejudices were not easily overcome, leaving Seven feeling that she could do more good to the galaxy outside of Starfleet and the Federation. \n\nSeeing the work that organisations such as the Fenris Rangers are doing in the border regions, Seven head to the frontier to change lives.
STH_hero_leader.45.name: Seven of Nine
STH_hero_leader.46.desc: It wasn't an easy conversation, but Seven had some high profile backers within the ranks of Starfleet and so, as a result the Admiralty have agreed that her exceptional skills and experience would only aid the Federation, offering her a fast-tracked position within the fleet.
STH_hero_leader.46.name: Seven of Nine
STH_hero_leader.47.desc: Considered by many to be one of the most decorated captains in Starfleet history, Robert April was responsible for launching the careers of many of Starfleet's greats, such as Pike, Number One & the Enterprise herself. Not to be outshone by others April was considered a pioneer of Starfleet and a commanding presence amongst the Admiralty.
STH_hero_leader.47.name: Robert April
STH_hero_leader.50.a.tooltip: Selecting this option will unlock a new decision on [Root.capital_scope.GetName], which will create the Daystrom Institute deposit.
STH_hero_leader.50.b: We have no need of this.
STH_hero_leader.50.desc: The death of noted Federation scientist Richard Daystrom has left a noticeable hole in the scientific community. \n\nA sizeable group of scientists have expressed a wish to set up a new scientific research institute named in honour of Dr Daystrom, it will become the leading light in scientific research and a place for the very best minds in Starfleet and the civilian sector to come together to produce the finest research for the federation.
STH_hero_leader.50.name: Death of noted Scientist Richard Daystrom
STH_hero_leader.60.a: They are welcome to remain with us.
STH_hero_leader.60.b: How dare they, but we need new slaves!
STH_hero_leader.60.desc: A mysterious presence living on [Root.capital_scope.GetName] has come forward, for thousands of years, the alien Lanthanites have been living amongst our people, undetected and unobserved... they now feel the time is right to reveal their society and are asking to join us formally.
STH_hero_leader.60.name: Lanthanites Revealed
STH_hero_leader.61.b: A new mad scientist sounds perfect.
STH_hero_leader.61.desc: Commander Pelia was a female Lanthanite Starfleet officer who lived on Earth from the 6th century BC through at least the 23rd century. She served as an instructor at Starfleet Academy before being assigned to Operational Support Services as chief fleet inspector, and later transferred to the USS Enterprise as chief engineer.
STH_hero_leader.61.name: I'm a Lanthanite. I've lived for thousands of years
STH_hero_mechanics.10.a: Archer's place is on the bridge.
STH_hero_mechanics.10.desc: After years of serving United Earth, there are many calls for Jonathan Archer to help [Root.Owner.GetName] in a new manner. The world of politics awaits and many would love to see Starfleet's hero take a leading role in governing and helping decide the future.
STH_hero_mechanics.10.name: Time for jonathan Archer to decide what's next
STH_hero_mechanics.11.desc: After a long and impressive career in service of [Root.Owner.GetName], [hero_retire.GetName]'s thoughts turn to [hero_retire.GetHerHis] future. Even though others may advise to never leave the bridge of your ship. Sometimes other opportunities to serve present themselves.\n\nSome leaders are suited to being Ambassadors, governors or serving as Fleet Admirals. Alternatively returning to the Academy as a lecturer maybe the right decision.
STH_hero_mechanics.11.name: Decision to be made for [hero_retire.GetName]
STH_hero_mechanics.11.tooltip: The Fleet Admiral trait is a unique trait for high ranking Admirals granting them perks to better manage the our fleets.
STH_hero_mechanics.15.desc: After treating many patients from many different species, [doctor_retire.GetName] has the opportunity to look at what they want to do in the future. What choice should they make?
STH_hero_mechanics.15.name: Time for our Doctor to decide
STH_hero_mechanics.150.a: Tell the Federation to keep their pets in line.
STH_hero_mechanics.150.desc: Our colony on [bajoran_rebels_target.GetName] has been attacked, reports from our local Obsidian Order operatives suggest that it was orchestrated by a Bajoran cell, not yet ready to move on from the past. \n\nWhat is more concerning it appears they used Federation technology to carry out this attack!
STH_hero_mechanics.150.name: Terrorist Attack on [bajoran_rebels_target.GetName]
STH_hero_mechanics.151.desc: Our intelligence services report that the Cardassian colony on [bajoran_rebels_target.GetName] suffered a significant terrorist attack, with preliminary reports suggesting a rogue Bajoran cell being to blame for the destruction.
STH_hero_mechanics.151.name: Cardassian planet attacked
STH_hero_mechanics.152.b: We do not follow your orders Cardassian.
STH_hero_mechanics.152.desc: Greetings Starfleet, you will no doubt by now be aware of the callous attack on our innocent civilians on the peaceful colony world of [bajoran_rebels_target.GetName]. \n\nOur reports suggest that your 'allies' the Bajorans have not moved on from the events of the past and continue to habour a grudge for the missgivings of the past. We invite you to deal with this situation before we send our fleets in to make a more permanent solution.
STH_hero_mechanics.152.name: Incoming Message from Cardassia Prime
STH_hero_mechanics.153.desc: Those cowards in the Federation have refused to condemn those Bajoran fools and will not stand with Cardassia in the face of these attacks. This insult will not go unpunished or forgotten by those on Cardassia Prime.
STH_hero_mechanics.153.name: Federation refuse our Request
STH_hero_mechanics.154.desc: The Federation has seen sense and condemned the acts of those Bajorans fools, pledging their support to track down the source of the leaked weapons and bring a stop to these senseless attacks on our planets.
STH_hero_mechanics.154.name: Federation condemns the Bajoran Republic
STH_hero_mechanics.155.desc: The Federation has expressed a desire to fully investigate the matter to determine who launched the attack on us and how they obtained the use of Starfleet-grade weapons. The Central Government has allowed them limited time to investigate on the condition that when they reach the logical conclusion, they act swiftly and decisively.
STH_hero_mechanics.155.name: Federations wishes to investigate
STH_hero_mechanics.156.a: Tell me more about Ro Laren.
STH_hero_mechanics.156.desc: Greetings I am [hero_choice_1.GetName] and I am preparing to lead the investigation into the bombings on [bajoran_rebels_target.GetName]. \n\n Starfleet has provided the services of a Bajoran national, Ro Laren, currently serving time in a penal colony for causing the deaths of a number of her crew mates whilst on a mission with the USS Wellington. Our diplomatic services believe that Ro would be able to provide valuable insights into the Bajoran cell but be warned of her reputation with her fellow officers. Many believe she has no right to be free from her confinement.
STH_hero_mechanics.156.name: Ro Laren
STH_hero_mechanics.157.desc_01: We have made contact with a respected Bajoran leader named Keeve Falor, who has become the senior member of this small colony of Bajorans displaced during their struggle with Cardassian. Keeve could provide valuable intel on who has been organising these attacks on Cardassian settlements, however, Ensign Ro recommends that we disregard this valuable lead and locate a made known only as Orta. \n\nIt is unclear where she obtained this information from or how reliable her sources are, but following an unverified source would be risky.
STH_hero_mechanics.157.desc_02: We have made contact with a respected Bajoran leader named Keeve Falor, who has become the senior member of this small colony of Bajorans displaced during their struggle with Cardassian. Keeve could provide valuable intel on who has been organising these attacks on Cardassian settlements.
STH_hero_mechanics.157.name: Contact made with Bajorans
STH_hero_mechanics.158.desc: Unfortunately Keeve Falor was unable to provide any meaningful evidence or information about the attacks, either he is unwilling to provide answers, or more likely has been left out of the loop. We must inform the Cardassians of our failure... they will not be pleased.
STH_hero_mechanics.158.name: Dead End...
STH_hero_mechanics.159.desc: The Starfleet officer in charge of investigating the attacks on our planet has reported that they have been unable to identify who was responsible for the bombing of our planet. We have no choice but to hold any and all Bajorans responsible for the atrocities and our faith in Starfleet remains justifiably low.
STH_hero_mechanics.159.name: Federation Failure
STH_hero_mechanics.16.b: A role as a Governor awaits.
STH_hero_mechanics.16.c: They should teach our new warriors.
STH_hero_mechanics.16.desc: One of our honoured soldiers who has answered the call of the warrior over many years has the chance to serve the Empire in new ways. [hero_retire_kdf.GetName] has defeated all enemies in their way over many years now and new challenges await them within the Empire.
STH_hero_mechanics.16.name: Honoured Warrior
STH_hero_mechanics.160.b: Turn Orta over to the Cardassians.
STH_hero_mechanics.160.desc: The away team is directed to a hidden facility in the hills overlooking the Bajoran camp and introduced to an individual known only as 'Orta', who appears to head up the remains of a cell. By the looks of his scarred appearance, it would seem he has been battling the Cardassians for most of his life. \n\nOrta explains that his cell is in no longer in a fit state to continue their operation against the Cardassians and that they have knowledge that heavily implies that the attack against the Cardassians was a setup, by a small corner of the Obsidian Order, intending to turn the Federation against the Bajorans and allow the Cardassians to blame them and retaliate and wipe out those who resisted during the Occupation.
STH_hero_mechanics.160.name: Meeting with Orta
STH_hero_mechanics.161.b: Instruct the Order to deal with it.
STH_hero_mechanics.161.desc: The Starfleet investigation into the recent attacks indicates that the Bajoran sect we suspected of the attacks is not responsible and is in no fit condition to have attacked anyone. Despite our inquiries, the Starfleet Captain claims they were unable to record the location of this cell, either that or they are unwilling to share. The likelihood of this being an internal job seems unlikely, but the Obsidian Order stands ready to investigate.
STH_hero_mechanics.161.name: Message from Starfleet
STH_hero_mechanics.162.desc_01: We have listened to your 'statement' Starfleet and whilst many would believe your statements you will find that the Cardassian Union is not so easily fooled by lies, we will continue the search for the terrorist cells without your aid, but we at least acknowledge you attempted to help us. Should these attacks continue you will find that we are not so cautious in how we respond. \n\nUntil we are next forced to speak.
STH_hero_mechanics.162.desc_02: We have read your report, perhaps we had too much confidence in your ability to track down those responsible, but we suppose you did your best given your limited abilities. We shall continue our investigations internally and let it be known if any of our agents... or your agents are acting against the Central Government then they will be dealt with severely.
STH_hero_mechanics.162.name: Response from Cardassia
STH_hero_mechanics.163.b: Her time in Starfleet is over.
STH_hero_mechanics.163.desc: Following the conclusion of the mission, Starfleet are after a recommendation on what they should do with Ro Laren, many in Starfleet would prefer that she is ushered out of the service, but she clearly has a method of getting things done.
STH_hero_mechanics.163.name: The Future of Ensign Ro
STH_hero_mechanics.164.desc: Starfleet has returned from their investigations with a prisoner in tow and ready to hand over to us! It appears the humans have apprehended a pitiful Bajoran terrorist with dreams of harming their former masters, whilst it seems a bit suspicious that this rag-tag group could have caused the amount of damage sustained in the attack a full set of questions by the Obsidian Order should reveal more and flush out the remains of his group and any resources they might have.
STH_hero_mechanics.164.name: Report from Starfleet
STH_hero_mechanics.165.desc: Greetings [Root.GetLeaderName], we thank you for turning over your captive and are pleased by your cooperation and assistance during this unfortunate event. Rest assured our operatives will take this investigation from here and we will see no further attacks from this group. \n\nIncoming Message: From Ensign Ro Lare... Please accept this message as formal resignation from Starfleet, and you can all formally go to he... Message Terminates.
STH_hero_mechanics.165.name: Response from Cardassia
STH_hero_mechanics.18.desc: Calls within the High Council for more proactive leadership are starting to gather momentum, with notable General Martok, famous for many victories becoming a strong candidate to move from the battlefield to a role within Klingon politics.
STH_hero_mechanics.18.name: General Martok
STH_hero_mechanics.2.desc:
After a long and distinguised career within [Root.Owner.GetName], it is time for [potential_retirement_leader.GetName] to consider their career options and potential retirement. 

[potential_retirement_leader.GetSheHe] may decide to retire from service, continue their service, move into academia - to help train the next generation or move into a senior role within their chain of command and behind the scenes.

What decision should be made?
STH_hero_mechanics.2.e: Take a role as an official.
STH_hero_mechanics.2.name: Career decision to be made
STH_hero_mechanics.20.a: Time to step out of the shadows.
STH_hero_mechanics.20.desc: For many years the loyal 'Number One' of Christopher Pike has served her Captain with dedication and loyalty. \n\nFollowing a recommendation from Pike a new command awaits Commander Una Chin-Riley and a chance to step into the captain's chair.
STH_hero_mechanics.20.name: Number One
STH_hero_mechanics.201.b: They should remain in their post.
STH_hero_mechanics.201.desc: While serving alongside [recruitment_leader.GetName] during their expedition Dr Selar has been recognised for her exemplary work as part of [recruitment_leader.GetName]'s medical staff. Earning the opportunity to make her own name amongst the fleet.
STH_hero_mechanics.201.name: Dr Selar Reporting for Duty
STH_hero_mechanics.202.desc: During [recruitment_leader.GetName]'s most recent mission on their current expedition one young nurse stood out amongst the medical staff, Nurse Ogawa has proven to be more than just another 'Lower Decker' and has been recommended for a promotion and a notable position amongst the fleet.
STH_hero_mechanics.202.name: Nurse Ogawa
STH_hero_mechanics.203.desc: Having served aboard the USS Voyager and learning his trade in the Delta Quadrant, Lt. Vorik has once again served with distinction on [recruitment_leader.GetName]'s current deep space mission, earning himself a deserved promotion.
STH_hero_mechanics.203.name: Engineer Vorik
STH_hero_mechanics.204.desc: Lon Suder has undergone an extreme journey whilst aboard the USS Voyager, but through hard work and mentoring from [recruitment_leader.GetName] during their current mission on the far reaches of [Root.GetName] space they are ready to play a larger part in fleet operations.
STH_hero_mechanics.204.name: Watch out Suder's About
STH_hero_mechanics.205.desc: Ranul Keru was an unjoined Trill with a reputation for fierce loyalty and unwavering devotion to his crew. This service has gained the notice of his commanding officer [recruitment_leader.GetName] and a promotion come the end of the current expedition.
STH_hero_mechanics.205.name: Security Officer Keru
STH_hero_mechanics.206.desc: Christine Vale has served with many of the finest Captains in Starfleet, before being promoted to XO, her recent service record, and acts of great service during [recruitment_leader.GetName]'s expedition has earned her a chance to take the Captain's chair herself.
STH_hero_mechanics.206.name: Ms Vale, I presume?
STH_hero_mechanics.207.desc: Xin Ra-Havreii was responsible for the design of the Luna Class Warp Core before a number of setbacks pushed him back into a Chief Engineer's role, but under the command of [recruitment_leader.GetName] Xin is ready to once again make a name for himself in Starfleet.
STH_hero_mechanics.207.name: Dr Xin in the House
STH_hero_mechanics.208.desc: Raffi has served loyally under Jean-Luc Picard for many years, becoming his new number one and whilst certain temptations always remain close to the surface, few have as keen an eye as Raffi for spotting suspicious activity. If 'J.L' believes she is ready to take the next step, perhaps a greater role in the fleet has been earned.
STH_hero_mechanics.208.name: The Star Gazer
STH_hero_mechanics.209.desc: The former Chief of Security onboard the USS Enterprise-D has pulled herself up the ranks of Starfleet, starting with so little but making a name of her own. Whilst serving alongside [recruitment_leader.GetName] Yar once again demonstrated her great value.
STH_hero_mechanics.209.name: Tasha Yar reporting for duty
STH_hero_mechanics.21.desc: Cadet Uhura was regarded as a bit of a communications prodigy at the academy securing a prestigious position within the fleet. Her recent performances whilst on expedition have earned her the right to be promoted.
STH_hero_mechanics.21.name: Open Hailing Frequences
STH_hero_mechanics.210.desc: Marked from a young age as a potential genius, Wesley Crusher served as a provisional ensign onboard the Federation's flagship even before attending the academy, Now is his chance to shine on the galactic stage.
STH_hero_mechanics.210.name: Shut up Wesley!
STH_hero_mechanics.211.desc: Lieutenant Commander Airiam was a female Human cyborg, a Starfleet science officer who lived during the mid-23rd century. Beginning in 2256, she served aboard the USS Discovery as the spore drive ops officer.
STH_hero_mechanics.211.name: Lieutenant Commander Airiam
STH_hero_mechanics.212.desc: Rhys was appointed chief tactical officer aboard the USS Discovery. In 2257, in recognition of his role in ending the Federation-Klingon War, Rhys was awarded the Starfleet Medal of Honor.
STH_hero_mechanics.212.name: Gen Rhys
STH_hero_mechanics.213.desc: Having earned her pilot's license at age 12, Keyla Detmer soon became one of the fleet's most promising young helm officers.
STH_hero_mechanics.213.name: Keyla Detmer
STH_hero_mechanics.214.desc: Lieutenant Commander Joann Owosekun, nicknamed 'Owo', was a female Human Starfleet officer who served during the mid-23rd century and the late 32nd century. Owosekun was raised in a Luddite collective on Earth and at the age of 15 she watched her best friend die without being able to help, which drove her decision to join Starfleet.
STH_hero_mechanics.214.name: Joann 'Owo' Owosekun
STH_hero_mechanics.215.desc: Ronald Altman Bryce was a male Human Starfleet communications officer who served on the USS Discovery in the mid-23rd century. As a child he listened to old radio emissions in space with his grandmother. This is why he loves comms.
STH_hero_mechanics.215.name: R.A. Bryce
STH_hero_mechanics.22.desc: George Samuel Kirk, Jr. was born the son of George and Winona Kirk in Riverside, Iowa on Earth. He is the brother of famed Starfleet Captain James T. Kirk; who believes he alone calls his brother by the nickname 'Sam'.
STH_hero_mechanics.22.name: So you've met my Brother?
STH_hero_mechanics.23.desc: Doctor Tracy Pollard was a female Human Starfleet officer in the 23rd century and later in the 32nd century. She was assigned to the USS Discovery in the 2250s as a physician.
STH_hero_mechanics.23.name: Doctor Pollard
STH_hero_mechanics.24.desc: This is the USS Discovery. My name's Commander Landry. I'm Chief of Security here. I see we're unloading all kinds of garbage today. Even Starfleet's first mutineer. On me.
STH_hero_mechanics.24.name: Ellen Landry
STH_hero_mechanics.25.desc: Commander Adreek-Hu was an 24th century Aurelian Starfleet command division officer who served as first officer aboard the USS Protostar.
STH_hero_mechanics.25.name: Commander Adreek-Hu
STH_hero_mechanics.26.desc: Commander Tysess was a male Andorian command division officer who served as the first officer of the USS Dauntless and the USS Voyager-A under Vice Admiral Kathryn Janeway in the late 24th century. He was on the bridge in 2384 when the Dauntless's sensors detected a third proto-warp signature originating from the USS Protostar.
STH_hero_mechanics.26.name: Commander Tysess
STH_hero_mechanics.302.desc_01: While under fire the [FromFrom.GetName]'s command crew was incapacitated, Eddington stepped up to the Captain's Chair and took command of the situation ensuring that the [FromFrom.GetName] was victorious! \n\nHis actions under fire have earned him a recommendation and a leadership position within the fleet.
STH_hero_mechanics.302.desc_02: Following a detailed investigation into an ongoing crime ring, Lieutenant Michael Eddington cracked the case wide open earning a recommendation from his commanding officer [From.GetName] and a promotion.
STH_hero_mechanics.302.name: Michael Eddington
STH_hero_mechanics.303.desc_01: Following it's recent battle [FromFrom.GetName] revealed that it was added in battle by the Captain and crew of a small civilian Freighter named the SS Xhosa, the brave ship was able to provide a distraction that earned the [FromFrom.GetName] victory. \n\nThe Captain of the Xhosa, Captain Kasidy Yates has been offered a battlefield commission and a chance to serve [Root.GetName].
STH_hero_mechanics.303.desc_02: In recognition of her humanitarian work, Captain Kasidy Yates has been offered the opportunity to serve the [Root.GetName] in a greater capacity at the personal recommendation of [From.GetName].
STH_hero_mechanics.303.name: Hey, batter, batter, batter, batter, batter
STH_hero_mechanics.304.desc_01: While under fire the [FromFrom.GetName]'s command crew was incapacitated, Jack Ransom was the man to step up, take the Captain's Chair, and guide the [FromFrom.GetName] to victory! \n\nHis actions under fire have earned him a recommendation and a leadership position within the fleet.
STH_hero_mechanics.304.desc_02: Having been sent on an away mission to deliver a sacred item to a fractious colony that went wrong, Jack Ransom repaid his Captain [From.GetName]'s faith in him by winning his trial by combat and delivering an impassioned speech calling out the Federation's ideals and in the process earning himself a promotion.
STH_hero_mechanics.304.name: From now on, I'll make sure to give you special attention
STH_hero_mechanics.305.desc_01: While under fire the [FromFrom.GetName]'s tactical officer, Shax was able to use the ship's limited weaponry to great effect and score many vital hits to their enemies vital systems, earning himself a promotion in the process.
STH_hero_mechanics.305.desc_02: Captain's Log, [From.GetName] reporting back on an example of the sort of officer that [Root.GetName] could do with more of, whilst under attack from a hostile vessel, Lt. Shax was able to suggest a maneuver involving the warp core that saved the lives of everyone aboard the ship. I fully recommend him for both a commendation and promotion.
STH_hero_mechanics.305.name: I've dreamed of this for so long. Eject.
STH_hero_mechanics.306.desc_01: While the [FromFrom.GetName] was under fire, with casualties coming into sickbay at alarming regularity Doctor T'Ana was able to keep the medical team working and get the crew back to their posts as quickly as possible to ensure the ship made it out of the battle in one piece.
STH_hero_mechanics.306.desc_02: While investigating a rage virus, which was turning the ship's crew into mindless zombies Doctor T'Ana was able to devise a cure from the saliva of a local spider-like domesticated beast. Earning her a promotion.
STH_hero_mechanics.306.name: Congratulations, you look like a scratching post
STH_hero_mechanics.307.desc_01: During the heat of the last battle, just as the situation was looking dire for the [FromFrom.GetName] a young Vulcan science officer named T'Lyn recommended a method of increasing shield output that gave the crew an edge in the battle.
STH_hero_mechanics.307.desc_02: Captain's Log Supplemental - Whilst it is rare to be provided officers from the Vulcan Science Directorate, I must say that I am grateful that it has happened on this occasion, I have been very impressed with Scientist T'Lyn and am putting them forward for a promotion and transfer to Starfleet.
STH_hero_mechanics.307.name: Vulcan as a...
STH_hero_mission.1.desc: El Servicio Diplomático de [Root.GetName] ha organizado una conferencia de alto nivel con la administración de [diplomomatic_target1.Owner.GetRulerTitle] [diplomomatic_target1.Owner.GetRulerName] en [diplomatic_target1.GetName]. \n\nEl [diplomacyHeroFleet1.GetFleetName] bajo el comando [diplomacyHeroLeader1.GetName] se ha seleccionado para transportar la delegación [Root.GetName] y ofrecer soporte completo mientras la conferencia está en curso.
STH_hero_mission.1.name: Extensión Diplomática
STH_hero_mission.10.desc: La conferencia sobre [diplomate_target1.GetName] ha concluido con éxito, dejando las relaciones entre el [Root.Owner.GetName] y el [diplomomatic_target1.Owner.GetName] mejorado. \n\nCuando las sesiones iniciales sufrieron reveses iniciales y parecían insinuar una incapacidad para llegar a un acuerdo sobre una serie de acuerdos mutuamente beneficiosos entre las dos potencias, al final de la segunda semana quedó claro que los principales objetivos de la conferencia serían Se reunió en parte debido a los esfuerzos del capitán [diplomacyHeroLeader1.GetName] para facilitar las negociaciones.
STH_hero_mission.10.name: Extensión Diplomática
STH_hero_mission.11.desc: Una conferencia diplomática liderada por [diplomacyHeroLeader1.Owner.GetName] en [diplomatic_target1.GetName] ha concluido con éxito, dejando las relaciones entre el [Root.GetName] y el [diplomomatic_target1.Owner.GetName] mejorado. \n\nCuando las sesiones iniciales sufrieron reveses iniciales y parecían insinuar una incapacidad para llegar a un acuerdo sobre una serie de acuerdos mutuamente beneficiosos entre las dos potencias, al final de la segunda semana quedó claro que los principales objetivos de la conferencia serían Se reunió en parte debido a los esfuerzos del capitán [diplomacyHeroLeader1.GetName] para facilitar las negociaciones.
STH_hero_mission.11.name: Extensión Diplomática
STH_hero_mission.12.desc: La conferencia sobre [diplomática_target1.GetName] ha superado las expectativas iniciales, dejando sustancialmente mejoradas las relaciones entre [Root.Owner.GetName] y [diplomomatic_target1.Owner.GetName]. \n\nLas sesiones iniciales fueron productivas y dieron a entender una serie de acuerdos mutuamente beneficiosos entre las dos potencias, mientras que al final de la segunda semana quedó claro que los principales objetivos de la conferencia se cumplirían debido a los colosales esfuerzos de Capitán [diplomacyHeroLeader1.GetName] para facilitar las negociaciones.
STH_hero_mission.12.name: Extensión Diplomática
STH_hero_mission.13.b: Se adhieren a los términos originales.
STH_hero_mission.13.desc:
Una conferencia diplomática liderada por [diplomacyHeroLeader1.Owner.GetName] sobre [diplomatic_target1.GetName] ha superado las expectativas iniciales, dejando las relaciones entre el [Root.GetName] y el [diplomatic_target1.Owner.GetName] mejorado sustancialmente. 

Las sesiones iniciales fueron productivas y dieron a entender una serie de acuerdos mutuamente beneficiosos entre las dos potencias, mientras que al final de la segunda semana quedó claro que los principales objetivos de la conferencia se cumplirían debido a los colosales esfuerzos de Capitán [diplomacyHeroLeader1.GetName] para facilitar las negociaciones. 

So impresionado con la delegación [diplomacyHeroLeader1.Owner.GetName] fueron muchos en [diplomática_target1.GetName], que [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName] está bajo presión de los diplomáticos para ofrecer una cooperación más sustancial, desde el comercio Derechos a los programas de intercambio de funcionarios.
STH_hero_mission.13.name: Extensión Diplomática
STH_hero_mission.15.desc: Tras el extraordinario éxito de la conferencia diplomática sobre [diplomatic_target1.GetName], el [diplomatic_target1.Owner.GetName] ha autorizado un programa de intercambio de oficiales entre las dos potencias. \n\nEl primer oficial para unirse al esquema es el científico [giftedLeader.GetName], que ahora está disponible para el servicio.
STH_hero_mission.15.name: Extensión Diplomática
STH_hero_mission.16.desc: Tras el extraordinario éxito de la conferencia diplomática sobre [diplomatic_target1.GetName], el [diplomatic_target1.Owner.GetName] ha autorizado un programa de intercambio de oficiales entre las dos potencias. \n\nEl primer oficial a unirse al esquema es el Almirante [giftedLeader.GetName], que ahora está disponible para el servicio.
STH_hero_mission.16.name: Extensión Diplomática
STH_hero_mission.17.desc: Tras el extraordinario éxito de la conferencia diplomática sobre [diplomatic_target1.GetName], el [diplomatic_target1.Owner.GetName] ha autorizado un programa de intercambio de oficiales entre las dos potencias. \n\nEl primer oficial a unirse al esquema es General [giftedLeader.GetName], que ahora está disponible para el servicio.
STH_hero_mission.17.name: Extensión Diplomática
STH_hero_mission.18.desc: Al no haber transportado la delegación diplomática a [diplomatic_target1.GetName] en el tiempo asignado, las relaciones entre [Root.GetName] y el [diplomatic_target1.Owner.GetName] se han visto gravemente afectadas, como [diplomatic_target1.Owner.GetRulerTitle] [diplomatic_target1.Owner.GetRulerName] reacciona enojado al insulto percibido.
STH_hero_mission.18.name: Extensión Diplomática
STH_hero_mission.19.desc: A falta de transporte de un equipo de la delegación diplomática a [diplomatic_target1.GetName] en el tiempo asignado, las relaciones entre el [Root.GetName] y el [diplomacyHeroLeader1.Owner.GetName] se han visto gravemente afectados, como [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root. GetRulerName] admistration reacciona enojado con el insulto percibido.
STH_hero_mission.19.name: Extensión Diplomática
STH_hero_mission.2.desc:
La conferencia sobre [diplomate_target1.GetName] ha terminado desastrosamente, dejando las relaciones entre [Root.Owner.GetName] y [diplomomatic_target1.Owner.GetName] tensas. 

Los primeros días fueron productivos y parecían sugerir un acuerdo mutuamente beneficioso entre las dos potencias. Sin embargo, para el final de la primera semana el negociador principal en el equipo diplomático de [diplomatic_target1.Owner.GetRulerTitle] [diplomatic_target1.Owner.GetRulerName] llegó a los golpes con el capitán [diplomacyHeroLeader1.GetName] después de concluir aparentemente que [diplomacyHeroLeader1.GetSheHe] Estaba románticamente involucrado con el cónyuge del negociador.

Defing su capitán, un número de [Root.GetFleetName] tripulación fueron arrestados como la pelea engullido el centro de conferencias.
STH_hero_mission.2.name: Extensión Diplomática
STH_hero_mission.20.desc: As our teams prepare to leave the conference a group of rebel ships opposed to the recent conference appear from an undisclosed location intent on attacking our vessels to disrupt the conference.
STH_hero_mission.20.name: Rebels attack!
STH_hero_mission.22.desc: A group of rebels frustrated with our invitation to invite a third party to our system have managed to get access to a small fleet of ships and are looking to disrupt proceedings. Our security services have requested that we deal with these rebels quickly.
STH_hero_mission.22.name: Rebels attack!
STH_hero_mission.3.desc:
Una conferencia diplomática liderada por [diplomacyHeroLeader1.Owner.GetName] en [diplomate_target1.GetName] ha terminado desastrosamente, dejando las relaciones entre el [Root.GetName] y el [diplomacyHeroLeader1.Owner.GetName] tensas. 

Los primeros días fueron productivos y parecían sugerir un acuerdo mutuamente beneficioso entre las dos potencias. Sin embargo, al final de la primera semana, el negociador líder en el equipo diplomático [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName] llegó a los golpes con el capitán [diplomacyHeroLeader1.GetName] después de concluir que aparentemente [diplomacyHeroLeader1.GetSheHe] El cónyuge del negociador. 

Algunos miembros de la [diplomacyHeroLeader1.Fleet.GetFleetName] fueron arrestados mientras la pelea engullía el centro de conferencias. 

El [diplomacyHeroLeader1.Owner.GetName] todavía tiene que disculparse formalmente por el incidente.
STH_hero_mission.3.name: Extensión Diplomática
STH_hero_mission.30.desc: Greetings, I have spent the last few years working on something that will be of great use to the [Root.GetName] and I am on the cusp of a breakthrough in robotics and positronic life, but I can no longer work within the confines of our research institutes. I need the seclusion of a planet without the bother of others trying to interfere or mess with my research... What do you say eh? But if note if I am reassigned then all my work will be in vain.
STH_hero_mission.30.name: Android Research
STH_hero_mission.31.b: Focus on imbuing with wonder for humanity.
STH_hero_mission.31.c: Focus on creating a basic model.
STH_hero_mission.31.desc: Bah, what is it with you [Root.GetName] types always disturbing by work? Argh whilst you are here you may as well know that I have made good progress with my positronic masterpiece and have reached the point where I now need to set the focus of my creation and seeing as you so rudely interrupted me, let me hear what you have to say.
STH_hero_mission.31.name: Android Research
STH_hero_mission.32.desc: Our colony world of [nooniensoong_planet_selected.GetName] has gone dark, but the last comms from them was concerning, with reports of a mysterious object in the sky attacking the colony and laying waste to the people on the planet.
STH_hero_mission.32.name: Lost Contact with Colony
STH_hero_mission.4.desc:
Una conferencia diplomática liderada por [diplomacyHeroLeader1.Owner.GetName] en [diplomatic_target1.GetName] ha terminado desastrosamente, dejando las relaciones entre el [Root.GetName] y el [diplomacyHeroLeader1.Owner.GetName] tensas. 

Los primeros días fueron productivos y parecían sugerir un acuerdo mutuamente beneficioso entre las dos potencias. Sin embargo, al final de la primera semana, el negociador líder en el equipo diplomático [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName] llegó a los golpes con el capitán [diplomacyHeroLeader1.GetName] después de concluir que aparentemente [diplomacyHeroLeader1.GetSheHe] El cónyuge del negociador. 

Algunos miembros de la [diplomaciaHeroLeader1.Fleet.GetFleetName] fueron arrestados mientras la pelea engullía el centro de conferencias. 

 [diplomacyHeroLeader1.Owner.GetRulerTitle] [diplomacyHeroLeader1.Owner.GetRulerName] se ha disculpado formalmente en nombre de [diplomacyHeroLeader1.Owner.GetName], con [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName] aceptando en nombre del [ Root.GetName].
STH_hero_mission.4.name: Extensión Diplomática
STH_hero_mission.5.desc:
La conferencia sobre [diplomatic_target1.GetName]] ha terminado en fracaso, dejando las relaciones entre [Root.Owner.GetName] y [diplomomatic_target1.Owner.GetName] tensas. 

La primera semana fue productiva y parecía sugerir un acuerdo mutuamente beneficioso entre las dos potencias. Sin embargo, al final de la segunda semana, el negociador principal en el equipo diplomático [diplomatic_target1.Owner.GetRulerTitle] [diplomatic_target1.Owner.GetRulerName] se negó a trabajar en la misma habitación que el capitán [diplomacyHeroLeader1.GetName] después de malinterpretar una broma [DiplomacyHeroLeader1.GetSheHe] hecho a expensas del negociador. 

Con el equipo de negociación [Root.Owner.GetName] poco dispuesto a excluir al capitán, la conferencia terminó pronto con pocos de sus objetivos cumplidos.
STH_hero_mission.5.name: Extensión Diplomática
STH_hero_mission.6.desc:
Una conferencia diplomática liderada por [diplomacyHeroLeader1.Owner.GetName] en [diplomate_target1.GetName] ha terminado en fracaso, dejando las relaciones entre el [Root.GetName] y el [diplomacyHeroLeader1.Owner.GetName] tensas. 

La primera semana fue productiva y parecía sugerir un acuerdo mutuamente beneficioso entre las dos potencias. Sin embargo, al final de la primera semana el negociador principal en el equipo diplomático [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName] se negó a trabajar en la misma habitación que Captain [diplomacyHeroLeader1.GetName] después de malinterpretar una broma [diplomacyHeroLeader1.GetSheHe ] A cargo del negociador. 

Con el equipo de negociación [diplomacyHeroLeader1.Owner.GetName] no dispuesto a excluir al capitán, la conferencia terminó pronto con pocos de sus objetivos cumplidos. 

El [diplomacyHeroLeader1.Owner.GetName] todavía tiene que disculparse formalmente por el incidente.
STH_hero_mission.6.name: Extensión Diplomática
STH_hero_mission.7.desc:
Una conferencia diplomática liderada por [diplomacyHeroLeader1.Owner.GetName] en [diplomatic_target1.GetName] ha terminado en fracaso, dejando las relaciones entre el [Root.GetName] y el [diplomacyHeroLeader1.Owner.GetName] tensas. 

La primera semana fue productiva y parecía sugerir un acuerdo mutuamente beneficioso entre las dos potencias. Sin embargo, al final de la primera semana el negociador principal en el equipo diplomático [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName] se negó a trabajar en la misma habitación que Captain [diplomacyHeroLeader1.GetName] después de malinterpretar una broma [diplomacyHeroLeader1.GetSheHe ] A cargo del negociador. 

Con el equipo de negociación [diplomacyHeroLeader1.Owner.GetName] no dispuesto a excluir al capitán, la conferencia terminó pronto con pocos de sus objetivos cumplidos. 

 [diplomacyHeroLeader1.Owner.GetRulerTitle] [diplomacyHeroLeader1.Owner.GetRulerName] se ha disculpado formalmente en nombre de [diplomacyHeroLeader1.Owner.GetName], con [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName] aceptando en nombre del [ Root.GetName].
STH_hero_mission.7.name: Extensión Diplomática
STH_hero_mission.8.desc: La conferencia sobre [diplomate_target1.GetName] ha concluido con resultados mixtos, dejando las relaciones entre el [Root.Owner.GetName] y el [diplomomatic_target1.Owner.GetName] mejorado marginalmente. \n\nMientras que las sesiones fueron productivas y parecían sugerir una serie de acuerdos mutuamente beneficiosos entre las dos potencias, era evidente al final de la segunda semana que los principales objetivos de la conferencia no se cumplirían a pesar de los mejores esfuerzos Del Capitán [diplomacyHeroLeader1.GetName] para facilitar las negociaciones.
STH_hero_mission.8.name: Extensión Diplomática
STH_hero_mission.9.desc: Una conferencia diplomática liderada por [diplomacyHeroLeader1.Owner.GetName] en [diplomatic_target1.GetName] ha concluido con resultados mixtos, dejando las relaciones entre el [Root.GetName] y el [diplomacyHeroLeader1.Owner.GetName] mejorado marginalmente. \n\nMientras que las sesiones fueron productivas y parecían sugerir una serie de acuerdos mutuamente beneficiosos entre las dos potencias, era evidente al final de la segunda semana que los principales objetivos de la conferencia no se cumplirían a pesar de los mejores esfuerzos Del capitán [diplomacyHeroLeader1.GetName] para facilitar las negociaciones.
STH_hero_mission.9.name: Extensión Diplomática
STH_hero_ship.2.a: Delay the decision by a year.
STH_hero_ship.2.desc_01: Durante años, los ingenieros han trabajado en mejoras a la clase NX de nave estelar, iniciada por la Enterprise. \n\nEstas mejoras incluyen componentes de otros Mundos Miembros de la Federación, con contribuciones particularmente grandes del [member_world_1.GetSpeciesName]. Si la Enterprise regresa a [Owner.Capital.GetName], el trabajo en las actualizaciones puede comenzar de inmediato.
STH_hero_ship.2.desc_02: Durante años, los ingenieros han trabajado en mejoras a la clase NX de nave estelar, iniciada por la empresa. \n\nSi la empresa regresa a [Owner.Capital.GetName], el trabajo en las actualizaciones puede comenzar de inmediato.
STH_hero_ship.2.name: Re acondicionamiento NX-01 Enterprise
STH_hero_ship.200.desc: With Earth's first Warp V vessel complete and ready to depart on her maiden voyage, it is now time for Starfleet to decide which of Earth's finest should command the ship and make history. \n\nTwo outstanding candidates exist Jonathan Archer, son of renowned scientist and key member of the Warp V project, Henry Archer and A.G. Robinson famed test pilot and first human to break the warp 2 barrier.
STH_hero_ship.200.name: Getting from there to here
STH_hero_ship.203.desc: With the World Razer now proudly orbiting Earth, it is time to select a commander who will be the Human race's figurehead as it asserts its dominance. \n\nTwo outstanding candidates exist Jon Archer, the brilliant son of the feared and famous scientist Henry Archer and Duvall whose steadfast belief in 'humans first' pushed him to the top of his field.
STH_hero_ship.203.name: Blazing from here to there
STH_hero_ship.4.desc:
The Enterprise has entered orbit of [Owner.Capital.GetName]. While the refit will take several months and a great deal of resources, the Starfleet Corps of Engineers are keen to assure that the benefits outweigh the costs. 

Requires 500 Minerals 

Warning: The fleet containing Enterprise will not be able to leave orbit until refit is complete! It is recommended to remove all other ships from this fleet! 

Dev Note: Ensure that the refit Enterprise is in place in the Fleet Management window as the only option for the Exploratory Starship before the event completes!
STH_hero_ship.4.name: Re acondicionamiento NX-01 Enterprise
STH_hero_ship.5.desc: Después de 3 meses en dique seco, la Enterprise ha sido equipada con los mejores sistemas y tecnologías que puede proporcionar [Owner.GetName]. La mayoría de sus sistemas son prototipos que por ahora no están disponibles para el resto de la flota.
STH_hero_ship.5.name: Re acondicionamiento NX-01 Enterprise
STH_hero_ship.50.desc_01:
El Proyecto NX, el ambicioso plan de [Root.GetName] para crear un buque Warp V, todavía está luchando. La visión original de Zefram Cochrane, que comenzó en 2119 con la fundación del nuevo complejo de investigación Faster-Than-Light en Bozeman, Montana, ahora tiene varias décadas y, sin embargo, la mayoría de las naves de la Humanidad aún se arrastran entre las estrellas en Warp II. 

El almirante Maxwell Forrest mira con disgusto al embajador vulcano Soval. Hoy temprano estaba eufórico con la noticia del exitoso lanzamiento del primer prototipo del proyecto NX. Ahora parece que su día empeoraba. 

'Con el debido respeto, Embajador, [Root.Capital.GetName] tiene una flota ahora. Nuestro motor Warp IV se instaló recientemente en el USS Franklin y - 'comenzó el Almirante, antes de ser cortado. 

' Fue hace menos de una década cuando su Comandante Robinson casi perdió la vida debido a esta imprudente persecución, Almirante . Simplemente aconsejamos precaución 'entonó Soval. 'Todavía sentimos que el proyecto NX debería suspenderse. No para siempre Solo por unos años más ". 

'Pasaré su sugerencia a [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName]".
STH_hero_ship.50.desc_02: When Zefram Cochrane and Henry Archer set about making the dream of Adam Soong come true, this dream had been reaffirmed a few years earlier when Cochrane and his General Sloane, gave her life, to fend off the attempted Vulcan invasion. Both knew that the only way for humankind to end up on top was to build a superior fleet. The construction of the new Soong Warp Complex had a single goal to build a vessel to push Earth's interests out into the stars and establish a dominance the lasts for centuries.
STH_hero_ship.50.name: El proyecto NX
STH_hero_ship.51.desc_01:
Ha sido un largo camino. Dos años después del exitoso vuelo de prueba del NX-Zeta [Root.Capital.GetName] celebra la colocación de la quilla para el primer buque Warp V: el primer buque insignia verdadero de la Flota Estelar, el NX Light Cruiser Class, Enterprise. 

Pero mientras La tecnología ha progresado a una velocidad vertiginosa, realizando los sueños de científicos como Archer, Tasaki y Cochrane, los vulcanianos siguen advirtiendo a la humanidad que no está preparada para los peligros que los enfrentan en el espacio profundo. 

Su presión política se puede sentir en todo el sector industrial en la Tierra. Los recursos del Proyecto están disminuyendo rápidamente, y muchos empleados del Complejo Warp Five han sido reasignados a diferentes proyectos. 

Mientras que el casco Enterprise flota con orgullo en Space Dock, en realidad no es más que una cáscara vacía. Los analistas y estrategas militares se ven obligados a admitir que no pueden estimar cuándo se completará este ambicioso proyecto. El éxito del proyecto es vital: la Enterprise es más que un banco de pruebas para las últimas tecnologías experimentales de propulsión y armas, sino un símbolo de las esperanzas y los sueños de la Humanidad.
STH_hero_ship.51.desc_02: Earth's mighty ship of war, the World Razer, named for the warship Lord Soong used to conquer the Northern Hemisphere, lies 95% complete in the space dock. Human kinds dream so close to completion but held back by political pressure and a shortage of funds after the resources pirated from the Kzinti raids run dry. \n\nHenry Archer makes one final push to give the World Razer the final push it needs to fly and bring terror to the galaxy.
STH_hero_ship.51.name: El proyecto NX
STH_hero_ship.52.desc_01: Se ha permitido que el proyecto NX continúe a un ritmo que satisfaga tanto a [Root.Capital.GetName] como a Vulcan. Todo lo que queda ahora es para el ajuste del buque prototipo en órbita con el motor Warp V y el equipo defensivo.
STH_hero_ship.52.name: El proyecto NX
STH_hero_ship.61.a.tooltip: Dev Note: Ensure that you send only the Enterprise in a fleet on its own to complete this project, all other ships will be deleted as part of the process.
STH_hero_ship.61.desc: For years the Constitution Class ships have been the pride of Starfleet engineers, pioneered by the Enterprise on her five year missions. New technologies can give this class of ship a new lease on life. \n\nIf the Enterprise returns to [Owner.Capital.GetName], work on the upgrades can be begin immediately.
STH_hero_ship.61.name: USS Enterprise Refit
STH_hero_ship.63.desc:
The Enterprise has entered orbit of [Owner.Capital.GetName]. While the refit will take several months and a great deal of resources, the Starfleet Corps of Engineers are keen to assure us the benefits outweigh the costs. 

Requires 1500 Minerals 

Warning: The fleet containing Enterprise will not be able to leave orbit until refit is complete! It is recommended to remove all other ships from this fleet!
STH_hero_ship.63.name: USS Enterprise Refit
STH_hero_ship.64.desc: As one of the first of the Constitution-class starships to undergo a major refit program the Enterprise's refit took many months of hard work and essentially a new vessel was built onto the bones of the old, replacing virtually every major system. These refits allowed USS Enterprise and her sister ships to continued to serve a prominent prominent role at the front line of Starfleet.
STH_hero_ship.64.name: USS Enterprise Refit
STH_hero_ship.67.desc: Starfleet have designated the USS Enterprise for refit to test out the new technologies on offer. The vessel selected is not the Enterprise, the engineers request that only the USS Enterprise NCC-1701 approach [upgrade_location.GetName] to start the project.
STH_hero_ship.67.name: USS Enterprise Refit
STH_hero_ship.68.desc: Starfleet have made the decision that the NX-01 Enterprise is the ideal candidate for refit to test out the new technologies on offer from the Columbia Class refit. The vessel selected is not the Enterprise, the engineers request that only the NX-01 approach [upgrade_location.GetName] to start the project.
STH_hero_ship.68.name: NX-01 Refit
STH_hero_ship_design.10.NX.desc_01: Un nuevo buque de exploración de largo alcance está listo para la construcción, utilizando tecnología experimental e ingeniería mejorada.
STH_hero_ship_design.10.ambassador.desc_: Un nuevo buque de exploración de largo alcance está listo para la construcción, utilizando tecnología experimental e ingeniería mejorada.

Un nuevo buque de exploración de largo alcance está listo para la construcción, utilizando tecnología experimental e ingeniería mejorada. Nuestras mejores tripulaciones deberían servir a bordo de este buque insignia. \n\nDebemos reasignar a nuestro capitán anterior y retirar primero nuestro buque insignia anterior, antes de comenzar la construcción de la nueva embarcación.

Nuestras mejores tripulaciones deberían servir a bordo de este buque insignia. \n\nDebemos reasignar a nuestro capitán anterior y retirar primero nuestro buque insignia anterior, antes de comenzar la construcción de la nueva embarcación.

STH_hero_ship_design.10.sovereign.desc_0: Un nuevo buque de exploración de largo alcance está listo para la construcción, utilizando tecnología experimental e ingeniería mejorada.

Un nuevo buque de exploración de largo alcance está listo para la construcción, utilizando tecnología experimental e ingeniería mejorada. Nuestras mejores tripulaciones deberían servir a bordo de este buque insignia. \n\nDebemos reasignar a nuestro capitán anterior y retirar primero nuestro buque insignia anterior, antes de comenzar la construcción de la nueva embarcación.
STH_hero_ship_design.1000.desc: A legend has ended too soon. The [FromFrom.GetName], has been destroyed in battle by the [FromFromFrom.GetName] of the [From.GetName]!\n\nThe destruction of one of our finest ships has raised concern amongst the people of [Root.GetName].
STH_hero_ship_design.1000.name: The End of a Legend
STH_hero_ship_design.2.desc: El orgullo de la flota, el [From.GetName] ha sido retirado. Si bien el buque nos ha servido bien, la retirada del barco permitirá construir un nuevo buque insignia.
STH_hero_ship_design.2.name: Nave insignia disuelta
STH_hero_ship_design.21.desc: The USS Enterprise (NCC-1701) was a 23rd century Federation Constitution-class starship operated by Starfleet. In the course of her career, the Enterprise became the most celebrated starship of her time.\n\nIn her forty years of service and discovery, through upgrades and at least two refits, she took part in numerous first contacts, military engagements, and time-travels. She achieved her most lasting fame through a five-year mission (2265-2270) under the command of Captain James T. Kirk.
STH_hero_ship_design.22.desc: On stardate 9715.5, in the year 2293, the Enterprise-B was commissioned. Soon thereafter, the starship was launched under the command of Captain John Harriman from a drydock orbiting Earth. The ship was christened with a bottle of Dom Pérignon, vintage 2265. Honoured guests aboard for the ship's maiden voyage included three legendary captains of previous Enterprise fame.
STH_hero_ship_design.23.desc: The Enterprise-C will go down in history for one famous act of heroism, an act that would prevent countless deaths in a war that never came to pass. While under the command of Captain Rachel Garrett, this Ambassador-class ship responded to a distress call from a Klingon colony on Narendra III, which was under attack by four Romulan warbirds. The Enterprise responded to the attack, and despite being destroyed there, defended it long enough to impress the Klingon High Council; this act of bravery in combat was seen as extremely honorable, thus securing a treaty between the Klingon Empire and the Federation.
STH_hero_ship_design.24.desc: The legendary command of Captain Jean-Luc Picard, the Galaxy-class Enterprise-D, became famous for its seven-year mission in which it made more first contacts then any ship in Starfleet, perhaps rivaled only by the Enterprise of James T. Kirk. It was famously said that 'For five hundred years, every ship that has borne the name of the Enterprise has become a legend. This one is no different.' The fifth Federation starship to bear the name Enterprise, during its career, the Enterprise served as the Federation flagship and was in service from 2363 to 2371.
STH_hero_ship_design.25.desc: On stardate 49827.5, the Sovereign-class Enterprise-E, seen as the pinnacle of Starfleet ship design, was launched from San Francisco Fleet Yards, with Captain Jean-Luc Picard in command once more. Much of the crew of the Enterprise-D had been reassigned there, including almost the entire senior staff. The latest in a long line of vessels called Enterprise, the sixth ships distinguished herself straight away in the Battle of sector 001 and then later against both the Son'a and the deadly Reman Scimitar.
STH_hero_ship_design.3.desc: ¡El orgullo de la flota, [FromFrom.GetName], ha sido destruido en la batalla por el [FromFromFrom.GetName] del [From.GetName]! \n\nLa destrucción de nuestro buque insignia ha enviado oleadas de descontento a través de la [Raíz .GetName]. Un nuevo buque debe construirse pronto.
STH_hero_ship_design.3.name: Buque insignia destruido.
STH_hero_ship_design.generic.desc: Once in a while a ship leaves drydock ready to weave its destiny amongst the stars. Such a ship has now been commissioned and is ready to make history; a new legend has begun...
STH_hero_ship_design.generic.name: A new ship has been commissioned
STH_hirogen_mechanics.2.desc: Hemos cazado a un grupo de [STH_hunter_mechanics_prey_pop.GetSpeciesNamePlural] en [STH_hunter_mechanics_prey_pop_planet.GetName] hasta el agotamiento.
STH_hirogen_mechanics.2.name: Hemos agotado un grupo de presas ...
STH_holo.1.desc: The combined skill of some of our best scientists and engineers have made an outstanding breakthrough in Holographic Technology.
