STH_minor_anomaly.403.name: Pulsos de croniton
STH_minor_anomaly.404.desc: Las exploraciones orbitales de [From.GetName] han demostrado ser incapaces de determinar la ubicación exacta de los pulsos del cronitón, aunque los indicadores principales sugieren que es probable que la fuente sea subterránea. \n\nSin ningún signo de disipación, se propone una encuesta completa para aislar y estudiar la fuente de la radiación de cronitón.
STH_minor_anomaly.404.name: Pulsos de croniton
STH_minor_anomaly.450.desc: Sensors have located a curious biosignature hidden under the surface on [From.Planet.GetName]. The recovered strands bear some resemblance to life found on other worlds in the galaxy, which is statistically improbable, however not impossible. Researchers may find this useful.
STH_minor_anomaly.450.name: DNA Strands
STH_minor_anomaly.451.desc:
Sensors have located a curious biosignature hidden under the surface on [From.Planet.GetName]. The recovered strands bear some resemblance to life found on other worlds in the galaxy, which is statistically improbable, however not impossible.

Further scans detect a small [Root.GetSpeciesName] transport drifting nearby, registered to an archeologist.

Upon investigating further, the ships hull has been breached after having been struck by energy weapons and the flash frozen body of the archaeologist drifts nearby. The ship appears to have been ransacked, though one time delayed capsule floats nearby emitting a weak signal.

Upon investigating the capsule, a message from the archaeologist begins to play concerning an embedded genetic pattern that is constant throughout many different species, and they speculate was left by an early race that pre-dates all other known civilizations. Coordinates for the hidden dig site are contained within.
STH_minor_anomaly.451.name: DNA Strands
STH_minor_anomaly.5.desc:
La explosión del taquión revela con éxito el objeto. Parece ser similar en función a una sonda [Root.Owner.GetSpeciesAdj], completa con algún tipo de tecnología de ocultación para ocultarla de la mayoría de los intentos de detección. 

 Al detectar que se ha descubierto, la sonda intenta activar un mecanismo de autodestrucción, sin duda una medida de seguridad para evitar la adquisición. Sin embargo, el pensamiento rápido del Oficial Táctico del [Root.GetName] coloca una descarga rápida [Root.Owner.GetMainEnergyWeapon] en la fuente del pico de poder suficiente para desactivar pero no destruir la sonda. 

La sonda se introduce en la bodega de carga donde los equipos de Ciencia e Ingeniería de [Root.GetName] pueden examinarla. 

Después de varias horas, creen que han accedido a su núcleo de memoria principal y a los datos que contiene. La sonda en sí parece ser antigua, tanto que las especies que la lanzaron ya no existen. 

Los datos están dañados y muy fragmentados, tanto que no se puede recuperar nada de valor.
STH_minor_anomaly.5.name: Objetos en el espacio
STH_minor_anomaly.500.desc: The signal we intercepted was a distress call from a ship in orbit around [From.GetName]. The ship is not giving off any heat signatures and seems to be drifting. A Special Project has been issued to investigate what has happened to the crew.
STH_minor_anomaly.500.name: Lost in Space
STH_minor_anomaly.501.desc: The crew of the derelict ship has been found dead, many from life support failure, but others seemingly acted suicidally in their final moments. Some members of our away team have begun to act strangely on their return, and scientist [Root.GetLeaderName] has discovered what looks like a brain parasite in one of the scans of the dead.
STH_minor_anomaly.502.desc: The ship in orbit around [From.From.From.GetName] seems to have been crippled, and our away team was nearly killed by the maddened crew apparently responsible. After a quick escape, they found a small group deep in the ship with an apparent cure for the contagious illness. The aliens seem to need our help to restore life support to disperse it before the ship is completely destroyed.
STH_minor_anomaly.503.desc_01: Despite madness all over the ship, scientist [Root.GetLeaderName] managed to synthesize a cure and distribute it throughout the ship's ventillation system. With the emergency over, the away team has suggested that the undamaged sensor logs on the derelict could be used to trace its origin.
STH_minor_anomaly.503.desc_02: Despite madness all over the ship, Doctor [derelictship_doctor.GetName] managed to synthesize a cure and distribute it throughout the ship's ventillation system. With the emergency over, the away team has suggested that the undamaged sensor logs on the derelict could be used to trace its origin.
STH_minor_anomaly.503.name: Chaos Clothed
STH_minor_anomaly.504.desc: Scientist [Root.GetLeaderName] was able to organize quarantining the away team and those they came in contact with to the ship in orbit around [From.From.From.GetName]. Unfortunately, while only some were infected, they all died along with the security personnel assigned when one of them managed to overload the derelict's main engines. Despite the loss, it is clear that if we had allowed them to stay on our ship, it may have been lost as well.
STH_minor_anomaly.504.name: Severed Chaos
STH_minor_anomaly.505.desc: As maddened crew were about to breach the laboratory, scientist [Root.GetLeaderName] finally managed to get a cure into the ventillation system. The parasites have left the survivors' brains with stroke-like effects. While the ship's hull can be easily repaired, scientist [Root.GetLeaderName] has lost many of the skills they possessed. The one bit of good news is that the recovering science officer believes the undamaged sensor logs on the derelict could be used to trace its origin.
STH_minor_anomaly.505.name: Scarred by Chaos
STH_minor_anomaly.506.desc: Scientist [Root.GetLeaderName]'s efforts were insufficient, and in the chaos of the mad crew, the main engines were overloaded. All hands were lost.
STH_minor_anomaly.506.name: Consumed by Chaos
STH_minor_anomaly.600.b: Leave the past, in the past.
STH_minor_anomaly.600.desc.mars: When the first explorers from Earth reached the red planet, Mars, efforts focused on the seemingly impossible task of taming the planet, with little time to reflect on the achievement of reaching Mars. \n\nOur scientists have now uncovered the potential location for one of those early, if not the earliest location of humanity's first touchdown on Mars.
STH_minor_anomaly.600.desc.moon: The moon has long hung bright and clear over the night skies of Earth's inhabitants. Ancient and full of mystery and promise, it has been a test of all humanity could accomplish. Today it looks very different, as much a hub for humanity as Earth itself, but our scientists have uncovered a potential site great historical importance, the location of one of the earliest Apollo missions.
STH_minor_anomaly.600.desc.praxis: Praxis, the great challenge, a new battleground for warriors to prove themselves whilst the ancient Great Houses raced for the glory of being the first house to put a Klingon on the surface of another world. \n\nNow is the time to uncover the glory of that momentous moment for the honour of all Klingons.
STH_minor_anomaly.600.desc.remus: Our dark twin has hung over the skies of Romulus since our ancestors first landed on the shores of Romulus after traveling across the stars since then we have conquered and mastered the harsh nature of Remus, bending it and its population to the Empire's mighty will. \n\nOur historians have discovered the location of our first landing on Remus and have asked us to investigate as a reminder of how far the Star Empire has come and the site of our first triumph.
STH_minor_anomaly.600.desc.titan: When early astronomers first saw clear pictures of the surface of Titan, its potential as an off-world colony became clear. Warp flight and the prospects of colonising planets in Alpha Centauri and Terra Nova meant that Titan was reduced to small colony research and training facility. Yet the memory of the journey to Titan and the efforts of Shango X-2 remain a key part of humanity's legend. Our Science Institute now believes it has found the location of one of these early landing sites.
STH_minor_anomaly.600.desc: From the earliest days of space flight for our empire, our ancient explorers set their eyes on landing on [From.GetName], hoovering tantalizingly close to our homeworld, just within touching distance. It became a goal for our early ventures off the surface of [From.Owner.capital_scope.GetName]. \n\nNow are scientists have found a site of historical importance, the location where the first of our species landed on an offworld site.
STH_minor_anomaly.600.name: One Small Step
STH_minor_anomaly.601.desc: The expedition team has located the site and has begun investigating, looking for signs of our early spaceflight to this planet. Scanners trained on any signs of the metals used to construct the spacecraft used to reach this planet, with local news reports keeping a close eye on the progress being made.
STH_minor_anomaly.601.name: One Small Step
STH_minor_anomaly.602.desc: Our archeology teams have successfully uncovered the site of vast historical significance, the site of our first landing on this world, a fantastic discovery. \n\nAs our teams prepare to handover to historians, this serves as a reminder of how far we have come as a species.
STH_minor_anomaly.602.name: One Small Step
STH_minor_anomaly.610.desc: On investigatng [This.GetName] can confirm the downed ship once belonged to a long-forgotten race of space farers, the ship appears to have been an archive of their civilization, there is very little left of the archive, except a small DNA Archive, unusable but contains some interesting elements for our scientists.
STH_minor_anomaly.610.name: Crashed Archive Ship
STH_mirrorMirror_event.1.a: Forzar al capitán y al equipo visitante a ir a la enfermería.
STH_mirrorMirror_event.1.b: Ignore el comportamiento y asuma que nada está mal.
STH_mirrorMirror_event.1.desc:
El capitán [Root.GetLeaderName] y el equipo visitante se dirigen hacia [From.GetName] para explorar el planeta. Las interesantes composiciones minerales parecen justificar un estudio más profundo, sin embargo la tormenta de iones que se aproxima obliga al equipo visitante a regresar al barco antes de que el análisis geológico pueda ser completado. 

 Con la creciente interferencia, el jefe del transportista casi pierde los patrones de los equipos del equipo visitante, pero es capaz de emitir el capitán [Root.GetLeaderName] y el equipo visitante hasta el [Root.GetFleetName] de forma segura. 

Después de su regreso, el capitán [Root.GetLeaderName] y los miembros del equipo visitante parecen estar actuando extrañamente, enojados y violentos con otros miembros de la tripulación con poca provocación. 

El Oficial Ejecutivo se ha preocupado de que pueda haber algo malo en el equipo visitante y cree que debe ser examinado por el médico del buque, justificando las acciones del equipo visitante como resultado de un extenso tiempo en el espacio profundo.
STH_mirrorMirror_event.1.name: Espejo Espejo
STH_mirrorMirror_event.10.a: Haz que el capitán te siga.
STH_mirrorMirror_event.10.b: Ignora el comportamiento y asume que nada está mal.
STH_mirrorMirror_event.10.desc:
El Capitán [Root.GetLeaderName] y el equipo visitante se dirigen hacia [mirrorPlanet.GetName] para explorar el planeta. Las valiosas composiciones minerales justifican un mayor estudio, sin embargo, la tormenta de iones que se aproxima obliga al equipo visitante a regresar al barco antes de que un equipo de ocupantes ilegales que ocupan varios sitios mineros principales pueda ser eliminado. 

Con la interferencia que aumenta rápidamente, el jefe de transporte casi pierde el los patrones de transporte del equipo visitante, pero pueden enviar al Capitán [Root.GetLeaderName] y al equipo visitante hasta el [Root.GetFleetName] de forma segura. 

Siguiendo su regreso, el Capitán [Root.GetLeaderName] y los miembros del equipo visitante parece estar actuando de manera extraña, tratando de resolver las disputas entre la tripulación utilizando la diplomacia y la lógica. 

El Oficial Ejecutivo se ha preocupado de que pueda haber algo mal con el equipo visitante y cree que deben seguirse de cerca; Si la interferencia externa ha afectado al Capitán y al equipo visitante, deben ser eliminados a toda costa.
STH_mirrorMirror_event.10.name: Espejo, Espejo
STH_mirrorMirror_event.11.a: Descubre más sobre el universo paralelo ...
STH_mirrorMirror_event.11.desc:
Después de seguir al Capitán [Root.GetLeaderName], el Oficial Ejecutivo descubre que el Capitán y el equipo visitante no son de este universo y son impostores de una dimensión paralela. Intentan secretamente regresar a su lugar de origen, pero el Oficial Ejecutivo cree que pueden intentar regresar luego con refuerzos para imponer su ideología retorcida a la gente de [Root.Owner.GetName]. 

Después de emboscar al Capitán y al equipo visitante, el Oficial Ejecutivo sufre una lesión grave. Sin embargo, el Capitán [Root.GetLeaderName] se niega a dejar morir al Oficial Ejecutivo e intenta revivirlo a costa de perder la oportunidad de volver a su propio universo. 

Cuando el Oficial Ejecutivo recupera la conciencia, presiona al Capitán y al fuera del equipo para obtener más detalles de su universo, y por qué sacrificarían su intento de llegar a casa por la vida de alguien que los detendría por cualquier medio necesario ...
STH_mirrorMirror_event.11.name: Espejo, Espejo
STH_mirrorMirror_event.12.a: Sigue al Capitán y al equipo visitante.
STH_mirrorMirror_event.12.desc: Un ingeniero senior ha intentado asesinar al Capitán [Root.GetLeaderName], ya que sus acciones recientes para promover la paz son vistas por la tripulación como un signo de debilidad. Sin embargo, el Capitán y el equipo visitante pueden frustrar el intento de asesinato y neutralizar al posible asesino sin matarlos. \n\nCon el protocolo, el Capitán perdona la vida de los amotinados. El Oficial Ejecutivo ahora tiene motivos suficientes para justificar la remoción del Capitán del mando, ya que estas no son las acciones normales de un comandante de nave espacial.
STH_mirrorMirror_event.12.name: Espejo, Espejo
STH_mirrorMirror_event.13.a: Sigue al Capitán y al equipo visitante.
STH_mirrorMirror_event.13.desc: El Capitán [Root.GetLeaderName] es ordenado por el Comando de Flota [Root.Owner.GetName] para obligar a los ocupantes ilegales en [mirrorPlanet.GetName] a entregar los minerales apuntando a su campamento y lanzando un bombardeo orbital del planeta. Sin embargo, el Capitán desobedece estas órdenes y en su lugar trata de hablar con ellos y hacer que cambien de opinión. \n\nEl Oficial Ejecutivo ahora tiene motivos para relevar al Capitán [Root.GetLeaderName] del comando y asumir el comando del [Root.GetName], por desobedecer las órdenes. Sin embargo, será difícil acercarse al Capitán para detenerlos o para detenerlos o asesinarlos.
STH_mirrorMirror_event.13.name: Espejo, Espejo
STH_mirrorMirror_event.14.a: En cada revolución hay una persona ...
STH_mirrorMirror_event.14.desc:
El Capitán y el equipo visitante del universo paralelo son escoltados por el Oficial Ejecutivo a la sala de transportadores, donde intentan repetir el proceso que los llevó a este universo y enviaron a su propio Capitán y equipo visitante al otro. 

Antes de desmaterializarse, el Capitán del otro universo le dice al primer oficial de este universo que el [Root.Owner.GetName] está destinado a desmoronarse y ser conquistado si no adoptan formas pacíficas. 

El Capitán [Root.GetLeaderName] y el equipo visitante de este universo se materializan en la plataforma del transportador, y se ven muy aliviados de haber regresado. 

Regresan con historias de una versión pacífica de [Root.GetName], donde no encajaban y fueron rápidamente detenidos y encarcelados. 

A pesar de la experiencia, el Oficial Ejecutivo ahora comienza a creer que el Capitán del otro universo tenía un punto, y que tal vez su futuro se encuentre en un camino más pacífico ...
STH_mirrorMirror_event.14.name: Espejo, Espejo
STH_mirrorMirror_event.15.a: En cada revolución hay una persona ...
STH_mirrorMirror_event.15.desc:
El Capitán y el equipo visitante del universo paralelo son escoltados por el Oficial Ejecutivo a la sala de transportadores, donde intentan repetir el proceso que los llevó a este universo y enviaron a su propio Capitán y equipo visitante al otro. 

Antes de desmaterializarse, el Capitán del otro universo le dice al primer oficial de este universo que el [Root.Owner.GetName] está destinado a desmoronarse y ser conquistado si no adoptan formas pacíficas. 

Hay un problema con el transportador y se acorta durante el ciclo de transporte. El oficial ejecutivo intenta recuperar la señal, pero ahora se ha ido para siempre. Es probable que ambas versiones del Capitán [Root.GetLeaderName] y los miembros del equipo visitante se hayan perdido durante el transporte. 

Aunque es lamentable que el Capitán y el equipo visitante de ambos universos se hayan perdido, el Oficial Ejecutivo ahora comienza a creer que el Capitán del otro universo tenía un punto, y que tal vez su futuro se encuentre en un camino más pacífico. ..
STH_mirrorMirror_event.15.name: Espejo, Espejo
STH_mirrorMirror_event.16.desc: El Oficial Ejecutivo decide que sería demasiado peligroso dejar que el Capitán [Root.GetLeaderName] y el equipo visitante vivan. Pretendiendo llevarlos a la sala de transportadores, en su lugar, son llevados a la esclusa de aire más cercana donde son arrojados al espacio. \n\nCon el asesinato del Capitán y el equipo visitante, el Oficial Ejecutivo ahora asume el mando de [Root.GetName ] y promete garantizar que las ideas peligrosas del universo paralelo no se propaguen aquí y causen disensión.
STH_mirrorMirror_event.16.name: Espejo, Espejo
STH_mirrorMirror_event.17.desc:
El Oficial Ejecutivo decide que sería demasiado peligroso dejar que el Capitán [Root.GetLeaderName] y el equipo visitante vivan. Pretendiendo llevarlos a la sala de transportadores, los llevan a la esclusa de aire más cercana. 

Sin embargo, el Capitán y el equipo visitante logran escapar de esta trampa y llegar a la sala de transporte y regresar a su universo. El Capitán de este universo [Root.GetLeaderName] y el equipo visitante aparecen en su lugar. 

 Llegan con historias de una versión pacífica de [Root.GetName], donde no encajaban y fueron rápidamente detenidos y encarcelados. Tanto el Capitán [Root.GetLeaderName] como el Oficial Ejecutivo de [Root.GetName] creen que las ideas del otro universo son peligrosas y no se debe permitir que se arraiguen aquí.
STH_mirrorMirror_event.17.name: Espejo, Espejo
STH_mirrorMirror_event.2.a: ¡El Capitán y el equipo visitante deben ser salvados!
STH_mirrorMirror_event.2.desc:
El médico del buque ha realizado un examen médico completo del capitán [Root.GetLeaderName] y del equipo visitante. Por lo que el médico puede decir, no hay nada malo con ninguno de ellos y no explination se puede proporcionar para explicar su comportamiento violento. El oficial ejecutivo tiene el capitán y el equipo de distancia confinados al bergantín hasta que puedan determinar la causa de su cambio en el comportamiento y cómo se puede resolver. 

Captain [Root.GetLeaderName] finalmente confía en el Oficial Ejecutivo que no son de este universo, y han transpuesto lugares con sus opuestos aquí. El Capitán había ordenado al equipo visitante que siguiera con la pretensión hasta que pudieran determinar una forma de regresar a su propio universo, pero las diferencias entre los dos universos lo hicieron imposible. 

El jefe del transportista cree que puede haber una manera de devolver el equipo visitante a su propio universo, y esperemos recuperar a su propio capitán y equipo visitante en el proceso. Sin embargo hay la posibilidad de que el enfoque puede fallar, y que terminaría perdiendo ambos equipos fuera.
STH_mirrorMirror_event.2.name: Espejo Espejo
STH_mirrorMirror_event.3.desc: Un miembro fuera del equipo ha sido atrapado tratando de asesinar a otro tripulante. Esto ahora ha obligado a la mano del primer oficial a ordenar a su oficial superior ya sus compañeros miembros de la tripulación a ser examinados médicamente. \n\nUn equipo de seguridad se ve obligado a luchar con el Capitán y, a la larga, a aturdir al Capitán ya los miembros del equipo y llevarlos a la enfermería inconsciente. El médico ahora puede realizar un examen médico para tratar de descubrir lo que ha sucedido con el equipo visitante y les ha hecho tan bárbaro y violento.
STH_mirrorMirror_event.3.name: Espejo Espejo
STH_mirrorMirror_event.4.desc: El capitán [Root.GetLeaderName] ordena al oficial táctico de [Root.GetFleetName] que abra el fuego en un conjunto de coordenadas en la superficie de [From.GetName]. Cuando el oficial pregunta por qué el Capitán ha ordenado este ataque, [Root.GetLeaderName] dibuja un phaser y ejecuta al oficial por desobedecer órdenes. \n\nAhora el Oficial Ejecutivo no tiene más remedio que ordenar la seguridad para arrestar, detener y obligar al Capitán y al equipo visitante a someterse a un examen médico para determinar lo que les sucedió y lo que los ha hecho tan bárbaros y violentos .
STH_mirrorMirror_event.4.name: Espejo Espejo
STH_mirrorMirror_event.5.a: ¡Es bueno tener al Capitán de vuelta!
STH_mirrorMirror_event.5.desc:
El capitán y el equipo visitante del universo paralelo son escoltados por un equipo de seguridad a la sala de transporte, donde el jefe de transporte intenta repetir el proceso que los llevó a este universo y envió a su propio capitán y equipo de distancia a la otra. Antes de desmaterializar, el capitán del otro universo le dice al primer oficial de este universo que este universo es débil y maduro para la conquista, y que se desmoronarán como lo hacen todas las civilizaciones débiles. 

Capitan [Root.GetLeaderName] y el equipo visitante de este universo se materializan en la almohadilla del transportista, pareciendo muy aliviado estar de vuelta.

El regreso con historias de horror de una versión torcida y bárbara del [Root.GetFleetName], donde la tortura y la violencia se utiliza para controlar y asesinar es la forma normal de ascender filas. El capitán más tarde confía en el oficial ejecutivo que en el otro universo había otra versión de ellos, y que el capitán esperaba que un discurso de salida dado al oficial ejecutivo de ese universo podría tener un efecto duradero.
STH_mirrorMirror_event.5.name: Espejo Espejo
STH_mirrorMirror_event.6.a: Un fin trágico y una pérdida de vida ...
STH_mirrorMirror_event.6.desc:
El capitán y el equipo de distancia del universo paralelo son escoltados por un equipo de seguridad a la sala de transporte, donde el jefe de transporte intenta repetir el proceso que los llevó a este universo y envió a su propio capitán y equipo de distancia a la otra. Antes de desmaterializar, el capitán del otro universo le dice al primer oficial de este universo que este universo es débil y maduro para la conquista, y que se desmoronarán como lo hacen todas las civilizaciones débiles. 

Hay un problema con el transportador, y se corta durante el ciclo de transporte. El jefe del transportista trata de recuperar la señal, pero ahora se ha ido para siempre. Es probable que ambas versiones del Capitán [Root.GetLeaderName] y los miembros del equipo fuera de casa se perdieran durante el transporte. 

El jefe del transportador aturdido promete corregir el error que llevó a que este incidente tenga lugar, pero no traerá de vuelta al capitán o perdió a los miembros de la tripulación.
STH_mirrorMirror_event.6.name: Espejo Espejo
STH_mirrorMirror_event.7.a: Un final desafortunado para todos los involucrados ...
STH_mirrorMirror_event.7.desc:
La otra versión del universo de Captain [Root.GetLeaderName] y los miembros del equipo fuera de casa deben ser transferidos a una instalación segura donde se mantendrán indefinidamente. 

Ellos son demasiado peligrosos para que se les permita salir libres, ya que se teme que puedan encontrar una manera de regresar a su universo brutal y regresar con refuerzos. 

 Si bien es una pérdida trágica que no podamos devolver al capitán y al equipo visitante de este universo, la información que obtendremos de sus homólogos del otro universo será, sin duda, de gran valor. También trabajaremos para asegurar que el accidente no se repita mejorando los procedimientos del transportador.
STH_mirrorMirror_event.7.name: Espejo Espejo
STH_mirrorMirror_event.8.a: ¡Es bueno tener al Capitán de vuelta!
STH_mirrorMirror_event.8.desc:
La otra versión del universo del Capitán [Root.GetLeaderName] y los miembros del equipo fuera de casa debían ser transferidos a una instalación segura, pero durante el proceso de transferencia dominaron a su equipo de escolta de seguridad y tomaron sus armas. 

Después de luchar en su camino a la sala de transporte, lograron transportar de vuelta a su universo, y en el proceso devolvió el capitán original y el equipo de distancia. 

Captain [Root.GetLeaderName] y el equipo visitante regresaron con historias de terror de una versión torcida y bárbara del [Root.GetFleetName], donde la tortura y la violencia se utiliza para controlar y asesinar es la forma normal de ascender filas. 

El Capitán más tarde confía en el Oficial Ejecutivo que en el otro universo había otra versión de ellos, y que el Capitán esperaba que un discurso de despedida dado al Oficial Ejecutivo de ese universo pudiera tener un efecto duradero.
STH_mirrorMirror_event.8.name: Espejo Espejo
STH_mirrorMirror_event.9.desc: El equipo visitante investigó el planeta sin embargo nada de nota fue encontrado antes de que la tormenta de iones que se aproximaba los obligó a regresar a la nave.
STH_mirrorMirror_event.9.name: Espejo Espejo
STH_mirror_invasion.0.b.tooltip: Select this option if your stellaris is crashing whilst setting up this old event. Dev team are working to optimise it for future patches.
STH_mirror_invasion.0.desc: Subspace telescopes across [Root.GetName] space have detected a massive surge in zeta radiation localised around the [mirror_universe_portal_system_1.GetName] black hole. \n\nThe Science Council has requested Starfleet dispatch a vessel to the anomaly.
STH_mirror_invasion.0.desc_cardassian: Subspace telescopes across the [Root.GetName] have detected a massive surge in zeta radiation localised around a region of space which appears to have recently generated a number of large plasma storms. This has been designated as the [mirror_universe_portal_system_1.GetName]. \n\nThe Central Command has ordered the dispatch a vessel to the anomaly.
STH_mirror_invasion.0.desc_generic: Subspace telescopes across the [Root.GetName] have detected a massive surge in zeta radiation localised around a region of space which appears to consist of spatial interphase. This has been designated as an [mirror_universe_portal_system_1.GetName].\n\nWe should dispatch a vessel to the anomaly.
STH_mirror_invasion.0.desc_klingon: Subspace telescopes across the [Root.GetName] have detected a massive surge in zeta radiation localised around a region of space which appears to have recently generated a number of large plasma storms. This has been designated as the [mirror_universe_portal_system_1.GetName]. \n\nThe High Council has tasked the Imperial Navy to dispatch a vessel to the anomaly.
STH_mirror_invasion.0.name: Unusual Radiation Surge
STH_mirror_invasion.1.desc: Sources within the [mirror_universe_target.GetName] report that a massive surge in zeta radiation has been detected localised around the [mirror_universe_portal_system_1.GetName] black hole. \n\nStarfleet appears to be assembling a survey mission to the anomaly.
STH_mirror_invasion.1.desc_kca: Sources within the [mirror_universe_target.GetName] report that a massive surge in zeta radiation has been detected localised around a newly emerged region of plasma storm activity designated the [mirror_universe_portal_system_1.GetName]. \n\nThe [mirror_universe_target.GetName] appears to be assembling a survey mission to the anomaly.
STH_mirror_invasion.1.desc_tho: Sources within the [mirror_universe_target.GetName] report that a massive surge in zeta radiation has been detected localised around a newly emerged region of interspace activity designated the [mirror_universe_portal_system_1.GetName]. \n\nThe [mirror_universe_target.GetName] appears to be assembling a survey mission to the anomaly.
STH_mirror_invasion.1.name: Unusual Radiation Surge
STH_mirror_invasion.10.desc_01: Apparently confirming [mirror_universe_escapee.GetName]'s story, the [Root.GetName] reports the detection of a large number of ships near the [mirror_universe_portal_system_1.GetName] black hole. The designs are Starfleet!
STH_mirror_invasion.10.desc_02: The [Root.GetName] reports the detection of a large number of ships near the [mirror_universe_portal_system_1.GetName] black hole. The designs are Starfleet!
STH_mirror_invasion.10.name: Broken Mirror
STH_mirror_invasion.11.c: As long as you agree to stay in this system...
STH_mirror_invasion.11.d: Return to your own universe. Now.
STH_mirror_invasion.11.desc:
Ah, [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.Leader.GetName]... My name is [terran_empire_vanguard_leader.GetName] and I come from the universe on the Other Side. 

Let's get straight to the point. Your time in this world is short, so try not to make it even shorter by getting in my way.

You can leave this system now, and you have my word my fleet won't venture beyond the singularity's sphere of influence. Remaining will be considered a hostile act.
STH_mirror_invasion.11.name: Broken Mirror
STH_mirror_invasion.12.desc_01: Apparently confirming [mirror_universe_escapee.GetName]'s story, the [Root.GetName] reports the detection of a large number of ships near the [mirror_universe_portal_system_1.GetName]. The designs appear to be familiar...
STH_mirror_invasion.12.desc_02: The [Root.GetName] reports the detection of a large number of ships near the [mirror_universe_portal_system_1.GetName]. The designs appear to be familiar...
STH_mirror_invasion.12.name: Broken Mirror
STH_mirror_invasion.13.c: As long as you agree to stay in this system...
STH_mirror_invasion.13.d: Return to your own universe. Now.
STH_mirror_invasion.13.desc:
Ah, [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.Leader.GetName]... My name is [klingon_cardassian_alliance_vanguard_leader.GetName] and I come from the universe on the Other Side. 

Let's get straight to the point. Your time in this world is short, so try not to make it even shorter by getting in my way.

You can leave now, and you have my word my fleet won't venture beyond the storms. Remaining will be considered a hostile act.
STH_mirror_invasion.13.name: Broken Mirror
STH_mirror_invasion.14.desc_01: Apparently confirming [mirror_universe_escapee.GetName]'s story, the [Root.GetName] reports the detection of a large number of ships near the [mirror_universe_portal_system_1.GetName]. The designs appear to be familiar...
STH_mirror_invasion.14.desc_02: The [Root.GetName] reports the detection of a large number of ships near the [mirror_universe_portal_system_1.GetName]. The designs appear to be familiar...
STH_mirror_invasion.14.name: Broken Mirror
STH_mirror_invasion.15.c: As long as you agree to stay in this system...
STH_mirror_invasion.15.d: Return to your own universe. Now.
STH_mirror_invasion.15.desc:
Ah, [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.Leader.GetName]... My name is [tholian_vanguard_leader.GetName] and I come from the universe on the Other Side. 

Let's get straight to the point. Your time in this world is short, so try not to make it even shorter by getting in my way.

You can leave now, and you have my word my fleet won't venture beyond the spatial interphase. Remaining will be considered a hostile act.
STH_mirror_invasion.15.e: Welcome home lattice kin. We will prepare for the emergence.
STH_mirror_invasion.15.name: Broken Mirror
STH_mirror_invasion.19.desc: The last Terran Empire ship in this universe has been destroyed, and with it the immediate threat to the [Root.GetName] has abated. \n\nWhile there is no guarantee that the threat from the Other Side will not re-emerge again in the future, their defeat in this universe is widely believed to have weakened the Terran Empire's war capacity considerably.

STH_mirror_invasion.2.b: We do not have time for this nonsense.
STH_mirror_invasion.2.desc: The surge in zeta radiation detected from the [mirror_universe_portal_system_1.GetName] black hole has yet to be investigated, but recently complications have emerged when a vessel containing a single occupant was intercepted on a course for [Root.Capital.GetName]. \n\nThe occupant, a [Root.GetSpeciesName] named [mirror_universe_escapee.GetName] claims to have crossed over from a 'mirror' universe via the [mirror_universe_portal_system_1.GetName]. Further, [mirror_universe_escapee.GetSheHe] claims to have information vital to the security of the [Root.GetName].

STH_mirror_invasion.20.desc_01:
[terran_empire_vanguard_leader.GetName]'s promise to restrict [terran_empire_vanguard_leader.GetHerHis] activities to scientific research appears to have been broken, as another series of high intensity zeta radiation bursts have been detected localised around the [mirror_universe_portal_system_1.GetName] black hole. 

The energies involved this time - coupled with unusual warp signatures from the system have prompted an urgent assessment of the situation in the area. 

A military expedition had been ordered, however a transmission from [terran_empire_vanguard_leader.GetName]'s ship near [mirror_universe_portal_system_1.GetName] singularity demanding a direct channel to [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName] has overshadowed matters.
STH_mirror_invasion.20.desc_02:
[mirror_universe_escapee.GetName]'s warning appears accurate, as another series of high intensity zeta radiation bursts have been detected localised around the [mirror_universe_portal_system_1.GetName] black hole. 

While no survey was conducted following the previous bursts, the energies involved this time - coupled with unusual warp signatures from the system have prompted an urgent assessment of the situation in the area. 

A military expedition had been ordered, however a transmission from a ship near the [mirror_universe_portal_system_1.GetName] singularity demanding a direct channel to [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName] has overshadowed matters.

STH_mirror_invasion.21.desc_01:
Ah, [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.Leader.GetName]... My name is [From.GetName] and I come from the universe on the Other Side. 

Let's get straight to the point. I am in command of an entire Terran Empire battle fleet. 

We have shifted an entire world into this universe, our beach head to conquer your pathetic [Root.GetName]. 

I'll give you time to think on your next move, but I suggest for your people's sake that it's a quick offer of surrender...
STH_mirror_invasion.21.desc_02:
Well [Root.GetRulerTitle], it appears your time in this universe is running shorter than you might have thought. As you can plainly see, I wasn't... entirely honest with you before. 

We have shifted an entire world into this universe, our beach head to conquer your pathetic [Root.GetName]. 

I'll give you time to think on your next move, but I suggest for your people's sake that it's a quick offer of surrender...
STH_mirror_invasion.21.name: Broken Mirror
STH_mirror_invasion.22.c: An opportunity too good to pass!
STH_mirror_invasion.22.desc:
My name is [From.GetName] and I come from the universe on the Other Side. Call it what you will, parallel... mirror... the place is the same. 

I represent the Terran Empire, and we are here to destroy the [mirror_universe_target.GetName]. I suspect this may interest you too. 

Join us, and together we can carve up their worlds between us.
STH_mirror_invasion.22.name: Broken Mirror
STH_mirror_invasion.23.desc: Reports from our agents in the [mirror_universe_war_joiner_hostile.GetName] government have indicated that they have joined the Terran Empire in their war against us.

STH_naval_museum.1000.desc_01: Saludos, [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName]. Mi nombre es Commodore [starfleet_naval_museum_administrator.GetName] y soy el enlace de la Flota Estelar para esta instalación. \n\n¿Cómo puede ayudarlo hoy el Museo Naval de la Flota Estelar?
STH_naval_museum.1000.desc_02: Larga vida al Imperio, [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName]. Mi nombre es Commodore [starfleet_naval_museum_administrator.GetName] y soy el enlace imperial de la Flota Estelar para esta instalación. \n\n¿Cómo puede el Museo Naval de la Flota Estelar Imperial servir al Imperio hoy?
STH_naval_museum.1000.desc_03: A safe galaxy is a Human galaxy! [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName]. My name is Commodore [starfleet_naval_museum_administrator.GetName] and I am the Confederation Corps liaison to this facility. \n\nHow can the Earth Naval Museum serve the Empire today?
STH_naval_museum.1000.j: Mostrar diseños del mundo de los miembros.
STH_naval_museum.1000.name: Museo naval
STH_naval_museum.1000.terran.a: ¿La 'Agencia Histórica de la Flota Estelar Imperial'?
STH_naval_museum.1001.a: Volver a poner en servicio la Clase Emmette.
STH_naval_museum.1001.c: Volver a poner en servicio la clase Engle.
STH_naval_museum.1001.desc_01:
Las fragatas son pequeñas embarcaciones diseñadas para tareas de defensa del sistema y otras operaciones limitadas. Están construidos para la velocidad y la maniobrabilidad. Por lo general, las fragatas pelearían en pequeñas cantidades. El término fragata se originó en el Mediterráneo - Tierra a fines del siglo XV, en referencia a un barco tipo galleass más ligero con remos, velas y un armamento ligero. Fragata famosa de los EE. UU. Constitución conocida como 'Old Ironsides' debido a su construcción robusta, el barco más viejo aún intacto en Estados Unidos sirve como museo en Boston, Massachusetts. Todavía a flote, tenía una vida útil generalmente larga, habiendo permanecido en comisión entre 1797 y todo el camino hasta la Guerra Civil, después de lo cual se le hizo un barco de entrenamiento y continuó navegando periódicamente hasta su desmantelamiento final en 1881. Por favor seleccione la clase de fragata que desea volver a poner en servicio. 

Las clases de barcos obsoletos se retirarán automáticamente al museo, aún podrán designarse durante 5 años después de ser retirados. Una vez que se hayan retirado por completo, podemos volver a poner en servicio las clases antiguas de barcos, consumiendo una ranura de la Lista de clases de barcos readmitidos.  La puesta en servicio de las clases de barcos antiguos requiere una gran cantidad de personal para mantener la producción, lo que resulta en un período de enfriamiento de 5 años antes de que podamos retirar esta clase nuevamente . 

Slots: [root.GetMuseumNumber] / [root.GetMuseumMax]
STH_naval_museum.1001.i: Volver a poner en servicio la Clase Challenger.
STH_naval_museum.1001.k: Volver a poner en servicio la Clase Federación.
STH_naval_museum.1001.name: Sociedad Histórica de la Flota Estelar
STH_naval_museum.1001.o: Volver a poner en servicio la Clase Sabre.
STH_naval_museum.1002.a: Volver a poner en servicio el Intrepid-Type.
STH_naval_museum.1002.c: Volver a poner en servicio la clase Bonaventure.
STH_naval_museum.1002.desc_01:
Un crucero ligero es un tipo de nave estelar multipropósito de tamaño pequeño a mediano. A lo largo de la historia, han servido en una variedad de roles, principalmente como escoltas de convoyes y naves de comando de destructores, pero también como exploradores y buques de apoyo de flota para flotas de batalla. En la Tierra, los primeros pequeños cruceros a vapor fueron construidos para la Royal Navy británica con el HMS Mercury lanzado en 1878. Alemania tomó la delantera en el diseño de pequeños cruceros en la década de 1890, construyendo una clase de cruceros rápidos, la clase Gazelle, copiada por otra Tierra naciones Seleccione la clase de Crucero Ligero que desea volver a poner en servicio. 

Las clases de barco obsoleto se retirarán automáticamente al museo, aún podrán ser designadas durante 5 años después de ser retiradas. Una vez que se hayan retirado por completo, podemos volver a poner en servicio las clases antiguas de barcos, consumiendo una ranura de la Lista de clases de barcos readmitidos.  La puesta en servicio de las clases de barcos antiguos requiere una gran cantidad de personal para mantener la producción, lo que resulta en un período de enfriamiento de 5 años antes de que podamos retirar esta clase nuevamente . 

Slots: [root.GetMuseumNumber] / [root.GetMuseumMax]

STH_naval_museum.1003.desc_01:
En la clasificación de naves espaciales, una nave exploradora es un tipo de nave espacial diseñada para explorar regiones desconocidas del espacio. El barco Charybdis de la NASA del siglo XXI fue clasificado como un 'barco explorador'. Seleccione la clase Explorer que desea volver a poner en servicio. 

Las clases de barcos obsoletos se retirarán automáticamente al museo, aún podrán designarse durante 5 años después de retirarse. Una vez que se hayan retirado por completo, podemos volver a poner en servicio las clases antiguas de barcos, consumiendo una ranura de la Lista de clases de barcos readmitidos.  La puesta en servicio de las clases de barcos antiguos requiere una gran cantidad de personal para mantener la producción, lo que resulta en un período de enfriamiento de 5 años antes de que podamos retirar esta clase nuevamente . 

Slots: [root.GetMuseumNumber] / [root.GetMuseumMax]

STH_naval_museum.1004.desc_01:
El surgimiento de la clase Multi-Mission Vessel ha reflejado la naturaleza evolutiva del poder marítimo en el siglo XXI en la Tierra. El Dr. Gareth Evans informa sobre cómo las embarcaciones de alta tecnología, capaces de asumir diversos roles, se estaban convirtiendo en el pilar de las armadas del siglo XXI. A lo largo de la historia, los Buques Multi-Misión abordaron la naturaleza evolutiva de la guerra naval y luego interestelar, enfrentando también desafíos como el contrabando y la piratería. Seleccione la clase de Buque Multi-Misión que desea Comisionar. 

Las clases de barco obsoleto serán retiradas automáticamente al museo, aún podrán ser designadas por 5 años después de ser retiradas. Una vez que se hayan retirado por completo, podemos volver a poner en servicio las clases antiguas de barcos, consumiendo una ranura de la Lista de clases de barcos readmitidos.  La puesta en servicio de las clases de barcos antiguos requiere una gran cantidad de personal para mantener la producción, lo que resulta en un período de enfriamiento de 5 años antes de que podamos retirar esta clase nuevamente .

STH_naval_museum.1005.desc_01:
Tomando el nombre de la clase de los antiguos buques de guerra navales, los cruceros pesados son un tipo de nave estelar diseñada para tener un largo alcance y una alta velocidad, siendo sus objetivos otros cruceros y buques más pequeños. El último crucero pesado antiguo que existe es el USS Salem, ahora un barco museo cuidadosamente conservado en Quincy, Massachusetts. Encargado en 1949, fue el último crucero pesado de la Tierra en entrar en servicio y el único que aún existe. Fue dado de baja en 1959, después de servir en el Atlántico y el Mediterráneo. Por favor, seleccione la clase de Crucero Pesado que desea volver a poner en servicio.

Las clases de naves obsoletas serán retirados automáticamente en el museo, seguirán siendo diseñables durante 5 años después de ser retirados. Una vez que se hayan retirado por completo, podemos volver a poner en servicio viejas clases de barcos, consumiendo un espacio de la lista de clases de barcos puestos en servicio. Recomisionar viejas clases de barcos requiere una gran cantidad de personal para mantener la producción, lo que resulta en un período de enfriamiento de 5 años antes de que podamos retirar esta clase de nuevo:

STH_naval_museum.1006.desc_01:
Los cruceros avanzados son más capaces y aerodinámicos que un crucero pesado normal. Este diseño es confiable y versátil, y todavía está en servicio activo junto con barcos mucho más nuevos. Seleccione la clase de crucero avanzado que desea volver a poner en servicio.

STH_naval_museum.1007.a: Vuelva a poner en marcha la Clase Embajador
STH_naval_museum.1007.desc_01:
Espacio de la última frontera. En la excelente tradición de la Flota Estelar, el Exploration Cruiser está destinado a explorar mundos nuevos y extraños, a buscar nueva vida y nuevas civilizaciones, para ir audazmente a donde nadie ha ido antes. Seleccione la clase Exploration Cruiser que desea comisionar. 

Las clases de barcos obsoletos serán retiradas automáticamente al museo, aún podrán ser designadas por 5 años después de ser retiradas. Una vez que se hayan retirado por completo, podemos volver a poner en servicio las clases antiguas de barcos, consumiendo una ranura de la Lista de clases de barcos readmitidos.  La puesta en servicio de las clases de barcos antiguos requiere una gran cantidad de personal para mantener la producción, lo que resulta en un período de enfriamiento de 5 años antes de que podamos retirar esta clase nuevamente .

STH_naval_museum.1008.a: Vuelva a poner en marcha la Clase Desafío
STH_naval_museum.1008.desc_01:
El Heavy Escort cuyo propósito principal es acompañar y proteger a otros buques. Son ideales para pequeñas formaciones tácticas y ataques de caballería en territorios enemigos: 'Cañón a la derecha de ellos, Cañón a la izquierda de ellos, Cañón delante de ellos Volley'd y truenos; Asaltados con disparos y proyectiles, Audazmente cabalgaron y bien, En las fauces de la Muerte, En la boca del Infierno Rodeó a los seiscientos. Un viejo poema de la Tierra. La función de escolta pesada es similar a la de los cruceros ligeros, sin embargo, son naves estelares mucho más avanzadas y versátiles. Relativamente dominados por su tamaño, pequeños pero resistentes son como avispones enojados, que pueden derribar naves espaciales mucho más grandes pero menos maniobrables. Seleccione la clase de escolta pesada que desea comisionar. 

Las clases de barcos obsoletos se retirarán automáticamente al museo, aún podrán designarse durante 5 años después de ser retirados. Una vez que se hayan retirado por completo, podemos volver a poner en servicio las clases antiguas de barcos, consumiendo una ranura de la Lista de clases de barcos readmitidos.  La puesta en servicio de las clases de barcos antiguos requiere una gran cantidad de personal para mantener la producción, lo que resulta en un período de enfriamiento de 5 años antes de que podamos retirar esta clase nuevamente .

STH_naval_museum.1009.desc_01:
The Troop Carrier's primary purpose is to ensure that marines, precious cargo and other valuable items make it to their destination. The Transports function is similar to Light Cruisers, but with heavier shielding and hulls, but fewer weapons. Please select the Carrier class you wish to Commission.

Obsolete ship classes will automatically be retired into the museum, they will still be designable for 5 years after being retired. Once they have been fully retired, we can recommission old ship classes again, consuming one slot of the Recommissioned Ship Classes Roster. Recommissioning old ship classes requires a huge amount of personnel to maintain production, resulting in a 5 year cooldown period before we are able to retire this class again.

STH_naval_museum.3000.desc: The Federation member world ships are an example of Federation membership pulling together to combine ideas and designs and these ships combine the best of Starfleet and the member worlds principles.
STH_naval_museum.3000.name: Starfleet Historical Society
STH_naval_museum.3010.desc: Federation Scouts a fast nimble ships designed to operate at the fringes of Starfleet territory. Once these ship designs have served their time they can be retired.
STH_naval_museum.3010.name: Starfleet Historical Society
STH_naval_museum.3020.name: Starfleet Historical Society
STH_naval_museum.4000.name: wo 'noH' angweD
STH_naval_museum.5000.desc: Every fleet reaches a point where operationally maintaining it becomes unproductive, especially as new vessels are commissioned. \n\nMothballing a fleet allows you to power down and put the vessel into storage within drydock, ready to be activated at a later date or converted into a museum ship. Mothballed fleets cannot be used until they are reactived, but have a significantly lower upkeep compared with active ships.
STH_naval_museum.5000.name: Fleet Mothballing Operations
STH_naval_museum.5001.a.tooltip: This fleet will move to the naval museum to begin the process of going into mothball.
STH_naval_museum.5001.desc: [This.GetName] has reached the Starfleet Naval Museum and the crews have disembarked and been reassigned, over the next few weeks and months Starfleet Corp. of Engineers will begin removing systems and packing away items onboard the ships.
STH_naval_museum.5001.name: Fleet Ready for Mothballing
STH_naval_museum.5003.desc: Placing a mothballed fleet back into service takes place here, there could be many reasons to reactive a fleet, such as defense or an offensive campaign. Reactivated Fleets will take some time to get back up to operational standards, so make sure you plan ahead. \n\nDev Note: Make sure you rename the fleet you plan to reactive to help identify it from the list.
STH_naval_museum.5003.name: Reactivate a Mothballed Fleet
STH_naval_museum.5004.a.tooltip: The restored fleet will take some time to become fully operational and until that time will operate under normal parameters.
STH_naval_museum.5004.desc: Our engineers have confirmed that [This.GetName] is now operational, albeit operating with a skeleton crew and with a number of systems still undergoing maintenance. It will take a little while for the ships to get up to full speed.
STH_naval_museum.5004.name: [This.GetName] Now Active
STH_naval_museum.5010.desc:
A mothballed fleet can be converted into a museum fleet and dispatched into the orbit of one of our colonies. A museum fleet can provide the colony with a big boost in popularity, as history fans flock in to view a piece of Starfleet history. 

Once the fleet is converted into a museum it can no longer serve as an active part of fleet operations. n
Dev Note: Make sure you rename the fleet you plan to reactive to help identify it from the list.
STH_naval_museum.5010.name: Convert to a Museum
STH_naval_museum.5011.desc: Now that a fleet has been selected to be converted into a museum fleet, we must decide which of our colonies should accept this honour.
STH_naval_museum.5011.name: Museum Fleet
STH_naval_museum.5012.desc: The Fleet museum above [This.GetName] is now open to all who wish to come and experience some of Starfleet's finest vessels preserved for all to visit and marvel at.
STH_naval_museum.5012.name: Museum Open to the Public
STH_naval_museum.5030.desc: All noble vessels must come to an end, and whilst a ship cannot make the glorious to Sto-Vo-Kor, it can still serve a purpose whilst mothballed. \n\nMothballing a fleet allows you to power down and put the vessel into storage within drydock, ready to be activated at a later date or converted into a war museum ship. Mothballed fleets cannot be used until they are reactivated, but have significantly lower upkeep compared with active ships.
STH_naval_museum.5030.name: Fleet Mothballing Operations
STH_naval_museum.5033.desc: The call of the warrior has taken place and you want to place a mothballed fleet back into service, there could be many reasons to reactive a fleet, such as defense or an offensive campaign. Reactivated Fleets will take some time to get back up to operational standards, so make sure you plan ahead. \n\nDev Note: Make sure you rename the fleet you plan to reactive to help identify it from the list.
STH_naval_museum.5033.name: Reactivate a Mothballed Fleet

STH_nemesis.1.a: Denied! Return them to the dark!
STH_nemesis.1.desc: [Root.GetRulerTitle], after years of sulking in their dark pits on that pitiful rock of a planet Remus, we have been approached by a group of Remans represented by an individual who only refers to himself as the 'Viceroy'. They are requesting equal rights within the [Root.GetName] and the same status as the ordinary Romulan citizen. Granting them these rights would appease the Reman population, but would cause uproar across the Romulan Empire and massive discontent within our Romulan population, who would never approve of being considered equal to these Reman veruul! \n\nOur military leaders have also reminded us that this would also hit our Dilithium production and rid us of valuable foot soldiers.
STH_nemesis.1.name: Reman Discontent
STH_nemesis.10.desc: The Romulan military was far too powerful for the small band of attack ships that the Remans were able to produce and they were quickly overwhelmed and destroyed. With the destruction of their small rebellion the Reman's returned to the shadows and dark tunnels within the near endless mines on Remus, defeated but unrepentant in their quest to emerge from under the wings of the Romulan raptor.
STH_nemesis.10.name: Reman forces destroyed
STH_nemesis.100.desc: [Root.GetRulerTitle] we have concerning news, those foul beasts from Remus have turned their backs on us and have dared to declare war against us in a bid to gain their independence. We have mobilized our fleets and will crush these foul beasts and return them to that black rock from which they came.
STH_nemesis.100.name: Reman's battle for indepance
STH_nemesis.101.desc: The Reman's have declared war on their former masters the Romulans in a quest for freedom. The Galaxy will soon see who is the stronger side.
STH_nemesis.101.name: Remans declare war!
STH_nemesis.11.desc: It seems a small rebellion that started on the planet Remus and spread to the skies above Romulus was crushed by the Romulan military. It is unclear what provoked the small band of Reman soldiers to attack the superior Romulan forces, but whilst their bravery was not in question they stood little chance against the might of the Romulan Navy.
STH_nemesis.11.name: Reman forces destroyed
STH_nemesis.2.desc: [Root.GetRulerTitle], news is escaping the Romulan Star Empire, it appears the long oppressed Reman people made a bold move to speak out against their living conditions and requested more freedom from their Romulan overlords. \n\nHowever as expected these freedoms were quickly rejected. The Romulan people rarely afford equality on those they consider beneath them.
STH_nemesis.2.name: Reman equal status rejected
STH_nemesis.20.a: What is the meaning of this intrusion.
STH_nemesis.20.b: What do those wretched creatures want?
STH_nemesis.20.c: Prepare the Senate for the [Root.GetRulerTitle]'s arrival.
STH_nemesis.20.d: Enough is enough, the Reman's must die.
STH_nemesis.20.desc: My [Root.GetRulerTitle], please let me introduce yourself, I am Senator Tal'Aura and I approach you now to ask for an audience to finally put the question on the Reman situation to an end once and for all. The discontent amongst the Reman population on Remus continues to grow whilst the output from the Dilithium mines continues to shrink. I have the backing of a number of high ranking Senators who now wish for this concern to be removed for good. \n\nWe await your presence on the homeworld to conclude this discussion.
STH_nemesis.20.name: The Reman Question
STH_nemesis.21.a: We have detected a small fleet heading this way.
STH_nemesis.21.desc: With the decision to overrule and disregard the request from Senator Tal'Aura and the high ranking officials to discuss the plight of the Reman's in the Romulan senate, all that remains is to dispatch a fleet of the Empire's most powerful warbirds to put the Reman's back in their place once and for all.
STH_nemesis.21.name: Time to end the Reman threat
STH_nemesis.23.a: What is the meaning of this treachery.
STH_nemesis.23.desc: You may not know or recognize me Romulan, I am Shinzon of Remus, a discarded plot that found a home amongst the discarded and abandoned deep within the Dilithium mines of Remus. For too long have my brethren and I suffered at the cruel whip of our Romulan masters... but no longer. We are desperate and desperation brings courage. Prepare to meet the Scimitar as we turn it's mighty weapons on the jewel that is Romulus.
STH_nemesis.23.name: Message from Shinzon of Remus
STH_nemesis.24.a: Wipe out the remaining Remans on Remus.
STH_nemesis.24.b: There has been enough death for one day.
STH_nemesis.24.desc:
With a mighty explosion the mighty Reman Warbird Scimitar is destroyed taking with it the last hope the Reman's have, along with their leadership and beloved Shinzon. 

The last outstanding questions is what should become of the remaining Reman population on the planet's surface. Purging them from the surface will serve as a stark reminder to all subjects of the empire that rebellions will not be tolerated, as well as a better understanding of how to build a replacement for the Scimitar - however it is unlikely the galactic community will take kindly to this action. 

Sparing them will boost our Dilithium output and demonstrate we are not nothing like the mindless Klingon's who would surely not hesitate to wipe out their foes.
STH_nemesis.24.name: Shinzon defeated
STH_nemesis.25.desc: Our tactical reports indicate that their has been a final showdown between the Romulan and Reman people. An uprising by an individual that our intelligence services can only identify as a Reman named Shinzon was put down by the Romulan fleet. What followed appears to have been a massacre, with Romulan forces wiping out the remaining Reman's dwelling on their homeworld.
STH_nemesis.25.name: Reman population slaughtered
STH_nemesis.26.desc: Our tactical reports indicate that their has been a final showdown between the Romulan and Reman people. An uprising by an individual that our intelligence services can only identify as a Reman named Shinzon was put down by the Romulan fleet. In an unexpected measure of mercy it appears the remaining Reman lives were spared any further punishment. Although many commentators suspect working conditions within the mines will become a lot less pleasant, if that is even possible!
STH_nemesis.26.name: Reman population slaughtered
STH_nemesis.3.desc: [Root.GetRulerTitle], in surprising news it appears the long oppressed Reman people made a bold move to speak out against their living conditions and requested more freedom from their Romulan overlords. \n\nIn a shock move it appears the Romulan senate granted the Reman's this request and have made them equal within the Star Empire.
STH_nemesis.3.name: Reman equal status granted
STH_nemesis.30.a: Send them back to that black rock they came from.
STH_nemesis.30.b: Allow the Reman's their freedom in society.
STH_nemesis.30.c: Set the Remans free to fend for themselves.
STH_nemesis.30.desc: As organised by Senator Tal'Aura and a number of high ranking military and political figures, the case for further integration of the Reman population into the Romulan empire was put forward. With assistance from the Reman forces the group believe the Star Empire could be in a position to challenge any empire in the Alpha or beta quadrant within the next few months, but only if a full alliance was struck with the Reman's and granting their representative, a mysterious figure only referred to as Shinzon, a high ranking position in the Senate.
STH_nemesis.30.name: The Reman Problem
STH_nemesis.31.a: What is the meaning of this...
STH_nemesis.31.desc: Before any further discussion or argument could take place, a number of Senators and military leaders stand and make their way from the Romulan Senate room, clearly believing that the discussion was indeed over. Further indication that the Reman's were inferior and without the wit or wisdom to last within the complex and cut throat world of Romulan politics. \n\nJust as [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName] was about to move onto to discuss a proposal from the delegation from Celus III, a small device left behind by one of the departed opens, sending a cascade of greenish energy particles over the room.
STH_nemesis.31.name: The Reman Problem
STH_nemesis.32.desc: Confused, the Praetor calls for security to bring the group back to the Senate room, but it is too late, the entire Senate begin to decay from inside, and the Praetor himself topples to the ground and crumbles into dust.
STH_nemesis.32.name: The Reman's Revenge
STH_nemesis.35.b: What do we know about the new regime?
STH_nemesis.35.desc: We have been approached by our contacts within the Romulan Star Empire. It appears there has been a Coup d'état within the Star Empire beginning with the elimination of the previous Praetor and most of former senate. The new government appears to be closely linked to the Reman's, an elusive caste within the Romulan empire. The spokesperson for the new regime, a high ranking Senator named Tal'Aura has specifically requested we send a delegation to meet with the new leadership, a Romulan named Shinzon, and he has asked that we send [nemesis_leader.GetName] in exchange the Romulans promise a lucrative future.
STH_nemesis.35.name: Urgent Message from Romulus
STH_nemesis.36.a: Continue to wait for a sign...
STH_nemesis.36.desc: On reaching the designated coordinates [nemesis_leader.GetName] and their crew have received no communications from Praetor Shinzon or from the Romulan government. The senior officers look out across the empty void, nervously awaiting a message from their hosts, keenly aware they are a long way from the protection of [Root.GetName].
STH_nemesis.36.name: Waiting in the Raptors Nest
STH_nemesis.37.desc: Just as the crew were about to abandon the wait and return to [Root.capital_scope.GetName] a massive vessel decloaked in front of [nemesis_fleet.GetName]. The massive ship could only be described as a predator, armed to the teeth and built with a single purpose and protected by the most perfect cloak that [nemesis_leader.GetName] had ever seen. \n\nThe beep beep of the incoming hail rang out in sharp contrast to the overwhelming silence that was on the bridge.
STH_nemesis.37.name: Out of the Shadows
STH_nemesis.38.a: You are doing this for the Remans?
STH_nemesis.38.b: How many Romulans died for your freedom?
STH_nemesis.38.c: What is a human doing leading the Romulan Empire?
STH_nemesis.38.desc: Greetings [nemesis_leader.GetName], I hope you'll forgive the darkness. ...We're not comfortable in the light, please let me introduce myself ... I am Shinzon of Remus, proud leader of the Reman people. You may ask what we seek & I say unity, Captain! ...Tearing down the walls between us to recognise that we are one. ...I'm talking of the thing that makes us the same. ...Peace. We want peace. ...Right now, you're thinking this all sounds too good to be true? But you're also thinking that the chance of peace is too promising to ignore. ...Am I right?
STH_nemesis.38.e: What is it you want from me?
STH_nemesis.38.f: What do you want between our two worlds?
STH_nemesis.38.g: We must think about all that has been said here.
STH_nemesis.38.name: Nemesis
STH_nemesis.38.ufp: How did you end up on Remus?
STH_nemesis.39.desc_01:
As Picard and the away team returned to their ship orbiting Romulus concerned and confused by the what had transpired in front of Shinzon. Was this individual really a clone of the Jean-Luc Picard and what does it mean for the future of the Federation-Romulan relations? Just as the question was being discussed Picard disappeared in the buzz of a Reman transporter. 

Aboard the Scimitar in the darkness the Captain found himself chained to an operating chair. Shinzon stepped forward and addresses Picard, 'I need your blood Jean-Luc, to repair the damage to my cells. I'm afraid you won't survive to witness the victory of the echo over the voice and once I have your future... We will no longer bow before anyone as slaves. Not the Romulans and not your mighty Federation.' 

'We ...are a race bred for war. And conquest'.
STH_nemesis.39.desc_02:
As [nemesis_leader.GetName] and the away team returned to their ship orbiting Romulus a number of concerns were raised. Was this individual really a clone of a Starfleet captain and does that make him more or less trustworthy? Suddenly [nemesis_leader.GetName] vanished in the hum of a Reman transporter. 

Aboard the Scimitar in the darkness [nemesis_leader.GetName] found themselves secured in the ships dark brig. Shinzon stepped forward and addressed the commanding officer, explaining that he is an imperfect clone and requires [Root.GetName] hand over one of their scientists in exchange for [nemesis_leader.GetName]'s life. The scientist will serve as a prisoner whose expertise will be used to keep the young human alive. 

With a knowing grin Shinzon declares, we will no longer bow before anyone as slaves. Not the Romulans and not your mighty Federation. 

'We ...are a race bred for war. And conquest'.
STH_nemesis.39.desc_03:
As [nemesis_leader.GetName] and the away team returned to their ship orbiting Romulus a number of concerns were raised. Was this individual really a clone of a human captain and could a fragile human really survive the harsh conditions on Remus? Suddenly [nemesis_leader.GetName] vanished in the hum of a Reman transporter. 

Aboard the Scimitar in the darkness [nemesis_leader.GetName] found themselves secured within a dark brig. Shinzon stepped forward and addressed the commanding officer, explaining that he is an imperfect clone and requires [Root.GetName] hand over one of their scientists in exchange for [nemesis_leader.GetName]'s life. The scientist will serve as a prisoner whose expertise will be used to keep the young human alive. 

With a knowing grin Shinzon declares, we will no longer bow before anyone as slaves. Not the Romulans, the Federation and certainly not the mighty [Root.GetName]. 

'We ...are a race bred for war. And conquest'.
STH_nemesis.39.name: Nemesis
STH_nemesis.4.a: Return the Remans to their proper place.
STH_nemesis.4.b: We do not submit to terrorists.
STH_nemesis.4.desc: In the early hours of the morning a small branch of the Romulan military, potentially supported by the Tal Shiar and an organised civilian group destroyed a Reman habitat on Remus in protest against the decision to grant the Reman's equal rights within the Empire. The group calling themselves 'The true Romulan way' have demanded that the Remans are once again forced into slavery and any notion of granting them freedom is removed. \n\nOur loyal military commanders believe they have enough evidence to destroy this faction or we can bow to their demands.
STH_nemesis.4.name: Reman population attacked
STH_nemesis.40.desc: The First Officer knew that [nemesis_leader.GetName] would not want to put their crew at risk to save their life, but also that they would lay down their life for any member of their crew. The chief of security presents an ambitious plan to rescue the [nemesis_leader.GetName], but it would be risky and risk their chances of a surprise escape. Alternatively making a surprise bid for freedom and heading towards the nearest system free from the communications blackout set up by the Scimitar would greatly increase the chances of escaping the clutches of Shinzon and his ship.
STH_nemesis.40.name: Nemesis
STH_nemesis.41.desc: In a daring rescue the crew of [nemesis_ship.GetName] were able to trace the transporter signal and locate and rescue [nemesis_leader.GetName] from the clutches of Shinzon and the Reman guards holding them captive. The extraction team were able to cause a small amount of damage to the Scimitar before escaping via a number of light attack ships. With [nemesis_leader.GetName] safely back onboard the [nemesis_ship.GetName] is free to rendezvous with the fleet at [nemesis_battle.GetName].
STH_nemesis.41.name: Nemesis - Leader rescued!
STH_nemesis.42.desc: Unfortunately for the the crew of [nemesis_ship.GetName] the rescue of [nemesis_leader.GetName] was a failure and the Remans were able to fend off the attempt killing [nemesis_leader.GetName] in the process. With their leader dead First Officer [nemesis_leader_1.GetName] is forced to step into the command chair and attempt to escape to rendezvous with the fleet at [nemesis_battle.GetName].
STH_nemesis.42.name: Nemesis - Rescue Failed
STH_nemesis.45.desc: Failing to reach the rendezvous point in time to mount a significant defense from the Scimitar left [nemesis_ship.GetName] vulnerable to the powerful Thalaron weapons harboured onboard Shinzon's vessel. With the ship destroyed Shinzon and his Reman forces are free attack the surrounding systems and create havoc.
STH_nemesis.45.name: Ship Fails to escape
STH_nemesis.46.desc_01: The failure of [nemesis_ship.GetName] to reach freedom before it was attacked and destroyed by the Scimitar and its destruction has caused concern across [Root.GetName]. Fleets have been put on alert for any further sign of the Scimitar leaving Romulan space. \n\nIt appears the Romulan military has taken this moment to rise up and reclaim the Empire in the name of the Romulan people. It is clear this is now an internal matter for the Romulan's to deal with.
STH_nemesis.46.desc_02: Our long range scanners are picking up the destruction following what appears to have been a brief battle involving one of our neighbours and a mysterious vessel claiming to be of Reman design. Whatever has occurred seems to now be an internal matter for the Romulans to deal with.
STH_nemesis.46.name: Reman Scimitar Victorious
STH_nemesis.47.desc: As [nemesis_ship.GetName] approaches the coordinates at [nemesis_battle.GetName] the astrometrical display flickers ominously, seeking clarity on the situation the communications officer explains that they are approaching a naturally occuring sensor blind spot and that should clear shortly. Spotting the danger quickly [nemesis_leader.GetName] calls out for Red Alert, shortly before torpedoes hammer into the ships hull. The Scimitar has tracked them and having had the chance to call for backup whilst the CO was rescued bears down on the [Root.GetName] vessels weapons raised and ready to destroy all that stand in its way.
STH_nemesis.47.name: Nemesis - Battle at [nemesis_battle.GetName]
STH_nemesis.48.desc: As [nemesis_ship.GetName] approaches the coordinates at [nemesis_battle.GetName] the astrometrical display flickers ominously, seeking clarity on the situation the communications officer explains that they are approaching a naturally occuring sensor blind spot and that should clear shortly. Spotting the danger quickly [nemesis_leader.GetName] calls out for Red Alert, shortly before torpedoes hammer into the ships hull. The fast escape means that the Scimitar was unable to call for any further Reman backup, but even on its own the deadly Reman Warbird poses a deadly adversary.
STH_nemesis.48.name: Nemesis - Battle at [nemesis_battle.GetName]
STH_nemesis.49.a: We owe you a bottle of Romulan Ale.
STH_nemesis.49.desc: As the battle was beginning to intensify, a new fleet decloaked ready to join the battle. A young romulan Subcommander named Donatra hailed the [Root.GetName] fleet. \n\nIt seems the Romulan's see this as an internal matter and Donatra and her fleet have been sent to see that the matter remains discreet.
STH_nemesis.49.name: Romulan Backup arrives
STH_nemesis.5.desc: It appears all is not well within the Romulan Empire since the decision to grant the Reman people equal status within the Empire. One of our covert operations teams provided us with evidence that the Romulan military had to put down a small uprising against the Reman people. \n\nIt looks like the Romulan government are now back in control of the situation, but this is clearly a situation they would prefer to have avoided.
STH_nemesis.5.name: Dissatisfaction amongst the population of Romulus
STH_nemesis.50.desc: The battle was fierce and many lives were lost as the fleets clashed, but finally the Scimitar lay defeated and with its Thalaron core going critical exploded into countless pieces of debris bringing an end to the rebellion and the threat that Shinzon posed to the galaxy.
STH_nemesis.50.name: Shinzon defeated
STH_nemesis.51.desc_01: With the crews from the [Root.GetName] and the Valdore both surviving the battle the ships are now free to return to their respective homeworlds knowing that they are now safe from any further attack from Shinzon. It was easy for the Commanding Officer to understand that circumstances could shape the lives of individuals very differently and the plight of the Reman people very much remained a matter of concern.
STH_nemesis.51.desc_04: In a complete victory for Shinzon and his Reman conspirators left the ships from the Romulan Star Empire and [Root.GetName] defeated, unable to match the power of the mighty Thalaron weapons. The [Root.GetName] must now wait and see what the fallout from the battle will be.
STH_nemesis.51.name: Shinzon defeated
STH_nemesis.52.desc: Hello [Root.GetRulerTitle] let me get straight to the point, we Reman's are a proud people and it is hard for us to approach you with this request. We have fought and died to obtain our freedom and the right to live our lives free from the Romulan whip and the darkness that is Remus. All we ask is for what you take for granted, the opportunity to rule ourselves. Please add your voice to ours and maybe Romulus will finally listen.
STH_nemesis.52.name: Incoming hail from Remus
STH_nemesis.53.a: Welcome to the Reman Free States.
STH_nemesis.53.desc: After years of struggle against the Romulans, the people of Remus can finally call themselves free, having won their independence from the Star Empire. They were granted the planet [reman_new_world.GetName] as part of a historic treaty with the Romulan [romulan_star_empire.GetRulerTitle] [romulan_star_empire.GetRulerName]. Time will now see how the Reman's fare in the future.
STH_nemesis.53.name: The Reman Free State
STH_nemesis.54.desc: Despite international pressure from multiple states the Romulan Empire has rejected the proposals to set the Reman's on Remus free. With all their options now exhausted the Remans must now resign themselves to remain second class citizens within the Empire.
STH_nemesis.54.name: Reman freedom rejected
STH_nemesis.55.desc: After many years of struggle the Reman's have forced their freedom from their Romulan oppressors and have been granted a small world of their own with the freedom to choose their own destiny.
STH_nemesis.55.name: Reman freedom granted
STH_nemesis.56.desc: Terrible news! Despite putting up a valiant fight against Shinzon [nemesis_leader.GetName] and their ship were defeated in battle. \n\nShinzon is now free to continue his reign of terror.
STH_nemesis.56.name: [nemesis_leader.GetName] killed in action
STH_nemesis.6.desc: It looks like the decision to grant the Reman's equal rights within the Romulan Star Empire was short lived according to our spy network. Following a brief uprising against the Remans on the planet Romulus the Praetor has once again rescinded the Reman's liberty.
STH_nemesis.6.name: Reman returned to slavery
STH_nemesis.7.a: Demand?! Suffer the wrath of a Romulan disruptor.
STH_nemesis.7.b: Return to work Reman or your people will suffer.
STH_nemesis.7.desc: [Root.GetRulerTitle] \n\nI am Viceroy [reman_rebel_leader.GetName] representing the Reman's of Remus, for too long we have been condemned to the dark and forced to dig the Dilithium that powers the empire, whilst we are treated little better than the machines that toil those deep mines. No longer will we be subject to your oppression, we demand the same rights as any Romulan or you will know our fury.
STH_nemesis.7.name: A demand from the Reman Free State
STH_nemesis.8.a: The talk of freedom is over.
STH_nemesis.8.desc_01: The meer threat of an attack from our mighty warbirds has sent those cowardly vermin back to that black hell hole of a planet where they belong and they will think twice before returning to the senate with such ludicrous ideas.
STH_nemesis.8.desc_02: As expected those vile cowards crumbled at the thought of a confrontation with our military, we shall insist that their working conditions are tightened over the next few months to ensure they are reminded that there are consequences for threatening the Empire.
STH_nemesis.8.name: Reman's back down
STH_nemesis.9.desc: The audacity of those Reman traitors knows no bounds, it appears they have managed to secure a number of attack ships from various sources and have launched an attack on our capital system. Whilst it appears only a very small section of the Reman population has dared to join in on this attack, we must not let this threat to our authority go unpunished.
STH_nemesis.9.name: Under attack from Reman Forces
STH_notifications.100.desc: Un número de científicos a través de la [Root.GetName] creen que han detectado signos de un cambio en la línea de tiempo, después de una ola de radiación cronitón que se detecta barrido a través del cuadrante a velocidades superluminal. \n\nAhora nadie ha podido identificar los supuestos cambios.
STH_notifications.100.name: Anomalía Temporal
STH_notifications.101.desc: Un número de científicos a través de la [Root.GetName] creen que han detectado signos de un cambio en la línea de tiempo, después de una ola de radiación cronitón que se detecta barrido a través del cuadrante a velocidades superluminal. \n\nAun así, nadie ha sido capaz de identificar los supuestos cambios, aunque dada la amplitud de la onda de radiación cronitónica, se espera que los cambios sean sustanciales.
STH_notifications.101.name: Anomalía Temporal
STH_observation.100.desc: [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName], los esfuerzos diplomáticos de [Root.GetName] han logrado convencer a mi gente de participar más plenamente en los asuntos galácticos. \n\nAunque habíamos renunciado a la explotación de la tecnología warp para enfocar nuestras energías hacia los asuntos en [FromFrom.GetName], las tecnologías subyacentes nos son conocidas desde hace algún tiempo. Creo firmemente que nuestros pueblos pueden prosperar juntos, en paz.
STH_observation.100.name: Éxito diplomático en [FromFrom.GetName]
STH_observation.110.d: [missing_scientist.GetHeSheCap] will remain in prison for [missing_scientist.GetHisHer] breaking the laws of our people.
STH_observation.150.car.desc: Los agentes de la Orden de Obsidiana en [From.GetName] han localizado algunos sitios estratégicos alrededor del planeta que serían el lugar ideal para puestos militares y fortalezas. Algunos materiales de contrabando sutil podrían ser utilizados para construir puestos militares ocultos en todo el planeta. \n\nAlgunas redes de túneles bien ubicadas debajo de las ciudades más grandes también deberían proporcionar seguridad a nuestros operativos y convencer al gobierno [From.Owner.GetName] de que tener un refugio seguro contra cualquier actividad rebelde.
STH_observation.150.fed.a: Alto gasto. Conoce a estos manifestantes cara a cara.
STH_observation.150.fed.desc: Muchos ciudadanos del movimiento de independencia en [From.GetName] han comenzado protestas contra lo que ven como la intromisión de la Federación en sus asuntos. Ejercer más presión diplomática y ofrecer concesiones al gobierno de [From.GetName] puede aplacar estas protestas. \n\n Alternativamente, podríamos dejar que las protestas sigan su curso y, en cambio, centrar nuestros esfuerzos en otros lugares.
STH_observation.150.fer.a: ¿Tarifas en los puertos espaciales? ¡Compre sus puertos espaciales!
STH_observation.150.fer.b: Bien, ¡pero no una tira más de lo requerido!
