STH_policy.21.desc: Our Intelligence services and Admirals have prepared a dossier covering the [From.GetName] - [FromFrom.GetName] war, with details on the origins, casualties and potential outcomes of the ongoing war. \n\nGiving us a number of potential options.
STH_policy.21.name: Condemn a War
STH_policy.22.desc: The [From.GetName] has issued a condemnation of our conflict with the [FromFrom.GetName] to the wider galactic community, calling on us to negotiate a peace settlement with the enemy.
STH_policy.22.name: War Condemnation
STH_policy.23.desc: The [From.GetName] has issued a condemnation of the [FromFrom.GetName] and their conflict against us to the wider galactic community, calling for a swift end to the [FromFrom.GetSpeciesAdj] aggression.
STH_policy.23.name: War Support
STH_policy.25.desc: Our diplomatic service has identified a number of Empires that harbor a negative view of us, one that has deteriorated steadily over the last couple of years. \n\nThe recommendation is to invite them to a conference and attempt to smooth over any ill feeling between our two nations.
STH_policy.25.name: Improve Relations
STH_policy.26.desc: We have received a communique from [Prev.GetName] they wish to invite us to a diplomatic conference in a bid to repair the deteriorating relationship between our two empires.
STH_policy.26.name: Inbound Communications
STH_policy.27.desc: Dear [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName] we acknowledge that the relationship between our worlds has reached a low point and we welcome this chance to mend this relationship. The planet [poorRelationPlanet.GetName] would make an ideal location to host this conference.
STH_policy.27.name: Improve Relations
STH_policy.28.desc: [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName], we see no need to change the current relationship between our worlds and suggest you focus your attention elsewhere, should you run the risk of further provoking our annoyance.
STH_policy.28.name: Improve Relations
STH_policy.30.desc: [Root.GetRulerTitle] I am pleased to report that the conference between our world and [poorRelationEvent.GetName] has been a success, with both sides making positive strides towards improving communications and trust. \n\nWhilst there maybe some ways to go before the relationship can be considered friendly, this is a significant step forward in our relations.
STH_policy.30.name: Update from Conference
STH_policy.31.desc: [Root.GetRulerTitle] I regret to inform you that the conference between our world and [poorRelationEvent.GetName] has been not been the success we had hoped for. The gulf between our two worlds could not be bridged during the Conference and has potentially widened further.
STH_policy.31.name: Update from Conference
STH_policy.33.desc: Over the years we have managed to create rivalries and whilst we have nothing to fear from these empires our diplomatic services are urging us to put some of this bad blood behind us and look to put an end to these ongoing rivalries.
STH_policy.33.name: Rivals
STH_policy.34.desc: We have been approached by [Prev.GetName] who are looking to call us into a diplomatic conference to discuss our ongoing rivalry in a bid to put aside our past differences and build a better future.
STH_policy.34.name: Incoming Communications
STH_policy.35.b: We no longer support this meeting.
STH_policy.35.desc: Greetings [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName], we are willing to accept your offer to meet and discuss our ongoing hostilities, we agree to your location on [poorRivalPlanet.GetName] and will send an envoy to meet with your delegation.
STH_policy.35.name: Diplomatic Response
STH_policy.36.desc:
Attention [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName], we have heard your plea to meet with us to end our ongoing rivalry. 

Consider your offer refused, we see no need to change the nature of our relationship. 

Be pleased that we do not worsen the relationship.
STH_policy.36.name: Diplomatic Rejection
STH_policy.38.desc: Sirs, we can report back that our diplomatic team has just exited from their meeting with our rivals with positive news, both sides have agreed to put aside past issues and look to forge a better future.
STH_policy.38.name: Diplomatic Conference Concluded
STH_policy.39.desc: Our rivals have stormed away from the conference in fury it appears the conference has not ended well and the relationship has gotten worse!
STH_policy.39.name: Diplomatic Conference Concluded
STH_policy.40.desc: When war spreads out across the Quadrant it threatens every world, the lives of countless, the safety of our allies, and the security of our own borders. Whatever reason most compels us to act we have a chance to use our power and influence to attempt to end an ongoing war.
STH_policy.40.name: Peacekeeping
STH_policy.41.a: We have no choice but to accept.
STH_policy.41.desc: [Root.GetRulerTitle], your war is unjust and your continued assaults against the innocent must not continue, we urge you to cease this campaign, should you not take this friendly advice you may find that you are forced to under the firepower of our starships.
STH_policy.41.name: End the War!
STH_policy.42.b: They must pay for what they have done.
STH_policy.42.desc: The ongoing [Root.From.GetName] cannot continue, the cost of the war is too high and spreads out across worlds outside of your own. We have convinced [Root.From.attacker.GetName] to lay down their weapons, we now ask that you put your weapons aside and end this conflict.
STH_policy.42.name: End the War!
STH_policy.43.b: We join the war against them.
STH_policy.43.desc: Unfortunately, [From.Attacker.GetName] has not responded well to our demand to call a ceasefire and the bloodshed is set to continue.
STH_policy.43.name: No Ceasefire
STH_policy.44.desc: Despite calls for a ceasefire in the ongoing conflict, it appears that neither side is prepared to stand down and negotiate a ceasefire. \n\nThe War rages on.
STH_policy.44.name: No Ceasefire
STH_policy.45.desc: Thanks to the timely intervention by an outside party the ongoing [From.GetName] has arrived at a ceasefire, with all parties withdrawing their forces back behind their borders and powering down their weapons. \n\nOnly time will tell how long this will last, but many are calling for the opportunity to give peace a chance.
STH_policy.45.name: Ceasefire Comes into Effect
STH_policy.46.desc: A ceasefire has been called to end the current hostilities between on worlds involved in the [FromFrom.GetName] conflict. All vessels must return to their own territory immediately.
STH_policy.46.name: Ceasefire in place
STH_policy.47.desc: Despite the best efforts of our ambassadors and envoys, they were unable to persuade [From.Defender.GetName] to abandon the war, it is regretable that peace was so close, yet could not be achieved on this occassion.
STH_policy.47.name: War will continue
STH_policy.48.desc: Following a failed attempt to end the war, the negotiating party has joined the war on the side of the defenders. \n\nThis could prove to turn the war in their favour, only time will tell.
STH_policy.48.name: The War continues
STH_policy.50.desc: Our ongoing [playerWar.GetName] continues to cost lives, ships, planets and resources with no end in sight. \n\nOur diplomats urge us to reach out to our neighbours and request they join us in defeating our enemies.
STH_policy.50.name: War Support
STH_policy.51.a: We shall join you in battle.
STH_policy.51.desc: Greetings [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName], we are willing to accept your offer to meet and discuss our ongoing hostilities, we agree to your location on [poorRivalPlanet.GetName] and will send an envoy to meet with your delegation.
STH_policy.51.name: War Support
STH_policy.52.desc: We regret to inform you that we cannot aid you in your ongoing wars, this is a battle you must win on your own. To drag us into your struggles is to put our own borders at risk, and that is not something we can afford right now.
STH_policy.52.name: War Support Rejected
STH_policy.53.desc: We hear your call for aid and the armies of [Root.GetName] will fly to your aid and together we shall be victorious!
STH_policy.53.name: War Support Accepted
STH_policy.54.desc: It appears our war enemy has decided that they cannot defeat us by themselves and have enlisted the help of [fromfromfrom.GetName] in a bid to defeat our forces.
STH_policy.54.name: New Party joins the war!
STH_policy.60.desc: We should establish a truce with one of our powerful and dangerous neighbours to secure our borders and prevent an attack. By reaching out to their envoys we can ask if they will be open to peace talks.
STH_policy.60.name: Establish a Truce
STH_policy.61.desc: We have been approached by [Prev.GetName] who are looking to call us into a diplomatic peace conference to discuss establishing a truce between our empires and the start of peace between our worlds.
STH_policy.61.name: Establish a Truce
STH_policy.62.desc: Greetings [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName], we are here to inform you that the people of [from.GetName] have no interest in your offer of peace and a truce. We would welcome the chance to challenge you in combat should it come to this.
STH_policy.62.name: Truce has been rejected
STH_policy.63.desc: Greetings [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName], we have listened to your offer and are open to further discussions on the topic of a short-term truce. We have opened our borders and will host an envoy at our capital to further discuss this proposal.
STH_policy.63.name: Open to peace talks
STH_policy.64.desc: We have listened to your representatives and we agree that peace is in the best interest of both our worlds at this point in time and we will sign a short-term truce and ensure that no hostilities erupt between our forces whilst this truce is in effect.
STH_policy.64.name: Truce Established
STH_policy.65.desc: We have listened to your representatives and we do not agree that peace is in the best interest of both our worlds at this point in time. This proposal is too restrictive and we are not inclined to offer you assurances that our forces will not defeat you in combat.
STH_policy.65.name: Truce Rejected
STH_policy.80.desc: Greetings, this is where you can manage the ongoing expeditions and multi-year missions that are currently in progress.
STH_policy.80.name: Expedition Menu
STH_policy.81.a: How many fleets are currently involved in the expedition?
STH_policy.81.desc: Greetings sir, please find a summary of the ongoing activity within our expedition to the Lembatta Cluster, allowing you to add extra ships, remove ships or recall and end the expedition.
STH_policy.81.h: Send another fleet to the Cluster.
STH_policy.81.name: Manage Lembatta Cluster Expedition
STH_policy.90.desc: This taskforce is dedicated to checking for Assimilated Pops Groups within our borders, use this screen to run an ad hoc check across our population.
STH_policy.90.name: Assimilated Pops
STH_policy.999.deassim.tooltip: Check and manage any assimilated pops within your empire.
STH_policy.999.desc: Welcome to the Advance Diplomacy Menu, from here you can interact with your agencies, and special contacts and engage in the galactic policy options from a single screen. \n\nAs you establish new agencies or special vassals they will be added to this menu.
STH_policy.999.name: Advance Diplomacy Menu
STH_prodigy.10.desc: [prodigy_target_country.GetName] we are the Vau N'Akat Order from the world of Solum, a world that returns to take its vengeance and I am the Diviner... Note that we bring you your destruction at the hands of one of your vessels of peace. \n\nPrepare for the revenge of the Vau N'Akat.
STH_prodigy.10.name: The Protostar arrives
STH_prodigy.100.desc: Our monitoring stations have picked up the incoming signal of what can only be a vessel equipped with a Proto-Drive, which in this case can only be the missing USS Protostar. \n\nA local task force fleet was sent to intercept the ship, but we have lost contact, we should send another fleet to investigate.
STH_prodigy.100.name: Proto-Warp Signal Detected
STH_prodigy.101.desc: On board the Protostar Dal and the ETH frantically attempt to shut down the Proto-drive before the ship reaches its destination, knowing that the ship now carries unwanted intruders, looking to attack [Root.GetName].
STH_prodigy.101.name: Supernova
STH_prodigy.102.desc_01:
Rok-Tahk and Murf head toward the Protostar's computer core in an attempt to scrap the core and with it the Living Construct, as the pair reach the core the terrifying sight of a Drednok robotic warrior stands between the pair and their destination. 

 The warrior launches an attack on the pair, Rok-Tahk uses her impressive strength to hold off the Drednok's first attack, but even the Brikarian strength is no match for the war machine. 

Just as Rok-Tahk is about to be defeated, Murf untangles himself and launches themselves at the Drednok with lighting fast speed and agility, driving the Drednok back and away from the computer core... allowing Rok-Tahk to reach the panel, the damage to the control panel was significant. Turning Rok-Tahk joins Murf in facing their robotic foe.
STH_prodigy.102.desc_02: Rok-Tahk heads toward the Protostar's computer core in an attempt to scrap the core and with it the Living Construct, as she reaches the core the terrifying sight of a Drednok robotic warrior stands between Rok and the computer core control panel. \n\n The warrior launches an attack on Rok-Tahk using her impressive strength to hold off the Drednok's first attack just about keeps Rok safe, but the more powerful Drednok begins to overwhelm even the powerful Brikarian, Rok-Tahk knows that the only way to reach the Computer Core is to defeat the robotic warrior.
STH_prodigy.102.name: Supernova
STH_prodigy.103.desc: The Drednok lies crumpled and defeated, overpowered by its attack, allowing Rok-Tahk to reach the computer core's main panel, reaching it they can unlock the mechanism, unfortunately, the Drednok has already been able to prevent the core from being overloaded and destroyed... but at least they can do no further damage.
STH_prodigy.103.name: Drednok Defeated
STH_prodigy.104.desc: Drednok stands over the still form of Rok-Tahk, the gentle Brikarian no longer moving or able to take any further action to help her friends. \n\nDrednok moves towards the computer core ready to unleash the Living Construct on the [Root.GetName] Fleet.
STH_prodigy.105.desc: Jankom Pog knows that the Living Construct needs to open communication channels with the other vessels to spread its virus throughout the approaching fleet, if Jankom Pog can disable the Protostar's communication array then the damage could be limited and the number of reinforcements could be reduced significantly.
STH_prodigy.105.name: Supernova
STH_prodigy.106.desc: Jankom Pog clenches his fist in celebration, only an engineer with Jankom Pog's unique skills could have accomplished the task, as the Tellarite looks over the 'disabled' communication relay, the strength of the Vau N'Akat's forces should be depleted as a result.
STH_prodigy.106.name: Jankom Pog to the rescue
STH_prodigy.107.desc: Jankom Pog slams his fist against the comms panel, the signal has already been sent and the message has left the Protostar, despite the Tellarite's efforts Jankom Pog was moments too slow.
STH_prodigy.107.name: Jankom Pog Defeated
STH_prodigy.108.desc: On the Bridge of the USS Protostar Zero hears the thoughts in Dal's head before he vocalizes it, the only way to prevent the Construct Virus from spreading is to destroy the Protostar and everyone onboard. An idea springs into Zero's mind, the Protostar need not be destroyed if Zero can trigger an electromagnetic surge to shut down all of the Protostar's systems in a single event.
STH_prodigy.108.name: Supernova
STH_prodigy.109.desc: Zero manages to generate a large EMP burst using the local sun's solar flares, as the lights onboard the Protostar go dark, rapidly followed by all the consoles throughout the ship. \n\nThe Protostar is out of the fight, but the Construct Virus can cause no further harm to nearby fleet.
STH_prodigy.109.name: Zero Successful
STH_prodigy.11.desc: As the Vau N'Akat's plans begin to come into effect as they declare war against us, the Construct's virus begins to take effect on a number of our ships, with their systems quickly succumbing and the vessels joining the Vau N'Akat's fleets against us.
STH_prodigy.11.name: Fleets compromised by the Construct
STH_prodigy.110.desc: The attempt to disable the Protostar was unsuccessful, the EMP burst was just not powerful enough to overcome the Protostar's shielding. \n\nWith no choice left Dal sets the auto-destruct and orders a full evacuation of their ship and home.
STH_prodigy.110.name: Zero Unsuccessful.
STH_prodigy.111.desc: Gwyn can sense her father's presence onboard the Protostar as he stalks Dal on the bridge, Gwyn leaps forward and uses her heirloom to deflect the Diviner's attack against her friend. The two warriors begin to battle, Gwyn fully aware that should she lose nothing will be able to stop the Diviner and the Order's plans for revenge.
STH_prodigy.111.name: Supernova
STH_prodigy.112.desc: After a furious battle between father and his progeny, the Diviner lies wounded and defeated. Reaching out to Gwyn the Diviner lays down his weapon and surrenders .
STH_prodigy.112.name: The Diviner Defeated
STH_prodigy.113.desc: Gwyn collapses to the floor, mortally wounded by her father blade, as the Diviner cries out in shock and horror, unable to contemplate what he has done to his progeny.
STH_prodigy.113.name: The Diviner Victorious
STH_prodigy.114.desc_01: [prodigy_hero_leader.GetName] wakes up onboard the Protostar's sickbay, unsure of how long they have been unconscious they head towards the ship's bridge. \n\nOn reaching the command deck [prodigy_hero_leader.GetName] notices a Vau N'Akat general lying prone on the floor, but crucially an open communication back to Solum urging the rest of the fleet to join the battle and open up a wormhole right into the heart of [Root.GetName] space. This could be the last chance to appeal to the Vau N'Akat to abandon their plans for revenge.
STH_prodigy.114.desc_02:
[prodigy_hero_leader.GetName] wakes up onboard the Protostar's sickbay, unsure of how long they have been unconscious they head towards the ship's bridge. 

On reaching the command deck [prodigy_hero_leader.GetName] they are attacked by the Diviner, who appeared to have been trying to open a Wormhole back to Solum via the same anomaly in the Proto-Drive that started this adventure. 

If the Captain can reach the panel they might be able to convince the Vau N'Akat that [Root.GetName] does not threaten them.
STH_prodigy.114.name: Supernova
STH_prodigy.115.desc: Using all their skills the Starfleet officer manages to reach the Vau N'Akat Order, using an impassioned speech, the Vau N'Akat Order agree that now is not the time for further escalation of the ongoing conflict and agree that for now, they will leave the Diviner to his plans.
STH_prodigy.115.name: Escalation Avoided
STH_prodigy.116.desc: The situation only escalates, the Order members are not convinced by [Root.GetName]'s efforts and their resolve to join the Diviner in Battle has only hardened.
STH_prodigy.116.name: Situation Escalates
STH_prodigy.117.desc_01: With all the efforts completed the fate of the mission now lands with Dal, his crew have done all they can to buy Dal enough time to get the Protostar and its dangerous Construct Virus away from the fleet and away from causing more harm. \n\nWith the ship in a critical state it will take all of Dal's skills and abilities as a pilot and captain!
STH_prodigy.117.desc_02: With all the efforts completed the fate of the mission now lands with Dal, his crew have done all they can to buy Dal enough time to get the Protostar and its dangerous Construct Virus away from the fleet and away from causing more harm. \n\nWith the ship in a critical state it will take all of Dal's skills and abilities as a pilot and captain, but first Dal must get passed the Diviner who now stands in his way.
STH_prodigy.117.name: Supernova Part II
STH_prodigy.118.a: The fleets are on a collision course.
STH_prodigy.118.desc_01: Dal manages to get the Protostar clear of the oncoming fleet and prevents any further [Root.GetName] ships from being consumed by the Construct Virus. Just as all seems ok, the original fleet that was investigating the Proto-Drive signal appears from behind a nearby asteroid, their crews have no control over their ships they have been fully taken over by the Construct Virus and belong to the Diviner and his minions!
STH_prodigy.118.desc_02: The Protostar is simply too damaged and disabled for Dal to successfully move it and the scout ship now drifts dangerously in the path of the oncoming fleet still broadcasting the deadly virus. \n\nTo make matters worse the original fleet which was investigating the Proto-Drive signal appears from behind a nearby asteroid, their crews have no control over their ships they have been fully taken over by the Construct Virus and belong to the Diviner and his minions!
STH_prodigy.118.name: Supernova Part II
STH_prodigy.119.desc_01: As Dal guides the Protostar clear of the battle zone, knowing that at least the outbound communications from the Protostar will not be able to impact any of the inbound ships. \n\nThe compromised fleet begins to power up its weapons ready to target the new fleet.
STH_prodigy.119.desc_02: As the Protostar begins its final auto-destruct sequence the young crew climbs into the escape pods, at least the destruction of their ship will help safeguard the lives on the ships heading towards them. \n\nThe compromised fleet begins to power up its weapons ready to target the new fleet.
STH_prodigy.119.desc_03: As the Protostar limps away from the battle site, its comm channel still broadcasting the Living Constructs dangerous virus, Dal can only hope that the damage will be limited by the distance the Protostar has now traveled from the battlezone.\n\n On the viewscreen he can see the compromised fleet begins to power up its weapons ready to target the new fleet.
STH_prodigy.119.desc_04: Failure to prevent the communications from leaving the Protostar risks infecting further ships with the Constructs Virus, but the untimely destruction of the Protostar might at the very least restrict the impact of the Living Construct. \n\nThe compromised fleet begins to power up its weapons ready to target the new fleet.
STH_prodigy.119.name: Supernova Part II
STH_prodigy.12.desc: As the Construct's effect makes its way through the fleet, our engineers propose a solution that involves cutting all intra-ship communications. to halt the spread of the Construct virus, at the short-term effect of our battle effectiveness.
STH_prodigy.12.name: Ships fall prey to the Construct
STH_prodigy.121.desc: With the battle over Dal watches as the remaining [Root.GetName] ships begin their search for the Protostar in a bid to try and salvage the ship and rescue what remains of his young crew.
STH_prodigy.121.name: The Protostar is safe
STH_prodigy.122.desc: With the battle over Dal watches as the remaining [Root.GetName] ships begin their search for the young crew of the Protostar in their escape pods.
STH_prodigy.122.name: The Protostar has been destroyed
STH_prodigy.123.desc: As the debris floats over the site of the battle, A message echoes across subspace, Solum will be avenged for the crimes the [Root.GetName] will commit against our people... the Diviner may have been defeated but the Vau N'Akat Order has been awakened and are coming for you.
STH_prodigy.123.name: Harsh Words from Solum
STH_prodigy.124.desc: As feared following the attack by the Diviner, the Vau N'Akat are declaring war on us for a perceived attack that they believe we would have committed in the future. \n\nThe tunnel that was opened by the Protostar has been stabilised and now offers a route between our worlds and a way fot them to attack us.
STH_prodigy.124.name: War with the Vau N'Akat
STH_prodigy.125.desc: As the preverbal dust settles in the aftermath of the Diviners attack on our fleets, a message comes in from Solum, it appears our Captain's message of peace has, for now, reached calmer ears. The Order appears to be content to leave us in peace for the moment.
STH_prodigy.125.name: A message of Peace from Solum
STH_prodigy.127.desc: Greetings all, I am [tempLeader] and I wanted to thank you for your efforts in resolving the Protostar crisis and time to reward these young leaders with a pace amongst our ranks.
STH_prodigy.127.name: Prodigy
STH_prodigy.2.desc: We have received an emergency message from the USS Protostar, which had to abandon the test of its proto-warp jump. It appears an instability in the protostar drive almost generated a temporal incursion, resulting in an emergency shutdown. \n\nResults of the test have been fed back to Starfleet who will roll out the fix to all ships of this class within the fleet.
STH_prodigy.2.name: USS Protostar Survives Proto-Warp jump
STH_prodigy.20.desc: On the remote slave-mining asteroid of Tars Lamora, the cruel overlord known only as The Diviner holds sway over his prisoners and slaves, using them to dig for a treasure greater than minerals. \n\nAmongst all the slaves one young adventurer, named Dal R'El makes an amazing discovery... an abandoned Starship.
STH_prodigy.20.name: Lost and Found
STH_prodigy.21.a: If I do this can I go free?
STH_prodigy.21.c: Why are you asking me to take Gwyn?
STH_prodigy.21.d: Go with Gwyn, escape will come later.
STH_prodigy.21.desc: Dal R'El, you stand before your Diviner and his progeny, my Gwyndala - my daughter. We are aware of your find within our mines and I demand that you take my Gwyn to its location. Failure to do as I command will see not only a punishment for yourself, but for your fellow prisoners.
STH_prodigy.21.e: Insist on going alone, it will be easier to escape.
STH_prodigy.21.name: Lost and Found
STH_prodigy.22.a: Agree to meet Zero on the ship.
STH_prodigy.22.desc_01: As Dal and Gwyn make their way down the mine tunnel, a strange voice fills Dal's mind. \n\nDon't be alarmed my name is Zero, I am a Medusan, and on the run from the Diviner. He would use my appearance as a Medusan to punish those who displeased him, I had no choice but to escape.
STH_prodigy.22.desc_02: As Dal makes his way down the mine tunnel, a strange voice fills Dal's mind. \n\nDon't be alarmed my name is Zero, I am a Medusan, and on the run from the Diviner. He would use my appearance as a Medusan to punish those who displeased him, I had no choice but to escape.
STH_prodigy.22.name: Fugitive Zero
STH_prodigy.23.desc_01: Having agreed to meet Zero on the ship, Dal progresses to be confronted by a towering Brikar and a diminutive Tellarite. 'Jankom Pog here's that you are getting out of here, Jankom Pog can help you!' grunts the Tellarite, the towering Brikar makes a low grumbling nose, which sounds like two boulders colliding. \n\nDal laughs, it is clear this escape has taken on a story of its own!
STH_prodigy.23.desc_02: Dal progresses to be confronted by a towering Brikar and a diminutive Tellarite. 'Jankom Pog here's that you are getting out of here, Jankom Pog can help you!' grunts the Tellarite, the towering Brikar makes a low grumbling nose, which sounds like two boulders colliding. \n\nDal laughs, how many more tag-alongs will this escape involve?
STH_prodigy.23.desc_03: Dal progresses to be confronted by a towering Brikar and a diminutive Tellarite. 'Jankom Pog here's that you are getting out of here, Jankom Pog can help you!' grunts the Tellarite, the towering Brikar makes a low grumbling nose, which sounds like two boulders colliding. \n\nDal laughs, it is clear this escape has taken on a story of its own!
STH_prodigy.23.name: Rok-Tahk and Jankom Pog
STH_prodigy.24.desc:
Dal, Jankom & the Brikar, who identify themselves as Rok-Tahk, the moment that they enter the bridge of the strange alien vessel, look around in excitement as interior lighting and control panels come to life. Dal looks around at his new crew, this is our chance to escape he yells. 

The crew looks to Gwyn, the young Vau N'Akat jumps to one of the consoles and powers up the ship's engines. 

It seems like we are in this together.
STH_prodigy.24.name: Lost and Found Part 2
STH_prodigy.25.desc: Dal and those he gathered reach the bridge of the vessel, which lights up to greet them, the chance to escape before them Dal looks to Gwyn, imploring her to join him. \n\nThe young Vau N'Akat warrior slowly shakes her head and taps her communicator.... 'Father I have the ship'.
STH_prodigy.25.name: Lost and Found Part 2
STH_prodigy.26.desc: Dal and Gwyn arrive on the bridge of the Protostar, which lights up to greet them, the chance to escape before them the pair look at each other, Gwyn looks out the viewport towards the approaching waves of Drednok warriors and calls to Dal, 'I have to escape my Father, I can no longer be apart of his cruel plans'.
STH_prodigy.26.name: Lost and Found Part 2
STH_prodigy.3.desc: Our long-range scans have picked up the debris from a small starship that appears to have exited Proto-Warp under an emergency, the ship can only be the USS Protostar, which was testing the new drive system. \n\nThere are no signs of any survivors from the ship and all remaining black box information has been passed back to Starfleet Engineering to attempt to prevent a repeat of this tragedy.
STH_prodigy.3.name: USS Protostar Destroyed
STH_prodigy.30.desc: The Protostar saws clear of Tars Lamora, its powerful phasers clearing the Diviner's battle units from its path, as the crew cheered and celebrated, the Diviner looked on with an angry look. \n\nBack on the Protostar Dal takes the centre seat and the adventure begins.
STH_prodigy.30.name: Prodigy Begins!
STH_prodigy.31.desc: The Protostar attempts to clear the surface of Tars Lamora, but the Diviner's forces swarm the ship, working their way through the hull and into the vital systems of the ship. \n\nAs Dal orders the ship to engage its powerful engineers a crucial system fails and a fatal cascade begins which ends with a catastrophic explosion that engulfs and destroys the Protostar, ending its journey early.
STH_prodigy.31.name: Prodigy Ends!
STH_prodigy.4.desc: In concerning news, the USS Protostar has vanished while undertaking its first Proto-warp jump, our long-range scans cannot find any evidence of debris or signs from the Protostar's small crew. \n\nWe can however see signs of a small temporal anomaly that appeared briefly following the disappearance of the ship.
STH_prodigy.4.name: USS Protostar vanishes in Protowarp Flight
STH_prodigy.40.desc: Six young heroes, former detainees at a mining colony overseen by the mysterious Diviner, discoverered the derelict Starfleet ship, the USS Protostar, hidden within the asteroid. This inexperienced crew launches a harrowing getaway aboard the starship. Together the escapees learn that the ship came from a far-off alliance of peaceful galactic explorers known as the Federation. They resolve to set a course for Starfleet, but the Diviner is hot on their trails. Their journey takes them on a wild adventure where they encounter strange new life and civilizations, learn about Starfleet's ideals, and hope to find a better future for themselves on the far side of the galaxy.
STH_prodigy.40.name: Prodigy Begins!
STH_prodigy.41.desc: The crew hear a strange noise emanating from the back of the bridge, on investigating they find a small, but friendly gelatinous being who calls himself Murf!
STH_prodigy.41.name: Murf
STH_prodigy.45.desc: As the Protostar exits a period of Proto-Warp with the drive requiring an extended period of cooldown, the crew finds themselves with some downtime. Exploring the ship Dal finds a holodeck and an interesting holodeck program named the Kobayashi Maru scenario - a test of a captains leadership skills.
STH_prodigy.45.name: Kobayashi
STH_prodigy.46.desc: The holographic simulation loads up placing Dal and the centre of a Starship bridge, with an incoming hail from a freighter in danger. Arriving at the scene the freighter is quickly attacked and destroyed by a fleet of Warbirds ending the simulation with the dreaded phrase.
STH_prodigy.46.name: Kobayashi
STH_prodigy.47.b: A no win scenario must be the point!
STH_prodigy.47.desc: As the number of attempts racks up, Dal's frustration levels only increase, at what point does this simulation become an unwinnable scenario? Is this what the simulations is trying to tell him or is the thing broken?
STH_prodigy.47.name: Kobayashi, 42 attempts later!
STH_prodigy.5.desc: Emerging from a wormhole, torn through space by the Protostar's malfunctioning drive core, the powerless ship comes crashing down on the distant planet of Solum in the Delta Quadrant.
STH_prodigy.5.name: Protostar crashes on Solum
STH_prodigy.50.a: The vines begin to follow the Runaway.
STH_prodigy.50.desc: Landing on a planet named Murder World, the crew begins to investigate their surroundings and collect samples from the local fauna. Gwyn who has been questioning her decision to abandon her father and side with the crew of the Protostar takes a small land vehicle named the 'Runaway' for a drive to clear her mind.
STH_prodigy.50.name: Dream Catcher
STH_prodigy.51.desc: Gwyn picks up a series of messages from the rest of the crew, it appears the whole planet is one giant superorganism, racing back to the crew she finds Dal and Murf bound by thick red vines. Grabbing her heirloom and rushing forward, Gwyn is stopped by a familiar voice... her father! \n\nThe Diviner calls out concerned for Gwyn's safety, 'come with me my Gwyndala' he pleads...
STH_prodigy.51.name: Dream Catcher
STH_prodigy.52.desc: Emergency Training Hologram's log, It has been almost a week since the cadets on this ship departed to explore the planet, and now I can no longer read their life signs, I can only assume they have been killed in the line of duty. With no hope of rescue, I am powering down the Protostar I can only hope one day this ship is found on this remote world and I can tell the story of these brave cadets.
STH_prodigy.52.name: Crew of the USS Protostar Lost
STH_prodigy.53.desc: Father... it cannot be you, what are you doing here? Shaking her head to try and clear the image of her father from her mind, the Diviner would never be this concerned about her wellbeing, unless he has changed?
STH_prodigy.53.name: Dream Catcher
STH_prodigy.54.desc: With the image of the Diviner cleared from her mind, Gwyn races towards Dal and the rest of the crew struggling for their lives. Using her heirloom to free the others, the crew race back to the Protostar and escape from this nightmare world.
STH_prodigy.54.name: Dream Catcher
STH_prodigy.55.desc: With Gwyn's loyalty to the crew of the Protostar secured and the crew safely rescued from Murder World, the Protostar once again sets course for Federation space.
STH_prodigy.55.name: Dream Catcher
STH_prodigy.6.a: What is this Construct you mention?
STH_prodigy.6.b: What did we ever do to you?
STH_prodigy.6.desc: Starfleet, I am [vau_nakat_order_leader.GetName] of the Vau N'Akat, we see that you have returned to finish off what you started so many years ago! The civil war that you started nearly destroyed our world, but we prepared for your return and to inflict our revenge upon you and your [prodigy_target_country.GetName]. \n\nCaptain [prodigy_hero_leader.GetName] you and your crew are now our prisoners and your vessel will become home to our 'Construct' a powerful weapon that will turn your ships against you as we wipe your people from the galaxy.
STH_prodigy.6.name: Message from the Vau N'Akat
STH_prodigy.60.desc: All is not well on the USS Protostar, as the crew discovers the living Construct hidden within the computer core, Rok-Tahk and Murf were exploring the lower levels of the ship when they came across the Construct, a weapon hidden within the ship providing their salvation that could destroy those they were seeking to flee to. \n\nThe thoughts and concerns of the crew were interrupted by the incoming presence of a massive Tachyon storm.
STH_prodigy.60.name: Time Amok
STH_prodigy.62.a: Coordination across time zones is the only option.
STH_prodigy.63.desc: As each member of the crew does their part it is left to Rok-Tahk whose section moves the slowest to complete the build. As time moves very slowly in this phase, a lonely Rok tries to reconnect to her absent friends by bidding their empty bunks good night when she goes to sleep each 'night' and hugging a small toy representation of Murf. \n\nRok-Tahk channels her inner cadet to complete the build, knowing that once complete she has a long time to wait alone by herself.
STH_prodigy.63.name: Time Amok
STH_prodigy.64.desc: An anti-matter warp core breach leaves very little in its wake, but this pales in comparison to the detention of a Proto-core drive, which atomises the ship and all aboard in the blink of an eye.
STH_prodigy.64.name: Protostar Destroyed
STH_prodigy.65.desc_01: Once the device is connected to the proto-drive, the timeline, and the rest of the crew, are restored, alive and well, and recalling they had each died in their time zones. Rok and the ETH meet up with the others gathered on the bridge and with Rok wiping away tears of happiness receives a group hug from the others, who are proud of her achievements. The hologram looks on with a smile, but then becomes saddened, which Gwyn notices and realizes why, going over to her, she quietly asks the hologram how long the young girl spent alone, to which she utters the phrase, 'too long'. As Rok tells the others about her self-education, Gwyn remarks that Rok may not just have found her friends, but also her calling in life.
STH_prodigy.65.desc_02: Once the device is connected to the proto-drive, the timeline, and the rest of the crew, are restored, alive and well, and recalling they had each died in their time zones. Rok and the ETH meet up with the others gathered on the bridge and with Rok wiping away tears of happiness receives a group hug from the others, who are proud of her achievements. The hologram looks on with a smile, but then becomes saddened, which Dal notices and realizes why, going over to her, she quietly asks the hologram how long the young girl spent alone, to which she utters the phrase, 'too long'. As Rok tells the others about her self-education, Dal remarks that Rok may not just have found her friends, but also her calling in life.
STH_prodigy.65.name: Time Amok
STH_prodigy.66.desc: After a few days of Murf acting strangely the young crew finds the young alien cocooned in a shell of his own making, clearly undergoing some form of metamorphosis! \n\nWith no one knowing the outcome this could pose a risk to the ship.
STH_prodigy.66.name: Murf's Metamurfphosis
STH_prodigy.67.desc: Murf emerges from the cocoon transformed into a new form, a very capable new life and very grateful and loyal to those who cared for him.
STH_prodigy.67.name: Murf's Metamurfphosis
STH_prodigy.7.desc: Captain [prodigy_hero_leader.GetName] and their First Officer take their chance to escape knowing that should the USS Protostar return to Earth it would bring about the destruction of [Root.GetName]. \n\nThe duo make it to the ship and forcing their way to a command panel successfully send the Protostar through the still open portal to parts unknown and with it the dangerous weapon hidden onboard.
STH_prodigy.7.name: Protostar Escapes to far off Asteroid
STH_prodigy.70.desc: The Protostar answers a distress call from Planet 0042692 and the crew are greeted by representatives of the colony of New Enda-Prize. A colony who were visited by the crew of the Enterprise and grew up idolising the ways of 'Those Old Scientists'. \n\nThe colony is suffering from a mysterious plague, from the mysterious Gallows.
STH_prodigy.70.name: All the World's a Stage
STH_prodigy.71.a: Force the shuttle into the Pit.
STH_prodigy.71.name: All the World's a Stage
STH_prodigy.72.desc: The Shuttle begins it's final decent into the cavern, and just as Jankom Pog is about to give up hope, the familiar hum of the transporter rings out as Dal and the Enda-Prizians beam him to safety. \n\nWith the colony safe. Jankom Pog having learned a valuable lesson the crew resumes the journey to Federation space.
STH_prodigy.72.name: All the World's a Stage
STH_prodigy.73.desc: The Shuttle begins it's final descent into the cavern, Jankom Pog looks on and the Shuttlecraft breaks up around him, knowing that once again his sacrifice has saved lives. \n\nThe crew of the Protostar having saved the colony, but lost a member of their family continue their voyage to Federation space.
STH_prodigy.73.name: All the World's a Stage
STH_prodigy.74.desc: Jankom Pog frantically completes the repairs to Galileo's damaged warp core meer seconds before the core goes critical and dumps its toxic warp plasma into the biosphere of the planet. \n\nJankom Pog falls to the floor relieved at having saved the day before the hum of the Protostar's transporter whisks him away and the ship resumes its journey back to Starfleet.
STH_prodigy.74.name: All the World's a Stage
STH_prodigy.75.desc:
Jankom Pog frantically completes the repairs to Galileo's damaged warp core meer seconds before the core goes critical and dumps its toxic warp plasma into the biosphere of the planet. 

Jankom Pog falls to the floor his body shaking from the toxic effects of the warp plasma exposure, knowing the signs of the overdose all too well, the engineer smiles and closes his eyes knowing that at least his sacrifice saved the colony. 

The crew of the Protostar having saved the colony, but lost a member of their family continued their voyage to Federation space.
STH_prodigy.75.name: All the World's a Stage
STH_prodigy.8.desc: Captain [prodigy_hero_leader.GetName] and their First Officer take their chance to escape knowing that should the USS Protostar return to Earth it would bring about the destruction of [Root.GetName]. \n\nThe duo make it back to where the Protostar was being held. but despite their efforts, they could not remotely free the ship, with no choice remaining the Captain sets the auto-destruct and the pair waits for the inevitable destruction of their ship and with it the weapon that could have caused so much impact.
STH_prodigy.8.name: Protostar Explodes
STH_prodigy.80.desc: As the crew debate options to remove the Living Construct from the Protostar, their scanners detect the presence of a large dormant Cuboid ship, which their Training Hologram identifies as a Borg Cube. Excited by the prospect of using the advanced technology of the Borg to defeat the Construct the crew prepare to board the Borg vessel.
STH_prodigy.80.name: Let Sleeping Borg Lie
STH_prodigy.81.desc: The crew wonder through the black corridors, feeling with each step that they are being watched by the rows and rows of sleeping drones. \n\nAs a Medusan Zero begins to hear the voice of the collective calling out seeking to know more about the young individuals who may pose a new threat to the collective.
STH_prodigy.81.name: Let Sleeping Borg Lie
STH_prodigy.82.desc: Zero leads the crew to the Borg Vinculum and brief discussion later it is clear that only Zero would be able to withstand the power of the Collective. As dangerous as it would be Zero needs to go into the mind of the Borg and look for the information they need.
STH_prodigy.82.name: Let Sleeping Borg Lie
STH_prodigy.83.desc: Zero and the crew reach the Protostar as the Cube springs back into life, the Borg Cube reaches out with it's tractor beam, but the Protostar is just too quick and leaps into warp before the Borg can react further... But it looks like the young rookies have alerted the Borg to the Federations possession of Proto-Warp and made Starfleet a target.
STH_prodigy.83.name: Let Sleeping Borg Lie
STH_prodigy.84.desc: Zero and the crew reach the Protostar, but the Borg Cube is already powering up and lashes out with a tractor beam and before the kids can react a dozen Borg Drones beam onto the ship, they say assimilation is painless... they were wrong. \n\nAs the Borg collective add other crew to their collective, Starfleet once again becomes of interest to the Collective.
STH_prodigy.84.name: Let Sleeping Borg Lie
STH_prodigy.85.desc: The lure of the Collective is all-encompassing, the pull of the hive, the embrace of the many... \n\n Resistance is Futile, we are Borg.
STH_prodigy.85.name: The Collective
STH_prodigy.86.desc: We are the Borg, your distinctiveness will join us, become one with us, be part of our hive, our collective, perfection.
STH_prodigy.86.name: The Collective
STH_prodigy.87.desc:
As the crew of the Protostar await for the return of Zero, they receive a communication from the Cube. 

The assimilated form of Zero appears on screen and utters the chilling phrase 'You will be assimilated'. 

As the Borg collective add other crew to their collective, Starfleet once again becomes of interest to the Collective.
STH_prodigy.87.name: Let Sleeping Borg Lie
STH_prodigy.88.desc: Breaking free of the Collective's hold was difficult but Zero manages it, whilst putting the Collective back into their slumber. \n\nThe Borg may not hold the answers to the Construct, but Zero found an inner strength and belief in themselves.
STH_prodigy.88.name: Let Sleeping Borg Lie
STH_prodigy.9.desc: The Vau N'Akat's plan is quickly put into effect, with the Protostar sent back through the wormhole and sent on a direct course for the very centre of [Root.GetName] carrying the Construct program and its ability to remote hijack friendly vessels and turn them against their former masters.
STH_prodigy.9.name: Protostar sent back to [Root.GetName]
STH_prodigy.90.a: We have to plan a rescue.
STH_prodigy.90.name: Last Flight of the Protostar
STH_prodigy.91.desc: The Protostar exits warp over Solum, and scanning the planet picks up the life signs of [prodigy_hero_leader.GetName], it will take as many of the crew as possible to pull of the rescue.
STH_prodigy.91.name: Last Flight of the Protostar
STH_prodigy.92.a: It's time to bring this adventure to an end.
STH_prodigy.92.desc:
The rescue is complete and a battered and bruised [prodigy_hero_leader.GetName] safely recovering in the Protostar's medical bay, Dal and the Protostar are free to return to Starfleet and warn them of the threat the Vau N'Akat bring. 

However, the threat of the Living Construct must be overcome...

Just as the crew are about to celebrate their success, the Intruder Alert alarm signals, a team from Solum has infiltrated the ship and set the Protostar on a course back to [Root.GetName] space.
STH_prodigy.92.name: Last Flight of the Protostar
STH_prodigy.93.a: It's time to bring this adventure to an end.
STH_prodigy.93.desc: The crew looks down at the body of [prodigy_hero_leader.GetName] the mission a complete failure, perhaps if the crew was better placed, or more able, but that doesn't matter now, it is time to return to Starfleet and bring the mission to an end. \n\nJust as the crew are about to calculate their next move, the Intruder Alert alarm signals, a team from Solum has infilatrated the ship and set the Protostar on a course back to [Root.GetName] space.
STH_prodigy.93.name: Last Flight of the Protostar
STH_qpedia.1.b: Can I find articles on specific Empires?
STH_qpedia.1.desc: Ah, so you have seen sense and have come to your favorite omnipotent being, Uncle Q for help?\n\nWell you are in the right place, I know all there is to know about everything, especially the STNH modiverse. Have a look at the list of questions below, the list will be continuously updated and of course feel free to let the Qs know if something is missing.
STH_qpedia.1.e.response:
Well, well... the human desire to have what is not allowed. You may be aware that there are now certain systems that you may want to own, but are unable to build an outpost in them. 

There are now two new types of systems which you cannot own. The 1st type are environmental systems, such as certain black holes, nebulas, or hazard systems, these are designed to split up the map for gameplay reasons. 

The second type called event systems, are added for event purposes and may serve a story purpose at a later stage.
STH_qpedia.1.name: The Q Continuum
STH_qpedia.10.c: Can I talk to the council at any time?
STH_qpedia.10.d: How do I form the Federation?
STH_qpedia.10.desc: I had a bet with my good pal Jean-Luc about how long it would take for a question on his precious Federation of Planets would take to come in .\n\nThe UFP has some of the mods most exciting and oldest mechanics and once you get used to them you'll be Federating the galaxy in no time, just like the Borg... but with a nicer smile!
STH_qpedia.10.name: The Humans
STH_qpedia.100.desc: Perfection. So close yet still so very very far for you. Take it from someone who knows. Now with what knowledge would you wish to improve yourself today?
STH_qpedia.100.e: Transwarp conduit and Transwarp Aperture Hub.
STH_qpedia.100.name: The Borg Collective
STH_qpedia.11.a: What or Where is Starbase One?
STH_qpedia.11.b: What can I do with the Council?
STH_qpedia.11.desc: As of our 3.6 update, you can summon the UFP council to Starbase one for a meeting to discuss key events and help manage the Federation and its member worlds. Some worlds will appreciate the support, while others might become frustrated with the micro-management. To open this mechanic simply click on the UFP emblem next to Starbase one or contact them via the contacts tab.
STH_qpedia.11.name: Starbase One
STH_qpedia.1111.name: Locked out system [From.GetName].
STH_qpedia.1112.desc:
Dev Note: As part of our 4.0 release, we have examined several performance improvements and decided that we need to modify how the decision techs operate. These techs, once researched, enable you to select from multiple specialisations. 

In the 4.0 update, these will now be checked every 3 months, which will cause a small delay between the research being completed and the decision being made. 

If you would like to force the event to run earlier, then run STH_custom.51 from the console.
STH_qpedia.1112.name: Decision Techs
STH_qpedia.12.desc: You may have noticed that an extra pop-up appeared after you formed the Federation, well that formed the council, as well as giving you lots of planets, leaders, and cool new ships.\n\nIf you look over Neptune in the Sol system or one of your gas giants if you are not playing as Earth you will see a new megastructure that resembles Starbase One, as seen in Discovery and Strange New Worlds... click on it, just like you would click on another countries ships to talk to them.
STH_qpedia.12.name: Starbase One
STH_qpedia.13.desc: Now that the Council has assembled you have a number of options to manage your members and run the Federation as you wish.
STH_qpedia.13.name: The Federation Council
STH_qpedia.2.desc:
The Q Continuum is bursting with knowledge and has introduced a new means of helping players play the many unique features of ST:New Horizons. 

The Q-pedia contains the collective knowledge of the Q with handy questions on how certain mechanics work and is worth a peak if you get stuck! 

If you need to open the help section, contact Q from the Contacts tab and there via Adv Diplomacy which now not only allows you to change the starting conditions but also find these helpful articles.
STH_qpedia.2.name: Q-pedia
STH_qpedia.200.a: How do I get out of fluidic space?
STH_qpedia.200.b: How can I avoid purging none fluidic pops?
STH_qpedia.200.c: How to become the crisis as Undine?
STH_qpedia.200.desc: Oh the Undine with their famous motto, 'the weak shall perish.' Such hostile creatures to visitors in their space. But if you are going to keep up with that attitude there might be some useful things for you to know.
STH_qpedia.200.name: The Undine
STH_qpedia.300.a: The Founder, Vorta and Jem'Hadar pops.
STH_qpedia.300.c: What is a Dominion Member World?
STH_qpedia.300.desc: Ah the Dominion, a bunch of shape-shifters who can do cute little form tricks while playing as gods for those species that cannot. How cute. What do you want to know about them?
STH_qpedia.300.name: The Dominion
STH_qpedia.50.desc: Well glory to you and to your tiny house and all that other Klingon nonsense. What do you wish to know more about?
STH_qpedia.50.name: The Klingon Empire
STH_qpedia.70.b: Why can't I impose my ideology?
STH_qpedia.70.desc: War... what is it good for? Well as it turns out, it's good for rather a lot. Whether you want to take some territory, defend yourself, or those many human words you have just don't seem as much fun as firing a torpedo at a helpless hull. Here you can find enough bits of info on war to make Sun Tzu want to write a second book.
STH_qpedia.70.name: War! What is it good for?
STH_qpedia.71.desc: This section of the Q-pedia list the requirement for each Casus Belli in STNH.
STH_qpedia.71.name: How do Casus Belli Work?
STH_qpedia.90.desc: Ah the Romulans, always scheming and plotting something in secrecy aren't you? Well as it turns out I know all of your petty little secrets, so just ask away.
STH_qpedia.90.name: The Romulan Star Empire
STH_redemption.10.desc: Ambassador K'Ehleyr from the Federation has confirmed that the Federation is sending one of their warriors to meet with us to discuss the Rite of Succession, and whilst they proclaim they will not directly interfere they will hear our ask.
STH_redemption.10.name: Rite of Succession
STH_redemption.11.desc: Our failure to attend our own conference has no doubt led to confusion within our own ranks and frustration within the decks of the Starfleet vessel that was waiting to rendezvous with us.
STH_redemption.11.name: Rite of Succession
STH_redemption.12.desc: The Klingons have never been known as a patient people and our failure to attend the agreed meeting has nothing to improve relations and will have harmed our efforts to build a peaceful quadrant.
STH_redemption.12.name: Rite of Succession
STH_redemption.13.desc: It appears the Klingons have failed to attend the arranged meeting with the Federation Flagship, a baffling and cowardly act from the Klingons, however one can never fathom the actions of such a foolish species. It does however. put an end to our schemes in this area.
STH_redemption.13.name: Klingons Fail to Attend
STH_redemption.14.desc: The Federation has demonstrated a lack of honour by failing to attend the agreed meeting, perhaps we have miss-judged them.
STH_redemption.14.name: Rite of Succession
STH_redemption.15.desc: The Klingons have never been known as a patient people and our failure to attend the agreed meeting has nothing to improve relations and will have harmed our efforts to build a peaceful quadrant.
STH_redemption.15.name: Rite of Succession
STH_redemption.16.desc: It appears the Federation have failed to attend the arranged meeting with the Klingon Flagship, a rather baffling and cowardly act from Starfleet, which puts an end to our schemes in this area.
STH_redemption.16.name: Federation fail to Attend
STH_redemption.17.a: Let the Rite of Succession begin.
STH_redemption.17.desc: Commander [klingon_redemption_1_leader.GetName] has successfully delivered Chancellor [klingon_leader.GetName] to their meeting with the Federation Flagship, now begins a hazardous situation for the Klingon Empire as a new ruler must be selected to guide them Empire through this new era.
STH_redemption.17.name: Redemption
STH_redemption.18.a: You had better be correct Sela.
STH_redemption.18.desc: As I predicted Praetor the Klingon Empire has run to his Federation betters to seek help with aiding in the selection of a new Chancellor, now is our chance to not only place a puppet on the throne of the Empire but destroy their relationship with Starfleet and plunge them into chaos.
STH_redemption.18.name: Redemption
STH_redemption.19.a: Let the Rite of Succession begin.
STH_redemption.19.desc: Captain [federation_redemption_1_leader.GetName] and the [federation_redemption_1_ship.GetName] have arrived at the coordinates, as a Klingon Battlecruiser the IKS Vor'cha, pride of the Klingon fleet decloaks in front of them carrying the Klingon Chancellor eager to meet and discuss the situation ahead.
STH_redemption.19.name: Redemption
STH_redemption.2.b: We have no time for this.
STH_redemption.2.desc: Greetings Praetor, I bring you a grave warning from the Tal Shiar.. we have been working with one of your up-and-coming Imperial Commanders who is requesting an audience with the council to discuss a matter which could impact the very security of the Empire.
STH_redemption.2.name: Message from the Tal Shiar
STH_redemption.20.desc:
Greetings [federation_redemption_1_leader.GetName] you bring great honor to yourself in joining us here today and I offer you my thanks. 

The great Klingon Empire stands on a D'k tahg edge... my time remaining is limited, forces cough have been working against me from the shadows and I cannot let these agents rule the Empire when I am gone. 

There are two leading candidates to replace me, both have strong support... cough Gowron commands the loyalty of those who wish to see the status quo remain, whilst Duras has the support of the ambitious forces within the Empire cough I know you cannot directly interfere, but you can help me put the right person in the Chancellor's seat, help me find out who has killed me.
STH_redemption.20.e: We must think on this decision.
STH_redemption.20.name: The Klingon Succession Crisis
STH_redemption.21.a: We suspect foul play, do you?
STH_redemption.21.b: Why did you reach out to us?
STH_redemption.21.desc: Greetings [Root.GetRulerName] from the United Federation of Planets, I am [ufp_leader.GetName] and I am here to answer the call for assistance from the Klingon High Council. \n\nAs requested my Chief Medical Officer has looked at your medical report and can confirm that the Veridium Six poisoning will soon take your life, our understanding is that two rival leaders stand ready to challenge for the leadership of the Empire. Gowron offers a stable option, whilst Duras desires power above all else. One of these is likely to have taken their desire to lead the empire to treacherous levels, we will attempt to judge who the culprit is.
STH_redemption.21.name: The Klingon Succession Crisis
STH_redemption.22.desc: Praetor, Command Sela reporting in, our spies from the House of Duras have confirmed that the meeting between the Starfleet Captain and the Klingon Chancellor has taken place, we have the perfect opportunity to sabotage this meeting and sow confusion and tension within both parties, laying the way for our puppets in the House of Duras to take control of the Empire.
STH_redemption.22.name: The Klingon Succession Crisis
STH_redemption.24.desc: As the meeting comes to an end, the deafening sound of an explosion rings out across the meeting room. Security Officers from Starfleet and KDF dive to protect their leaders and sirens bellow while the emergency lights flicker hopelessly. \n\nIt is clear that some force does not want this meeting to have taken place.
STH_redemption.24.name: Explosion Rocks the Chamber!
STH_redemption.25.a: Off the record... what do you think?
STH_redemption.25.desc: Captain, this is K'Ehleyr the Federation Ambassador to the Klingon Empire, the Federation Council has tasked me with aiding you in this investigation into this attack given my knowledge of the current state of Klingon affairs.\n\nI need not warn you that the Empire stands on the edge of a civil war that could drag in powers from across the quadrant, I have my suspicions on who is responsible here, but we cannot act without evidence and a wrong move could easily turn the average Klingon warrior against us.
STH_redemption.25.name: Investigate the Bombing
STH_redemption.26.desc: Unfortunately, we must report back with bad news, despite a thorough investigation of the bomb site we have been unable to find any more evidence to suggest who was behind the attack. \n\nI cannot offer any additional information for you at this point.
STH_redemption.26.name: No Evidence Found
STH_redemption.27.desc:
Our investigation has been completed and we have made a startling discovery, it appears the substance used in the bomb carries a unique signature which has been known to have been used by Romulan agents and appears to have been carried in the meeting by a member of the Duras household guard. 

What makes this even more interesting is that we were able to go through our records and find similar readings also present at the site of the Khitomer Massacre. 

This implies a connection between Duras, the Romulans and the events at Khitomer all those years ago.
STH_redemption.27.name: Evidence Found
STH_redemption.28.a: What does this mean for Duras?
STH_redemption.28.b: The Council must see this evidence!
STH_redemption.28.desc_01:
I have spoken with Worf, son of Mogh, a ...close associate of mine, his family was killed at Khitomer, and Mogh his father has long taken the blame for the failings the Empire suffered on that fateful day. 

This new evidence not only links Duras to those events but strongly suggests that his bid for leadership of the Empire is backed by the Romulans. This could prove a great threat to the Federation, as no doubt the Romulans would have more plans that are not in the best interests of the UFP. 

Worf and I must work together to present this evidence, Worf can confront Duras whilst I gather the evidence and bring it to the Council and Starfleet.
STH_redemption.28.desc_02: This new evidence not only links Duras to the events at Khitomer but strongly suggests that his bid for leadership of the Empire is backed by the Romulans. This could prove a great threat to the Federation, as no doubt the Romulans would have more plans that are not in the best interests of the UFP. \n\nThis evidence could completely derail Duras' bid to take over the leadership of the Empire as no self-respecting warrior would stand alongside this traitor.
STH_redemption.28.name: Evidence against Duras
STH_redemption.29.d: Ambassador K'Ehleyr dies in the arms of Worf.
STH_redemption.29.desc_01: As Ambassador K'Ehleyr downloads the evidence and prepares to take it to her superiors, the door to her quarters opens, and a tall and dangerous figure enters the room, Duras armed with a vicious-looking d'k tahg blade. He advances on the Ambassador, with a cruel smile, uttering the words 'this evidence can go no further'. \n\nThe two warriors trade blows, but Duras is stronger and more experienced, in the end K'Ehleyr lies mortally wounded and Duras has his prize.
STH_redemption.29.desc_02:
As Ambassador K'Ehleyr downloads the evidence and prepares to take it to her superiors, the door to her quarters opens, and a tall and dangerous figure enters the room, Duras armed with a vicious-looking d'k tahg blade. He advances on the Ambassador, with a cruel smile, uttering the words 'this evidence can go no further'. 

The two warriors trade blows, but although Duras is stronger and more experienced, K'Ehleyr has something truly powerful to fight for and is able to hold her ground forcing Duras to use tactics unworthy of a Klingon warror... but it gets the job done. 

K'Ehleyr lays crippled on the floor and Duras, evidence in hand makes a hasty retreat.
STH_redemption.29.desc_03:
As Ambassador K'Ehleyr downloads the evidence and prepares to take it to her superiors, the door to her quarters opens, and a tall and dangerous figure enters the room, Duras armed with a vicious-looking d'k tahg blade. He advances on the Ambassador, with a cruel smile, uttering the words 'this evidence can go no further'. 

The two warriors trade blows, the fight is fierce, two warriors with everything to lose fighting with all their might. 

When it is clear that Duras cannot win he changes tact and manages to target the evidence with a well aimed shot from his Disrupter, the explosion from the console knocking the Ambassador to thr ground, whilst Duras makes his escape.
STH_redemption.29.name: Duras finds K'Ehleyr alone
STH_redemption.3.b: We will have no part in this plan.
STH_redemption.3.desc: Greetings Praetor [Root.GetLeaderName], my name is Commander Sela and I have been working on fostering a relationship with the leadership of one of the Great Houses within the Klingon Empire my source has revealed that the relationship between the Klingon Empire and the Federation is continuing to grow at an alarming pace since they signed the treaty at Khitomer. \n\nMy contact within the House of Duras is ready to make a bid for the leadership of the Empire, but they will need our support and a little help ensuring the current leader of the High Council meets an 'unfortunate' end...
STH_redemption.3.name: A meeting with Sela
STH_redemption.30.b: Why did K'Ehleyr have to die?
STH_redemption.30.c: Today is a Good Day to Die Duras.
STH_redemption.30.desc: Duras! I am here to claim the Right of Vengeance for your actions here today, you will die at my hands here today and pay for your crimes against K'Ehleyr and for the sins of your father against the House of Mogh.
STH_redemption.30.name: Right of Vengance
STH_redemption.31.desc: The two Klingons are locked in fierce combat, one fighting for vengeance and fueled by the rage of the injustices caused by the other. As the sounds of battle echos around, one man emerges triumphant, Worf, while Duras lies dead at his feet.
STH_redemption.31.name: Worf Emerges Victorious
STH_redemption.32.desc: The two Klingons engage in combat, with both warriors fighting for their lives, Duras moves quickly to his right feigning an attack and drawing Worf into a lunge enabling Duras to capitalise and stab Worf in the back ending the battle with Duras standing victorious.
STH_redemption.32.name: Duras Emerges Victorious
STH_redemption.33.desc: Worf, you stand before the High Council, whilst you were in your rights as Klingon to enact the Right of Vengence, with it lies any chance of clearing your family name. If we may speak off the record... your father knew too well that challenging Duras at this time would lead to open revolt within the Empire. The House of Duras is too strong right now to challenge and the death of their leader at the hands of a member of Starfleet only strengthens the backing they have. \n\nFor the good of the Empire we need you to bear this disgrace a while longer whilst the future of the Empire is solidified.
STH_redemption.33.name: Discommendation
STH_redemption.34.desc: As the meeting comes to an end, the deafening sound of an explosion rings out across the meeting room. Security Officers from Starfleet and KDF dive to protect their leaders and sirens bellow while the emergency lights flicker hopelessly. \n\nIt is clear that some force does not want this meeting to have taken place.
STH_redemption.34.name: Bomb Explodes
STH_redemption.35.a: This is all going to plan.
STH_redemption.35.desc: As planned by Sela and her team, an explosion has rocked the conference being held by the Klingons and Federation the resulting chaos has hampered the succession process, and built support for our puppets in the House of Duras.
STH_redemption.35.name: Bomb Explodes at UFP-Klingon Conference
STH_redemption.36.desc: Our plans to plant Duras as head of the Klingon Empire have suffered a serious blow, as it appears the fool was killed in single combat against a Starfleet officer. Only in Klingon society could an act of such barbarism be tolerated, we will need to re-assess and adapt our plans without the fool Duras.
STH_redemption.36.name: Duras Dead
STH_redemption.37.desc: Despite the bombing, the Klingons are insisting on a decision, the Rite of Succession must be completed and a recommendation must be put forward. \n\nWhat has been made clear is that no matter the victor today this matter is far from settled and both sides will not take the decision lightly. We cannot directly influence the internal matters of the Empire, but we can make a judgment on who has been trying to kill the Chancellor.
STH_redemption.37.name: Time to Decide
STH_redemption.38.desc: The time has come to make a judgment on the next Chancellor of the Klingon Empire. Our investigations have shown that Duras, working with the Romulans, attempted to sabotage the meeting and kill his rivals in a very un-Klingon display. However, it is clear that Duras has the backing of a large number of the 'old-guard' and his family name carries a lot of weight within the noble families. Whereas Gowron offers stability, a clear career politician he lacks the big family name and military history, but he is in no rush to take the Empire to war. The Chancellor wants to know who is working against the Empire, which answer should we give?
STH_redemption.38.name: Time to Decide
STH_redemption.39.a: Gowron should be the new leader.
STH_redemption.39.b: Duras should be the new leader.
STH_redemption.39.desc_01: The Arbiter has made their decision and believes that Gowron son of M'Rel played no part in the poisoning and later attempted assassination of the Chancellor. After studying the evidence they are in no doubt that Duras, conspiring with other forces is the traitor!
STH_redemption.39.desc_02: The Arbiter has made their decision and believes that Duras of the House of Duras is the innocent party, and despite the rumors played no part in the attempted assassination of the Chancellor's life. \n\nInstead the ambitious Gowron took it upon himself to open up a route to the Chancellorship.
STH_redemption.39.name: The Arbiter Has Spoken
STH_redemption.4.desc: Qapla' it is with great concern I must report that my health continues to deteriorate, the greatest doctors within the Empire feel my passage to Sto-vo-kor rapidly approaches. \n\nI speak now grave words for I fear that treachery runs deep within the Empire and I will be reaching out to our allies in the Federation to play a part in the Arbiter of Succession ritual for I can no longer trust the warriors within these halls.
STH_redemption.4.name: High Council Meeting
STH_redemption.40.desc: The Klingon's have reached a decision and Gowron will be the new leader of the Klingon Empire on the death of their current Chancellor.
STH_redemption.40.name: Gowron the Heir to the Empire
STH_redemption.41.desc: The Klingon's have reached a decision and Duras will be the new leader of the Klingon Empire on the death of their current Chancellor.
STH_redemption.41.name: Duras Heir to the Empire
STH_redemption.45.desc_01: Word has reached the Federation Council from the Klingon Empire, Chancellor K'mpec has passed on to Sto-Vo-Kor. We have passed on our condolences and will mourn the passing of a leader who held he peace treaty in high regard, as we move on to more uncertain times.
STH_redemption.45.desc_02: Our warriors cry out to let the dead know a new warrior travels the barge to the Great Halls of Sto-Vo-Kor. [Root.GetRulerName] As one regime ends and new one begins.
STH_redemption.45.name: The Chancellor is Dead...
STH_redemption.46.desc: Our spies in the Klingon Empire have confirmed that their Chancellor has finally succumbed to the poison that our agents have been pumping into the fool's Bloodwine. The next phase of our plan comes into effect.
STH_redemption.46.name: Klingon Chancellor Dies
STH_redemption.47.desc: It is time to confirm the position of Chancellor [Root.GetRulerName] in front of the Klingon High Council and our allies. We should send out invites and begin a new era in the Empires grand history.
STH_redemption.47.name: The New Chancellor
STH_redemption.48.desc: Qapla' to our allies! I invite you to travel to Qo'noS to celebrate in my ascension to my rightful position as ruler of the Klingon Empire, no thanks to the help that you provided. \n\nI look forward to showing your representative a true Klingon welcome!
STH_redemption.48.name: Message from the Klingon Empire
STH_redemption.5.a: What role does the Arbiter play?
STH_redemption.5.desc: Greetings I am K'Ehleyr, Federation Ambassador to the Klingon Empire, I've been approached by my counterpart from the Empire to ask for our attendance at an important meeting, it appears the Chancellor of the Empire is in dire health and has asked for one of our finest to act as the Arbiter of Succession in the upcoming ceremony. This act will go a long way in cementing our relationship with the Empire and ensuring that the best possible outcome is reached for future Federation-Klingon relations.
STH_redemption.5.name: Inbound Message from the Klingon Ambassador
STH_redemption.50.desc_01: As Gowron dons the ceremonial robe of the High Chancellor, his forces make their move to arrest members of the House of Duras who had not previously fled Qo'noS a fleet of rebel sympathisers decloak and begin to open fire in an attempt to cover the escape of Duras' forces.
STH_redemption.50.desc_02: As Duras dons the ceremonial robe of the High Chancellor, his forces make their move to arrest members of Gowrons team who had not previously fled Qo'noS a fleet of rebel sympathisers decloak and begin to open fire in an attempt to cover the escape of Gowron and his commanders.
STH_redemption.50.name: Rebel Attack
STH_redemption.52.desc: The Rebel fleet has been destroyed, but not before allowing the rebel leaders to escape the planet's surface. Could this be the first skirmish in a Klingon Civil War?
STH_redemption.52.name: Rebel Fleet Destroyed
STH_redemption.53.desc_01: Greetings Praetor our chance to fuel the Rebel faction of House Duras has arrived, with all eyes within the Klingon Empire now turning towards the inevitable civil war that our agents have fueled now is the time to provide them with weapons and resources that will forever place them in our debt, so that once they come to power we will have ourselves a new ally eager to turn on the Federation, paving the way for Romulus to come and take her rightful place as rulers of the Alpha-Beta Quadrants.
STH_redemption.53.desc_02: Greetings Praetor our chance to fuel the Rebel faction of House of Gowron has arrived, with all eyes within the Klingon Empire now turning towards the inevitable civil war that our agents have fueled now is the time to provide them with weapons and resources that will forever place them in our debt, this means that whatever side wins the upcoming civil war, they will owe the Romulan Star Empire a debt that can only be paid with the blood of our enemies.
STH_redemption.53.name: Klingon Civil War
STH_redemption.54.desc: It is now unfortunately clear that the situation within the Klingon Empire is only heading one way... towards a civil war between the Houses of Duras and Gowron. However, whilst this remains quite clear, what is concerning is how the rebel faction is managing to build up a rapid supply of ships, weapons and resources well beyond their means. \n\nThe influence of another major empire working behind the scenes is now all but confirmed.
STH_redemption.54.name: Klingon Civil War
STH_redemption.56.desc: Exposing the House of Duras has started a chain of events that has set in motion a Klingon Civil War, far earlier than anyone could have expected and has forced the hand of the Romulans, pulling them into the war. Now we shall see which faction is strong enough to lead the Empire as a true Klingon!
STH_redemption.56.name: Klingon Civil War
STH_redemption.57.desc: As many predicted, forcing the House of Duras' hand has resulted in the Klingon Empire descending into a potentially brutal civil war. \n\nWe shall soon see which House is strongest and who will lead the Empire.
STH_redemption.57.name: Klingon Empire Decends into Civil War
STH_redemption.58.desc: It appears the fools within the House of Duras, having been humiliated in front ot their allies have taken the foolish move to begin the planned Klingon Civil war far too early, in their desperation they appear to have named our involvement and dragged us into their war!
STH_redemption.58.name: Klingon Empire Decends into Civil War
STH_redemption.6.desc: Having worked with the Klingons and gained their respect, I am willing to speak with the Empire regarding the Rite of Succession. It would be remiss of me if I did not flag my concern about involving ourselves in the internal affairs of the Klingon Empire and we will have to inform them that whilst we can observe the event our role must remain neutral. \n\nRemaining on good terms with the Empire will be a key outcome of this meeting and ensuring stability along our borders.
STH_redemption.6.name: Rite of Succession
STH_redemption.60.desc: Our military council grows ever more concerned that our enemy is managing to build up its forces at an alarming rate, they must be getting help from an outside power, most likely those Romulan cowards, who would never dare confront us directly! \n\nWith all our forces searching for the rebels we must once again turn to the humans to help.
STH_redemption.60.name: Klingon Civil War
STH_redemption.61.a: Agree to set up a Tachyon Grid.
STH_redemption.61.desc: Qapla' [Root.GetRulerName] the cowardly Romulans are clearly attempting to bolster the fighting power of our rebel factions, over the past weeks weapons, ships and resources have found their way into the Rebel's hands. We seek the aid of our friends to stop this from happening.
STH_redemption.61.name: Incoming Message
STH_redemption.62.desc: The Federation has responded to our ask and agreed to set up a Tachyon Dection Grid along our border, whilst it would have been better for them to have provided ships and torpedos, this action should remove the Romulan threat.
STH_redemption.62.name: Federation agrees to help
STH_redemption.63.desc: The Federation has sent their regrets that they cannot involve themselves in 'internal Klingon matters' and they will not be offering to help us. We must deal with this situation on our own.
STH_redemption.63.name: Federation refuses to help
STH_redemption.64.desc: The fleet continued to scan the border for any sign of ships attempting to cross the border, suddenly one of the ships broke formation and fired a volley of low-yield torpedoes into a seemingly empty section of space. \n\nAll of a sudden a shimmer and energy spike is seen on viewers, this can only be a cloak fleet attempting to break the border blockade!
STH_redemption.64.name: Cloak Ship Detected
STH_redemption.65.desc: The fleet continues to scan the border for any sign of ships attempting to cross the border to aid the Klingon rebels. Several hours pass with no sign of any ships attempting to breach the blockade. It appears our attempt to stop the Romulans crossing the border has either failed or the Romulans have thought better of it.
STH_redemption.65.name: No sign of any ships
STH_redemption.66.desc: The Federation's attempts to prevent our ships from delivering resources to the Klingon Rebels has failed to prevent us from crossing the borders, once again displaying the superiority of Romulan technology and leadership.
STH_redemption.66.name: Fleet Breaches the Blockade
STH_redemption.67.desc: The Starfleet ships are locking weapons onto our cloak fleets, they must have detected us with that Tachyon Grid. Continuing to press on could result on them firing on us whilst our shields are down!
STH_redemption.67.name: Fleet Detected!
STH_redemption.68.a: How could Yar be your mother?
STH_redemption.68.c: We will not be backing down.
STH_redemption.68.desc: No, Captain. I am Commander Sela. The woman you knew as Tasha Yar was my mother. Deploying an invasion fleet along our border is a clear act of aggression.
STH_redemption.68.name: ... Tasha?
