STH_theCrossing_event.52.name: El cruce
STH_theCrossing_event.55.desc:
[Root.GetFleetName] continúa con el plan original para escapar del interior de la nave alienígena, restringiendo la tripulación poseída detrás de los mamparos de emergencia. 

Limpiando un agujero en el costado de la nave alienígena, el [Root.GetFleetName] emerge en el espacio abierto y acelera al máximo impulso. 

La extensión del daño a la nave alienígena es mucho mayor de lo esperado. Estalla en una explosión de plasma, momentos después de que [Root.GetFleetName] despeja el radio de la explosión. 

A medida que se destruye la embarcación, los monitores internos detectan sobretensiones electrostáticas en las áreas de la nave donde se había contenido la tripulación poseída. La totalidad de la tripulación afectada se encuentra muerta por electrocución.
STH_theCrossing_event.55.name: El cruce
STH_theSilentEnemy_event.1.b: Espere a que la nave regrese.
STH_theSilentEnemy_event.1.c: Deja la órbita, no queremos una confrontación.
STH_theSilentEnemy_event.1.desc:
Después de un día en órbita de [From.From.GetName] sin progreso en localizar el origen de las lecturas subespaciales inusuales, Captain [Root.GetLeaderName] se prepara para dar el orden de pasar a la siguiente [Root.GetFleetName] Objetivo de la mision. 

Más tarde, los sensores dela nave detectan un aumento en la actividad del subespacio seguido de la aparición de una nave extraterrestre fuera del arco del puerto. 

Las comunicaciones entre las naves son infructuosas y después de varios minutos de silenciosa confrontación la nave alienigena entra en warp.
STH_theSilentEnemy_event.1.name: El Enemigo Silencioso
STH_theSilentEnemy_event.10.a: ¡Los equipos de seguridad, repelen a los pensionistas!
STH_theSilentEnemy_event.10.desc: El oficial de comunicación de [Root.GetFleetName] repite un saludo de todas las frecuencias al buque alienígena. El equipo del puente aguarda la respuesta en tenso silencio, con sólo el zumbido de los motores de la nave en el fondo. \n\nUn carillón de alerta. Las vigas transportadoras de la nave extraterrestre han sido detectadas en todo el buque y las advertencias de fuego de armas se han disparado a través de una serie de cubiertas. Al mismo tiempo, el buque enemigo se ha desvanecido, la acción de abordar aparentemente era una distracción.
STH_theSilentEnemy_event.10.name: El Enemigo Silencioso
STH_theSilentEnemy_event.11.desc:
El [Root.GetFleetName] avanza hacia adelante en un curso de intercepción con el buque. Se ordena al oficial táctico deshabilitar el arsenal de armas del enemigo, y tras varios disparos bien colocados más tarde, el buque muestra daños visibles. 

Una extensión de torpedos de forma desconocida se lanzan desde el barco enemigo y golpean el casco de [Root.GetFleetName]. Los torpedos impactan contra nuestra nave, pero los armónicos del campo de integridad estructural parecen haber hecho inútiles los proyectiles enemigos. 

Retornamos el fuego hacia los motores del enemigo, un disparo afortunado penetra hasta el núcleo del motor de warp, y en un destello de luz azul brillante el buque enemigo es aniquilado. 

El escaneo de los desechos revela que una serie de componentes sobrevivieron a la destrucción, y rápidamente se transportan a bordo del [Root.GetFleetName] para su estudio.
STH_theSilentEnemy_event.11.name: El Enemigo Silencioso
STH_theSilentEnemy_event.12.desc: El [Root.GetFleetName] avanza hacia adelante en un curso de intercepción con el buque. Se ordena al oficial táctico deshabilitar el arsenal de armas del enemigo, y varios disparos bien colocados más tarde, muestra daños visibles en el buque alienijena. \n\nUna extensión de torpedos de forma desconocida se lanzan desde el buque enemigo y golpean el casco de [Root.GetFleetName]. La nave zozobra violentamente. Los armónicos del campo de integridad estructural parecen haber hecho que los proyectiles enemigos sean más potentes. Un proyectil penetra hasta el núcleo de motor warp de la [Root.GetFleetName], y en un destello de luz azul brillante la nave es destruida, matando a toda la tripulación.
STH_theSilentEnemy_event.12.name: El Enemigo Silencioso
STH_theSilentEnemy_event.13.desc:
Los equipos de seguridad se reúnen en secciones clave de la nave, rifles de phaser en la mano. El ruido de las armas del disruptor del enemigo suena a través de los corredores, mientras que la tripulación informa que sus propios ajustes de aturdimiento son ineficaces. 

El oficial táctico se mueve por la cubierta de [Root.GetFleetName] por cubierta con un equipo de oficiales veteranos de combate, empujando a los alienigenas más y más hasta que finalmente se embotellan alrededor de la bodega de carga principal. 

Capitan [Root.GetLeaderName] usa el intercomunicador para exigir la rendición de los alienígenas, pero ellos no entienden o se niegan a aceptar. 

Los alienigenas restantes, cinco en total, parecen conversar brevemente antes de disparar colectivamente sus armas en las puertas de la bahía de carga. La descompresión en el compartimiento de carga los empuja al espacio junto con los asaltantes restantes. 

Los campos de fuerza de emergencia se ponen en su lugar alrededor de la sección y la nave queda asegurada, y el equipo de seguridad recoge varias piezas de armas y equipos desechados extraterrestres.
STH_theSilentEnemy_event.13.name: El Enemigo Silencioso
STH_theSilentEnemy_event.14.desc:
Los equipos de seguridad se reúnen en secciones clave de la nave, rifles laser en la mano. El ruido de las armas del disruptor del enemigo suena a través de los corredores, mientras que la tripulación informa que sus propios ajustes de aturdimiento son ineficaces. 

El oficial táctico se mueve por la cubierta de [Root.GetFleetName] por cubierta con un equipo de oficiales veteranos de combate, empujando a los asaltantes más y más hasta que finalmente se embotellan alrededor de la bodega de carga principal. 

Capitan [Root.GetLeaderName] usa el intercomunicador para exigir la rendición del alienígena, pero ellos no entienden o se niegan a aceptar. 

Los asaltantes restantes, cinco en total, parecen conversar brevemente antes de disparar colectivamente sus armas en las puertas de la bahía de carga. El aire en el compartimiento de carga los empuja al espacio junto con los tableros restantes y un contingente grande del personal de seguridad. 

Los campos de fuerza de emergencia se ponen en marcha alrededor de la sección y el buque está asegurado, y los equipos de ingeniería recolectan varias piezas de armas y equipos extraterrestres abandonados durante el combate. Varios artículos estaban manipulados para autodestruirse y cinco técnicos mueren tratando de colocarlos en el almacenamiento.
STH_theSilentEnemy_event.14.name: El Enemigo Silencioso
STH_theSilentEnemy_event.15.desc:
Los equipos de seguridad se reúnen en secciones clave de la nave, rifles laser en la mano. El ruido de las armas del disruptor del enemigo suena a través de los corredores, mientras que la tripulación informa que sus propios ajustes de aturdimiento son ineficaces. 

El oficial táctico se mueve por la cubierta de [Root.GetFleetName] por cubierta con un equipo de oficiales veteranos de combate, empujando a los asaltantes alienigenas más y más hasta que finalmente se embotellan alrededor de la bodega de carga principal. 

Capitan [Root.GetLeaderName] se dirige al área para exigir la rendición del alienígena, pero ellos no entienden o se niegan a aceptar. 

Los asaltantes restantes, cinco en total, parecen conversar brevemente antes de disparar colectivamente sus armas en las puertas de la bahía de carga. El aire en la bahía de carga los empuja hacia el espacio junto con los restantes huéspedes y el capitán [Root.GetLeaderName]. 

Los campos de fuerza de emergencia se colocan alrededor de la sección y la nave está asegurada. Los equipos de ingeniería recolectan varias piezas de armas y equipos extraños desechados, aunque varios elementos son manipulados para destruirse y cinco técnicos son asesinados tratando de almacenarlos. 

La tripulación restante del [Root.GetFleetName] lamenta la pérdida de su oficial al mando.
STH_theSilentEnemy_event.15.name: El Enemigo Silencioso
STH_theSilentEnemy_event.2.a: Al menos esto no era un desperdicio completo.
STH_theSilentEnemy_event.2.desc: En cuestión de minutos de que los alienígenas entren en warp, el bloqueo del sensor de [Root.GetFleetName] en su nave comienza a deteriorarse. Cinco minutos más y la nave ha desaparecido de los sensores por completo. El capitán [Root.GetLeaderName] ordena potencia adicional a los sensores y otra perturbación subespacial se detecta varias horas-luz de distancia. \n\nExplorando en las coordenadas la nave no se ve en ninguna parte, sin embargo la perturbación subespacial tiene en su corazón una fuente de partículas exóticas, que se descompone rápidamente en intensidad. Aceptando que la nave extraterrestre no pueda ser localizada, el Capitán [Root.GetLeaderName] compromete el [Root.GetFleetName] al estudio de la fuente de partículas antes de que se colapse.
STH_theSilentEnemy_event.2.name: El Enemigo Silencioso
STH_theSilentEnemy_event.3.desc:
En cuestión de minutos de que los alienígenas entren en warp, el bloqueo del sensor de [Root.GetFleetName] en su buque comienza a deteriorarse. Cinco minutos más y la nave ha desaparecido de los sensores por completo. El capitán [Root.GetLeaderName] ordena potencia adicional a los sensores y otra perturbación subespacial se detecta varias horas-luz de distancia.

Al llegar a las coordenadas, el buque parecía estar tratando de ensanchar la perturbación a través de una emisión de taquiones de naturaleza desconocida, sin embargo, a medida que el [Root.GetFleetName] se acerca a las emisiones se cortan. 

El buque levanta sus escudos y activa lo que parece ser sus sistemas de armas.
STH_theSilentEnemy_event.3.name: El Enemigo Silencioso
STH_theSilentEnemy_event.4.desc: Dos días pasan y la tripulación del [Root.GetFleetName] crece convencido de que los alienígenas no tienen planes de regresar a [From.From.GetName]. En el tercer día, los sensores de la nave detectan otra oleada de actividad subespacial antes de que la nave alienígena salga de warp junto a [Root.GetFleetName]. \n\nEl buque levanta sus escudos y activa lo que parece ser sus sistemas de armas.
STH_theSilentEnemy_event.4.name: El Enemigo Silencioso
STH_theSilentEnemy_event.5.a: Al menos esto no era un desperdicio completo.
STH_theSilentEnemy_event.5.desc: Dos días pasan y la tripulación del [Root.GetFleetName] crece convencido de que los alienígenas no tienen planes de regresar a [From.From.GetName]. En el tercer día, los sensores de la nave detectan otra oleada de actividad subespacial en el lado opuesto de [From.From.GetName]. \n\nArrendiendo en las coordenadas el buque no se ve en ninguna parte, sin embargo la perturbación subespacial tiene en su corazón una fuente de partículas exóticas, que se descompone rápidamente en intensidad. El capitán [Root.GetLeaderName] confirma el [Root.GetFleetName] en el estudio de la fuente de partículas antes de que se colapse.
STH_theSilentEnemy_event.5.name: El Enemigo Silencioso
STH_theSilentEnemy_event.6.a: Al menos esto no era un desperdicio completo.
STH_theSilentEnemy_event.6.desc: El [Root.GetFleetName] deja la órbita. A varios minutos de distancia de [From.From.GetName], los sensores de la nave detectan otra oleada de actividad subespacial en el lado opuesto de [From.From.GetName]. \n\nArrendiendo en las coordenadas el buque no se ve en ninguna parte, sin embargo la perturbación subespacial tiene en su corazón una fuente de partículas exóticas, que se descompone rápidamente en intensidad. El capitán [Root.GetLeaderName] confirma el [Root.GetFleetName] en el estudio de la fuente de partículas antes de que se colapse.
STH_theSilentEnemy_event.6.name: El Enemigo Silencioso
STH_theSilentEnemy_event.7.desc: El [Root.GetFleetName] deja la órbita. A varios minutos de distancia de [From.From.GetName], los sensores de la nave detectan otra oleada de actividad subespacial en el lado opuesto de [From.From.GetName]. Al llegar a las coordenadas el buque parecía estar tratando de ampliar una perturbación subespacial a través de una emisión de táquion de naturaleza desconocida, sin embargo, como el [Root.GetFleetName] se acerca a las emisiones se cortan. \n\nEl buque levanta sus escudos y activa lo que parece ser sus sistemas de armas.
STH_theSilentEnemy_event.7.name: El Enemigo Silencioso
STH_theSilentEnemy_event.8.desc:
El oficial de comunicación de [Root.GetFleetName] repite un saludo de todas las frecuencias al buque alienígena. El equipo del puente aguarda la respuesta en tenso silencio, con sólo el zumbido de los motores de la nave en el fondo. 

Un carillón de alerta. Los alienígenas han devuelto el saludo. 

Una forma extraña, casi fúngica aparece en la pantalla y una serie de chasquidos se oyen a través del sistema de comunicaciones. 

'Soy Capitán [Root.GetLeaderName] del [Root.GetFleetName] ', el Capitán comienza antes de detallar la misión de la nave en este sistema. Con cada nuevo sonido del extraterrestre, el sistema de traductor universal de [Root.GetFleetName] empieza a construir un modelo de su lenguaje hasta que finalmente se superponen frases rotas, seguidas de frases completas. 

El diálogo se inicia. El alienígena se describe a sí mismo como "Elachi" y dice que está en una misión para su señor que no puede fallar. Sus órdenes no eran para interferir con otras especies, pero nuestros saludos despertarón su curiosidad. 

10 minutos de conversación más tarde, anuncia que es hora de que se vaya y lamenta lo improbable que sea reunirse con el capitán [Root.GetLeaderName] de nuevo. El departamento de xenología está ansioso por revisar los datos recopilados durante este encuentro.
STH_theSilentEnemy_event.8.name: El Enemigo Silencioso
STH_theSilentEnemy_event.9.desc:
El oficial de comunicación de [Root.GetFleetName] repite un saludo de todas las frecuencias al buque alienígena. El equipo del puente aguarda la respuesta en tenso silencio, con sólo el zumbido de los motores de la nave en el fondo. 

Un carillón de alerta. La nave extraterrestre ha propulsado sus motores y ha vuelto a entrar en warp. 

Aceptar que los extraterrestres no deseen ser contactados, el capitán [Root.GetLeaderName] ordena un curso trazado al siguiente sistema. El departamento de física espera revisar los datos subespaciales reunidos durante este encuentro.
STH_theSilentEnemy_event.9.name: El Enemigo Silencioso
STH_theSilentEnemy_fail.1.desc: Después de un día en órbita de [From.From.GetName] sin progreso en localizar el origen de las lecturas de subespacio inusuales, Captain [Root.GetLeaderName] da el orden de pasar al siguiente objetivo de la mision [Root.GetFleetName] .
STH_theSilentEnemy_fail.2.desc:
Después de un día en órbita de [From.From.GetName] sin progreso en localizar el origen de las lecturas de subespacios inusuales, Captain [Root.GetLeaderName] se prepara para dar el orden de pasar a la siguiente [Root.GetFleetName] Objetivo de la mision. Momentos después los sensores de la nave detectan un aumento en la actividad subespacial seguida por la aparición de una nave alienígena en el arco del puerto. 

El buque alienígena comienza a disparar en el [Root.GetFleetName] y con sus escudos bajados es un blanco facil. 

El jefe de ingenieria informa de una brecha en el motor warp y el capitán [Root.GetLeaderName] ordena el abandono inmediato de la nave. A medida que las vainas de escape comienzan a escapar de la nave, una ronda de fuego de armas de la nave enemiga destruye las vainas de escape , y en una brillante explosion la nave es aniquilada matando a todos sus miembros.
STH_theSkinOfEvil_event.1.a: Te saludamos en nombre de [Root.Owner.GetName]. ¿Quién eres tú?
STH_theSkinOfEvil_event.1.desc:
El Capitán [Root.GetLeaderName] lidera un equipo para investigar el misterioso accidente de la lanzadera [Root.GetFleetName] en [From.GetName], un prometedor candidato a la colonización. 

Como el equipo visitante busca en el sitio del accidente, El tripulante descubre un extraño líquido negro que lentamente se reforma en la forma de un cuerpo humanoide. 

Está bloqueando el camino al lugar del accidente de la lanzadera.
STH_theSkinOfEvil_event.1.name: Piel del mal
STH_theSkinOfEvil_event.10.desc_01: ¡Será divertido matarte! dice el ser con júbilo, procediendo a disparar un haz de energía coherente al Capitán [Root.GetLeaderName]. El capitán está vaporizado. \n\nCon la demostración brutal de puro poder, el resto del equipo se retira a su lanzadera, antes de regresar a [Root.GetFleetName]. El primer oficial ordena una boya de cuarentena desplegada en todo el planeta, para que nadie más pueda levantar tontamente la ira del ser en el planeta.
STH_theSkinOfEvil_event.10.desc_02: ¡Será divertido matarte! dice el ser con júbilo, procediendo a disparar un haz de energía coherente al Capitán [Root.GetLeaderName]. El capitán está vaporizado. \n\nCon la exhibición brutal de puro poder, el resto del equipo se retira y regresa a [Root.GetFleetName]. El primer oficial ordena una boya de cuarentena desplegada en todo el planeta, para que nadie más pueda levantar tontamente la ira del ser en el planeta.
STH_theSkinOfEvil_event.10.name: Piel del mal
STH_theSkinOfEvil_event.11.desc: El oficial táctico [Root.GetFleetName] ha estado vigilando de cerca la superficie dell planeta, disparando sobre el ser tan pronto como el capitán [Root.GetLeaderName] esta fuera de peligro [shipCaptain.GetHerHis]. El ser se vaporiza bajo el primer bombardeo orbital. \n\nEl planeta está ahora seguro para la colonización.
STH_theSkinOfEvil_event.11.name: Piel del mal
STH_theSkinOfEvil_event.2.b: ¿Estás enojado porque nunca podrías pertenecer a ellos?
STH_theSkinOfEvil_event.2.c: Sin tristeza no hay contexto para la felicidad ...
STH_theSkinOfEvil_event.2.desc: "Pareces como aquellos con los que una vez me uní", dice el ser, en un tono contemplativo. 'Afirmaron venir en paz también ... hablaron de la iluminación y de la calidad. Pero dejaron todo lo demás aquí, la basura, los rencores, el odio. Se quedó dentro de mí. \n\nEl ser se identifica como Armus. Aunque al principio parecía peligroso, el capitán [Root.GetLeaderName] ahora ve una medida de tristeza al respecto.
STH_theSkinOfEvil_event.2.name: Piel del mal
STH_theSkinOfEvil_event.3.b: Sin tristeza no hay contexto para la felicidad ...
STH_theSkinOfEvil_event.3.desc: Soy Armus. Mal encarnado. Cuando una sociedad entera derrama su dolor y su resentimiento, sus resentimientos y odio ... Yo soy lo que queda.
STH_theSkinOfEvil_event.3.name: Piel del mal
STH_theSkinOfEvil_event.4.a: Coge el transbordador, pero deja el piloto aquí.
STH_theSkinOfEvil_event.4.desc: Sí Sí. Sólo quiero ser amado, como todo el mundo lo hace. Quiero encontrar a mis antepasados. ¿Puedo usar uno de sus buques para buscarlos?
STH_theSkinOfEvil_event.4.name: Piel del mal
STH_theSkinOfEvil_event.5.a: Coge el transbordador, pero deja el piloto aquí.
STH_theSkinOfEvil_event.5.desc: Tal vez.' Armus responde. Quizás sólo quiero volver a conectarme con los que me han creado. ¿Me ayudarás a salir de este lugar para poder finalmente encontrarlos?
STH_theSkinOfEvil_event.5.name: Piel del mal
STH_theSkinOfEvil_event.6.a: Parece que la compasión irrita a la bestia.
STH_theSkinOfEvil_event.6.desc_01:
¡NO NECESITO TUS PIES!' truena al ser, su voz hace temblar el suelo. 

 Enfurecido, agarra y sumerge al Capitán en su estanque aceitoso. En unos instantes [shipCaptain.GetSheHe] es expulsado del fluido, muerto. 

El resto del equipo visitante entra en pánico y vuelve corriendo a su lanzadera, antes de regresar a [Root.GetFleetName]. El primer oficial ordena una boya de cuarentena desplegada en todo el planeta, para que nadie más pueda levantar tontamente la ira del ser en el planeta.
STH_theSkinOfEvil_event.6.desc_02:
¡NO NECESITO TUS PIES!' el ser truena, su voz sacude el suelo. 

 Enfurecido, agarra y sumerge al Capitán en su piscina aceitosa. En unos instantes [shipCaptain.GetSheHe] es expulsado del fluido, muerto. 

El resto del equipo visitante entra en pánico y regresa a [Root.GetFleetName]. El primer oficial ordena una boya de cuarentena desplegada en todo el planeta, para que nadie más pueda levantar tontamente la ira del ser en el planeta.
STH_theSkinOfEvil_event.6.name: Piel del mal
STH_theSkinOfEvil_event.7.desc:
Gracias. Me da esperanza ... "dice Armus, y se aparta. El médico de campo del equipo exploracion socorre al piloto herido y comienza a tratar sus heridas. 

Algunos momentos después, el ser acompaña al capitán y entra en la lanzadera, despegando poco después. 

Las exploraciones adicionales muestran que Armus fue único - no hay otros seres en el planeta. [From.GetName] se declara seguro para la colonización.
STH_theSkinOfEvil_event.7.name: Piel del mal
STH_theSkinOfEvil_event.8.a: Tal vez deberíamos haber ahorrado el shuttlecraft ...
STH_theSkinOfEvil_event.8.desc_01: La negativa del Capitán [Root.GetLeaderName] enfurece a Armus, y con un movimiento de sus extremidades Armus dirige un haz de energía que envuelve [shipCaptain.GetHerHim]. El resto del equipo visitante entra en pánico, regresando rápidamente a su lanzadera antes de regresar a [Root.GetFleetName]. El primer oficial ordena una boya de cuarentena desplegada en todo el planeta, para que nadie más pueda levantar tontamente la ira del ser en el planeta.
STH_theSkinOfEvil_event.8.desc_02: La negativa del Capitán [Root.GetLeaderName] enfurece a Armus, y con un movimiento de sus extremidades Armus dirige un haz de energía que envuelve [shipCaptain.GetHerHim]. El resto del equipo visitante entra en pánico, regresando a la [Root.GetFleetName]. El primer oficial ordena una boya de cuarentena desplegada en todo el planeta, para que nadie más pueda levantar tontamente la ira del ser en el planeta.
STH_theSkinOfEvil_event.8.name: Piel del mal
STH_theSkinOfEvil_event.9.desc: La observación de Armus desde la órbita revela la extensión de la ira de la criatura. Se extiende a través de [From.GetName], el ser parece consumir toda la vida animal y vegetal en su camino. De sus restos produce un gas muy tóxico, creando vastas nubes en la atmósfera que aceleran la muerte del ecosistema restante y bloquean la luz solar. \n\nToda la capacidad de mantenimiento de la vida en este planeta se agota, y [From.GetName] es ahora estéril.
STH_theSkinOfEvil_event.9.name: Piel del mal
STH_theSkinOfEvil_fail.1.desc: El capitán [Root.GetLeaderName] ordena a [Root.GetFleetName] que reduzca la altura a una órbita sincrónica sobre el lugar del accidente de la lanzadera, cuando un misterioso campo de energía engulle al buque. Los motores de la nave son inhabilitados y la nave cae a la superficie, en él mismo sitio donde se estrello la lanzadera que iban a rescatar.
STH_theSkinOfEvil_fail.1.name: Piel del mal
STH_theSkinOfEvil_fail.2.desc: La tripulación comienza los preparativos para llevar a un equipo de exploracion a la superficie, cuando el sensor del buque detecta una reacción en cascada en las vainas de contención de antimateria de la lanzadera. En unos momentos la contención falla y la lanzadera y todo en un radio de cincuenta kilómetros se destruye en la reacción resultante de materia / antimateria. \n\nLa lanzadera y su piloto son declarados muertos.
STH_theSkinOfEvil_fail.2.name: Piel del mal
STH_theTerratinIncident_event.1.a: Deje la órbita y prepárese para deformarse.
STH_theTerratinIncident_event.1.b: Prepara un equipo visitante mientras podamos.
STH_theTerratinIncident_event.1.desc:
El capitán [Root.GetLeaderName] ordena a [Root.GetFleetName] más cerca de [From.GetName] para aumentar la recepción de la señal desde la superficie. Después de varios minutos el oficial de comunicaciones es capaz de limpiar la señal hasta el punto de que es reconocido como el código de Interstat, un protocolo que no se ha utilizado desde el amanecer de los viajes de urdimbre. El mensaje es una sola palabra repetida: '[From.GetName]'. 

La fuente de la señal no devuelve las salidas del barco directamente, sin embargo una luz blanca brillante parece pasar a través aparentemente en respuesta. 

Casi inmediatamente la tripulación comienza a notar la nave que crece alrededor de ellos, hasta que los cheques de los instrumentos confirman que en su lugar han comenzado a encogerse. El oficial de la ciencia conjetura que a la tasa actual de contracción el buque será inoperable en un día ...
STH_theTerratinIncident_event.1.name: El incidente [From.From.GetName]
STH_theTerratinIncident_event.10.b: Deje la órbita y prepárese para deformarse.
STH_theTerratinIncident_event.10.desc: El capitán [Root.GetLeaderName] lleva a un equipo de exploración a la superficie. Descubriendo vastos campos de un material cristalino, el equipo vaga por horas hasta que finalmente se encuentran con una amplia red de cañones. Al entrar en él pierden toda comunicación con la nave, y después de varias horas vagando son incapaces de determinar una ruta fuera de la red.
STH_theTerratinIncident_event.10.name: El incidente [From.From.GetName]
STH_theTerratinIncident_event.11.desc:
La tripulación del [Root.GetFleetName] envía un segundo equipo de exploración a la superficie para buscar al primero. Después de horas de búsqueda, el segundo equipo se ven obligados a abandonar su misión y regresar a la nave para el reabastecimiento. El transporte de retorno parece haber revertido el equipo de exploración a sus tamaños originales - un efecto secundario del transportador que conserva sus patrones originales.

Utilizando este método para restaurar el resto de la tripulación a su tamaño original, más partidas de búsqueda se envían a la superficie del planeta. Después del sexto día, un grupo de búsqueda hace un sombrío descubrimiento: el cadáver del capitán [Root.GetLeaderName] y el primer equipo de exploración, no más alto que dos centímetros, aplastado bajo un guijarro. 

El Primer Oficial ordena que la nave abandone la órbita.
STH_theTerratinIncident_event.11.name: El incidente [From.From.GetName]
STH_theTerratinIncident_event.12.desc:
La tripulación del [Root.GetFleetName] envía un segundo equipo a la superficie para buscar el primero. Después de horas de búsqueda, el segundo equipo se ven obligados a abandonar su misión y regresar a la nave para el reabastecimiento. El transporte de retorno parece haber revertido el equipo a sus tamaños originales - un efecto secundario del transportador que conserva sus patrones originales.

Utilizando este método para restaurar el resto de la tripulación a su tamaño original, más partidas de búsqueda se envían a la superficie del planeta. Después del tercer día un grupo de búsqueda descubre un pequeño campamento improvisado, donde el Capitán [Root.GetLeaderName] y el primer equipo de exploración parecen estar descansando. Volviéndolos a la nave y a su tamaño original.

El Capitán ordena que la nave salga de la órbita.
STH_theTerratinIncident_event.12.name: El incidente [From.From.GetName]
STH_theTerratinIncident_event.13.desc:
El capitán [Root.GetLeaderName] ordena el bombardeo inmediato del planeta desde la órbita, con la esperanza de eliminar cualquier tecnología de la superficie que pueda haber causado el encogimiento de la tripulación. Después de varios minutos se recibe una transmisión y una mujer se presenta como la líder de una ciudad, oculta en virtud de su pequeño tamaño. 

Declarando su intención de eliminar la amenaza [Root.GetFleetName] a su mundo, ella se disculpa por lo que está a punto de ocurrir. 

Desde la superficie se lanzan un enjambre de torpedos de plasma de cientos de miles de lanzaderas terrestres. A medida que impactan con los escudos de [Root.GetFleetName] ,su potencia individual es mínima, sin embargo después de varios segundos - y varios miles de impactos - que penetran la barrera protectora y destruyen el [Root.GetFleetName], matando a toda la tripulación.
STH_theTerratinIncident_event.13.name: El incidente [From.From.GetName]
STH_theTerratinIncident_event.14.desc: La población en miniatura ha sido reubicada con éxito en [From.From.GetName].
STH_theTerratinIncident_event.14.name: El incidente [From.From.GetName]
STH_theTerratinIncident_event.2.desc: El [Root.GetFleetName] sale de la órbita y se prepara para entrar en warp. Todavía está disminuyendo, el capitán [Root.GetLeaderName] conjetura que la retirada del sistema parará o invertirá el proceso, dando a científicos en el tiempo del hogar investigar el asunto más a fondo. A medida que las bobinas de warp empiezan a encenderse, el ingeniero jefe advierte un ligero desequilibrio de fase en los reguladores de flujo de plasma, pero lo rechaza. A medida que la nave rompe la barrera de warp el desequilibrio de fase se intensifica, rasgando un agujero en la bobina del motor warp a estribor. La nave se desgarra, matando todas los miembros.
STH_theTerratinIncident_event.2.name: El incidente [From.From.GetName]
STH_theTerratinIncident_event.3.desc:
El [Root.GetFleetName] sale de la órbita y se prepara para entrar en warp. Todavía está disminuyendo, el capitán [Root.GetLeaderName] conjetura que la retirada del sistema parará o invertirá el proceso, dando a científicos en el tiempo del hogar investigar el asunto más a fondo.

La órbita de abandono detiene el encogimiento, sin embargo no se invierte. Pasan varios días mientras la tripulación intenta revertir su estado, hasta que por fin un especialista en transporte propone usar los transportes para "restablecer" la estructura molecular de cada tripulante.

El proceso funciona, sin embargo varios miembros de la tripulación siguen sufriendo enfermedades psicológicas como un efecto secundario del proceso.
STH_theTerratinIncident_event.3.name: El incidente [From.From.GetName]
STH_theTerratinIncident_event.4.desc:
El [Root.GetFleetName] sale de la órbita y se prepara para entrar en warp. Todavía está disminuyendo, el capitán [Root.GetLeaderName] conjetura que la retirada del sistema parará o invertirá el proceso, dando a científicos en el tiempo del hogar investigar el asunto más a fondo. 

 Abandornar la órbita detiene el encogimiento, sin embargo no se invierte. Pasan varios días mientras la tripulación intenta revertir su estado, hasta que por fin un especialista en transporte propone usar los transportadores para "restablecer" la estructura molecular de cada tripulante. 

El proceso funciona, sin embargo varios miembros de la tripulación continúan sufriendo enfermedades psicológicas como un efecto secundario del proceso, incluyendo al Capitán [Root.GetLeaderName] que se convierte en abuso de sustancias como resultado de su experiencia.
STH_theTerratinIncident_event.4.name: El incidente [From.From.GetName]
STH_theTerratinIncident_event.5.a: Abre un canal a la ciudad, negociemos.
STH_theTerratinIncident_event.5.b: Deje la órbita y prepárese para deformarse.
STH_theTerratinIncident_event.5.desc:
El capitán [Root.GetLeaderName] lleva a un equipo de exploración a la superficie. Descubriendo vastos campos de un material cristalino, el equipo vaga durante horas hasta que finalmente se encuentran con lo que parece ser una ciudad en miniatura cerca de una gran roca. Al inspeccionar lo que parecen ser vehículos de tamaño de insecto y la gente pasea por las calles. El oficial científico sospecha que este es el origen de la señal, y puede ser la clave para el problema del proceso de encogido de la tripulación. 

Más tarde, el transportista de retorno automatizado envía el equipo exploración a la nave. 

El transporte de retorno parece haber revertido al equipo visitante a su tamaño original - un efecto secundario del transportador que conserva sus patrones originales. El logro es de corta duración, sin embargo, ya que descubrir el resto de la [Root.GetFleetName] tripulación están desaparecidos. 

Se recibe una transmisión desde la superficie y el líder de la ciudad miniatura solicita formalmente el intercambio del regreso seguro del equipo de [Root.GetFleetName] a cambio de la evacuación de la población de la ciudad de [From.From.GetName ]a un planeta cercano. Ella afirma que [From.From.GetName] reciente oleada de actividad volcánica amenaza con destruir su colonia.
STH_theTerratinIncident_event.5.name: El incidente [From.From.GetName]
STH_theTerratinIncident_event.6.desc: Después de una breve confirmación de los términos, el capitán [Root.GetLeaderName] negocia con éxito el regreso de la tripulación - totalmente restaurado a su tamaño original por la viga transportadora, y el transporte de la población de la ciudad a la nave para el reasentamiento. Como el transportista de la nave carece de los patrones originales de los habitantes de la ciudad, su tamaño sigue siendo microscópico. \n\nLa comunicación se produce con los habitantes, y el capitán [Root.GetLeaderName] es contado por su líder como la ciudad llegó a ser. Los habitantes son los descendientes de un carguero que pasa por este sistema hace décadas, que sufrió el efecto encogimiento sin posibilidad de detenerlo hasta que alcanzó su máximo efecto. Con el tiempo construyeron una civilización en [From.From.GetName], distinta de sus orígenes. Su líder pide al capitán [Root.GetLeaderName] que encuentre a su gente un nuevo hogar más seguro.
STH_theTerratinIncident_event.6.name: El incidente [From.From.GetName]
STH_theTerratinIncident_event.7.desc: Las negociaciones fracasan. A medida que el Capitán [Root.GetLeaderName] trata de arreglar el regreso de la tripulación, una mafia de la ciudad asalta la instalación en la que están encerrados.\n\nEnredado por su creencia de que [Root.GetFleetName] es responsable de la actividad volcánica en El planeta, matan a los prisioneros con armas láser . En órbita El capitán [Root.GetLeaderName] ordena al [Root.GetFleetName] que abandone el sistema, dejando a los habitantes de la ciudad a su destino.
STH_theTerratinIncident_event.7.name: El incidente [From.From.GetName]
STH_theTerratinIncident_event.8.desc: El [Root.GetFleetName] se prepara para partir, dejando a la tripulación a su destino. En la superficie, una muchedumbre de la ciudad asalta las instalaciones de la tripulación de la nave. \n\nEnredado por su creencia de que el [Root.GetFleetName] es responsable de la actividad volcánica en el planeta, matan a los prisioneros con Armas láser. En órbita El capitán [Root.GetLeaderName] ordena a [Root.GetFleetName] que entre en warp, dejando a los habitantes de la ciudad a su destino.
STH_theTerratinIncident_event.8.name: El incidente [From.From.GetName]
STH_theTerratinIncident_event.9.desc: El capitán [Root.GetLeaderName] ordena a la tripulación restante del [Root.GetFleetName] bombardear la ciudad desde la órbita, condenando a la tripulación a su destino. En la superficie, una muchedumbre de la ciudad asalta las instalaciones de la tripulación de la nave \n\nEnredado por su creencia de que el [Root.GetFleetName] es responsable de la actividad volcánica en el planeta, se preparan para matar a los prisioneros con armas láser, antes de que todo a su alrededor se vaporice en una lluvia de energía del cielo. En órbita El capitán [Root.GetLeaderName] ordena a [Root.GetFleetName] que entre en modo warp, dejando a los habitantes de la ciudad a su destino.
STH_theTerratinIncident_event.9.name: El incidente [From.From.GetName]
STH_theTerratinIncident_fail.1.desc:
El capitán [Root.GetLeaderName] ordena a [Root.GetFleetName] más cerca de [From.From.GetName] para aumentar la recepción de la señal desde la superficie. Después de varios minutos el oficial de comunicaciones es capaz de limpiar la señal hasta el punto de que es reconocido como el código de Interstat, un protocolo que no se ha utilizado desde el amanecer de los viajes de warp. El mensaje es una sola palabra repetida: '[From.From.GetName]'. 

La fuente de la señal no devuelve las salidas de la nave directamente, sin embargo, una luz blanca brillante parece pasar a través, aparentemente, en respuesta. 

 Casi inmediatamente la tripulación comienza a notar que la nave crece alrededor de ellos, hasta que los informes de los instrumentos de la nave confirman que en su lugar han comenzado a encogerse. El oficial de ciencia supone que al ritmo actual de la contracción, la nave será inoperable dentro de un día.

El [Root.GetFleetName] sale de la órbita y se prepara para entrar en warp. Todavía está disminuyendo, el capitán [Root.GetLeaderName] conjetura que la retirada del sistema parará o invertirá el proceso, dando a científicos tiempor para una investigación mas a fondo. 

La órbita de abandono detiene el encogimiento, sin embargo no se invierte. Pasan varios días mientras la tripulación intenta revertir su estado, hasta que por fin un especialista en transporte propone usar los transportadores para "restablecer" la estructura molecular de cada tripulante. 

El proceso funciona, sin embargo varios miembros de la tripulación siguen sufriendo enfermedades psicológicas como un efecto secundario del proceso.
STH_theTerratinIncident_fail.1.name: El incidente [From.From.GetName]
STH_theTerratinIncident_fail.2.desc:
El capitán [Root.GetLeaderName] ordena a [Root.GetFleetName] más cerca de [From.From.GetName] para aumentar la recepción de la señal desde la superficie. Después de varios minutos el oficial de comunicaciones es capaz de limpiar la señal hasta el punto de que es reconocido como el código de Interstat, un protocolo que no se ha utilizado desde el amanecer de los viajes de warp. El mensaje es una sola palabra repetida: '[From.From.GetName]'. 

La fuente de la señal no devuelve las señales de la nave directamente, sin embargo, una luz blanca brillante parece pasar a través, aparentemente, en respuesta. 

 Casi inmediatamente la tripulación comienza a notar que la nave crece alrededor de ellos, hasta que los informes de los instrumentos confirman que en su lugar han comenzado a encogerse. El oficial de ciencia supone que al ritmo actual de la contracción la nave será inoperable dentro de un día. 

El [Root.GetFleetName] sale de la órbita y se prepara para entrar en warp. Todavía está disminuyendo, el capitán [Root.GetLeaderName] conjetura que la retirada del sistema parará o invertirá el proceso, dando a científicos mas tiempo para investigar el asunto más a fondo. A medida que las bobinas de warp empiezan a encenderse, el ingeniero jefe advierte un ligero desequilibrio de fase en los reguladores de flujo de plasma, pero lo rechaza. A medida que la nave rompe la barrera de warp el desequilibrio de fase se intensifica, rasgando un agujero en la bobina del motor warp a estribor. La nave se desgarra, matando todas los miembros.
STH_theTerratinIncident_fail.2.name: El incidente [From.From.GetName]
STH_the_royale.10.desc: With the interference that initially blocked even communications still present, the transport signal fluctuates violently. With the signal weakening the transport operator tries desperately to boost it, but it is no use. The away team is gone.
STH_the_royale.10.name: Transporting
STH_the_royale.11.a: Send an away team to find out what is inside.
STH_the_royale.11.desc: While analysing the old ship debris in orbit of [FromFrom.GetName] our scans have picked up some kind of structure on the planets surface with an atmosphere inside of it noticeably different from the planet's and a lot more friendly for us. However our scans cannot penetrate the walls of the structure to reveal what exactly is inside of it. What should we do?
STH_the_royale.11.name: A structure found on the planet's surface
STH_the_royale.12.a: Try to find a way to re-establish communications.
STH_the_royale.12.b: Abandon the away team to their fate and leave.
STH_the_royale.12.desc:
Upon entering the structure the away team notices that it is inhabited by several sentient bipedal creatures most of which seem to be preoccupied with some sort of games. On the other side of a long table there is a creature standing who welcomes the away team to something called Hotel Royale and offers them some currency apparently used in said games. 

The away team spends time analysing and interacting with the bipedal creatures collecting as much information as possible from them. After being satisfied with the data collected the away team leader [the_royal_leader.GetName] tries to contact the ship, but is unable to get trough to it. Also any attempt the away team members make to try to exit from the door which they came just lead straight back to the same scenario and hand weapons seem ineffective as well in breaching a hole to the walls. 

It would appear that we are stuck here for sometime now.
STH_the_royale.12.name: The structure on the surface
STH_the_royale.13.desc:
With no escape in sight, the away team focuses its efforts on other floors of the structure in and effort to find somesort of power source etc. that is blocking both communications and their escape. 

In one of the upper levels, they stumble across the remains of a bipedal humanoid that has with it some sort of archive of written notes mostly produced with a machine. Between the machine-typed notes they also discover a handwritten note. 

[the_royal_leader.GetName] orders team members to begin translating the notes the best they can in order to see if it could help them understand the situation. With the handwritten one getting first focus, as the team leader suspects it was produced by the deceased humanoid personally at the site.
STH_the_royale.13.name: Stuck inside
STH_the_royale.14.a: Stay and study the alien culture.
STH_the_royale.14.b: Try to beam out from the structure.
STH_the_royale.14.desc:
With the team still working on the translation on most of the notes, a signal arrives from the [Root.GetName] indicating that they have managed to overcome the communications block. The ship crew informs the away team of a possibility to try and transport them away from the structure, but also warns that because of all the interference it might prove to be risky. 

In the mean time the translation crew has also finished an early approximation of what the hand written note contains. It says roughly that the dead alien was kidnapped by some other unknown aliens. Who then brought the alien here and constructed this artificial environment for him using the machine typed notes about the alien's culture and way of life as a guide. 

So now an opportunity presents itself, as we could stay awhile inside the structure and learn all we can about the alien's culture through the translation of the notes and by observing. And even try to find a natural way to exit the environment. Alternatively we could just try to beam out now.
STH_the_royale.14.name: Contact with the ship re-established
STH_the_royale.15.desc: After carefully studying the notes the translation team has come up with an approximation of how the situation progresses in the notes. Apparently, a group of foreigners arrive at the establishment, play the games available there, win big and purchase the whole place. \n\nWith this information and with the help of the local aliens the away team spends time carefully analysing the various games available, how they are played and which game offers the best odds of winning. This analysis points to a game the aliens call blackjack.
STH_the_royale.15.name: Foreign investors
STH_the_royale.16.desc: Trying their hand in the game of blackjack the winnings start to slowly amount to bigger and bigger sums for the away team, with the local aliens cheering them on. It seems that these so called 'humans' really like these kinds of games. Some away team members also point out that based on the odds alone they shouldn't be winning this easily, but probably this artificial environment is improving their odds somehow. \n\nAfter collecting enough winnings and celebrating with the locals it is time to finish this thing, buy the place and see what happens.
STH_the_royale.16.name: Big winners!
STH_the_royale.17.a: We learned a lot about humans here.
STH_the_royale.17.desc: With the place bought it is time for the away team to head for the exit once more to see if the simulation would now end. This time around it is possible to exit the structure after which the away team can finally safely transport aboard their ship.
STH_the_royale.17.name: Time to leave
STH_the_royale.18.desc: With the interference that initially blocked even communications still present, the transport signal fluctuates violently. At times looking like the crew of [Root.GetName] is just about to loose the signal the experience and skill of the transport operator finally saves the day as the away team materializes on board of the ship.
STH_the_royale.18.name: Transporting
STH_the_royale.19.desc: With the interference that initially blocked even communications still present, the transport signal fluctuates violently. With the signal weakening the transport operator tries desperately to boost it, but it is no use. The away team is gone.
STH_the_royale.19.name: Transporting
STH_the_royale.2.a: Send an away team to find out what is inside.
STH_the_royale.2.desc: The analysis of the old ship debris turns up to be parts from an old Earth space ship. In addition some kind of structure is also detected on the surface of [FromFrom.GetName] with an atmosphere inside of it noticeably different from the planet's. However our scans cannot penetrate the walls of the structure to reveal what is inside of it. What should we do?
STH_the_royale.2.name: A structure found on the planet's surface
STH_the_royale.3.a: Try to find a way to re-establish communications.
STH_the_royale.3.b: Abandon the away team to their fate and leave.
STH_the_royale.3.desc:
Upon entering the structure, the away team notices that it is inhabited by several humans dressed in very old-fashioned clothes, most of whom seem to be preoccupied with some sort of games. On the other side of a long desk, there is a more formally dressed human who is member of the staff here. He welcomes the away team to something called Hotel Royale and offers them some currency used in the games. 

The away team spends some time analysing and interacting with the humans, collecting as much information as possible from them. After being satisfied with the data collected, the away team leader [the_royal_leader.GetName] tries to contact the ship, but cannot get through to it. And what is worse any attempt the away team members make to try to exit from the door they came in just leads straight back to the same scenario. Even hand weapons seem ineffective in breaching a hole in the walls. 

It would appear that we are stuck here for some time now.
STH_the_royale.3.name: The structure on the surface
STH_the_royale.4.a: Examine the note and the book.
STH_the_royale.4.desc: With no escape in sight, the away team focuses its efforts on other floors of the structure in an effort to finding some sort of power source that is used in blocking both communications and their escape. \n\nIn one of the upper levels, they stumble across the remains of a human male who has with him a book of some sort and in between the book pages they find a handwritten note. [the_royal_leader.GetName] orders team members to read through the note and skim through the book in the hopes of getting additional information about the place. The book is titled the Hotel Royale.
STH_the_royale.4.name: Stuck inside
STH_the_royale.5.a: Stay and study the artificial environment.
STH_the_royale.5.b: Try to beam out from the structure.
STH_the_royale.5.desc:
With the team still trying to establish the general story of the book, a signal arrives from the [Root.GetName] indicating that they have managed to overcome the communications block. The ship crew informs the away team of the possibility to try and transport them away from the structure, but also warns that because of all the interference, it might prove to be risky. 

In the meantime the translation crew has also finished an early approximation of what the handwritten note contains. It says roughly that the dead human was kidnapped by some other unknown aliens that then brought him here and constructed this artificial environment for him using the book as an instruction manual about human culture and way of life. 

So now an opportunity presents itself, as we could stay awhile inside the structure and learn all we can about the artificially created environment through the translation of the notes and observing. And even find a natural way to exit the environment. Alternatively, we could just try to beam out now.
STH_the_royale.5.name: Contact with the ship re-established
STH_the_royale.6.desc: After carefully studying the notes the translation team has come up with an approximation of how the situation progresses in the book. Apparently a group of foreigners arrive at the establishment, play the games available there, win big and purchase the whole place. With this information and with the help of the local humans the away team spends time carefully analysing the various games available, how they are played and which game offers the best mathematical odds of winning. This analysis points to a game the humans call black jack.
STH_the_royale.6.name: Foreign investors
STH_the_royale.7.desc: Trying their hand in the game of black jack the winnings start to slowly amount to bigger and bigger sums for the away team, with the local humans cheering them on. It seems that these people really love these kinds of games. Some away team members also point out that based on the odds alone they shouldn't be winning this easily, but probably this artificial environment is improving their odds. \n\nAfter collecting enough winnings and celebrating with the locals it is time to finish this thing, buy the place and see what happens.
STH_the_royale.7.name: Big winners!
STH_the_royale.8.a: We learned a lot about old human customs here.
STH_the_royale.8.desc: With the place bought it is time for the away team to head for the exit once more to see if the simulation would now end. This time around it is possible to exit the structure after which the away team can finally safely transport aboard their ship.
STH_the_royale.8.name: Time to leave
STH_the_royale.9.desc: With the interference that initially blocked even communications still present, the transport signal fluctuates violently. At times looking like the crew of [Root.GetName] is just about to loose the signal the experience and skill of the transport operator finally saves the day as the away team materializes on board of the ship.
STH_the_royale.9.name: Transporting
STH_tng.1000.desc:
Certain technologies require specialisation and branching paths to advance further in that area of research.

Moving forward into new technological eras requires a selection of these technologies for human and AI players, once a decision has been made you are bound to that path for this playthrough as you would be in a regular STNH playthrough so pick carefully. 

Dev Note: You must select all specialisations before progressing to the next screen.
STH_tng.1000.name: Technology Specialisation
STH_tng.12.desc: We can see that you have selected to play as one of the individual Federation Founders, we would recommend that for the best experience on this Pre-TNG era scenario that you play as the United Federation of Planets (2300) from the Empire Select screen. This will ensure you have the designed Federation experience whilst playing this map.
STH_tng.12.name: Warning!
STH_tng.15.desc: The Neutral Zone was established by treaty after the end of the Earth-Romulan War and has existed ever since, creating a buffer between the United Federation of Planets and the Romulan Star Empire. Entry into the Neutral Zone is an constitutes act of war and has kept the peace between these two major powers for many years.
STH_tng.15.name: Romulan Neutral Zone
STH_tng.16.desc: The Neutral Zone was established by treaty after the end of the Earth-Romulan War and has existed ever since, creating a buffer between the United Federation of Planets and the Romulan Star Empire. Entry into the Neutral Zone is an constitutes act of war and has kept the peace between these two major powers for many years.
STH_tng.16.name: Federation Neutral Zone
STH_tng.21.desc: The Neutral Zone separating the Romulans and Federation has been dismantled, following the agreed cut-off date in the establishing treaty. \n\nSystems ceded to the Neutral Zone have been returned to their previous owners.
STH_tng.21.name: Federation-Romulan Neutral Zone Dismantled
STH_tng.23.desc: The onset of War has effectively made the need for a Neutral Zone between our two states redundant. We must now decide whether to abolish the zone or leave it in place.
STH_tng.23.name: Disband Neutral Zone
STH_tng.50.desc:
President [Root.GetRulerName], we have had word from our shipyards above Earth confirming that the USS Enterprise-B has successfully returned from it's last mission ahead of the her proposed refits. 

We need confirmation from yourself on what direction we should the refit in. 

You can build either

Science Focused Flagships: This version of the flagship is a weaponised science ship and allows Scientists or Heroic Scientists to survey and explore new systems.



Military Focused Flagships: This version of the flagship is military ship with increased performance modifiers and allows Admirals or Heroic Admirals to lead fleets into conflict to defend the Federation borders.
STH_tng.50.name: The Enterprise-B
STH_tng.54.desc: I am Ditagh and I am here to challenge the leadership of the Empire, for too long have we been ruled by those who fear war, who are too weak to challenge other empires, we are Klingon warriors and this cannot be allowed to continue!
STH_tng.54.name: Leadership Challenge
STH_tng.55.desc: [Root.GetRulerName], you lead the Empire without honor and your refusal to help our allies in their battle with the Romulans is an act of cowardice, I challenge you for leadership of the Empire.
STH_tng.55.name: Leadership Challenge
STH_tng_mu.2.desc: Our alliance has brought us glory and honour in equal amounts, but most importantly it has crushed the foul Terran Empire and placed us as the dominant power across the Quadrant. \n\nThe time has come to cement the alliance as more than just a battle force, we must decide where to base our capital and centre of our new Empire.
STH_tng_mu.2.name: Klingon-Cardassian Alliance
STH_tng_mu.20.desc:
Times have been harsh on the Terrans since the glory days, the short-lived Republic became a target for the newly formed Klingon-Cardassian Alliance, who rose to power and swept across former Terran Empire worlds pushing them back to Earth and subjecting its people. 

Under a band of war heroes a brave resistance was started and managed to reclaim key Terran strongholds whilst The Alliance's attentions were elsewhere. 

The future of the Terrans now sits with a new generation, led by heroes from a past era.
STH_tng_mu.20.name: Terran Resistance
STH_tng_mu.21.desc: Having fought for their freedom the Cardassians and Klingons banded together to form a powerful new force in the Quadrant, one capable of taking on the Terrans and that is what happened. The Alliance purged the remains of the Terran Empire pushing them back to Sol. \n\nDistractions from races that were keen to take advantage of the fall of the Terran Empire meant that the Alliance lost control of Sol, but confidence remains high that the threat from Earth has been crushed.
STH_tng_mu.21.name: Klingon-Cardassian Alliance
STH_tng_mu.22.desc: As the Terran Empire fell, many of the races long-held as subjects by the Terrans took their chance to break free and reclaim their worlds from the iron grip of the Terran Emperor and formed a new republic, one built on peace, hope, and freedom. \n\nMany within this Republic are aware of the looming threat of the Alliance and others cannot forget the risk that even a defeated Terran Empire could pose.
STH_tng_mu.22.name: Republic of Hope
STH_tng_mu.3.desc: The Prophets have brought us our liberation from the Terrans, but many pragmatists believe the real source of our liberation is the powerful Klingon-Cardassian Alliance and they stand before us offering us the chance to join their forces so that we may never again fear the phaser of another empire.
STH_tng_mu.3.name: Join the Alliance
STH_tng_mu.4.desc: The Bajorans have now agreed to become the latest member of our grand alliance. They will join as a show of strength and a reminder to anyone that there is a new power in the Quadrant.
STH_tng_mu.4.name: Bajor joins the Alliance
STH_tng_mu.5.desc: We have lived too long under the oppression of the Terrans and our freedom came at a terrible price, but with the Terran Empire in ruins now is our chance to form our republic, a republic free from those who seek to oppress us.
STH_tng_mu.5.name: Republic of Hope
STH_tomed.10.a: We are moving into a dangerous situation.
STH_tomed.10.desc: [ufp_tomed_military_leader.GetName] has reported in from their mission to reinforce our borders along the boundary with the Romulan Star Empire. \n\nThe additional fleets are in position and are preparing to take a defensive stance, should Romulus attempt to cross into our territory.
STH_tomed.10.name: Military Operation Complete
STH_tomed.11.desc: The build-up of Starfleet forces along our borders cannot be allowed to continue! They flaunt the sovereignty of our worlds and stir the wrath of the populace, we urge you to take action and allow us to unleash our forces and stage the first-strike attack before they can complete the defense structures around their colony. This maybe the only opportunity we have to strike from the shadows.
STH_tomed.11.name: Federation provocation
STH_tomed.12.desc: Reports are coming in from our outposts on [tomed_incident_planet2.GetName] it appears an unknown force has launched an unprovoked attack on the planet. Starfleet Command has every reason to believe this is an attack from the Romulan Star Empire and has the intention of destroying the colony and all Federation citizens who live there.
STH_tomed.12.name: The Tomed Incident
STH_tomed.14.desc: Reports are coming in from our fleet, it appears they have made it into the Federation's territory and are preparing to launch their attack against the Federation. They will soon witness the full power of our forces.
STH_tomed.14.name: The Tomed Incident
STH_tomed.17.desc: The commander of our fleet has reported in and we are pleased to announce that they have delivered a crippling blow against the Federation. The world that mocked us has been rendered nothing but ashes. \n\nJustice has been delivered by the swift hand of the raptor.
STH_tomed.17.name: The Tomed Incident
STH_tomed.18.a: We must decide our next move.
STH_tomed.18.desc: Our fleets have successfully repelled the attack, defending our colonists and preventing a potentially devastating loss of lives. \n\nIt is clear from the wreckage that the attackers were from the Romulan Star Empire and that this is a clear act of open hostility towards us.
STH_tomed.18.name: The Tomed Incident
STH_tomed.19.desc: Word has reached the senate that our attack against the Federation has been repelled by their defence forces and the planned destruction of their colony was not completed. \n\nHowever our point has been made.
STH_tomed.19.name: The Tomed Incident
STH_tomed.2.c: This is not in our interests.
STH_tomed.2.desc:
Greetings Praetor, I am [tomed_romulan_leader.GetName] and I bring word from our Tal Shiar field operatives from within the United Federation of Planets 

Those Federation veruul appear to be on the cusp of a new technological breakthrough that could threaten our security. 

It may be the time that we remind those Federation cowards why they should fear our Imperial Might.
STH_tomed.2.name: Tal Shiar Report
STH_tomed.21.desc: Word has reached our ears that the Romulans have launched a cowardly attack against the United Federation of Planets, Many warriors will ask why this should concern us and we answer their recent actions at Khitomer have demonstrated a small degree of honour and this attack by those Romulan fools is cowardly and without honour. \n\nMeaning many of our warriors feel we should aid them and many are just hungry for war.
STH_tomed.21.name: Romulus attacks the Federation
STH_tomed.22.desc: Greetings President [Root.GetRulerName], I bring you aid from your friends in the Klingon Empire. We are aware of the cowardly attack against you by the Romulans and we offer you our warriors, who are eager to taste Romulan blood. \n\nGive us the word and we shall strike.
STH_tomed.22.name: Klingon aid
STH_tomed.23.desc: With the Klingon's at our side, we are in a strong position to respond to this attack from Romulus. There is a strong faction within Starfleet and the Federation that is strongly demanding we respond to this attack with the full force of Starfleet, bringing open war to the quadrant. \n\nYet there are calmer voices who urge that we resolve this with words and not torpedoes, if the Romulan's really wanted war, then surely they would have already declared war on us?
STH_tomed.23.name: Starfleet Security Meeting
STH_tomed.24.desc: We stand alone against the power of Romulus. There is a strong faction within Starfleet and the Federation that is strongly demanding we respond to this attack with the full force of Starfleet, bringing open war to the quadrant. \n\nYet there are calmer voices who urge that we resolve this with words and not torpedoes, if the Romulan's really wanted war, then surely they would have already declared war on us?
STH_tomed.24.name: Starfleet Security Meeting
STH_tomed.25.desc_01: We have been contacted by the Federation, backed by the Klingons, they wish to open talks with us in a bid to avoid a full-scale war. \n\nThey state that this is a chance to de-escalate the situation. This involvement from the Klingon Empire is an unwelcomed development...
STH_tomed.25.desc_02: We have been contacted by the Federation, they wish to open talks with us in a bid to avoid a full-scale war. \n\nThey state that this is a chance to de-escalate the situation.
STH_tomed.25.desc_03: We have been contacted by the Federation, backed by the Klingons, they wish to open talks with us in a bid to avoid a full-scale war. \n\nThey state that this is a chance to de-escalate the situation as well as discussing the future usage of the cloaking technology that they have been developing. This involvement from the Klingon Empire is an unwelcome development...
STH_tomed.25.desc_04: We have been contacted by the Federation, they wish to open talks with us in a bid to avoid a full-scale war. \n\nThey state that this is a chance to de-escalate the situation as well as to discuss the future usage of the cloaking technology that they have been developing.
STH_tomed.25.name: Incoming Message from Starfleet
STH_tomed.26.desc: The Federation has demonstrated a backbone that few in the senate believed they could possess and have declared war on us. \n\nWe shall crush them under the heel of the Romulan war machine.
STH_tomed.26.name: War has been declared
STH_tomed.27.desc: Our attempts to avoid war with Romulus have failed and they have declared war on us. We shall soon see who is the stronger force.
STH_tomed.27.name: War has been declared
STH_tomed.28.desc: We have received word from the Romulan Praetor, it appears they are willing to talk and have named [treaty_of_algeron_planet.GetName] as a suitable location to host this critical conference.
STH_tomed.28.name: The Treaty of Algeron
STH_tomed.29.a: Insist on the withdrawal of all troops.
STH_tomed.29.desc: As [algeron_negotiator.GetName] on board the [algeron_ship.GetName] arrives at the talks, both sides sit around the table and prepare to attempt a diplomatic solution.
STH_tomed.29.name: The Treaty of Algeron
STH_tomed.3.a: Provide our own demonstration of power.
STH_tomed.3.c: Speed up our cloaking tech research.
STH_tomed.3.desc: Sir! [tomed_federation_leader.GetName] reporting! Our sensors trained on the Romulan border have detected a massive build-up of the Romulan Navy, a clear demonstration of their military might, and a provocation against us. \n\nIn addition we have had multiple reports of Imperial Warbirds crossing the border and making incursions into our territory around [tomed_incident_planet2.GetName] which houses a number of vital research projects.
STH_tomed.3.name: Starfleet Security Report
STH_tomed.30.b: Agree, but insist on a cloaking ban.
STH_tomed.30.desc: As both sides sit down to talk, the Federation open the talks with a reasonable request for both sides to withdraw their ships to their own sides of the border. \n\nNow is our turn to propose a counter proposal.
STH_tomed.30.name: The Treaty of Algeron
STH_tomed.31.desc_01: The Romulan negotiator takes a moment and then agrees that both sides should de-escalate the situation and stand down their forces and withdraw behind the established borders. \n\nWith many now suffering on the worlds that were attacked, many are calling on reparations from Romulus to help the colony recover, however this is unlikely to be an agreeable option.
STH_tomed.31.desc_02:
The Romulan negotiator takes a moment and then agrees that both sides should de-escalate the situation and stand down their forces and withdraw behind the established borders. 

Additionally, the Romulans are demanding that we abandon our research into Cloaking and place a ban on all further research into this area. 

With many now suffering on the worlds that were attacked, many are calling on reparations from Romulus to help the colony recover, perhaps trading cloaking tech for aid helping our citizens is an agreeable trade off?
STH_tomed.31.name: The Treaty of Algeron
STH_tomed.32.desc_01: The Federation diplomats return to the meeting table, they agree to our terms and will implement a ban on all current and future research into Cloaking technology and abandon their current plans. However, as a compromise, they would like to see additional support and funds from Romulus to help recover the colony that was damaged during our attack.
STH_tomed.32.desc_02: The Federation diplomats return to the meeting table, they agree to our terms and will implement a ban on all current and future research into Cloaking technology and abandon their current plans. They state this is an act of good faith and a demonstration of their focus on peace.
STH_tomed.32.desc_03: The Federation diplomats return to the table with a new request. In order to establish peace, we need to undo the damage caused and as such we wish to receive reparations to help the damaged colony recover and repair the damaged caused in the attack.
STH_tomed.32.desc_04: The Federation diplomats return to the table their appearance is rigid, and their faces are tense. \n\nThey reject any proposal to ban cloaking and insist they will continue this line of research. Furthermore than are insisting that Romulus pays a form of reparation for the damage caused during the attack on their colony.
STH_tomed.32.name: The Treaty of Algeron
STH_tomed.33.desc: It appears the negotiations are over, as the Romulans storm away from the conference. Declaring that the talks are over and this will be settled by Phasers and Torpedoes.
STH_tomed.33.name: War declared
STH_tomed.34.desc_01: The Romulans return to the negotiating table and declare that the Romulan Star Empire will sign the Treaty of Algeron and as long as we abandon our plans to develop cloaking technology they will provide aid to our damaged colony.
STH_tomed.34.desc_02: The Romulans return to the negotiating table and declare that the Romulan Star Empire will sign the Treaty of Algeron and put an end to our research into Cloaking Technology.
STH_tomed.34.desc_03: The Romulans return to the negotiating table and declare that the Romulan Star Empire will sign the Treaty of Algeron and despite our refusal to put a ban on cloaking and insistence on receiving reparations, they are still willing to sign the treaty.
STH_tomed.34.name: The Treaty of Algeron
STH_tomed.35.desc: Today is a historic day between the United Federation of Planets and The Romulan Star Empire. The Treaty of Algeron has been signed creating a truce between our worlds and bringing both sides back from certain war. \n\nWe can only see what the future now holds.
STH_tomed.35.name: The Treaty of Algeron
STH_tomed.36.desc: It appears the Federation and Romulan Star Empire have signed a new treaty, which draws a line under their recent conflicts and maintains peace in the Alpha and Beta Quadrants for now.
STH_tomed.36.name: The Treaty of Algeron
STH_tomed.37.desc: The Romulan delegations storms out of the meeting room, loudly declaring they will not sign this 'sham of a treaty'. It looks like all chances of signing the treaty have now been lost and the Alpha and Beta Quadrants must hold their breath and await an uncertain future.
STH_tomed.37.name: Treaty Rejected
STH_tomed.39.desc: After the dedicated team of engineers, doctors and volunteers complete the many nights of hard work, much of the damage has been cleared and many parts of the core infrastructure have been returned to working order. Whilst many of the scars will remain long in the minds of the survivors at least much of the physical damage could be repaired.
STH_tomed.39.name: Colony receives help
STH_tomed.4.desc: It is clear the threat of directly challenging the Romulan Star Empire, was just too much for the cowards on [ufp_country_tomed.capital_scope] and they have backed down. The Romulan Star Empire remains supreme.
STH_tomed.4.name: Federation Backs Down
STH_tomed.40.desc: Our colony on [tomed_attacked_colony.GetName] has been attacked by a force of Romulan ships in an episode that has [tomed_attacked_colony.GetName] Incident. \n\nWork has already started to repair the colony and heal the wounds, we need a moment to gather our thoughts before we respond to this attack.
STH_tomed.40.name: Planet [tomed_attacked_colony.GetName] attacked
STH_tomed.41.b: It is not worth the risk.
STH_tomed.41.desc: Hi, I am [fed_science_leader.GetName] our science division has come to me with an exciting new proposal. Since we first engaged the Romulans and Klingon's we have been in fear and awe of their cloaking devices. \n\nOur scientists believe we have uncovered its secrets and with some investment could replicate this technology to bring us onto a level playing field. It is remiss of me to mention that proceeding with this line of research could be seen as an act of aggression towards the Romulan or Klingon Empires...
STH_tomed.41.name: Cloaking Technology
STH_tomed.42.desc: Our time and efforts have finally paid off, we have unlocked the secrets of cloaking technology, which is now available to complete via the research screen.
STH_tomed.42.name: Now they see us...
STH_tomed.5.a: This is not in our interests.
STH_tomed.6.b: We cannot confront them at this time.
STH_tomed.6.desc:
Our Tal Shiar operatives have completed their assignment and bring back troubling news. 

It seems that the Federation scientists have made a significant breakthrough in Cloaking Technology. This technology has given us an advantage over the Federation for many years now and the fact they are now actively progressing this line of research is a direct threat against the Empire! 

We cannot let this threat to our territory remain unchallenged.
STH_tomed.6.name: Federation Cloaking Technology
STH_tomed.7.desc: Our bid to uncover the secrets of the Federation's mystery technology has been unsuccessful and to make matters worse our operative has failed to return from Federation space. As any good operative should do, they will have ensured they were not taken alive.
STH_tomed.7.name: Tal Shiar Operation Failed
STH_tomed.8.c: We do not back down from bullies.
STH_tomed.8.desc:
Federation fools I bring you a threat from your superiors in the Romulan Star Empire. 

We know all about your attempt to duplicate our Cloaking Technology, which you no doubt stole from us in the first place. 

You will cease all efforts to duplicate our cloaking devices and withdraw your fleets from our borders or face the consequences of displeasing Romulus.
STH_tomed.8.name: Transmission From Romulus
STH_tomed.9.desc_01: It appears the Federation has more courage than we gave them credit for, and have refused to cower like the varuul that they are. \n\nOur monitoring stations are picking up increased activity along our borders and a build-up of Starfleet warships.
STH_tomed.9.desc_02: As expected the Federation has no backbone and they have crumpled beneath the threats we have made. \n\nOnce again the Romulan Star Empire has demonstrated its rightful place at the top of pack.
STH_tomed.9.name: Reponse from Starfleet Command
STH_traditions.50.d: There is no profit in this venture.
STH_traditions.50.desc: As rule of Acquisition 98 states, every man has his price... our agents have identified a number of key leaders from some foreign powers who might be worth testing how much latinum it would take for them to provide us with a few 'details' about their empire. This could be very profitable if one of our leaders could seal the deal.
STH_traditions.50.name: Every Man Has His Price
STH_traditions.51.d: There is no profit in this venture.
STH_traditions.51.desc: Now that we have selected who will lead the bribery bid, we must decide which of our relations we should target. Any information we can gleam from these foreign powers could be very profitable and will please our Nagus greatly.
STH_traditions.51.name: Every Man Has His Price
STH_traditions.52.d: There is no profit in this venture.
STH_traditions.52.desc: Now down to business, a bribe is only as successful as the amount of Latinum willing to be gambled. \n\nShould we gamble high or low in our attempt to obtain this intel.
STH_traditions.52.name: Every Man Has His Price
STH_traditions.54.desc: Never doubt the flow of the great material Continuum for it always delivers and once again it has provided. Our efforts to bribe the official in [bribe_country_choosen_intel.GetName] has been successful and [bribe_leader_choosen_intel.GetName] now has a wealth of information, we must put this to good use.
STH_traditions.54.name: Bribe Successful
STH_traditions.55.desc: Curses! Curses! Clearly [bribe_leader_choosen_intel.GetName] was not wise or capable of navigating the Great River and they have been grounded on its shores. \n\nAll this means that we have wasted our Latinum and failed to gain the precious intel on [bribe_country_choosen_intel.GetName].
STH_traditions.55.name: Bribe Failed
STH_traditions.60.desc: The spirit of Kahless lives in the heart of every Klingon warrior, no matter which house or faction. The Call of Kahless strengthens the bonds between the Great Houses and the Empire. Uniting the Klingons and making the empire powerful.
STH_traditions.60.name: The Spirit of Kahless
STH_traditions.70.a: Research the Excelsior II and Obena.
STH_traditions.70.desc: The great workhorse of our fleet the Excelsior is starting to show its age, it has served the fleet well for many years. Now the Starfleet Corp of Engineers has come to us with a proposal, the Excelsior II-Class and Obena-Class two major transformations of the Excelsior bringing the class into the modern era. \n\nCommissioning the research will allow us to build this new class of ship ready to take on the work of its venerable predecessor. Buildable as either the Excelsior II or the Obena.
STH_traditions.70.name: Time for the Next Great Experiment
STH_traditions.72.desc: The Deimos class was a 24th century Federation starship class, a Synth frigate and later pilot destroyer in Synth and Starfleet service in the 2380s and again from the 2410s decade. It was named for Deimos, the smaller moon of Mars, a planet in the Sol star system.
STH_traditions.72.name: The God of Fear
STH_traditions.80.desc: For too long our neighbours have converted our territory and claimed worlds that should be right belong to us. Our governing body has granted us the chance to right this wrong and place a casus belli against them.
STH_traditions.80.name: What is rightfully ours
STH_traditions.81.desc:
You have unlocked the Starfleet Ships of the Line Tradition tree, this grants access to technology which unlocks new models of Starfleet ships, loosely based off the series of the same name. These ships are optional extras which add a bit of extra flavour to your fleets. 

Each tradition unlocks a number of ships which then become available to add as research items via the Jupiter Station Research Megastructure (Science Station) once you reach the appropriate era. 

 Enjoy!
STH_traditions.81.name: Starfleet Ships of the Line
STH_traditions.85.desc: Section 31 stealth ships shared a design lineage that was based on the smaller intel science destroyer. Apart from a size difference, the S31 ship classes shared the design for warp nacelles, nacelle pylons, bridge module and deflector dish.
STH_traditions.85.name: Section 31
STH_traditions.86.desc: The Osler-type Federation starship was in use by Division 14 in the early 2380s. This ship type was smaller than California-class in size, and capable of atmospheric entry and landing. The Osler had a black hull with a darkened interior and was typically used in circumstances where a classically designed Federation ship would draw too much attention.
STH_traditions.86.name: Division 14
STH_tribbles.10.desc: In its weakened condition with systems failing due to Tribbles shorting out key systems the [tribble_ship.GetName] was unable to mount an adequet defence and was overwhelmed by its attackers and ultimately destroyed.
STH_tribbles.10.name: Ship destroyed!
STH_tribbles.11.desc: The trouble with Tribbles is they get everywhere including places they should not go. In their attempts to find more food to eat a number of the critters made their way into key systems aboard the [tribble_ship.GetName]. Their presence in these vital systems caused widespread damage as vital systems shorted out and overloaded, leaving the ship severely damaged. The good news is that power cores and Tribbles do not mix, leaving fewer animals for the crew to remove.
STH_tribbles.11.name: Ship damaged
STH_tribbles.12.desc: After the crews pulled double and triple shifts, the crew of [tribble_ship.GetName] managed to clear all Tribbles from within the ship and return life to normal.
STH_tribbles.12.name: A Tribble free zone
STH_tribbles.2.desc: [Root.GetLeaderTitle] [Root.GetLeaderName], we have had word from our colony on [tribble_invasion_planet.GetName] it appears the local Tribble population has begun breeding uncontrollable and has started destroying our harvests. It is predicted that if they continue to breed at this rate they will do significant damage to our food reserves on the planet. They may be cute and fluffy, but they most be stopped!
STH_tribbles.2.name: Troublesome Tribbles
STH_tribbles.21.desc: [Root.GetName]! One of our colonies on [tribble_crisis_planet.GetName] presents us with a glorious opertunity, the cursed Tribble patak have been sighted in the fields nearly the colony. We should organise a grand Tribble Hunt to round up and destroy every one of these beasts.\n\nGreat songs will be song of our victories here.
STH_tribbles.21.name: The Great Tribble Hunt
STH_tribbles.22.desc: Our weapons fire was unable to obliterate all of the accursed creatures and it seems like the fire has stirred up their bloodlust, resulting in increased breeding.\n\nWe now have a much larger population of Tribbles on the planet our estimates say it could take up to five years to fully clean up the planet.
STH_tribbles.22.name: Revenge of the Tribble
STH_tribbles.23.a: Songs will be song of this day.
