STH_vulcan_story.1024.desc: While positions were hardened by the recent protests, an effective civil information campaign has reduced tensions and offered the chance for calm to prevail on [Root.Capital.GetName]. \n\nWhile not embraced, the Syrrannites feel secure with the new tolerant attitude of the Council.
STH_vulcan_story.1024.name: Civil Reconciliation
STH_vulcan_story.1025.desc: The [From.GetName] has walked itself back from the brink of civil war by lessening restrictions on its Syrrannite minority.
STH_vulcan_story.1025.name: [From.GetSpeciesName] Civil Reconciliation
STH_vulcan_story.1026.desc:
With the Kir'Shara revealed to the public, the teachings of Surak are no longer mired in assumption and the vagaries of oral tradition. The Syrrannite movement is shown to be an accurate representation of Surak's beliefs. 

As this new reality is absorbed, Syrrannites old and new sweep into power. 

Reflecting the broader changes to [Root.GetSpeciesName] culture that are about to take place, the [Root.GetName] will be reformed into the Confederacy of Surak.
STH_vulcan_story.1026.name: [Root.GetSpeciesName] Reformation
STH_vulcan_story.1027.desc:
With the revelation to the [From.GetSpeciesName] public of the Kir'Shara and the collected teachings of Surak, the minority Syrrannite movement is shown to be an accurate representation of Surak's beliefs. 

As this new reality is absorbed, Syrrannites old and new sweep into power. 

Reflecting the broader changes to [From.GetSpeciesName] culture that are about to take place, the Vulcan High Command will be reformed into the Confederacy of Surak.
STH_vulcan_story.1027.name: [From.GetSpeciesName] Reformation
STH_vulcan_story.1028.desc: The [Root.GetName] investigator [FromFrom.GetName], tasked with uncovering the truth of the embassy bombing on [From.Capital.GetName] has been instrumental in revealing the innocence of the Syrrannite movement to [From.GetSpeciesName] authorities, and reclaiming the Kir'Shara artefact containing the complete writings of Surak. \n\nHow the [From.GetSpeciesName] people absorb and deal with this information now is hard to gauge, but [FromFrom.GetName] has completed [FromFrom.GetHerHis] task and will be returning to service.
STH_vulcan_story.1028.name: Kir'Shara
STH_vulcan_story.1029.desc:
Breaking free from restraint, [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName] once again attempts to attack the Kir'Shara. 

In the ensuing struggle however, [Root.Leader.GetSheHe] is killed by [Root.Leader.GetHerHis] own weapon. 

As the Council requests time to consider what must be done next, [FromFrom.GetName] is returned to the care of the [From.GetName] embassy staff. 

Most of the Council believe that the Kir'Shara must be immediately revealed to the public, however some argue it represents unwelcome instability.
STH_vulcan_story.1029.name: Kir'Shara
STH_vulcan_story.1030.desc: [FromFrom.GetName] has been killed while attempting to leave [From.Capital.GetName] in a stolen shuttle, vaporised by an orbiting cruiser after refusing to be boarded. \n\nCarrying the Kir'Shara, the destruction of this ancient artefact is of immeasurable loss. While the [From.GetName] itself claims it was performing a legal operation, relations between [Root.Capital.GetName] and [From.Capital.GetName] are strained as a result.
STH_vulcan_story.1030.name: Destroyed Shuttle
STH_vulcan_story.1031.desc: [FromFrom.GetName] has been killed with a number of Syrrannite rebels while attempting to leave [Root.Capital.GetName] in a stolen shuttle, vaporised by an orbiting cruiser after refusing to be boarded. \n\nWhile the [Root.GetName] itself was performing a legal operation, relations between [Root.Capital.GetName] and [From.Capital.GetName] are strained as a result.
STH_vulcan_story.1031.name: Destroyed Shuttle
STH_vulcan_story.1032.desc:
[FromFrom.GetName] has been forced to make a crash landing while attempting to leave [From.Capital.GetName] in a stolen shuttle, attacked by an orbiting cruiser after refusing to be boarded. 

Carrying the Kir'Shara, [FromFrom.GetName] now attempts to make the journey from the crash site to the Capital on foot. 

While the [From.GetName] itself claims it was performing a legal operation, relations between [Root.Capital.GetName] and [From.Capital.GetName] are strained as a result.
STH_vulcan_story.1032.name: Disabled Shuttle
STH_vulcan_story.1033.desc:
Syrrannite rebels have been forced to make a crash landing while attempting to leave [From.Capital.GetName] in a stolen shuttle, attacked by an orbiting cruiser after refusing to be boarded. 

Arriving at the scene of the crash too late to capture the criminals, their location is now unknown. 

While the [From.GetName] itself claims it was performing a legal operation, relations between [Root.Capital.GetName] and [From.Capital.GetName] are strained as a result.
STH_vulcan_story.1033.name: Disabled Shuttle
STH_vulcan_story.1034.desc: The [From.GetName] has refused the [Root.GetName]'s formal request to investigate the embassy bombing on [From.Capital.GetName]. Assuring [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName] that [From.GetSpeciesName] authorities are better placed to uncover the truth of the attack, many on [Root.Capital.GetName] are angered by the snub.
STH_vulcan_story.1034.name: Embassy Bombing
STH_vulcan_story.1035.desc:
After several days analysing the rubble of the embassy building and questioning identified witnesses, investigators have concluded that the evidence does indeed point to the Syrrannites as the perpetrators of the bombing. 

Some on the investigation team believe the evidence seems almost too strong, however. 

Several chemical traces which link to materials found in the Vulcan's Forge region of the planet, where the Syrrannites are known to keep a hidden commune, should have decayed within minutes of the blast and be barely detectable now.
STH_vulcan_story.1035.name: Embassy Bombing
STH_vulcan_story.1036.desc: A [From.GetName] team has arrived on [Root.Capital.GetName] to begin their investigation of the embassy bombing.
STH_vulcan_story.1036.name: Embassy Bombing
STH_vulcan_story.1037.desc: Having failed to dispatch an team to [From.Capital.GetName] in time, [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName] has been forced to allow the [From.GetName] to take control of the embassy bombing investigation.
STH_vulcan_story.1037.name: Embassy Bombing
STH_vulcan_story.1038.desc:
Despite receiving permission to dispatch a team to investigate the embassy bombing, the [From.GetName] has failed to do so. As such the [Root.GetName] is now in charge of the operation. 

After several days analysing the rubble of the embassy building and questioning identified witnesses, investigators have concluded that the evidence does indeed point to the Syrrannites as the perpetrators of the bombing. 

Some on the investigation team believe the evidence seems almost too strong, however. 

Several chemical traces which link to materials found in the Vulcan's Forge region of the planet, where the Syrrannites are known to keep a hidden commune, should have decayed within minutes of the blast and be barely detectable now.
STH_vulcan_story.1038.name: Embassy Bombing
STH_vulcan_story.1050.desc:
The [From.GetName] has accepted the superiority of [Root.GetSpeciesName] local investigators in the matter of the embassy bombing and offered material support. 

After several days analysing the rubble of the embassy building and questioning identified witnesses, investigators have concluded that the evidence does indeed point to the Syrrannites as the perpetrators of the bombing. 

Some on the investigation team believe the evidence seems almost too strong, however. 

Several chemical traces which link to materials found in the Vulcan's Forge region of the planet, where the Syrrannites are known to keep a hidden commune, should have decayed within minutes of the blast and be barely detectable now.
STH_vulcan_story.1050.name: Embassy Bombing
STH_vulcan_story.1051.desc: The [From.GetName] has publicly blamed the minority Syrrannite group for the bombing of the [Root.GetName] embassy on [From.Capital.GetName]. Promising to increase security and prevent further incidents, [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName] accepts the results of the investigation.
STH_vulcan_story.1051.name: Embassy Bombing
STH_vulcan_story.1052.desc: Following accusations of Syrrannite involvement in recent terrorist activity, many individuals sympathetic to the group have begun protests declaring their innocence in cities across [Root.Capital.GetName]. \n\nSecurity forces have been instructed to document the agitators.
STH_vulcan_story.1052.name: Syrrannite Protests
STH_vulcan_story.1053.desc: Syrrannite protests continue to ramp up across [Root.Capital.GetName]. While society is divided in support and opposition to the group the risk of conflict grows from an absurdity to a real threat. \n\nIn a victory for the [Root.GetSpyService], the location of the Syrrannite commune in the Vulcan's Forge region has been revealed. Several in the administration now argue for a strike to capture or kill the terrorist leadership.
STH_vulcan_story.1053.name: Syrrannite Protests
STH_vulcan_story.1054.desc: The operation against the Syrrannite commune appears to be a success. While several Syrrannites were able to escape, we have confirmed the death of their leader Syrran. \n\nWhile it is regretable that lives weren't saved and the terrorists brought to justice, [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName] insists that the strike has made [Root.Capital.GetName] safer.
STH_vulcan_story.1054.name: Syrrannite Protests
STH_vulcan_story.1055.desc_01: Despite the destruction of their commune, rumours suggest that several prominent Syrrannites have made their way to the Capital itself. Unbelievably, it is implied they have brought with them the legendary Kir'Shara, an artefact containing the complete writings of Surak, thought lost to time. \n\nWhat the terrorists plan to do next is a mystery.
STH_vulcan_story.1055.desc_02: Rumours suggest that several prominent Syrrannites have made their way to the Capital itself. Unbelievably, it is implied they have brought with them the legendary Kir'Shara, an artefact containing the complete writings of Surak, thought lost to time. \n\nWhat the terrorists plan to do next is a mystery.
STH_vulcan_story.1055.name: Syrrannite Protests
STH_vulcan_story.1056.desc_01:
Despite ample security in the Council Chambers, the Syrranite leader himself, Syrran has infiltrated and taken hostage the Councillors present. 

Presenting the Kir'Shara and the Syrrannites claims of innocence, many were awed by the presence of the artefact. 

[Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName] reacted with uncharacteristic anger and attempted to destroy the artefact and kill Syrran in the process, but was [Root.Leader.GetHerHim]self killed in the struggle. 

With his evidence presented, Syrran withdraws leaving the Kir'Shara to the Council to decide on how to proceed in the interests of the [Root.GetSpeciesName] people.
STH_vulcan_story.1056.desc_02:
Despite ample security in the Council Chambers, the Syrranite known as T'Pau has infiltrated and taken hostage the Councillors present. 

Presenting the Kir'Shara and the Syrrannites claims of innocence, many were awed by the presence of the artefact. 

[Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName] reacted with uncharacteristic anger and attempted to destroy the artefact and kill T'Pau in the process, but was [Root.Leader.GetHerHim]self killed in the struggle. 

With his evidence presented, T'Pau withdraws leaving the Kir'Shara to the Council to decide on how to proceed in the interests of the [Root.GetSpeciesName] people.
STH_vulcan_story.1056.name: Syrrannite Protests
STH_vulcan_story.1057.desc: Following dramatic scenes in the [From.Capital.GetName] Capital, the [From.GetRulerTitle] has been killed following an alteraction with Syrrannites in the Council Chambers. \n\nClaiming to have been falsely implicated by the former [From.GetRulerTitle] in the recent embassy bombing, the Syrrannite leadership have presented the legendary Kir'Shara artefact to the Council to attempt to steer them to a path closer to Surak's teachings.
STH_vulcan_story.1057.name: Bombardeo de la embajada
STH_vulcan_story.1060.desc: The investigation team's suspicions continue to be raised by evidence regarded as too strongly in favour of blaming the Syrrannite movement for the embassy bombing. \n\nThe lead investigator herself suggests that only a fullscale mission into the dangerous Vulcan's Forge region may be able to decisively conclude matters.
STH_vulcan_story.1060.name: Bombardeo de la embajada
STH_vulcan_story.1061.desc:
Evidence has led the embassy bombing investigation team to the Vulcan's Forge region, where the Syrrannites are known to keep at least one hidden commune. 

A vast desert, The Forge is subject to electrical sandstorms and geomagnetic anomalies that interfere with scanning technologies. 

Progress through The Forge is slow made more dangerous by the constant threat of Sehlats attack.
STH_vulcan_story.1061.name: La forja
STH_vulcan_story.1062.desc: [From.GetName] ha confirmado a [Root.GetName] que el grupo minoritario sirio era de hecho responsable del reciente bombardeo de la embajada en [From.Capital.GetName]. \n\nIndicando que ya se están tomando medidas para limitar más ataques, [From.GetName] ha lamentado que no se haya evitado el bombardeo.
STH_vulcan_story.1062.name: Bombardeo de la embajada
STH_vulcan_story.1063.desc:
Después de refugiarse de una tormenta de arena eléctrica con un peregrino que pasa, el equipo de investigación de bombardeos de la embajada ha hecho un descubrimiento notable. Al referirse a sí mismo inicialmente como Arev, el peregrino es de hecho sirio, el líder del movimiento sirio. 

Profesando ignorancia del bombardeo de la embajada, promete llevar al equipo de regreso al complejo sirio en el Santuario T'Karath para probar el asunto. 

 Siguiendo el protocolo, el equipo de investigación esperó hasta que pasara la tormenta y regresó a su lanzadera, enviando la ubicación de la comuna siria al Consejo.
STH_vulcan_story.1063.name: La forja
STH_vulcan_story.1064.desc: Habiendo reducido el área provista por el equipo de investigación para la ubicación de la comuna siriana, [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName] argumenta al Consejo que un ataque ahora terminaría con el riesgo de nuevos ataques contra los sirios. \n\nAunque la pérdida de vidas sería lamentable, el [Root.GetRulerTitle] sugiere que quizás nunca haya un mejor momento para eliminar la amenaza terrorista.
STH_vulcan_story.1064.name: La forja
STH_vulcan_story.11.desc: After several days debating the available evidence, the Admiralty has authorised the dispatch of a strike team to [FromFrom.GetName].
STH_vulcan_story.11.name: Shadows of P'Jem
STH_vulcan_story.2.desc: The stealth subspace listening post has been constructed to schedule on [Root.GetName] and sensor range has been boosted significantly.
STH_vulcan_story.2.name: Listening Post on P'Jem
STH_vulcan_story.20.desc: [Root.Owner.GetCaptainTitle] [Root.GetLeaderName] has completed [Root.Leader.GetHerHis] work to assemble a strike team to head deep into [vulcanCountry.GetName] space and expose the stealth listening post on [vulcanCountry.GetSpeciesName] holy world of [pjem_planet.GetName]. \n\nThe mission is expected to take a long time to complete, as the team must use civilian transport for much of the journey.
STH_vulcan_story.20.name: Shadows of P'Jem
STH_vulcan_story.30.desc: The [FromFrom.GetSpeciesName] holy world of [FromFromFrom.GetName] appears to many on [Root.Capital.GetName] to be a paradox. The [FromFrom.GetSpeciesName] people claim to be innately logical, eschewing superstitions. However at the heart of their culture are a number of rituals and beliefs that are distinctly religious in nature. \n\nBy dispatching a cultural mission to the holy world of [FromFromFrom.GetName], we may be able to understand our neighbours better.
STH_vulcan_story.30.name: Monastery on P'Jem
STH_vulcan_story.31.desc: Arriving at [pjem_planet.GetName], [Root.Owner.GetCaptainTitle] [Root.GetLeaderName] is greeted in a perfunctory manner by T'Kosh, the monastery elder. \n\nAfter a short tour of the monastery entrance hall, T'Kosh appears eager to send [Root.GetLeaderName] on [Root.Leader.GetHerHis] way. Unsure whether the odd behaviour is typical for the monks to all visitors, or whether something else is bothering the elder [vulcanCountry.GetSpeciesName], [Root.GetLeaderName] mulls investigating further.
STH_vulcan_story.31.name: Monastery on P'Jem
STH_vulcan_story.32.desc: [Root.Owner.GetCaptainTitle] [Root.GetLeaderName]'s concern appears to have been unfounded. After investigating the monastery grounds further, the [Root.Owner.GetCaptainTitle] was able to determine nothing amiss other than the increasing frustrations of T'Kosh. \n\nAfter agreeing to the elder's insistence that they return to the [Root.Fleet.GetName] and leave the monks to their solitude, the away team is presented with a small gift - a number of scrolls, replicas of various ancient [From.GetSpeciesName] texts.
STH_vulcan_story.32.name: Monastery on P'Jem
STH_vulcan_story.33.b: This is none of our concern!
STH_vulcan_story.33.desc_01: [Root.Owner.GetCaptainTitle] [Root.GetLeaderName]'s concern appears to have been warranted, as further investigation of the grounds has uncovered the presence of an [andorianCountry.GetSpeciesName] strike team holding most of the monks hostage. \n\nClaiming that the monastery is being used to hide a subspace listening post targeting the [andorianCountry.GetName], their leader threatens to detain [Root.Owner.GetCaptainTitle] [Root.GetLeaderName] and the rest of the away team as [From.GetSpeciesName] collaborators.
STH_vulcan_story.33.desc_02: [Root.Owner.GetCaptainTitle] [Root.GetLeaderName]'s concern appears to have been warranted, as further investigation of the grounds has uncovered the presence of a strike team of blue-skinned aliens holding most of the monks hostage. \n\nIdentifying themselves as Andorians, they claim that the monastery is being used to hide a subspace listening post targeting their people. The strike team leader threatens to detain [Root.Owner.GetCaptainTitle] [Root.GetLeaderName] and the rest of the away team as [From.GetSpeciesName] collaborators.
STH_vulcan_story.33.name: Monastery on P'Jem
STH_vulcan_story.34.desc: The strike team leader is convinced by [Root.Owner.GetCaptainTitle] [Root.GetLeaderName]'s argument that the [Root.Owner.GetName] is not involved in the subterfuge, and agrees to let the team return to the [Root.Fleet.GetName] only after verifying the destruction of the ship's communications array to prevent warning the [From.GetName] government.
STH_vulcan_story.34.name: Monastery on P'Jem
STH_vulcan_story.35.desc: The strike team sent to [FromFromFrom.GetName] have signalled an update on their progress. Having secured the main monastery building and disabling the communications with the rest of the [FromFrom.GetName] complications arose when a [From.GetName] ship proceeded to visit the holy world on a cultural mission. \n\nConvinced that the [From.GetSpeciesName]s were not part of the plot to spy on the [Root.GetName], the strike team leader allowed the crew to return to their vessel after verifying the destruction of its communications array to prevent any signaling to the [FromFrom.GetSpeciesName]s before the mission completed.
STH_vulcan_story.35.name: Monastery on P'Jem
STH_vulcan_story.36.desc:
[Root.Owner.GetCaptainTitle] [Root.GetLeaderName]'s attempt to rescue the [From.GetSpeciesName] monks has failed. While overpowering several of the attackers seemed easy at first, their reinforcements were quick to fatally subdue to away team. 

Worse, they were subsequently able to transport aboard the [Root.Fleet.GetName] and fight their way to the ship's warp core. Deploying charges, a single attacker was able to hold off the crew until the rest of the strike team could return to the surface. 

Detonating the charges, the ship was obliterated as the warp core was breached.
STH_vulcan_story.36.name: Monastery on P'Jem
STH_vulcan_story.37.desc:
[Root.Owner.GetCaptainTitle] [Root.GetLeaderName]'s attempt to rescue the [From.GetSpeciesName] monks has succeeded as the away team quickly overpower the attackers. 

Damage to several monastery walls reveals a number of hidden spaces leading to a vast underground chamber. Inside, the subspace listening post the Andorians claimed was being used against them. 

Faced with the knowledge that the [From.GetName] had subverted one of their own holy sites for covert surveillance, [Root.Owner.GetCaptainTitle] [Root.GetLeaderName] mulls [Root.Leader.GetHerHis] options on how to respond.
STH_vulcan_story.37.name: Monastery on P'Jem
STH_vulcan_story.38.desc: The strike team sent to [FromFromFrom.GetName] have signalled an update on their progress. Having secured the main monastery building and disabling communications with the rest of the [FromFrom.GetName] complications arose when a [From.GetName] ship proceeded to visit the holy world on a cultural mission. \n\nThe [From.GetSpeciesName]s were subsequently killed after attempting to attack the strike team, and their vessel destroyed in orbit.
STH_vulcan_story.38.name: Monastery on P'Jem
STH_vulcan_story.39.desc.all: News that the [FromFrom.GetName] was using its holy sites to hide covert listening posts has spread rapidly after a strike team of [FromFromFrom.GetSpeciesName] elite forces struck [FromFromFromFrom.GetName]. \n\nWhile the [FromFromFrom.GetSpeciesName]s were subdued by the crew of a [From.GetName] ship on a cultural mission, when the [From.GetSpeciesName]s uncovered evidence supporting the [FromFromFrom.GetName]'s claims they allowed the strike team to return to [FromFromFrom.Capital.GetName] with their proof.
STH_vulcan_story.39.desc.andorian:
The strike team sent to [FromFromFromFrom.GetName] have signalled an update on their progress. Interrupted in their mission by a [From.GetName] cultural mission, the [From.GetSpeciesName]s were able to overpower and subdue our forces on [FromFromFromFrom.GetName]. 

Fortunately, the [From.GetSpeciesName] [From.GetCaptainTitle] unearthed evidence of the [FromFrom.GetSpeciesName] surveillance apparatus hidden underneath the monastery and has allowed our assets on [FromFromFromFrom.GetName] to share the news with the entire quadrant. 

It is clear that the [From.GetSpeciesName] people are more than just [FromFrom.GetSpeciesName] lap-sehlats, and many on [Root.Capital.GetName] believe we should attempt to improve relations with their government.
STH_vulcan_story.39.desc.instigator: News that the [FromFrom.GetName] was using its holy sites to hide covert listening posts spread rapidly after the [FromFromFrom.GetSpeciesName] strike team on [FromFromFromFrom.GetName] were allowed to depart. \n\nWhile the implications for [Root.Capital.GetName] - [FromFrom.Capital.GetName] relations are worrying, it appears that the [FromFromFrom.GetName] will be open to further dialogue in the future.
STH_vulcan_story.39.desc.vulcan:
Our covert subspace listening post on [FromFromFromFrom.GetName] has been revealed to the entire quadrant. 

An elite [FromFromFrom.GetSpeciesName] strike team had taken control of the monastery briefly before a [From.GetName] cultural mission arrived at the planet. While attempting a rescue of our people, the [From.GetSpeciesName]s uncovered evidence of the listening post. 

In an irrational display typical of their species, the [From.GetSpeciesName]s then proceeded to allow the [FromFromFrom.GetSpeciesName] team to withdraw and broadcast their findings. 

The listening post has been damaged beyond repair in the fighting, and worse our relations with the wider quadrant have suffered.
STH_vulcan_story.39.name: Monastery on P'Jem
STH_vulcan_story.40.desc.all: The [FromFromFrom.GetName] has shamed itself in the eyes of the galactic community, after dispatching a military mission to the defenceless [FromFrom.GetSpeciesName] holy world of [FromFromFromFrom.GetName]. \n\nThe [FromFromFrom.GetSpeciesName] strike team on [FromFromFromFrom.GetName] appear to have been thwarted by the crew of a passing [From.GetName] ship on a cultural mission to the planet.
STH_vulcan_story.40.desc.andorian: The strike team sent to [FromFromFromFrom.GetName] have failed in their mission and appear to have been captured by the [FromFrom.GetName] in conjunction with their [From.GetName] lap-sehlats. \n\nWhile the admiralty remains convinced that [FromFromFromFrom.GetName] harbours a covert listening post, we have no evidence to convince the wider quadrant of the [FromFrom.GetSpeciesName]'s subterfuge.
STH_vulcan_story.40.desc.instigator: By turning the [FromFromFrom.GetSpeciesName] strike team over to the authorities on [FromFrom.Capital.GetName], we have solidified our partnership with the [FromFrom.GetName]. \n\nWhile this may mean future relations with the [FromFromFrom.GetName] are strained, it is in [Root.Capital.GetName]'s interests to ensure relations with the [FromFrom.GetSpeciesName]s remain strong.
STH_vulcan_story.40.desc.vulcan:
Our patronage of the [From.GetName] has proven beneficial to both parties, with the crew of a [From.GetSpeciesName] ship on a cultural mission to our holy world of [FromFromFromFrom.GetName] aiding in the rescue of [Root.GetSpeciesName] monks held hostage by a strike team from the [FromFromFrom.GetName]. 

Looking for evidence of our covert listening post, the [FromFromFrom.GetSpeciesName]s were subdued by the [From.GetSpeciesName]s. 

While the [From.GetName] ship uncovered the listening post, the [From.GetCaptainTitle] decided to honour the spirit of our partnership and render the [FromFromFrom.GetSpeciesName] strike team into [Root.GetSpeciesName] custody.
STH_vulcan_story.40.name: Monastery on P'Jem
STH_vulcan_story.51.desc: Our strike team has infiltrated the [From.GetSpeciesName]'s covert subspace listening post on [FromFrom.GetName]. While casualties were not intended, several [From.GetSpeciesName] monks were killed in the process as they attempted to prevent the strike team from searching the monastery grounds. \n\nWith evidence of the [From.GetSpeciesName]'s subterfuge, the wider quadrant will surely be questioning the trustworthiness of the [From.GetName].
STH_vulcan_story.51.name: Monastery on P'Jem
STH_vulcan_story.52.desc.all: News that the [From.GetName] was using its holy sites to hide covert listening posts has spread rapidly after a strike team of [FromFrom.GetSpeciesName] elite forces struck [FromFromFrom.GetName] and a facility beneath the [From.GetSpeciesName] monastery. \n\nThe [From.GetName]'s reputation across the quadrant has suffered, while sympathy for the [FromFrom.GetName] has grown.
STH_vulcan_story.52.desc.earth_01:
The upcoming cultural mission to the [From.GetSpeciesName] holy world of [FromFromFrom.GetName] has been cancelled following shocking news from the [FromFrom.GetName]. 

An elite [FromFrom.GetSpeciesName] strike team infiltrated the monastery on [FromFromFrom.GetName] and uncovered incontrovertible proof that the [FromFrom.GetSpeciesName]s had located a covert subspace listening post beneath it. 

The [FromFrom.GetName]'s reputation across the quadrant has suffered, while sympathy for the [FromFromFrom.GetName] has grown.
STH_vulcan_story.52.desc.earth_02:
The ongoing cultural mission to the [From.GetSpeciesName] holy world of [FromFromFrom.GetName] has been cut short following shocking news from the [FromFrom.GetName]. 

An elite [FromFrom.GetSpeciesName] strike team infiltrated the monastery on [FromFromFrom.GetName] and uncovered incontrovertible proof that the [FromFrom.GetSpeciesName]s had located a covert subspace listening post beneath it. 

The [FromFrom.GetName]'s reputation across the quadrant has suffered, while sympathy for the [FromFromFrom.GetName] has grown.
STH_vulcan_story.52.desc.vulcan:
Our covert subspace listening post on [FromFromFrom.GetName] has been revealed to the entire quadrant. 

An elite [FromFrom.GetSpeciesName] strike team had taken control of the monastery before uncovering evidence of the listening post beneath. 

The listening post has been damaged beyond repair in the fighting, and worse our relations with the wider quadrant have suffered.
STH_vulcan_story.52.name: Monastery on P'Jem
STH_war.1.c: Debemos completar nuestros objetivos existentes primero.
STH_war.1.desc: Tenemos una transmisión entrante de nuestros servicios de inteligencia relacionados con nuestro conflicto en curso. Hemos identificado un objetivo estratégico ubicado en un planeta dentro del espacio enemigo. El comando militar ha pedido que enviemos una fuerza de invasión para capturar este planeta como una prioridad para obtener la información táctica.
STH_war.1.name: Información táctica entrante
STH_war.10.desc: [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName] estamos recibiendo una llamada de socorro de una de nuestras naves de transporte de largo alcance que creíamos perdida al comienzo de la guerra. Nuestros agentes de inteligencia están verificando la legalidad de la transmisión]
STH_war.10.name: Llamada entrante de socorro
STH_war.100.desc: [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName] hemos determinado tres sistemas a los que deberíamos apuntar para capturarlos, lo que proporcionará recursos valiosos en el esfuerzo de guerra. Estos sistemas han sido nombrados como el sistema [sth_war_target1.GetName], debemos preparar una flota de inmediato
STH_war.100.name: Sistema dirigido a la adquisición hostil
STH_war.1000.desc: Nuestra flota ha recuperado con éxito el objetivo de la superficie del planeta.
STH_war.1000.name: Objetivo recuperado con éxito
STH_war.102.desc: Hemos capturado con éxito el sistema objetivo, hemos logrado obtener muestras de deuterio y dilitio que se agregarán a nuestra reserva. Esto ayudará a nuestros esfuerzos de guerra y afectará seriamente los recursos propios de nuestros enemigos.
STH_war.102.name: Sistema capturado con éxito
STH_war.103.desc: Hemos capturado con éxito los sistemas seleccionados, hemos logrado liberar a varios prisioneros detenidos como prisioneros de guerra, están ansiosos por volver al servicio y ayudar en nuestros esfuerzos de guerra. Es bueno que hayamos liberado a estos hombres y mujeres.
STH_war.103.name: Sistema capturado con éxito
STH_war.104.desc: Hemos capturado con éxito el sistema objetivo, hemos logrado obtener una gran cantidad de aleaciones que parecen haber sido rescatadas de nuestras propias naves perdidas durante la guerra, que se sumarán a nuestro arsenal y ayudarán a nuestros esfuerzos de guerra. los recursos propios de nuestros enemigos
STH_war.104.name: Sistema capturado con éxito
STH_war.105.desc: Hemos capturado con éxito el sistema objetivo, hemos logrado obtener un gran impulso de energía y una selección de bienes de consumo de contraprestación, que se sumarán a nuestro arsenal y ayudarán a nuestros esfuerzos de guerra, lo que afectará seriamente los recursos propios de nuestros enemigos.
STH_war.105.name: Sistema capturado con éxito
STH_war.106.desc: Hemos invadido con éxito el sistema, este es un excelente logro de todos los involucrados y un paso más cerca de la victoria.
STH_war.106.name: Sistema invadido con éxito
STH_war.11.desc: Parece que el barco fue derribado en los primeros días del conflicto contra [war_country_enemy.GetName] y logró llegar a las cápsulas de escape. La tripulación ahora está varada en war_country_target_planet_2.getName]. No pasará mucho tiempo antes de que nuestros enemigos los detecten
STH_war.11.name: Nave perdida encontrada
STH_war.12.desc: [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName] nuestra flota de rescate ha recuperado con éxito la tripulación de la superficie del planeta, hay algunos miembros de la tripulación gravemente heridos, ahora debemos devolverlos a nuestras instalaciones médicas en Root.Capital.GetName].
STH_war.12.name: Tripulación rescatada del planeta
STH_war.13.desc: [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName] nuestra flota de rescate no pudo salvar a la tripulación de la superficie del planeta, esto ha tenido un impacto negativo en la moral de [Root.Capital.GetName].
STH_war.13.name: No se puede salvar a la tripulación desaparecida
STH_war.15.desc: [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName] nuestra flota de rescate ha regresado con éxito a la órbita segura de nuestro mundo natal, nuestros equipos médicos podrán ayudar a la tripulación a recuperar la salud y devolverlos a la acción. Como beneficio adicional, pudimos recuperar un tesoro de minerales que la tripulación había obtenido antes de ser derribados. Esto proporcionará una bonificación útil para nuestro esfuerzo de guerra.
STH_war.15.name: El regreso seguro de nuestra tripulación desaparecida
STH_war.2.desc: [root.owner.GetName] nuestras fuerzas han asegurado el objetivo en el planeta, estamos obteniendo la información táctica ahora.
STH_war.2.name: Invasion Planetaria Exitosa
STH_war.2000.desc_01: On the outbreak of war with [From.GetName] our intelligence operatives and scouts have produced a rough map of our enemies territory in order to help coordinate our war efforts. \n\nThis map will help us coordinate our efforts in the war.
STH_war.2000.desc_02: Assimilation protocols have produced intelligence reports against our enemy designated [From.GetName]. Detailed reports on territory have been produced to expedite cultural assimilation of this species.
STH_war.2000.name: War Intel Received
STH_war.21.name: Misión de guerra: Nave perdida encontrada
STH_war.22.desc: Nuestro buque insignia [rescue_ship.getName] está listo para responder, reunir todas las escoltas disponibles y prepararse para partir hacia territorio enemigo, esta será una misión peligrosa y a nuestro enemigo sin duda le encantaría asestar un golpe contra nosotros destruyendo nuestro buque insignia.
STH_war.22.name: Buque Insignia de la Federación lista para responder
STH_war.24.desc: Fantásticas noticias del Almirante [rescue_leader.GetName], han informado que han rescatado con éxito a los sobrevivientes, esta es una gran noticia y reforzará la moral de nuestras tripulaciones en la línea del frente.
STH_war.24.name: Tripulación rescatada del planeta
STH_war.25.desc: [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName] el [rescue_ship.GetName] no pudo salvar a la tripulación de la superficie del planeta, esto ha tenido un impacto negativo en la moral de [Root.Capital.GetName].
STH_war.25.name: No se puede salvar a la tripulación desaparecida
STH_war.26.desc: [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName] nuestra flota de rescate ha regresado con éxito a la órbita segura de nuestro mundo natal, nuestros equipos médicos podrán ayudar a la tripulación a recuperar la salud y devolverlos a la acción. Como beneficio adicional, pudimos recuperar un tesoro de minerales que la tripulación había obtenido antes de ser derribados. Esto proporcionará una bonificación útil para nuestro esfuerzo de guerra.
STH_war.26.name: El regreso seguro de nuestra tripulación desaparecida
STH_war.27.desc: El Capitán de la nave destruida señala que su antigua nave contiene una serie de proyectos altamente sensibles que la Flota Estelar no puede permitirse dejar caer en manos enemigas. No podemos permitir que la nave en ruinas permanezca rescatable, por lo menos nuestro enemigo obtiene datos cruciales de investigación
STH_war.27.name: Operación de salvamento de naves
STH_war.28.desc: Felicitaciones, hemos restaurado con éxito la nave para que funcione, pero no está en condiciones de luchar. Debemos escoltarlo de regreso al espacio de la Flota Estelar
STH_war.28.name: Nave reparada con éxito
STH_war.29.name: No se puede reparar la nave
STH_war.3.desc: Nuestras fuerzas han descifrado los códigos de nuestros enemigos de la instalación capturada. Hemos identificado con éxito el paradero de varias de sus flotas; debemos utilizar esta información táctica para aprovechar nuestra ventaja.
STH_war.3.name: Objetivo estratégico identificado
STH_war.30.desc: Mientras intentaba reiniciar el núcleo warp, aparentemente se volvió rápidamente, el daño fue demasiado severo, con poca advertencia de la ruptura del núcleo warp. No hubo tiempo para evacuar al equipo de reparación. Sin nada que salvar, es hora de regresar a territorio seguro
STH_war.30.name: El barco explota con el equipo de reparación a bordo
STH_war.4.desc: Parece que el enemigo ahora ha cambiado sus códigos, la información de la flota que teníamos ya no es relevante.
STH_war.4.name: Códigos de enemigos cambiados
STH_war.40.b: No vale la pena el riesgo
STH_war.40.desc: [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName] hemos recibido una transmisión codificada desde el espacio enemigo. Uno de sus principales científicos afirma que quiere desertar, afirman tener información militar de primer nivel, pero solo nos la darán una vez que los hayamos extraído.
STH_war.40.name: Transmisión entrante desde la estación enemiga
STH_war.42.desc: [Root.GetRulerTitle] hemos confirmado la ubicación del desertor. Están en una pequeña embarcación orbitando [sth_war_defector_location.GetName], en el corazón del espacio [war_country_defector.GetName]. Debemos preparar un barco y una tripulación para viajar a territorio hostil y recuperar el objetivo. Esto suena como una misión ideal para nuestro buque insignia, pero será peligroso, deberíamos considerar enviar una copia de seguridad
STH_war.42.name: Defector localizado, envía la flota
STH_war.43.desc: [Root.GetRulerTitle] hemos confirmado la ubicación del desertor. Están en una pequeña embarcación orbitando [sth_war_defector_location.GetName], en el corazón del espacio [war_country_defector.GetName]. Debemos preparar un barco y una tripulación para viajar a territorio hostil y recuperar el objetivo. nuestro buque insignia, pero será peligroso, por lo que no debemos dejar que viajen sin apoyo.
STH_war.43.name: Defector localizado, envía la flota
STH_war.44.desc: Nuestro equipo de extracción ha recuperado con éxito al científico sin ser detectado por [war_country_defector.GetName], están siendo tratados por nuestros médicos y deberían estar listos para hablar con nosotros en breve.
STH_war.44.name: Defector recuperado con éxito
STH_war.45.name: Defector recuperado con éxito
STH_war.46.c: Por favor únete a nuestro equipo científico
STH_war.46.d: La información que proporcione nos ayudará
STH_war.46.desc: Saludos [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName], humildemente gracias por el rescate y por extraerme. Esta guerra ha durado demasiado y se están poniendo en peligro vidas inocentes, mi trabajo se está utilizando para prolongar el esfuerzo de guerra y no puedo ver el trabajo de mi vida de esta manera. Gracias por la ayuda
STH_war.46.name: Desertor desea hablar con nosotros
STH_war.47.c: Te salvamos, ¡ahora te unirás a nosotros!
STH_war.47.desc: [Root.GetRulerName], su intervención oportuna ha llegado en el momento oportuno, mi investigación y mi vida son demasiado valiosas como para desperdiciarlas trabajando para mi antiguo imperio. Siento que puedo lograr mucho más con sus recursos, pero primero debemos ganar esta guerra y me aseguraré de que se haga. ¡Conmigo a tu lado no podemos perder! Pero mi lealtad tiene un precio, ¿no puedes esperar que arriesgue mi vida por nada ...?
STH_war.47.name: Desertor desea hablar con nosotros
STH_war.48.desc: Hemos completado con éxito la operación y recuperado el desertor del territorio enemigo, esto nos ayudará en nuestros esfuerzos de guerra. Deberíamos devolver nuestras flotas a nuestro espacio. Ahora podemos asignar al miembro más nuevo de nuestro equipo científico para que trabaje para nosotros.
STH_war.48.name: Operación del desertor completada con éxito
STH_war.49.desc: El desertor fue fiel a su palabra y nos ha proporcionado información de encuestas e investigaciones críticas que ayudarán en gran medida a nuestra investigación científica. Como parte del trato, han pedido que se les dé acceso a un transbordador y se les permita encontrar un hogar dentro de nuestras fronteras.
STH_war.49.name: Operación del desertor completada con éxito
STH_war.50.desc: El desertor nos traicionó, recibimos un mensaje codificado de una banda de piratas que nos regalaron nuestra ubicación, antes de que pudiéramos detenerlos, habían robado un transbordador y se habían ido a la flota pirata. Parece que traicionado está en su naturaleza. Solo podemos esperar que no hayan causado demasiado daño
STH_war.50.name: ¡Hemos sido traicionados!
STH_war.501.desc: Following the defeat of [destroyed_fleet.GetName] our military experts have concluded that the war does not go well for us, recent defeats and an increasing exhaustion at the war have taken a heavy toll on our forces.\n\nMany of our top military brass strongly recommend approaching one of our galactic allies to help ensure we are not defeated by [war_attacker_country.GetName].
STH_war.501.e: We will win this war without help.
STH_war.501.name: A call for help
STH_war.502.a: We will go to their defence.
STH_war.502.b: This war is not our concern.
STH_war.502.desc: Our galactic neighbours [war_defender_country_1.GetName] have sent out an urgent SOS for help in their ongoing war with [war_attacker_country.GetName].\n\nRecent defeats have severelly hit their military and they are at risk of losing the war. They have called on us for help. Should we go to their aid?
STH_war.502.name: A call for help
STH_war.503.desc: After some deliberation it looks like [war_sos_help_country.GetName] has agreed to come to our aid and join the war alongside us.\n\nHopefully our combined forces will be able to turn the tide of this war and help us win.
STH_war.503.name: Aid in our darkest hour
STH_war.504.desc: After some deliberation it looks like [war_sos_help_country.GetName] has rejected our pleans for aid. If we are to win this war it will need to be with those already involved in the struggle.
STH_war.504.name: Abandoned by our neighbours
STH_war.505.desc: Our intelligence services have confirmed that [war_sos_help_country.GetName] has joined the war against us on the side of our enemies. We now fight multiple enemies in our efforts to defeat [war_defender_country.GetName].
STH_war.505.name: [war_sos_help_country.GetName] joins the war against us
STH_war.51.desc: El desertor dejó un explosivo de plasma sin ser detectado a bordo de una de las naves de rescate, el daño a nuestra nave fue extenso. El desertor ahora está a bordo de las navespiratas en el sistema. La elección es nuestra, regresar al espacio amigable o vengarse.
STH_war.51.name: El barco ha sido saboteado.
STH_war.52.desc: El desertor nos traicionó, recibimos un mensaje codificado de una banda de piratas que nos regalaron nuestra ubicación, antes de que pudiéramos detenerlos, habían robado un transbordador y se habían ido a la flota pirata. Desafortunadamente no hemos podido contactar al Capitán, parece que fueron asesinados. Con el desertor ahora a bordo de las naves piratas en el sistema. La elección es nuestra, regresar al espacio amigable o vengarse.
STH_war.52.name: ¡Hemos sido traicionados!
STH_war.53.desc: El desertor nos traicionó, recibimos un mensaje codificado de una banda de piratas que nos regalaron nuestra ubicación, antes de que pudiéramos detenerlos, habían robado un transbordador y se habían ido a la flota pirata. Nuestros escaneos muestran que una enorme flota pirata ha entrado en el sistema y en un curso directo con nuestras naves, no podemos perder más naves, debemos defendernos
STH_war.53.name: ¡Hemos sido traicionados!
STH_war.54.desc: La misión se ha completado, ahora podemos usar la información para un buen uso en la guerra en curso contra [war_country_defector.GetName]
STH_war.54.name: Misión cumplida
STH_war.99.a: We have enough targets for now.
STH_war.99.desc: [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName] hemos recibido una información táctica de nuestros exploradores de largo alcance, nuestros tácticos creen que una invasión estratégica de estos sistemas identificados podría proporcionarnos recursos adicionales que serán valiosos en nuestros esfuerzos de guerra en curso
STH_war.99.name: Sistema dirigido a la adquisición hostil
STH_war_story.10.desc: As our ships drop out of warp, it is clear we have dropped into an ambush... by vessels flying the flag of [Root.GetName], how could this be? Someone is clearly trying to set us up... \n\nIt matters not we must stop this attack and uncover what is going on here.
STH_war_story.10.name: Ambush!
STH_war_story.101.desc: With the conclusion of the war, generals on both sides of the conflict have recommended the creation of a Neutral Zone situated in the space between the Romulan Star Empire and the Federation. \n\nThis zone will create a neutral space between the two powers that act as a buffer that many hope will keep the peace, the only remaining decision is to decide how long the zone should remain established.
STH_war_story.101.name: The Neutral Zone
STH_war_story.11.desc: Reports are coming in from our remote drones, they have begun their attack on our foes disguised as a fleet from United Earth. We shall task the fleet with causing as much damage as possible before retreating to ensure that no evidence links the attack back to the Star Empire.
STH_war_story.11.name: Drone Mission Update
STH_war_story.12.desc:
The combined efforts of our fleet and our hosts have managed to repel the attack on our allies, it is clear from the manner in which the ships operate and our ability to account for all our ships, these ships cannot be part of our fleet. 

With the fleet in retreat [earth_rom_unity_attack_drone.GetName] spots something unusual, one of the ships appears to shimmer and revert back into a small green vessel covered in what appears to be a series of antennae. 

With its shields down, there is no better chance to send in a strike team and find out who is behind these attacks!
STH_war_story.12.name: System Defended
STH_war_story.14.a: Ensure it stays this way, or else.
STH_war_story.14.desc: Our remote drone command centre has provided some very concerning news, one of our remote ships was damaged during its confrontation with the Coalition forces and as a result, lost its holo-camouflage disguise. Whilst we have no evidence at the moment that the Coalition members have linked the ship back to us this is a concerning development.
STH_war_story.14.name: Drone Ship Boarded
STH_war_story.15.desc: As [earth_rom_unity_attack_drone.GetName] and their team make their way through the ship, it is clear that the vessel is devoid of any crew, and the abundance of exposed conduits and data transfer networks suggest that the vessel is controlled externally. The team begins to follow the pulsing conduits in a bid to find the ship's command centre. \n\nSuddenly the unmistakable sound of the ship going to warp rings out, the Drone has gone to warp.
STH_war_story.15.name: Inside the Drone
STH_war_story.16.desc: [Root.GetRulerTitle] we have managed to re-establish contact with the damaged drone ship only to find that the Coalition forces have managed to infiltrate the ship. They could track the ship back to us and undo our efforts to destabilise the region. \n\nOur only option is to attempt to isolate the crew and force them to cease their investigations.
STH_war_story.16.name: Intruders onboard
STH_war_story.17.desc: The team successfully makes their way to what appears to be the vessel's control room, noting that the power conduits and data nodes all terminate at a central control console. Leading off from this is what appears to be an engineering station behind an access door. [earth_rom_unity_attack_drone.GetName] heads through the access door, if they can disable the ship's warp drive and download the data core the mysteries of this alien ship could be revealed.
STH_war_story.17.name: Inside the Drone
STH_war_story.18.desc: With the Drones onboard sensors back online we are able to track the intruder's movements, in a stroke of good fortune we can see that one member of the party has entered the small engineering bay, quickly sealing the bay and locking the alien officer inside and isolated from their team. The technical team recommends boosting the power through the closest conduit to the alien leader causing the radiation levels to rise. \n\nWe should test how attached they are to their crew and issue an ultimatum. Restore our access to the ship or lose their leader.
STH_war_story.18.name: Intruders onboard
STH_war_story.19.desc: Suddenly the door to the engineering alcove slams shut trapping [earth_rom_unity_attack_drone.GetName] inside. The plasma conduits inside the room begin to come to life, flooding the room with potentially lethal radiation... A voice crackles over the ship's intercom, Intruders, we give you a simple choice, restore our access to the ship or your officer dies, you have five minutes.  \n\nThe choice appears simple, restore the ship's power or let their Captain die.
STH_war_story.19.name: Inside the Drone
STH_war_story.2.a: This is not a plan fit for the Empire.
STH_war_story.2.desc: Greetings senators I am [valdore_romulan_leader.GetName] chief military adviser to the Praetor, I must make you aware of trouble building on our borders. The burgeoning alliance between the Humans, Andorians, Tellarites, and our despised cousins on Vulcan has moved from being mere hearsay to an actual possibility. Any alliance they form will turn them into a potential superpower and putting our borders at risk. \n\nIt is time you allowed me to unleashed the holo-projection technology that my team has been working on and turn these races against each other.
STH_war_story.2.name: Threats to Romulus
STH_war_story.20.desc: In a surprise move, the Coalition forces decide to sacrifice their leader, in another lifetime we could have respected this move as almost Romulan. However, the leader must die and the ship must not be allowed to fall into enemy hands.\n\nOverload the engines, destroy the ship...
STH_war_story.20.name: They sacrifice their leader
STH_war_story.21.desc: As predicted the Coalition soldiers show no backbone in electing to save their leader from a most painful death. The engineering tech indicates that power has been restored to the drone, however, the intruders cannot be permitted to survive, despite our promise, With power restored prepare to disable artificial gravity and engage the engines, it is a shame they will not be able to survive this maneuver.
STH_war_story.21.name: Power restored to the Drone
STH_war_story.22.desc: As [earth_rom_unity_attack_drone.GetName] breathes their last breath the boarding party takes solace that at least they have captured the drone, suddenly one of the team members notices that whilst their attention was focused on their dying leader the ships masters have managed to gain access to the ship's engines and set them to overload.\n\nIn the last few moments before the ship explodes the team are able to transmit a few bits of non-vital information about the craft in the hope their lives were not given in vain.
STH_war_story.22.name: Drones engines overload
STH_war_story.23.desc: With the Drones destroyed at least the suspicion and tension between the Coalition members can be brought to an end, however, the source of these attacks remains much of a mystery. \n\nIt is clear that some antagonistic force is keen to end the burgeoning alliance that has formed and it is unlikely to be the last attempt they make.
STH_war_story.23.name: Drones destroyed
STH_war_story.24.a: We must get off this ship.
STH_war_story.24.desc: As the door slowly slides open, the crew help [earth_rom_unity_attack_drone.GetName] to their feet, before the Captain can berate them for giving in to their captors, the security chief pushes the Captain to the ground shortly before the console explodes into a shower of sparks. The lights in the command centre immediately go dark. \n\nThe crew rush from the bridge.
STH_war_story.24.name: Captain freed
STH_war_story.25.desc: As the boarding party rush to get into their EV suits sounds of explosions echo throughout the ship as a cascade of explosions rings out spelling the end of the drone vessel. \n\nThe away team reach an airlock and with the data they managed to get clear of the ship before it is destroyed.
STH_war_story.25.name: Escape
STH_war_story.3.desc: We are receiving a distress call from [rom_earth_war_unity_ship_1.GetName], the ship appears to have been attacked whilst on a routine mission. At this stage we have no view of who carried out this attack, we must first secure our ship and ensure our people are ok.
STH_war_story.3.name: Distress call from [rom_earth_war_unity_ship_1.GetName]
STH_war_story.30.b: Romulus will not follow this path.
STH_war_story.30.desc: My Praetor, the threat that both the military and Tal Shiar have been warning you of has finally come to haunt us. The Coalition of our enemies has finally formally united to form a new major power, calling themselves the United Federation of Planets. A despicable name for a weak entity, but our intelligence reports clearly show that this Federation is still looking to recruit. Before long they will be the major power in the Alpha/Beta Quadrants and an unstoppable danger to the Star Empire. \n\nWe demand that now is the time to act and inflict damage onto them whilst they are still unifying their efforts into a single body.
STH_war_story.30.name: Threats to the Raptors Rule
STH_war_story.32.a: Who would want to harm us?
STH_war_story.32.desc: The skies above our homeworld have been filled with terrible green disrupter fire and the horrifying sound of plasma torpedos destroying our buildings and burning our people. \n\nThe attack came swiftly and without warning, we must call on our allies for help.
STH_war_story.32.name: Homeworld Attacked
STH_war_story.33.desc: As the northern hemisphere of [ufp_attacked_planet.GetName] slept a savage and unprovoked attack rained down on the colony. A fleet of ships filled the sky firing bolt of plasma and disrupter energy at the settlement. \n\nThe attack ends as suddenly as it starts with no messages from the attackers.
STH_war_story.33.name: [ufp_attacked_planet.GetName] Attacked
STH_war_story.34.desc: President [Root.GetRulerName], your Federation of Planets promised [new_member_planet_owner.GetName] safety and protection as part of your union, yet we have been targeted because we bringing our worlds closer. Your promised us protection, so now we ask you make good on this promise. Help us.
STH_war_story.34.name: Incoming Message
STH_war_story.35.desc: Our failure to aid the attacked colony has not only damaged our reputation and our colonist's conviction that we will aid them when they are in need, but also we may have lost any chance of getting a vital clue to find out who has been attacking us.
STH_war_story.35.name: Failed to aid planet
STH_war_story.36.desc: The aid we were able to provide has greatly helped the colony recover from the attack. \n\nWhilst amongst the colonists' many reports catching a glimpse at the attacking ships, describing giant winged raptors or birds of prey, interesting these reports match up with previous incidents where we were attacked from the shadows.
STH_war_story.36.name: Aid provided to Planet
STH_war_story.37.desc: Our sensors in orbit of [attacked_member_planet2.GetName] have picked up a potential early warning sign that this world is about to be attacked by the same foes that has attacked us repeatedly over the last few years. \n\nWe should send a fleet to engage and defend our border world and hopefully uncover who is attacking us.
STH_war_story.37.name: Another Planet under attack!
STH_war_story.38.desc: The second of our planned assaults against the Federation colony worlds is about to begin, our fleets are on route to once again rain down destruction on those hapless fools.
STH_war_story.38.name: Attack on the Federation
STH_war_story.39.desc: Thanks to the early warning provided our fleet has arrived just in time to face off against the mysterious attackers. We must stand firm and defeat the invaders and if possible give our forces enough time to uncover who is behind this.
STH_war_story.39.name: Attack in Progress
STH_war_story.40.desc: Praetor, it is with great delight that I can report our forces were successfully able to infiltrate Federation space and launch an attack against one of their worlds. \n\nThis will act as a clear message to anyone that the Federation alliance is weak and if they cannot even protect themselves, how can they protect future members.
STH_war_story.40.name: Attack Successful
STH_war_story.41.desc: Our failure to defend our colony has not gone unnoticed by those worlds in and outside of the Federation, our security experts point out that this makes us appear weak and ripe for invasion. \n\nWhat little advance we may have had to go on the front foot has now been lost. We can only wait and see what will happen next.
STH_war_story.41.name: Failed to Defend our Planet
STH_war_story.43.a: We will not be forced into War.
STH_war_story.43.desc: We have demonstrated the weakness and cowardice that the Federation demonstrate, they failed to raise the muster to defend their own borders which demonstrates their lack of confidence in their military. Now is our time to attack and put these upstarts back into their place. \n\nWe urge you now is the time to declare war!
STH_war_story.43.name: Weak Federation
STH_war_story.44.desc: For too long now you have expanded into territory that rightfully belongs to the Romulan Star Empire. we have threatened and warned you, attacked and harmed your people and yet you continue your goal of federating this quadrant. It ends here and now, Consider this a declaration of war from your betters.
STH_war_story.44.desc_01: We have watched from afar as you united our enemies against us, you move into territory that rightfully belongs to the Romulan Star Empire. We will stand for this disrespect no longer and you will know the wrath of the Raptor. \n\nConsider this a declaration of war!
STH_war_story.44.name: Romulans Declare War
STH_war_story.5.desc: The first trial of our Drone Ship appears to have been an unprecedented success, it's first attack managed to destroy its target. We are now engaging in phase 2 and framing their ally for the attack.
STH_war_story.5.name: Update from our Drone Ship
STH_war_story.50.desc_01: With the attackers defeated, our Intelligence services have been analyzing the wreckage and the evidence from the previous attacks and with some certainty, they report back their belief that we have been victims of the Romulan Star Empire. From the first strike against us in the minefield to the drone attacks all evidence points towards the involvement of the Romulans. The evidence is compelling enough to allow us to confront the Romulans and demand an explanation or more!
STH_war_story.50.desc_02: With the attackers defeated, our Intelligence services have been analyzing the wreckage but unfortunately, the lack of evidence obtained during the targeted attacks against us means that they are unable to provide solid evidence as to who is behind the attacks. Many suggest it could either be the Romulan Star Empire or the Klingon Empire, but we do not have enough evidence to convince the Federation Council to accuse either of these major powers.
STH_war_story.50.name: Attack Averted
STH_war_story.51.a: This is your final and only warning.
STH_war_story.51.desc: Praetor [romulan_star_empire.ruler.GetName], your continued attacks against the United Federation of Planets has been noted and will stop now, you have endangered the lives of our citizens and caused significant damage to our property. The Federation might be new, but we are not weak and we will defend ourselves against any and all threats.
STH_war_story.51.name: Confront the Romulans
STH_war_story.52.desc: Praetor [romulan_star_empire.ruler.GetName], your continued attacks against the United Federation of Planets has been noted and will stop now, you have endangered the lives of our citizens and caused significant damage to our property. The Federation might be new, but we are not weak and we will defend ourselves against any and all threats. \n\nPrepare for War!
STH_war_story.52.name: Message from Earth
STH_war_story.53.desc: Sirs, as the War against the Romulan's rages on our intelligence services have 'uncovered' strong evidence that the Romulans are using the planet [planet_cheron.GetName] as a massive staging ground to launch a full-scale offensive against [Root.capital_scope.GetName] in a bid to end the war quickly and cripple any chance we may have of launching a counter-offensive. \n\nA victory at [planet_cheron.GetName] could go a long way in winning this war for either side.
STH_war_story.53.name: Battle of [planet_cheron.GetName]
STH_war_story.54.desc: The Fleet has arrived at [planet_cheron.GetName] [planet_cheron_leader.GetName] knows that victory could be key to ending this war.
STH_war_story.54.name: Battle of [planet_cheron.GetName]
STH_war_story.55.name: Victory at [planet_cheron.GetName]
STH_war_story.58.desc: Your attacks against our worlds end now Romulans! We have long suspected you of causing the death and destruction of our people and property. \n\nOn this occasion we are giving you a final warning, your fleets are denied access to our territory and any further incursions will be met with the full force of our fleet.
STH_war_story.58.name: Message from Earth
STH_war_story.59.desc: It appears that our threats against the Romulans have convinced them to stand down and war has been averted. Whilst many within our colonies remain fearful of a repeat attack, it seems the Star Empire have withdrawn behind their borders for the time being.
STH_war_story.59.name: Romulan Empire Stands Down
STH_war_story.6.desc_01: As our fleet reaches our damaged vessel, which has clearly taken a significant amount of damage from the attack, sensors pick up another fleet inbound with weapons hot. \n\nThe vessels appear to be Vulcan in design... why would the Vulcan's be attacking us?
STH_war_story.6.desc_02: As our fleet reaches our damaged vessel, which has clearly taken a significant amount of damage from the attack, sensors pick up another fleet inbound with weapons hot. \n\nThe vessels appear to be Vulcan in design... It appears our military experts were right not to trust those green-blooded cowards after all!
STH_war_story.6.desc_03: As our fleet reaches our damaged vessel, which has clearly taken a significant amount of damage from the attack, sensors pick up another fleet inbound with weapons hot. \n\nThe vessels appear to from the Andorian Empire, those fools appear to be attacking us without reason!
STH_war_story.6.desc_04: As our fleet reaches our damaged vessel, which has clearly taken a significant amount of damage from the attack, sensors pick up another fleet inbound with weapons hot. \n\nThe vessels appear to be from the United Earth... this is a most illogical move.
STH_war_story.6.name: Ambush
STH_war_story.7.desc: Our fleet has held off the attack by our supposed allies, but before we could scan the ships they appear to self-destruct! This behaviour is very out of character and extremely concerning, we should contact them immediately and demand an explanation.
STH_war_story.7.name: Attack Defeated
STH_war_story.8.desc: Our field units have reported back another successful attack by our holo-drone, successfully sowing the seeds of doubt amongst our foes. \n\nWe should proceed with the next stage of our test and launch an attack against one of their worlds.
STH_war_story.8.name: Battle Report
STH_war_story.9.a: We have ordered no attack on your people.
STH_war_story.9.b: We will send a ship to support you.
STH_war_story.9.c: How dare you speak to us this way.
STH_war_story.9.desc: [Root.GetRulerTitle]! You must explain the meaning behind the hostile intent coming from a fleet of your ships. Our outlying sensor stations have picked up a fleet of your ships on an inbound course with their defenses up and weapons registering as active. \n\nYou will explain this act or it will lead to a response in kind!
STH_war_story.9.name: Ally under attack
STH_warpcapable.10.desc: The Bajoran Kai formally invites us to visit one of their temples on Bajor, we are told this is a rare honour and a chance to experience first hand the importance the Bajoran faith has on the populace.
STH_warpcapable.10.name: Bajoran Temple Visit
STH_warpcapable.11.desc: [bajor_visit_leader.GetName] returns from their trip from the Bajoran surface feeling refreshed and enlightened and whilst they didn't hear from the Prophets the peace and tranquility of the setting has given them a fresh energy and a valuable life experience.
STH_warpcapable.11.name: Bajoran Temple Visit
STH_warpcapable.12.a: Accept the game from the Ktarians.
STH_warpcapable.12.b: We have no time to play such games.
STH_warpcapable.12.desc: On returning from the visit to Ktaria our away teams noticed that many Ktarians were using a strange single-player headset device worn over the ears and using a visual interface. On questioning the locals it turns out it is a new game sweeping the planet, the game displayed a grid pattern on which the player had to direct hovering disks into cone shaped objects over the course of multiple levels. The ever friendly Ktarians offer us the game to take away.
STH_warpcapable.12.name: A mysterious game
STH_warpcapable.14.a: Can't wait to play it again.
STH_warpcapable.14.desc: The Ktarian game was an instant success and played across our planets and even during downtime on our military ships, and it has had a fantastic effect on our people and military greatly improving the moral of everyone who played the game. However with all crazes they come and go and now the next big thing has come along and taken the spotlight.
STH_warpcapable.14.name: The Game
STH_warpcapable.15.a: Ban and destroy all copies of the game.
STH_warpcapable.15.desc: The Ktarian game very quickly became a big hit amongst our people and was played across our cities and military installations, picking the game up was easy, putting it down was more difficult and the addictive nature of the game became apparent when many found they could not put it down. Thankfully a young wizkid was able to devise a cure using an optical burst from a modified palm beacon to counter the effect of the device on their bridge crew, subsequently programming the ship's internal lighting to broadcast the same frequency throughout the ship. \n\nFollowing this near miss the game has been banned across [Root.GetName].
STH_warpcapable.15.name: The Game
STH_warpcapable.17.a: Plan the visit to the caves.
STH_warpcapable.17.desc: The Caves of Mak'ala were a series of interconnecting underground caves on Trill. Deep within these caves were kilometers of interconnecting pools of a milk-like substance which was the natural environment of the Trill symbionts. An invitation to visit the caves from the Guardians, a group of unjoined Trill who had devoted their lives to making sure the pools were perfect for the symbionts, is a great privilege.
STH_warpcapable.17.name: The Caves of Mak'ala
STH_warpcapable.18.desc: The caves contained many unjoined symbionts containing hundreds of years of experience between them passed across generations. Much knowledge could be gleaned from the Trill if the right questions are asked.
STH_warpcapable.18.name: The Caves of Mak'ala
STH_warpcapable.20.a: Make the offer for the Dilithium.
STH_warpcapable.20.b: This deal is not for us.
STH_warpcapable.20.desc: The planet Xahea has an abundance of natural Dilithium, which the locals are experts in harvesting, resulting in some of the purest Dilithium anywhere in the Quadrant. The friendly Xahean Council are willing to provide us with a sample of this Dilithium in exchange for a small quantity of minerals.
STH_warpcapable.20.name: Natural Dilithium
STH_warpcapable.21.desc: At the request of [primitive_visit_country.GetName] we have been invited to join a host of notable leader, military experts and scientists as part of an exchange of knowledge and culture at a conference on [primitive_capital_loc.GetName]. This will be an opportunity to represent [Root.GetName] and build relations with the [primitive_visit_country.GetName] should we want to.
STH_warpcapable.21.name: Knowledge Sharing
STH_warpcapable.22.desc: The conference was packed with some of the best scientific minds from across the hosts planet and despite our technological advances there were a few pieces of information that would be of interest to our scientists back on [Root.capital_scope.GetName] making this a worthwhile outing.
STH_warpcapable.22.name: Knowledge Sharing
STH_warpcapable.23.desc: Despite the best efforts of the hosts there was little [prim_mission_leader.GetName] found to be of any real benefit. \n\nHowever pleasantries were upheld and useful contacts made, so it was not a complete waste of time.
STH_warpcapable.23.name: Knowledge Sharing
STH_warpcapable.24.desc: Our hosts were thrilled with our attendance at their conference and found the whole experience really enlightening, so much so that they have offered us a small reward as a token of their thanks for coming to their planet and sharing some of our knowledge and culture with them.
STH_warpcapable.24.name: Knowledge Sharing
STH_warpcapable.25.desc: Designated as a 'pleasure planet', Risa was an inhabited planet orbiting the primary Risa, originally a dismal, rain-soaked, and geologically unstable planet covered with vast jungles and plagued by violent earthquakes. However, the planet was transformed by the native Risians with a technologically sophisticated weather control system created the perfect paradise and an ideal location for shore leave. The Risans have offered one of our ships and crew some rest and relaxation.
STH_warpcapable.25.name: Risan Shore Leave
STH_warpcapable.26.a: A rest for the mind and body.
STH_warpcapable.26.desc: [risa_captain.GetName] and the crew of [risa_ship.GetName] return to duty following a fantastic shore leave with glowing reviews of the beautiful Risan sunsets and clear blue seas. The captain and crew feel refreshed and ready for their next mission.
STH_warpcapable.26.name: Risan Shore Leave
STH_warpcapable.27.a: Maybe it will be better next time.
STH_warpcapable.27.desc: Not every holiday can be a good one, and despite the idyllic conditions events transpired to ruin the perfect shore leave. Lost luggage, and a malfunctioning weather system meant rain and glum moods. The crew of [risa_ship.GetName] were only too happy to return to the warmth and dryness of their own cabins.
STH_warpcapable.27.name: Risan Shore Leave
STH_warpcapable.5.b: The United Federation of Planets would welcome you as a member.
STH_warpcapable.5.baul2: Investigate mysterious signal from a Kelpien Village.
STH_warpcapable.5.c: Join the Cardassian Union for a stronger future.
STH_warpcapable.5.d: Imagine the profit that could be made if you were to join us in an economic union.
STH_warpcapable.5.e: The Romulan Star Empire can offer you protection and power.
STH_warpcapable.5.freeze: Switch to regular Diplomatic Window for 30 days.
STH_warpcapable.5.gen: Would you be willing for one of our scientists to visit your planet?
STH_warpcapable.5.kta: May we try this famous Ktarian game we have heard so much about?
STH_warpcapable.5.name: Warp-Capable Species Contact
STH_warpcapable.5.xah: Request a sample of purified Dilithium.
STH_warpcapable.50.a: What are you looking to sell?
STH_warpcapable.50.desc: Greetings [Root.GetRulerName], I am [barzans_leader.GetTitle] [barzans_leader.GetName] leader of the Barzan Planetary Republic. We understand you would like to join the bidding for unique Wormhole. We have no interest in such things, but the Latinum from the sale would work wonders for our small world.
STH_warpcapable.50.name: The Barzan Wormhole
STH_warpcapable.51.desc: Bidding for the Wormhole is about to begin, [Root.GetName] has been joined by [barzan_bid_rival_1.GetName], [barzan_bid_rival_2.GetName], [barzan_bid_rival_3.GetName] all bidding for the prize. Our financial advisers have suggested that we could make one of three bids.
STH_warpcapable.51.name: The Barzan Wormhole
STH_warpcapable.52.b: It is not worth the risk.
STH_warpcapable.52.desc: After a tense auction process, the Barzan leaders are pleased to announce that we have won the bid and now have first rights to examine this supposed Wormhole and see what mysteries lurk on the other side.\n\nWe should send a science expedition to the site provided by the Barzan delegates and attempt to claim the exlusive gateway.
STH_warpcapable.52.name: The winning bid!
STH_warpcapable.54.desc: Despite winning the bid to secure access to the wormhole our scientists were unable to secure the mouth of the Wormhole and make its mysteries ours.
STH_warpcapable.54.name: Successfully won the Barzan Bid
STH_warpcapable.55.desc: It appears our bid was not high enough to secure the auction and we lost out on the chance to examine the wormhole. The Barzan delegate cheerfully informs us that [barzan_winner.GetName] were successful in winning the bid.\n\nAs our scientists scan their efforts to open the wormhole it appears that they have failed to capitalise on their successful bid and the wormhole's entrance remains locked.
STH_warpcapable.55.name: Auction Lost, but Wormhole remains closed
STH_warpcapable.56.desc: Our scientists are proud to confirm that following our successful bid to secure the location of the Barzan Wormhole, we have been able to trace the location of the wormhole as it bounces around the Barzan system. \n\nBy applying a tachyon field we were able to force the location of the wormhole to remain stable and direct it into our teritory. The wormhole has now opened in [barzan_winner_system.GetName] and terminates in the [barzan_wormhole_destination.GetName] system.
STH_warpcapable.56.name: Barzan Wormhole secured
STH_warpcapable.57.desc: Following a tightly fought bidding process, [barzan_winner_country.GetName] secured the rights to the Barzan Wormhole and using a tachyon pulse secured the entrance in the [barzan_winner_system.GetName] System. The wormhole appears to exit in the [barzan_wormhole_destination.GetName] System.
STH_warpcapable.57.name: The Barzan Wormhole
STH_warpcapable.58.desc: A mysterious new wormhole which appears to originate in the distant Alpha Quadrant has opened up in the [barzan_wormhole_destination.GetName].\n\nOnly time will tell if this is a fortuitous occurrence or a threat to our borders.
STH_warpcapable.58.name: The Barzan Wormhole
STH_warpcapable.6.desc: This civilisation has not yet left their homeworld and developed faster than light capabilities, and whilst their potential is limitless right now they need to progress on their own journey towards leaving their own planet and beyond if they are to ever influence the wider galaxy.
STH_warpcapable.6.name: Pre-Warp Cultures
STH_warpcapable.60.desc: [Root.GetRulerTitle]! The crew of [barzan_ship.GetName] have reported back with good news. [barzan_ship_leader.GetName] and their crew have secured the entrance to the Barzan Wormhole and confirmed that it leads to the distant [barzan_wormhole_destination.GetName]. This new stable means of travel opens up new possibilities and opportunities for [Root.GetName].
STH_warpcapable.60.name: The Barzan Wormhole
STH_warpcapable.61.desc: [Root.GetRulerTitle] our science advisers are saddened to announce that [barzan_ship_leader.GetName] and the crew of [barzan_ship.GetName] was lost with all hands whilst investigating the mouth of the Barzan Wormhole. It seems the entrance shifted on the crew whilst they were traversing, it seems the forces within the wormhole during the shift overwhelmed the ship destroying it. \n\nTo make matters worse we can no longer find any sign of the Wormhole and it appears to have been lost forever.
STH_warpcapable.61.name: [barzan_ship,GetName] Lost exploring Wormhole
STH_warpcapable.62.b: Our bioscans show no sign of disease... strange.
STH_warpcapable.62.d: We will repair your ship for a price.
STH_warpcapable.62.desc: This is a distress call from T'Jon of the Ornaran Freighter Sanction, we have been damaged by a solar disruption from our Delos, our local star, we are in need of urgent assistance. \n\nWe are on an urgent mission to deliver urgent supplies to our homeworld, failure to return the supplies could cost the lives of thousands back home, we need this fix, I mean fixed.
STH_warpcapable.62.e: One of medical teams will need to look at you first.
STH_warpcapable.62.f: This is no business of ours.
STH_warpcapable.62.name: Symbiosis
STH_warpcapable.63.a: This sounds like a drug deal to us.
STH_warpcapable.63.c: You have a wise business mind.
STH_warpcapable.63.desc: Hello [ROOT.GetRulerName] you have reached the Brekkian Alliance, we believe you have received a communication from a freighter bound for Brekka. \n\nWe are afraid that until we receive our payment the shipment of Felicium remains with us... I am sure you understand that this is basic business sense?
STH_warpcapable.63.e: The Ornarans are attempting to take the Felicium without paying!
STH_warpcapable.63.name: Symbiosis
STH_warpcapable.64.a: I am sure we have really helped them.
STH_warpcapable.64.desc: Our engineering team has successfully managed to fix the Ornaran freighter, and whilst the damage was quite severe you should never underestimate the resourcefulness of a determined engineer. \n\nT'Jon thanks us for our help and sets course to resume his strange mission to Brekka.
STH_warpcapable.64.name: Symbiosis - Ship Fixed
STH_warpcapable.65.a: It is a shame we couldnt help them.
STH_warpcapable.65.desc: Unfortunately, some ships are simply too damaged to be salvaged, and unfortunately, the Sanction was one such ship. \n\nWhich unusual panic the Ornarans accept our apologies and frantically begin looking for help elsewhere.
STH_warpcapable.65.name: Symbiosis - Unable to fix
STH_warpcapable.66.desc:
The medical team that we sent to investigate the Ornaran disease has come back with mixed results, the lead consultant firmly believes the drug they are desperately seeking has long since cured any traditional illness but has left them with terrible addiction. 

Despite all efforts no cure could be found to help the Ornarans combat this long-term addiction, the only option for them is to choose to go cold turkey, but this is a choice they will have to make for themselves. 

At this stage we cannot interfere any further.
STH_warpcapable.66.name: Symbiosis - Unable to cure
STH_warpcapable.67.desc:
The medical team that we sent to investigate the Ornaran disease has come back with positive results, the lead consultant firmly believes the drug they are desperately seeking has long since cured any traditional illness but has left them with terrible addiction. 

The consultant has however managed to identify a substitute for the Felicium drug that with only a short treatment cycle can completely alleviate the side effects of the drug and give the Ornarans a new lease of life. 

Doing so will likely change the balance between the Brekkians and the Ornarans.
STH_warpcapable.67.name: Symbiosis - Cure found
STH_warpcapable.68.desc:
What have you done?! 

You will have ruined our economy and made an enemy of the Brekkian Alliance this day! 

You may come to regret this action.
STH_warpcapable.68.name: Symbiosis
STH_warpcapable.70.desc: Our invasion of [FROMFROM.GetName] has been successful and we have defeated the [From.GetName] defense forces. Our armies now control the planet. \n\nWe should now decide what to do with [FROMFROM.GetName].
STH_warpcapable.70.name: Invasion of [FROMFROM.GetName] Complete
